Feint / Evaluate / Aim / Ready / WaitSet-up or preparation; no attack this turn (Wait banks one for a trigger)Normal

Defences — how many, and when

One free defence per attack. Each active defence is taken out of turn — it does not cost your maneuver, and you get one against every attack you’re aware of. Defending against a second attack in the same turn costs −4 cumulative (−4, −8, −12…); the trained-weapon discount (TbaM/WM) makes it −2 cumulative for that weapon only. Retreat (+3 Dodge/fencing/unarmed parry, +1 other) is declared on your first defence against a foe and applies to all your defences against that foe that turn; it costs your step. Feverish Defence (+2 for 1 FP) and Acrobatic Dodge (+2/−2) each apply once per turn and not during Committed/All-Out Attack. A counter-defence (Riposte, Aggressive Parry, Counterattack) rides on a successful defence; see Counter-Strike Defenses.

Posture in Context

Crouching is the standout posture for active combat — no defense penalty, −2 to ranged attacks against you. Often used to take cover behind low obstacles or gain a brief defensive edge.

Prone has the most severe defense penalty (−3) and an attacker bonus (+3 close, −2 ranged). Avoid being knocked down — a fallen Force-user is in catastrophic trouble.

Changing Posture

Change Posture Maneuver. Full maneuver to switch postures. No step, no attack. Active defenses defend AS your starting posture. Standing ↔ kneeling / crouching / prone. Prone → standing via normal Change Posture. Cannot change mid-Attack unless using the MA free changes below.

MAFree Posture Changes During Attacks. Per Martial Arts p. 98, lowering posture is FREE within an Attack-class maneuver (Attack, AoA, Committed, Move and Attack) when grappling, slamming, shoving, or striking at reach C. Standing → kneeling/crawling/prone uses step (Attack/Committed) or 1 movement point (AoA/M&A), at end of movement. Kneeling → crawling/prone uses full movement allowance. Standing → sitting/supine (fall backward) uses full movement (or 1 of M&A, at end). Kneeling/sitting → supine: all movement. On battle map, upper body (or lower for face-up) ends in your choice of front, side, or back hex.

MAAcrobatic Stand. From prone, attempt a flip / kip-up / roll to standing in one move. Change Posture maneuver — cannot be done as step of another maneuver. Acrobatics −6 − encumbrance level. Success: stand. Crit success: also use as step of Attack/Committed/Defensive Attack/Feint/Ready. Failure: end turn sitting. Crit fail: remain prone. From sitting/crawling, spring to standing as a STEP of a maneuver: Acrobatics −6 − enc. Success: stand and execute. Failure: stand, but turn consumed. All-Out variant: +4 to Acrobatics, no active defenses for the turn.

Part VII

Encumbrance

Basic Lift · Five tiers · Move, Dodge, and DX-skill effects
Encumbrance Weight Move Dodge DX-Skill Common Equivalent
None≤ BL× 1.000Robes, light clothing, sidearm.
Light≤ 2 × BL× 0.8−1−1Padded armour, leather, light battle armour.
Medium≤ 3 × BL× 0.6−2−2Chainmail, brigandine, scout armour, medium laminate.
Heavy≤ 6 × BL× 0.4−3−3Full plate, heavy battle armour, Sith juggernaut.
Extra-Heavy≤ 10 × BL× 0.2−4−4Beyond practical combat use — siege loads.

Basic Lift

Formula. BL = ST² ÷ 5 kg. ST 10 → BL 20 kg. ST 12 → BL ~29 kg. ST 14 → BL ~39 kg. ST 16 → BL ~51 kg. ST 18 → BL ~65 kg. ST 20 → BL 80 kg. ST 24 → BL ~115 kg.

  • BL = maximum one-handed overhead lift. Encumbrance tiers are multiples of BL.
  • Lifting ST is the relevant stat — modified by the Lifting ST advantage if applicable.

Computing Encumbrance

Total carried weight (armour + weapons + gear) determines the encumbrance tier. Round Move multiplier output DOWN (Move 6 × 0.8 = 4.8 → Move 4). Move and Dodge penalties apply at the full weight of your encumbrance category.

DX-Skill Encumbrance

Effect on DX-Based Skills. Encumbrance penalises DX-based physical skills equal to encumbrance level (−1 Light, −2 Medium, etc.). Applies to Acrobatics, Climbing, Jumping, Stealth, Swimming, and similar. Combat skills (weapon use) are NOT directly penalised — Move and Dodge already account for weight.

MAArmor Familiarity (MA Style Perk). Reduces DX-skill encumbrance for one specific combat skill by one tier. Bought separately per skill — Armor Familiarity (Karate), Armor Familiarity (Broadsword), etc.

Part VIII

Grappling

Grapple · Takedown · Pin · Choke · Breaking free
Action Roll Effect Prerequisites / Notes
GrappleDX, Wrestling, Judo, or grappling skill vs. target’s active defenseTarget at −4 DX, cannot move freelyClose combat (adjacent or same hex).
TakedownQuick Contest: ST or DX (best) vs. target’s ST or DXTarget prone; grapple persistsAfter successful grapple.
PinQuick Contest: ST vs. ST (+3 for two hands, +3 for size)Target helpless until escapeAfter takedown.
Choke (carotid)Quick Contest: ST +3 (2H) vs. HTCr damage; KO on 2nd successful roundAfter neck grapple.
Choke (trachea)Quick Contest: ST +3 (2H) vs. HTCr damage; KO after 10 secondsAfter neck grapple.
Break FreeQuick Contest: ST vs. grappler’s STEscape grapple; step permittedYour full turn — no other action.
Strike a GrapplerNormal attack at −4 (grappled)Damage; pain may force releaseDoesn’t end grapple by itself.

Basic Grapple

Grapple. Roll DX or grappling skill; target makes an active defense. If grapple succeeds and defense fails, target is grappled — at −4 to DX and all DX-based skills. Cannot move except by breaking free or being moved. Grapple targets a body part of your choice — arm, neck, weapon. Close combat only. No direct damage unless you follow up (choke, takedown, etc.).

Takedown & Pin

Takedown. After grapple, attempt to put the foe on the ground. QC: your ST or DX (best) vs. target’s ST or DX. Win: target prone; stay standing or go prone with them. Tie or loss: target stands; grapple persists. Grappled targets already at −4 DX — combine with off-balance penalties as GM judges.

Pin. From a takedown, attempt to pin. QC: ST vs. ST. Bonuses: +3 two hands, +3 size advantage, −4 for being grappled. Win: target helpless. Cannot move or attack until they win a subsequent QC. Pinned foe: hit automatically (attacker rolls to hit, no defense). Pinned foe attempts to escape on each of their turns.

Choke & Strangle

Carotid Choke (Blood). QC: your ST (+3 two hands) vs. target’s HT. Margin = HP of crushing damage. No torso DR (neck is exposed). Targets blood flow to brain. On the SECOND successful round, target rolls HT or falls unconscious. Fastest grappling kill.

Tracheal Choke (Air). QC: your ST (+3 two hands) vs. target’s HT. Margin = HP of crushing damage. No torso DR. Targets airflow. After 10 seconds of successful choking, target rolls HT — failure = unconscious. Slower than carotid but produces sustained pain.

Breaking Free

Break Free. QC: your ST vs. grappler’s ST (with their bonuses). Win: escape; free step permitted same turn. Tie or loss: still grappled. Your full action — no other maneuver, no attack. Defenses normal.

MAStrike the Grappler. Alternative: strike the grappler at −4 (grapple penalty) without breaking free. Pain and damage may force their release. Pain-and-Breaking-Free (MA p. 119): a target in pain (just took damage at a critical area) may get additional escape rolls or temporary bonuses.

Part VIII ½

Saber Lock & Lightning Lock

The blade bind · the Force down the blade · catching lightning

HouseThe Lock. Two fighters whose blades are in contact may stop trading cuts and strain blade-to-blade — the crossed-guard shoving match, a moment of strength, leverage and (for a Force-user) the Force poured down the blade. It resolves as a Quick Contest, but the Saber Lock is a contest of the wielder's Force attribute (ST/DX/Will/HT), not weapon skill — a Force-user's effective saber skill runs as much as +20 above raw, so a skill-based shove would be a foregone conclusion; a raw attribute keeps it a real contest. Force Lightning caught on a lit blade resolves as the same struggle — a Lightning Lock — its FP cost mirroring heavy-energy Tutaminis (1 FP per 4 damage held).

Saber Lock House

What it is. Two melee fighters with blades in contact stop trading cuts and strain blade-to-blade — the crossed-guard shoving match. A moment of strength, leverage, and (for a Force-user) the Force poured down the blade. Usually lightsaber-to-lightsaber, but any two ready melee weapons can lock.

Forming a lock — on purpose. Declare a Saber Lock as your Attack maneuver if, last turn, you parried that foe's attack OR they parried yours OR your blades are already crossed (the same qualifier as Beat). Step permitted. The targeted foe may refuse by retreating ≥1 hex on their turn (a Dodge to disengage; success = no lock, and they make no attack against you that turn). A foe who cannot retreat (wall, ledge, close combat, pinned) is locked regardless.

Forming a lock — by accident. When two lightsabers clash — or a lightsaber and a saber-resistant blade (beskar, cortosis, phrik, songsteel) — i.e. one parries the other, roll 1d; on a 6 the blades catch and a Saber Lock forms whether either fighter meant it or not. (Tune to taste — a 5–6 for more frequent binds.) This only fires blade-on-blade between saber-survivable weapons; an ordinary blade is simply sheared, so it never locks a saber by accident.

Once locked — pick ONE each turn. The contest is the heart of it:

  • Force / sword contest (the main event). A Quick Contest of your Force attribute — the primary attribute your connection runs on (ST = Force Torrent · DX = Blade Velocity · Will = Force Vessel · HT = Tempered Blade): the Force floods the blade through whatever attribute is yours. It is not a Lightsaber-skill roll. A non-Force fighter rolls the higher of ST or DX (leverage or blade control). Strength tells: add +1 per 2 full points of ST you have over your opponent — a Force-Torrent strongman or a powerful Mandalorian leans the bind their way. Other modifiers: +1 two-handed grip · +1 per point of SM advantage · −2 off-balance / kneeling / bad footing (or +1 high ground / braced).
  • Break away (costs your turn). Step out and end the lock — no contest, no other action. But if your opponent did not also break, they take the Bend-the-guard result on you as you go (−2 to your defences vs them, or a free 1-hex shove).
  • Bear down (costs your turn + your defence). An All-Out shove: +2 to the contest this turn, but no active defence until your next turn — the "I win this lock no matter what" option.

Contest outcomes — the winner picks one, scaled by margin of victory.

  • Win by 1–2 — Bend the guard: the loser is −2 to all active defences against you until the end of your next turn; or shove them back 1 hex (they keep their feet).
  • Win by 3–5 — Break the guard: take an immediate free Attack at +2; or shove them 1 hex per 2 points of margin and they roll DX or fall; or a Disarm attempt at +2 (a bonded saber leaps free but answers its owner's hand on a later turn).
  • Win by 6+ — Overpower: as 3–5, and the loser is knocked back and prone (or staggered — your choice); a free Attack taken instead is at +4.
  • Critical success: the win-by-6+ result with the strongest option; you also ignore the multi-defence penalty on your next turn.
  • Tie — Blades stay locked: neither gains; the lock persists into next turn (pick again — contest, break away, or bear down). While locked, both are −2 to defend against anyone else — locking in a crowd is a gamble.
  • You lose: the winner applies their chosen result to you. Critical failure: you overcommit — fall prone, and your opponent gets the win-by-6+ result against you.

Limits & blade survival. Forms that lower a foe's defences or raise your to-hit — Juyo's Overwhelming Tempo, Vaapad's Channel the Current, Ataru's Momentum Strike — stack into the contest as written. SW Blade survival: beskar, cortosis, phrik and songsteel weapons lock a lightsaber safely; an ordinary blade that locks a lit lightsaber is sheared and destroyed at end of turn — so only a saber-resistant weapon, or another saber, can sustain a lock against a lightsaber (and only such weapons can trigger the accidental lock above).

Lightning Lock House SW

Catching the storm. Force Lightning is always parryable with a lit lightsaber (Lightsaber Parry, normal Deflect mode — the blade earths the arc). A successful parry does not merely scatter it: the attacker keeps channelling and a Lightning Lock forms — the storm pouring down the blade while the defender holds it back. (Bare-handed Tutaminis still simply catches the energy as its own power; the Lightning Lock is the saber answer.)

Resolving it. A sustained Quick Contest, re-rolled each turn the attacker keeps channelling. The attacker rolls Force Lightning skill (their casting roll). The defender rolls their Force attribute (ST/DX/Will/HT — the connection holding the storm on the blade), +1 with the Tempered Blade signature or an active Form that aids it; a defender trained in Tutaminis may roll that skill instead. This is not the Lightsaber-Parry skill: the parry that starts the lock can lean on the saber's big skill bonus (so a saber reliably catches lightning — "always parryable"), but holding it is a contest of the wielder's Force connection.

Attrition. Each turn the lock holds, both pay FP: the attacker pays the lightning's normal upkeep; the defender pays 1 FP per 4 points of the lightning's damage they are holding back (the heavy-energy Tutaminis rate). It is a battle of attrition — who runs dry first. Either side may bear down — spend the turn All-Out for +2 to that turn's contest at the cost of any active defence until their next turn.

Outcomes (per turn).

  • Defender wins by 1–2: holds — no damage leaks; the lock continues.
  • Defender wins by 3–5 — ground it: the arc earths out (no damage) and the attacker takes 1d feedback; the defender may end the lock and act freely.
  • Defender wins by 6+ / critical — redirect: hurl the storm back — re-roll the attacker's own Force Lightning attack against the attacker (or another target in line); the attacker defends at −2 (no one expects their own lightning).
  • Tie: the lock holds, both pay FP, re-contest next turn; neither takes another action.
  • Defender loses by 1–2: takes half the lightning's rolled damage (the blade bled the rest); the lock breaks.
  • Defender loses by 3+: full damage and any secondary effect (Stun, etc.); the blade could not hold.

Breaking off. The attacker may stop channelling for free (ends the lock). The defender may Dodge clear on their turn — success ends the lock and the lightning; a failed Dodge takes a parting 1d. A defender running Tempered Blade still reduces any leaked damage by HT ÷ 3 (that power), making the HT-primary the natural lightning-eater.

Part IX

Combinations Reference

What stacks · What does not · Quick lookup

Legal combinations follow below; forbidden ones come after. Each entry is derived from rules stated elsewhere in this document. Combine subject to FP cost, skill caps, and timing. Several entries reference advantages — see GURPS Characters for the source rules; only their interaction with maneuvers is noted here.

Legal Combinations — Comprehensive List

Each table below focuses on a single maneuver, modifier, defense, or action and lists every legal combination it can enter. Some combinations appear in more than one table by design — that way you can find them from whichever side you’re looking from. Subject to FP cost, skill caps, and per-turn timing as stated elsewhere in the document.

Rapid Strike Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Rapid Strike alone2 attacks at −6Same weapon (or two unarmed). −3 with TbaM/WM. Melee only.
+ Deceptive (per hit)2 attacks, each with own −2/−1 tradeEach strike’s Deceptive penalty is independent.
+ Targeted (per hit)2 attacks at separate locationsEach strike picks its own hit location.
+ Telegraphic (per hit)Both at +4 hitFoe at +2 to defense on each strike.
+ Committed (Det)2 attacks at +2 hit, −6 each−2 to other defenses; attacking limb cannot defend.
+ Committed (Strong)2 attacks at +1 dmg, −6 eachThr/sw only. Same defense penalty as Det.
+ Defensive Attack2 attacks at −2 dmg, −6 each+1 to one defense.
+ AoA (Det)2 attacks at +4 hit, −6 eachNo defenses.
+ AoA (Strong)2 attacks at +2 dmg, −6 eachThr/sw only. No defenses.
+ AoA (Long)2 attacks at reach +1, −6 eachSwing at −2 dmg / −1 die. No defenses.
+ AoA (Double)3 attacks total, all at −6One of the two AoA slots becomes a Rapid Strike of 2.
+ Extra Attack 1Substitutes for ONE attack slotRemaining attacks at normal skill.
Cinematic (MA)3+ attacks at −6 each−3 with TbaM/WM. Three attacks at −12 (−6 TbaM). GM may set skill-12 minimum.

Dual-Weapon Attack Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Dual-Weapon Attack alone2 attacks (one per hand) at −4 eachOff-hand suffers extra −4 without Ambidexterity or OHWT. Defender at −1 vs the second attack.
+ Targeted2 attacks at separate locationsEach hand picks its own location.
+ AoA (Double)3 attacks totalOne AoA slot becomes DWA (2 attacks); other slot is one normal attack.
+ AoA (Det)2 attacks at +4 hit, −4 eachNo defenses.
+ AoA (Strong)2 attacks at +2 dmg, −4 eachThr/sw only. No defenses.
+ Committed (Det)2 attacks at +2 hit, −4 each−2 other defenses.
+ Committed (Strong)2 attacks at +1 dmg, −4 eachThr/sw only.
+ Defensive Attack2 attacks at −2 dmg, −4 each+1 to one defense.
DWA techniqueBuys off the −4Hard technique, default skill −4. Improves the off-hand strike specifically.

Combination (MA Technique) Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Combination alone2+ trained strikesBought-off Rapid Strike penalty per technique level. One per maneuver max.
+ Deceptive (per strike)Mix & match per strikeAny or all strikes may be Deceptive.
+ Telegraphic (per strike)Mix & match per strikeAny or all strikes may be Telegraphic.
+ AoA (any variant)AoA bonus to every strikeAoA(Strong) gives +2 dmg to all strikes; AoA(Det) +4 hit to all; etc.
+ Committed (any variant)Committed bonus to every strikeDet: +2 hit on each; Strong: +1 dmg on each.
+ Defensive AttackCautious combo+1 to one defense; all strikes at −2 dmg.
+ Feint setup (prior turn)Penalty applies to whole sequenceFeint margin lowers ONE defense vs all parts.
+ Riposte (from your parry)Counts as a single attackRiposte penalty applies to all parts; only one defense suffers full penalty.

Deceptive Attack Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Deceptive alone−2 hit / −1 foe defenseNo upper limit beyond your skill. Melee only.
+ TargetedPenalties sumFoe defends at penalty against that hit location.
+ Committed (Det)Penalties sum + +2 hitCommitted bonus offsets some Deceptive trade.
+ Committed (Strong)Penalties sum + +1 dmgSame defense penalty as Det.
+ Defensive AttackCautious + deceptive trade+1 to one defense; less damage but foe defends worse.
+ AoA (Det)+4 hit offsets DeceptiveNo defenses, but Deceptive penalty on foe.
+ AoA (Strong)+2 dmg + DeceptiveFoe defends at penalty.
+ AoA (Feint) on the strikeFree Feint margin + Deceptive tradeTwo-stage defense reduction.
+ Rapid Strike (per hit)Each strike own DeceptiveIndependent per-strike.
+ Combination (per strike)Mix & match per strikeAny or all strikes may be Deceptive.
Evaluate counters foe DeceptiveOn YOUR defense, up to net 0Cancels foe Deceptive vs your defense until you attack.
TbaM defense halvingDoes NOT reduce foe DeceptiveMulti-defense halving is separate from Deceptive penalties.

Targeted Attack Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Targeted aloneSkill penalty by locationDamage and wound effects apply to that location (Doc III).
+ DeceptivePenalties sumFoe defends at penalty for that location.
+ TelegraphicLocation penalty offset by +4 hitFoe at +2 to defense.
+ Committed (Det)Location penalty + +2 hitOffsets to roughly even.
+ Committed (Strong)Location penalty + +1 dmgDrives the location effect harder.
+ AoA (Det)+4 hit + locationNo defenses.
+ AoA (Strong)+2 dmg + locationNo defenses. Thr/sw only.
+ AoA (Long)Reach +1 + locationSwing −2 dmg / −1 die.
+ Rapid Strike (per hit)Separate locations per strikeTwo attacks, each targeting different parts.
+ Dual-Weapon AttackEach hand → different locationOne hand at face, other at arm, etc.
+ Wild SwingCap 9 + location penaltyRough hit-and-hope.
Predictability penalty+1 foe defense on 3rd+ useRepeating the same Targeted Attack twice on a foe: third attempt grants +1 defense.

Telegraphic Attack Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Telegraphic alone+4 hit, foe +2 defenseMelee or ranged. Rarely worth taking at high skill.
+ Targeted+4 offsets location penaltyFoe at +2 to defense vs the location.
+ Committed (Det)+6 hit totalFoe still at +2 to defense.
+ Committed (Strong)+4 hit + +1 dmgFoe at +2 to defense.
+ Wild SwingCap 9, then +4 = effective 13Best for hitting unseen foes when accuracy matters.
+ AoA (any variant)+4 hit + AoA bonusFoe still +2 to defense.
+ Rapid Strike (per hit)Each at +4 hitFoe +2 to defense on each strike.
+ Combination (per strike)Mix & match per strikeAny or all strikes may be Telegraphic.
Contradicts DeceptiveMutually exclusiveOne makes foe defend better, other worse — pick one.

Wild Swing Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Wild Swing aloneSkill capped at 9For obscured/unseen targets, backward attacks, pitch dark.
+ TelegraphicCap 9, then +4 = 13Foe at +2 to defense.
+ TargetedCap 9 + location penaltyRarely worth it; mostly desperate.
+ AoA (Det)Cap 9 + +4 hit = 13No defenses.
+ Committed (Det)Cap 9 + +2 hit = 11−2 other defenses.
Not Move and AttackDistinct mechanicWild Swing is about visibility; M&A about movement.

Beat Stacks (requires Feint maneuver)

CombinationNetEffect
Beat aloneQC weapon skill (ST) vs foe (DX or ST)Eligibility: blocked/parried each other last turn, OR foe is grappled.
vs specific ParryWin margin → penalty to that parryVs ALL attackers until end of your next turn.
vs specific BlockWin margin → penalty to that blockSame scope.
vs unarmed guardWin margin → penalty to unarmed defenseSame scope.
vs grappled foe (Dodge)Win margin → penalty to their DodgeSet up follow-up attacks against a held foe.

Ruse Stacks (requires Feint maneuver)

CombinationNetEffect
Ruse aloneQC weapon skill (IQ) vs foe Per/DX skill or TacticsPenalty to one defense against your next attack.
vs wary foeFoe resists with Per-based weapon skillHarder to land.
vs unwary foe (reset guard)Foe resists with DX-based weapon skillEasier.
vs Tactics userFoe resists with TacticsSensible foes see through this.
→ Defensive Feint conversionPenalises their next ATTACK on youUse when you don’t intend to strike that target next.

Feint Maneuver Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Feint maneuver aloneQC of best melee/unarmed/cloak skill vs foe’s same or DXStep permitted. Weapon must be ready. Resisting uses best weapon skill even with unfamiliar weapon.
→ next-turn AttackMargin → penalty to one defenseYour strike that turn.
→ next-turn Defensive FeintMargin → penalty to their attack on youUse when you don’t intend to attack them next.
→ next-turn Riposte (on a parry)Stacks with Riposte penaltyBoth apply to defending foe.
+ Beat (within Feint maneuver)Physical guard-batterTargets one specific defense vs all attackers.
+ Ruse (within Feint maneuver)Verbal/gesture misdirectionPenalises one defense vs your next attack.
+ Combination (next strike)Penalty applies to whole sequenceOnly one defense vs combo suffers full penalty.
Multiple feints same maneuverOnly most recent appliesDon’t stack feints within one Feint maneuver.

Attack (base maneuver) Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Attack alone1 attack, defenses normalStep permitted. Any ready weapon.
+ Rapid Strike2 attacks at −6 each−3 with TbaM/WM. Melee.
+ Dual-Weapon Attack2 attacks (one per hand) at −4Off-hand suffers extra −4 without Ambi/OHWT.
+ CombinationTrained multi-strike sequenceOne per maneuver max.
+ Deceptive / Targeted / Telegraphic / Wild SwingVarious modifiersStack with each other per their own rules.
+ Extra Attack+1 attack per levelAdds to your slot count.
+ Force Push/Pull (QA)Strike + shove or redirectFull FP. 1 QA/turn.
+ Fast-DrawDraw + strikeSkill roll. Crit fail drops weapon.
+ Special Reaction (defensive)Strike + redirect a separate threatSR is on top of active defenses.
+ maintained Concentrate (with CM)Strike + sustain a Force powerForce Speed, Grip, etc.
Aim bonus carry-over+Acc to ranged shotIf you Aimed last turn and didn’t take another maneuver.
Evaluate bonus carry-over+1 / +2 / +3 to hitMelee, against that foe.

Defensive Attack Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Defensive Attack alone−2 dmg / −1 die. +1 one defenseOr +2 vs Leg Grapples / +2 DX vs falling for kicks. Step permitted.
+ Rapid Strike2 attacks at −2 dmg, −6 each+1 to one defense.
+ Dual-Weapon Attack2 attacks (one per hand) at −2 dmg, −4 each+1 to one defense.
+ CombinationAll strikes at −2 dmg+1 to one defense.
+ DeceptiveCautious probe with deceptive trade−2 dmg, plus the Deceptive penalty exchange.
+ Targeted−2 dmg + location penalty+1 to one defense.
+ grappling attemptTarget +1 to defendCost of using DA to grab/grapple.
Balanced weapon+1 Parry SAME weapon allowedException to normal rules.
Unbalanced (U) weapon+1 Parry DIFFERENT weaponOR parry with same at no bonus (exception to U-rule).

Committed Attack (Determined) Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Committed (Det) alone+2 hit. −2 other defenses. Attacking limb can’t defend. No retreat.Step or two steps (2nd → −2 hit; net +0 with two steps).
+ Rapid Strike2 attacks each at +2 hit, −6 each−2 other defenses.
+ Dual-Weapon Attack2 attacks (one per hand) at +2 hit, −4 each−2 other defenses.
+ Combination+2 hit on every strikeOne Combination max.
+ Deceptive+2 hit offsets some Deceptive tradeNet positive on hit roll.
+ Targeted+2 hit offsets location penaltyLighter hit penalty.
+ Telegraphic+6 hit (4+2), foe +2 defenseMaximum hit-force.
+ two steps (attack-and-fly-out)Net +0 hitStep in, attack, step out. Tactical mobility.

Committed Attack (Strong) Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Committed (Strong) alone+1 dmg. −2 other defenses. Attacking limb can’t defend. No retreat.Thr/sw only — not lightsabers.
+ Rapid Strike2 attacks at +1 dmg, −6 each−3 with TbaM. Thr/sw only.
+ Dual-Weapon Attack2 attacks (one per hand) at +1 dmg, −4 eachThr/sw only.
+ Combination+1 dmg on every strikeThr/sw only.
+ TargetedLocation penalty + +1 dmgDrive location effects harder.
+ DeceptivePenalties sum + +1 dmgFoe defends worse, slightly more damage.
High-ST scaling+1 dmg per 2 full dice of basic damageGM’s option for very strong attackers.

Move and Attack Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Move and Attack aloneFull Move + 1 attackMelee cap 9 or DX+2. Ranged −2 or Bulk. No retreat.
+ DeceptiveCap 9 still appliesLimited utility unless your skill is very high.
+ TargetedCap 9 + location penaltyRough but possible.
+ TelegraphicCap 9 + +4 = effective 13Foe +2 to defense.
+ Force Push/Pull (QA)Charge + shove or redirectFull FP. Offensive or defensive QA.
+ Special Reaction (def)Charge + redirect a separate threatDefensive SR allowed (not AoA).
+ Fast-DrawCharge + draw + strikeCrit fail drops weapon.

All-Out Attack (Determined) Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
AoA (Det) alone+4 hit, single attack. NO defenses.Step or full Move; attack during or at end.
+ Rapid Strike2 attacks each at +4 hit, −6 each−3 with TbaM/WM.
+ Dual-Weapon Attack2 attacks (one per hand) at +4 hit, −4 eachOff-hand penalty applies.
+ Combination+4 hit on every strikeOne Combination max.
+ Deceptive+4 hit offsets Deceptive tradeFoe defends at penalty.
+ Targeted+4 hit offsets location penaltyLighter location penalty net.
+ Telegraphic+8 hit totalFoe +2 to defense. Maximum hit chance.
Aim bonus carry-over+4 hit + accumulated AccHeavy ranged staple.
+ offensive Force Push (QA)Shove + +4 hit strikeFull FP. 1 QA/turn.
+ offensive Special ReactionPull/push foe + AoA strikeFull FP. Force-only.
+ Fast-DrawDraw + AoA strike same turnUseful for sudden engagement.

All-Out Attack (Strong) Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
AoA (Strong) alone+2 dmg / +1 per die, single attack. NO defenses.Thr/sw only — NOT lightsabers.
+ Rapid Strike2 attacks at +2 dmg, −6 eachThr/sw only.
+ Dual-Weapon Attack2 attacks (one per hand) at +2 dmg, −4 eachThr/sw only on both.
+ Combination+2 dmg on every strikeIf strikes are thr/sw.
+ TargetedLocation penalty + +2 dmgDrive location effects very hard.
+ Deceptive+2 dmg + Deceptive tradeFoe defends worse, hits harder.
+ Telegraphic+4 hit + +2 dmgFoe +2 to defense.
+ offensive QA / SR / Fast-DrawSetup or drawAs with AoA (Det).

All-Out Attack (Double) Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
AoA (Double) alone2 attacks. NO defenses.Same or different targets. No Extra Attack required.
+ Rapid Strike (one slot)3 attacks total, all at −6One AoA slot becomes RS of 2.
+ Dual-Weapon Attack (one slot)3 attacks total (DWA + 1 normal)One AoA slot becomes DWA of 2 (one per hand).
+ Feint (one slot)1 attack + free FeintSubstitute one of the two attacks with a Feint.
+ Extra Attack 13 attacks totalBase + Extra + Double.
+ Extra Attack 1 + Rapid Strike4 attacks totalThree slots; one becomes RS of 2.
+ Deceptive (per attack)Each independentEach strike makes its own −2/−1 trade.
+ Targeted (per attack)Each strike → different locationOne attack to face, one to torso, etc.
+ Telegraphic (per attack)Each at +4 hit, foe +2 defenseIndependent.
+ offensive Force Push (QA)Shove + 2 attacksFull FP.
+ offensive Special ReactionPull/push + 2 attacksFull FP. Force-only.
+ Fast-DrawDraw + 2 attacksUseful when starting a turn unarmed.

All-Out Attack (Feint) Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
AoA (Feint) aloneFree Feint + 1 attack. NO defenses.Same target. Melee only.
+ Deceptive on the strikeFeint margin + Deceptive tradeTwo-stage defense reduction.
+ Targeted on the strikeFeint margin + location penaltyLocation modifier on the follow-up.
+ Telegraphic on the strike+4 hit + Feint marginFoe at +2 to defense, but Feint cuts that too.
+ Combination on the strikeFeint margin to all partsCounts as one attack; only one defense per part suffers full penalty.
+ Rapid Strike on the strikeFree Feint + 2 strikes at −6Free Feint applies to the first strike’s defense.

All-Out Attack (Long) Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
AoA (Long) aloneReach +1, no hit penalty. Thr full dmg, sw −2 dmg / −1 die. NO defenses.Melee only.
+ Rapid Strike2 attacks at reach +1, −6 eachSwing at −2 dmg.
+ TargetedLocation penalty + reach +1Reach the eye, neck, etc. otherwise out of range.
+ Deceptive−2 hit per −1 defense + reach +1Foe defends at penalty.
+ Telegraphic+4 hit + reach +1Foe +2 to defense.
+ CombinationEach strike at reach +1Sustained extended-reach assault.

All-Out Defense (Increased) Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
AoD (Increased) alone+2 to ONE chosen defenseStep permitted. No attacks. Other defenses normal.
+ Retreat (eligible defense)+5 on the one defense+2 AoD + +3 retreat (Dodge or fencing/Box/Judo/Karate parry).
+ Retreat (other parry/Block)+3 on the one defense+2 AoD + +1 retreat.
+ Acrobatic Dodge+4 Dodge+2 AoD + +2 acrobatics on success.
+ Feverish Defense+4 on the one defense+2 AoD + 1 FP for +2.
+ Defensive Grip+3 Parry frontal+2 AoD + +1 grip.
+ Combat ReflexesBase +1 addedStacks with all of the above.
Balanced variant (MA)+1 to TWO different defense typesTrade the single +2 for spread coverage.
+ offensive Force Push (QA)Defensive stance + offensive setupSet up next turn while staying safe.
+ defensive Special ReactionSuper-defensive turnBuffed defenses + SR on a separate threat.

All-Out Defense (Double) Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
AoD (Double) alone2 defense rolls per attackDifferent types, OR two parries with different weapons. NOT two Dodges. Step. No attacks.
+ Retreat (on one defense)+3 to one of the two rollsOnly the first defense gets retreat bonus.
+ Acrobatic DodgeOne of two is acrobatic Dodge ±2Multi-defense penalty applies to second.
+ Feverish Defense+2 on one of the two rollsPer Feverish rules.
+ Defensive Grip+1 Parry frontal on relevant rollStance bonus.
Multi-defense penaltySecond defense at −4 (or −2 with TbaM/WM only if defending with the trained weapon/skill HOUSE)Cumulative if you stack with more defenses elsewhere.

Aim Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Aim (1 turn) alone+Acc to next ranged attackStep permitted; do not turn more than one hex face.
Aim (extra turns)+1 per turn, cap +2 over AccDiminishing returns past 3 turns.
→ Attack+Acc on the shotPlus any modifiers you apply.
→ AoA (Det)+Acc + +4 hitHeavy ranged staple.
→ Attack + Targeted / TelegraphicModifiers apply after AimNote: Deceptive is melee only.
Will roll on hitRetain Aim if successfully hitFail → lose accumulated Aim.
Multiple targetsRequires Enhanced TrackingOtherwise restricted to one Aim target.

Evaluate Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Evaluate (1 turn) alone+1 to hit next attack on that foeStep permitted.
Evaluate (2 turns)+2 to hitSustained study.
Evaluate (3+ turns)+3 to hit (cap)Maximum bonus.
→ Attack / Committed / AoA+Evaluate bonus appliedSpent on the strike.
While defendingCancels foe Deceptive on YOUR defenseUp to net 0, until you attack and claim the bonus.

Retreat Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Retreat (Dodge or fencing/Box/Judo/Karate Parry)+3 to that defenseOnce per turn. Melee. Costs your step.
Retreat (other Parry / Block)+1 to that defenseSame scope.
+ Combat Reflexes+1 base on top+4 Dodge total with retreat.
+ Acrobatic Dodge+5 Dodge (max)+3 retreat + +2 acrobatics.
+ AoD (Increased Dodge)+5 Dodge+3 retreat + +2 AoD.
+ AoD (Increased Parry, fencing)+5 Parry+3 retreat + +2 AoD.
+ Feverish Defense+5 on the one defense+3 retreat + 1 FP for +2.
+ Defensive Grip+4 Parry frontal+3 retreat + +1 grip.
+ Defensive Attack benefit+4 on one defense+3 retreat + +1 from DA.
Via Dive / Sideslip / SlipRetreat applied through MA variantSee those tables for net effects.
Movement does not multiplyRetreat is one hexCannot stack with other movement bonuses.

Acrobatic Dodge Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Acrobatic Dodge aloneAcrobatics roll → +2 / −2 DodgeOnce per turn. Crit fail = fall.
+ Retreat+5 Dodge (success)+3 retreat + +2 acrobatics.
+ AoD (Increased Dodge)+4 Dodge (success)+2 AoD + +2 acrobatics.
+ Combat Reflexes+1 base addedStacks with all of the above.
Risk on failure−2 Dodge on failDon’t attempt unless your Acrobatics is solid.

Dive Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Dive alone−1 + retreat bonusStanding or kneeling → prone under a melee attack.
End proneFuture attackers: +3 to hitTrade-off: defense now, vulnerability after.
+ Combat Reflexes+1 baseMitigates the −1.
Use caseCornered with allies nearbyAllies can cover your prone state next turn.

Sideslip Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Sideslip alone−1 + retreat bonusStanding only. Step sideways — same distance from foe.
+ Dodge or fencing/Box/Judo/Karate ParryNet +2 on that defense−1 sideslip + +3 retreat = +2.
+ other Parry / BlockNet 0−1 sideslip + +1 retreat = 0.
Use caseCannot cede groundGeometry advantage without backing off.

Slip Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Slip alone−2 + retreat bonusStanding only. Step CLOSER to foe (potentially into close combat).
+ Dodge or fencing/Box/Judo/Karate ParryNet +1Best case.
+ other Parry / BlockNet −1Worst case.
vs Stop ThrustAdditional −1Their thrust gains velocity bonus on you.
Use caseDistance more dangerous than closingGet inside reach of polearm wielder, etc.

Feverish Defense Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Feverish Defense alone1 FP → +2 to one defenseOnce per turn. Crit fail: 1 HP injury.
+ Retreat (eligible)+5 on one defense+3 retreat + +2 FP.
+ AoD (Increased)+4 on one defense+2 AoD + +2 FP.
+ Defensive Attack +1+3 on one defense+1 DA + +2 FP.
+ Defensive Grip +1+3 Parry frontal+1 grip + +2 FP.
Forbidden during Committed / AoAMutually exclusiveCannot use during those maneuvers.

Sacrificial Dodge Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Sacrificial Dodge aloneDodge −2; take hit for allyStanding, crouching, or kneeling only — not prone.
On successAlly safe, you end proneMulti-defense penalty applies afterwards to your own defenses.
On failureYou take the hit, end proneDefensive sacrifice with full risk.
Forbidden after AoA / Committed / M&A this turnConditionalYou must have defensive capacity to volunteer it.

Riposte Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Riposte aloneTrade 1–4 from Parry; next attack forces penalised defenseFloor 8 incl. Enhanced Parry and Combat Reflexes.
+ prior FeintBoth penalties apply to RiposteFeint margin + Riposte penalty stack.
+ prior BeatDefense penalty combinedBeat’s penalty + Riposte’s penalty.
+ Combination on the RiposteCounts as single attackRiposte penalty applies to all parts; only one defense suffers full penalty.
Defense type mappingParried weapon → Parry penalty; shield → Block; unarmed → DodgeHalf (drop fractions) to OTHER defenses they try against the Riposte.
Cannot + Deceptive on RiposteRiposte IS deceptiveDouble-dip forbidden.
Cannot + Counterattack techniqueSame chainMutually exclusive — pick one.
Cannot + Defensive Attack +1Both consume defense capacityOne defensive payoff at a time.

Counterattack (Technique) Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Counterattack aloneAfter Dodge / Parry / Block → next attack at −2 foe defenseHard tech, default skill −5. Per combat skill.
+ Feint setupFeint margin + −2 Counterattack penaltyCombined defense reduction.
+ Targeted on CounterattackLocation penalty + −2 defenseDrive home a precise hit.
+ Telegraphic on Counterattack+4 hit + −2 defenseFoe +2 to defense, but net is still favourable.
Cannot + RiposteSame chainPick one.
Cannot during AoARequires prior defenseAoA forbids active defenses.

Stop Hit Stacks (Wait variant)

CombinationNetEffect
Stop Hit aloneBoth attack; lower margin defends at −1 (or −3 if parrying with attacking weapon)Wait spent; cannot parry after.
+ unbalanced weaponLegal exceptionStop Hit + parry are one move.
Tie outcomeBoth penalties applyBoth fighters defend at penalty.
Use casePredicting an incoming attackTrade tempo and defense for damage timing.

Stop Thrust Stacks (Wait variant)

CombinationNetEffect
Stop Thrust aloneWait + thrust into charging foe+1 damage per 2 hexes of their Move, max +3.
vs foe using Slip−1 to their defenseThey’re closing the gap into your thrust.
Knockdown interruptsFoe’s attack cancelledMassive HP loss can stop their charge entirely.
Cannot + parry same weaponStop Thrust consumes your attackUse a different weapon to parry, if any.

Aggressive Parry Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Aggressive Parry aloneParry −2 → thrust damage to attacking limbPer Karate or weapon skill. Crit fail: self-injury.
+ Retreat−2 + retreat bonusMitigates the parry penalty.
+ Combat Reflexes+1 base on parryMitigates the −2.
Use caseCrippling vs deflectingDamage their weapon arm or hand HP.

Hand-Clap Parry Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Hand-Clap Parry aloneKarate −2 → parries AND grapples weaponSmall weapons only (knives, fists, short blades).
RestrictionNOT polearms or lightsabersReach and danger preclude the move.
+ Combat Reflexes+1 base on parryHelps the −2.
Use caseDisarm via grappleHighly cinematic; control the weapon afterwards.

Defensive Grip Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Defensive Grip alone+1 Parry frontal; −2 attack; reach −1Switching in or out is a Ready maneuver.
+ Retreat+4 Parry frontal+1 grip + +3 retreat.
+ AoD (Increased Parry)+3 Parry frontal+1 grip + +2 AoD.
+ Feverish Defense+3 Parry frontal+1 grip + +2 FP.
Trade-off−2 to your own attacksOptimised for defense, not aggression.

Force Push / Pull as Quick Action Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Force Push/Pull QA aloneFull FP cost; once per turnRange and effect as standard power. Offensive or defensive use.
+ AttackStrike + shove or redirectOffensive shove or defensive redirect.
+ Defensive AttackCautious + shove+1 to one defense.
+ Move and AttackCharge + shoveKnock foe off-line as you arrive.
+ AoA (offensive use only)Shove + AoA strikeDefensive uses forbidden during AoA.
+ Concentrate (different power, with CM)Shove + maintain a Force powerRequires Compartmentalised Mind.
+ AoDDefensive stance + offensive setupStay safe, set up next turn.
Cannot during defensive AoADefensive QA forbidden in AoAOnly offensive QA + Fast-Draw allowed.

Special Reaction Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Special Reaction aloneForce-only, once per turn, full FPON TOP of active defense — does NOT consume defense slot.
+ Active DefenseBoth applySR redirects a separate threat; active defense handles the primary attack.
+ Attack (defensive SR)Strike a foe + redirect a separate threatBolt off-line, ally pushed clear, etc.
+ AoA (offensive SR only)Push/pull foe + AoA strikeDefensive SR forbidden during AoA.
+ AoDSuper-defensive turnBuffed defenses + SR.
+ Move and Attack (def SR)Charge + redirectDefensive SR allowed in M&A.
+ Vaapad (Doc IV)NO — shares Reaction slotPick one per turn.
Forbidden while unconscious / stunnedMust perceive the threatConditional.

Fast-Draw Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Fast-Draw aloneSkill roll; success = weapon drawn as QACritical failure drops the weapon.
+ Combat Reflexes+1 to Fast-DrawImproved success odds.
+ AttackDraw + strikeStandard combo.
+ AoA (any variant)Draw + AoA strikeAllowed even during AoA.
+ Move and AttackCharge + draw + strikeDynamic engagement.
LimitationHeavy or two-handed weaponsFast-Draw typically not available.

Concentrate Stacks (with Compartmentalised Mind)

CombinationNetEffect
Concentrate aloneMaintain one Concentrate Force powerStep permitted; no attacks. Will roll on hit to maintain.
+ another Concentrate (with CM)Two simultaneous powerse.g., Force Speed + Force Grip.
+ Attack (with CM)One mental + one physicalStrike with saber, sustain a power.
+ Defensive Attack (with CM)SameCautious strike + maintained power.
+ Move and Attack (with CM)SameCharge + maintained power.
+ AoA (with CM)SameAggressive strike + maintained power.
Instant Force powersFire from normal action slotNot Concentrate — separate mechanic.

Grapple Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Grapple aloneDX or grappling skill vs target’s active defenseClose combat only. Target at −4 DX, cannot move freely.
→ TakedownQC ST/DX (best) vs theirsWin = foe prone, grapple persists.
→ Choke (carotid or tracheal)After neck grappleQC ST (+3 two hands) vs HT.
→ Strike at −4Attack the grappled foeDoesn’t end the grapple.
+ Defensive Attack (grab attempt)Target +1 to defendCost of using DA to initiate.
+ Beat (target their Dodge)Grappled foe’s Dodge is valid Beat targetSetup for follow-up attackers.
Two-handed Grapple+3 bonusFor grappling a specific body part with both hands.

Takedown Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Takedown alone (requires Grapple)QC: your ST/DX (best) vs target’s ST/DXWin = foe prone, grapple persists. Tie/loss = foe stands.
→ PinQC ST vs ST+3 two hands, +3 size, −4 for being grappled.
+ off-balance modifierGrappled target already at −4 DXGM combines with prone penalties as appropriate.
ChoiceStay standing or go prone with foeYour call after winning.

Pin Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Pin alone (requires Takedown)QC ST vs ST+3 two hands, +3 size advantage. Win = foe helpless.
→ Auto-hit attacksAttacker rolls to hit; no defensePinned foe cannot defend at all.
Pinned foe escape attemptEach of their turns: QC to escapeMust win to free themselves.
Pinned foe cannotMove or attack until they win QCTotal incapacitation.

Carotid Choke Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Carotid Choke alone (requires neck Grapple)QC ST (+3 2H) vs HTCrushing damage (margin = HP). No torso DR.
Second successful roundHT roll or KOFastest grappling kill.
Two-handed Choke+3 to your STBoth hands required.
Targets blood flow to brainMechanical effectFaster than tracheal, less painful.

Tracheal Choke Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Tracheal Choke alone (requires neck Grapple)QC ST (+3 2H) vs HTCrushing damage (margin = HP). No torso DR.
After 10 successful secondsHT roll or KOSlower than carotid.
Two-handed Choke+3 to your STBoth hands required.
Targets airflowSustained painMore interrogation-style than execution.

Break Free / Strike a Grappler Stacks

CombinationNetEffect
Break Free aloneQC ST vs grappler’s ST (with their bonuses)Win = escape + free step. Tie/loss = still grappled. Full turn.
Strike a Grappler (alternative)Attack at −4 (grapple penalty)Damage; doesn’t end the grapple by itself.
+ Pain-and-Breaking-Free (MA)Bonus escape rolls after damageA target in pain (just took damage at a critical area) may get additional escape rolls.
Cannot Retreat from a grappleMust Break Free firstRetreat is unavailable while grappled.

Forbidden Combinations — Comprehensive List

Every combination below is illegal based on the rules in this document. Organised by category for quick lookup. ”Forbidden” means the maneuver/option cannot be selected; ”blocked” means the underlying action cannot happen for a stated reason.

Action Economy

  • Two Maneuvers in a single turn (one Maneuver per turn — no exceptions).
  • Two physical attacks in a turn without Extra Attack, AoA(Double), Rapid Strike, Dual-Weapon Attack, or Combination.
  • Two Quick Actions in a turn unless a specific power grants more.
  • Two Special Reactions in a turn (1/turn cap).
  • Special Reaction AND Vaapad’s Absorbed Fury (Doc IV) in the same turn — they share one Reaction slot.
  • Special Reaction while unconscious, stunned, or unable to perceive the threat.
  • Defensive Quick Action OR defensive Special Reaction during any All-Out Attack (offensive uses + Fast-Draw remain permitted).

Ranged Weapons — Forbidden Modifiers

  • Rapid Strike with a ranged weapon (use Rate of Fire / Rapid Fire — Doc II).
  • Deceptive Attack with a ranged weapon.
  • Beat with a ranged weapon.
  • Ruse with a ranged weapon.
  • Feint, Defensive Feint, or AoA(Feint) with a ranged weapon (Feint requires Melee, Unarmed, or Cloak).
  • Combination with a ranged weapon.
  • Riposte with a ranged weapon (Riposte requires Parry — ranged weapons cannot parry).
  • Aggressive Parry or Hand-Clap Parry against a ranged attack at full effect (Hand-Clap is for small melee weapons only).

Move and Attack — Forbidden Combinations

  • Rapid Strike during M&A.
  • Combination during M&A.
  • Dual-Weapon Attack during M&A (M&A is one attack).
  • Feint during M&A (Feint is a separate maneuver).
  • Beat or Ruse during M&A (both require the Feint maneuver).
  • Retreat during M&A.
  • Melee skill higher than 9 or DX+2 (whichever is higher) — hit cap.
  • Ranged attack without −2 or full Bulk penalty (whichever is worse).

Committed Attack — Forbidden Combinations

  • Defensive Attack benefit during Committed Attack (the maneuvers are mutually exclusive).
  • Riposte on the parry during Committed Attack — your defenses already at −2, no defensive capacity to trade.
  • Feverish Defense during Committed Attack.
  • Retreat during Committed Attack.
  • Defending with the attacking limb at all (no parry with attacking hand, no block with attacking shield, no dodge if you kicked).
  • Committed Attack (Strong) with a lightsaber (Strong requires ST-based thrust/swing damage).

All-Out Attack — Forbidden Combinations

  • Any active defense — Dodge, Parry, or Block — for the entire turn.
  • Retreat during AoA.
  • Riposte during AoA (requires a Parry).
  • Counterattack (technique) — requires a successful prior defense, which AoA forbids.
  • Stop Hit (Wait variant) — Wait and AoA are different maneuvers, and Stop Hit needs defensive capacity.
  • Stop Thrust — also a Wait variant.
  • Aggressive Parry, Hand-Clap Parry, Defensive Grip benefit (all require defense).
  • Sacrificial Dodge same turn (the rule explicitly bars it if you used AoA this turn).
  • Defensive Quick Action during AoA (offensive QA + Fast-Draw still allowed).
  • Defensive Special Reaction during AoA (offensive SR still allowed).
  • Feverish Defense during AoA.
  • AoA(Strong) with a lightsaber (Strong requires ST-based thrust/swing damage).
  • AoA(Long) full swing damage — swing must take −2 dmg or −1 per die.

All-Out Defense — Forbidden Combinations

  • Any attack during AoD.
  • AoD(Double): two Dodges against the same attack (must be two different types, or two parries with different weapons).
  • AoD(Increased) Balanced variant: bonus to two defenses of the SAME type (must be two DIFFERENT defense types, +1 each).
  • Step beyond one hex (full Move is forbidden).

Attack Modifier Conflicts

  • Rapid Strike + Dual-Weapon Attack in the same maneuver.
  • Rapid Strike + Combination in the same maneuver.
  • Dual-Weapon Attack + Combination in the same maneuver.
  • More than ONE Combination in a single maneuver.
  • Telegraphic + Deceptive on the same attack — they contradict (one gives the foe better defense, the other forces worse defense).
  • Wild Swing + Targeted Attack on the same swing (Wild Swing caps skill at 9; precise location targeting requires you to see clearly).
  • Repeating the same Targeted Attack three or more times on one foe (third and later attempts grant +1 to their defense — predictability).
  • Combination from Move and Attack maneuver.

Defense Conflicts

  • Dodge against an attack you do not perceive (rear arc unless aware; invisible foes; pitch dark).
  • Parry without a ready weapon.
  • Block without a ready shield, buckler, or cloak.
  • Parry with an unbalanced (“U”) weapon the turn after attacking with it.
  • Off-hand Parry without the −4 penalty unless you have Ambidexterity or Off-Hand Weapon Training for that skill.
  • Lightsaber Parry without a ready Lightsaber.
  • Any active defense against an unperceived rear-arc attack. If the attacker is behind you and you don’t know they’re there, you get NO Dodge, Parry, or Block — the hit lands if their attack roll succeeds. You must first become aware (Per roll, Combat Reflexes, or Danger Sense) to defend at all — then at −4, or −2 with 360° Vision.
  • More than one active defense vs. the same attack (unless using AoD(Double) — but the second defense still suffers multi-defense penalty).

Movement-Based Defense Conflicts

  • Retreat more than once per turn.
  • Retreat if you have already stepped this turn (Retreat IS your step).
  • Retreat from a grapple (you must Break Free first).
  • Retreat during Committed Attack, Move and Attack, or AoA.
  • Sacrificial Dodge from prone.
  • Sacrificial Dodge if you used AoA, Committed Attack, or Move and Attack this turn.
  • Acrobatic Dodge more than once per turn.
  • Acrobatic Stand as a step of another maneuver (must be the Change Posture maneuver, with exceptions for sitting/crawling on crit success).

Counter-Strike Conflicts

  • Riposte + Counterattack (technique) on the same defense → attack chain — mutually exclusive.
  • Riposte + Deceptive Attack on the Riposte itself — Riposte IS a Deceptive Attack mechanically.
  • Defensive Attack benefit + Riposte on the same parry (both consume defensive capacity).
  • Riposte reducing your Parry below 8 (after Enhanced Parry and Combat Reflexes).
  • Stop Hit + parry after it fires (Wait is spent).
  • Stop Thrust + parry with the same weapon (Stop Thrust consumes your attack).
  • Stop Hit, Stop Thrust, Riposte, or Counterattack during AoA (no defense to act on).
  • Counterattack without a prior successful Dodge, Parry, or Block.
  • Counterattack without having bought the specific technique for that skill.

Extra Effort & Force Restrictions

  • Feverish Defense during Committed Attack or AoA.
  • Feverish Defense more than once per turn.
  • Concentrate as a Quick Action (Concentrate IS a Maneuver).
  • Two Concentrate maneuvers in one turn without Compartmentalised Mind.
  • Attack AND Concentrate-tagged Force power same turn without CM.
  • Two simultaneous Force powers without CM.
  • Special Reaction without paying full FP cost (no Quick Action discount).
  • Force Push/Pull as a Quick Action without full FP cost.

Aim / Evaluate / Feint Restrictions

  • Aim at more than one target across multiple turns without Enhanced Tracking.
  • Aim and a different maneuver in the same turn — Aim IS the maneuver.
  • Aim bonus retained after stun, change of target, another maneuver, or a successful hit on you (Will roll to retain on hit).
  • Aim bonus higher than +2 over Acc, no matter how many turns spent.
  • Evaluate followed by anything other than attacking that foe to claim the bonus (otherwise the bonus is lost).
  • Multiple Feints in a single Feint maneuver — only the most recent applies.
  • Feint, Beat, or Ruse without ready weapon (or fist for Unarmed).

Grappling Prerequisites

  • Grapple from outside close combat (target must be adjacent or in the same hex).
  • Takedown without a prior successful Grapple.
  • Pin without a prior successful Takedown.
  • Choke (carotid or tracheal) without first grappling the neck.
  • Break Free with any other maneuver or attack the same turn (Break Free consumes your full action).
  • Strike a Grappler without the −4 grapple penalty (unless you Break Free first).
  • Two-handed Grapple / Choke bonus without both hands free.

Posture-Based Restrictions

  • Step during Do Nothing or Change Posture.
  • Full Move during Aim, Evaluate, Feint, Concentrate, Ready, Wait, Attack, Defensive Attack, or AoD (only step permitted).
  • Active defenses at standing values while crouching, kneeling, sitting, crawling, prone, or supine — use the posture penalty.
  • Standard Move while crawling (1 hex / turn).
  • Any Move while prone or supine.

Common Turns at a Glance

Standard saber duel. Attack with Lightsaber + maintained Force Speed (Concentrate, second compartment via CM). Add Deceptive −4/−2 if skill is high enough.

Heavy ranged. Aim last turn → AoA (Det) this turn. +4 hit + full Acc + accumulated Aim. No defenses, but no need versus soft targets.

Defensive saber. AoD (Increased Parry) + retreat. +2 Parry + +3 retreat = +5 on the one parry vs. priority threat. Use Special Reaction to redirect a separate bolt.

High-skill saber burst. AoA (Double) + Rapid Strike on one attack = 3 attacks. TbaM → RS at −3 each. Add Deceptive −2/−1 on the decider. No defenses.

Ranged screen for an ally. Sacrificial Dodge to intercept a critical hit on a downed teammate. Dodge −2. On success, bolt misses. On failure, you take it and end prone.

Closing the Core Reference

This closes the core combat rules. A few supplementary sections follow — battle stances and finer points of melee — after which, continue to:

  • Document II — Ranged Combat.
  • Document III — Hit Locations, Damage & Wounds.
  • Document IV — The Force, Lightsaber Forms & Star Wars Rules.
  • Document V — Social Mechanics & Out of Combat.

All rulings at GM’s discretion. The Force & the Fight — GURPS 4e · Martial Arts.

Battle Stances — Fighting Styles of the Galaxy

Not every warrior touches the Force. Across the galaxy, soldiers, hunters, duelists, and killers train in battle stances — disciplined ways of holding the body and mind that trade one advantage for another. A fighter adopts a stance as a free declaration at the start of their turn (or by spending a Ready maneuver to shift mid-fight); its benefit lasts while its conditions hold and ends the moment the trade-off is violated.

Using a stance. You may know any number of stances (treat each as a 1-point Perk if your GM tracks it, or as free flavour for trained fighters). You may be in one stance at a time. Switching is a free action at the top of your turn unless noted. A stance’s bonuses are situational modifiers, not flat power — they stack with skill, never replace it.

Reading the cards. Focus tells you whether the stance favours blaster work, melee, or a mix. Stance is the edge you gain; Trade-off is what you give up to hold it. Origins are cultural — anyone may learn any stance, but they are taught within the traditions named.

Mandalorian Iron Wall

Mixed · Mandalorian (Neo-Crusader drill)
Hold the line in beskar; let them break on you.
Stance — While you take no step, +2 to all active defenses, ignore the first 2 points of shock penalty each turn, and reduce knockback against you by half. A foe who fails a melee attack against you by 5+ leaves an opening: you may immediately Parry-riposte.
Trade-off — You may not Move and Attack in the same turn; surrender ground and you lose the bonus.

Mandalorian Hunter’s Crouch

Blaster · Mandalorian (bounty-hunter tradition)
Patience, then a single clean bolt.
Stance — Bracing one knee: +2 to hit with any blaster at Aim, your first shot each combat ignores 2 points of the target’s cover or armour DR, and Aim accumulates an extra +1 per turn (to a max of +3 bonus).
Trade-off — Move is halved while crouched; rising is a Change Posture.

Supercommando Sweep

Blaster · Mandalorian (supercommando doctrine)
Volume of fire is its own kind of armour.
Stance — Suppressive fire stance: +2 to hit when firing on full-auto or making Rapid Strikes with a blaster carbine or rifle, treat the weapon’s Rcl as 1 lower, and you may suppress a 2-metre area forcing enemies in it to take cover or be at -2.
Trade-off — -1 to your own active defenses while sweeping.

Echani Mirror Stance

Melee · Echani (mirror-duel art)
To know how one fights is to know who they are.
Stance — Reading the opponent: after one turn observing a single foe, +3 to Parry and +2 to hit that foe unarmed or with a blade, and you may attempt a free disarm on any successful parry against them.
Trade-off — The read is lost if you switch targets or are surprised.

Echani Flowing Hand

Melee · Echani (unarmed tradition)
The body answers before the mind asks.
Stance — Unarmed redirection: +2 to Parry unarmed, a successful parry lets you attempt a Judo throw at no penalty, and you may parry twice per turn without the multiple-defense penalty.
Trade-off — Only works unarmed or with light weapons; no benefit in heavy armour.

Republic Line Stance

Blaster · Republic Army (infantry drill)
Shoulder to shoulder, the line holds.
Stance — Disciplined firing line: +2 to hit with blaster rifles when an ally is within three metres, you may shift Aim between targets freely, and the formation grants +1 to your active defenses against ranged fire.
Trade-off — The bonus vanishes if you are flanked or fighting alone.

Republic Guard Posture

Mixed · Republic (officer / honour-guard)
The body between the blade and the one who matters.
Stance — Protective stance over a chosen ally: +3 to defend that ally with Block or a parry, +2 to your own Block, and you may interpose against an attack on them as your Reaction (once per turn).
Trade-off — -1 to your own attacks while guarding.

Judicial Takedown

Mixed · Republic Judicial Forces
Bring them in breathing.
Stance — Non-lethal control: +3 to grapple, disarm, or pin, you may switch any successful hit to subdual damage freely, and your subdual blows do +1 per die of fatigue damage to drop a foe faster.
Trade-off — -2 to inflict lethal harm while in this stance.

Temple Guard Vigil

Melee · Jedi (sentinel drill, mundane footwork)
Watchfulness is the first discipline.
Stance — Defensive readiness: +2 to all active defenses, you never suffer the -4 for defending against an unseen melee attack, and you may defend against ranged attacks you are aware of without the usual penalty.
Trade-off — No attack bonus; this is a watchman’s stance, not a duelist’s.

Sith Trooper Advance

Blaster · Sith Empire (trooper doctrine)
Forward, always forward.
Stance — Aggressive push: +2 to hit when you Move toward a target and fire the same turn (Move and Attack), and your advancing fire ignores 1 point of the target’s cover DR.
Trade-off — -1 to active defenses on any turn you advance.

Assassin’s Repose

Mixed · Sith (assassin / Czerka wetwork)
One breath. One opening. One strike.
Stance — Stillness before the strike: from a held, unmoving posture your first attack each combat gains +3 to hit, counts as a surprise attack, and deals +2 per die of damage if the target is unaware.
Trade-off — The benefit is spent the instant you act or are noticed.

Wookiee Rage Stance

Melee · Wookiee (warrior tradition)
Let them hear the roar.
Stance — Overwhelming force: +2 per die to crushing melee damage, +3 to break free of or initiate grapples, and your blows knock back foes as if your ST were 4 higher.
Trade-off — -2 to your own active defenses; you must attack or advance each turn.

Trandoshan Hunt-Lock

Mixed · Trandoshan (hunter creed)
The hunt has only one quarry.
Stance — Once you mark a single prey, +2 to hit and to track that target, you ignore 2 points of its Dodge while it flees, and you deal +1 per die against it.
Trade-off — -1 to hit anyone who is not your marked prey.

Zabrak Dual-Guard

Melee · Zabrak (Iridonian martial art)
Two blades, one will.
Stance — Two-weapon defense: while wielding a weapon in each hand, +2 to Parry, you may parry three times without the multiple-defense penalty, and a successful parry with one blade lets you riposte with the other.
Trade-off — Requires two ready melee weapons; no benefit with a single weapon.

Twi’lek Coil Step

Melee · Twi’lek (dancer-fighter art)
Never where the blow lands.
Stance — Fluid evasion: +2 to Dodge and +3 to Acrobatic Dodge, a successful dodge lets you Step for free, and once per turn you may dodge an attack that normally cannot be dodged.
Trade-off — No benefit in medium or heavier encumbrance.

Corsair’s Boarding Stance

Mixed · Space pirate (boarding crews)
Take the ship hold by hold.
Stance — Close-quarters fury: +2 to hit with pistols and short blades in confined spaces, +2 to maintain footing aboard a pitching deck, and your close attacks ignore 1 point of armour DR.
Trade-off — No benefit in open ground.

Gunslinger’s Ease

Blaster · Fringe (quick-draw tradition)
Slow is smooth; smooth is fast.
Stance — Relaxed readiness: +3 to Fast-Draw a sidearm, +2 to hit with the first snap-shot from the draw, and that first shot may be made as a free action once per combat.
Trade-off — Only one sidearm; no benefit with rifles or two-handed weapons.

Smuggler’s Cover-Fire

Blaster · Fringe (smuggler survival habit)
Shoot, duck, and live to spend it.
Stance — Fighting from cover: +2 to hit and +2 to defenses while at least half-covered, you may reload as a free action each turn, and you treat your cover’s DR as 2 higher against return fire.
Trade-off — Step out of cover and the stance ends until you find more.

Heavy Weapons Brace

Blaster · Mercenary / heavy-weapons doctrine
Plant your feet and let it roar.
Stance — Braced firing: +3 to hit with heavy or crew-served weapons, reduce the recoil penalty per shot by 2, and ignore the weapon’s minimum ST penalty while braced.
Trade-off — You may not Move while braced; setting up the brace takes one turn.

Skirmisher’s Dance

Mixed · Mercenary (light-infantry doctrine)
Never a target twice.
Stance — Shoot-and-scoot: you suffer no penalty to fire a blaster on the same turn you take a normal Move, gain +1 to defenses on any turn you move at least 3 metres, and may Step after firing.
Trade-off — -1 to hit on the moving shot; no benefit when stationary.

Teras Kasi Open Hand

Melee · Teras Kasi (Followers of Palawa)
The mind quiet, the hand certain.
Stance — Anti-Force unarmed art: +2 to hit and Parry unarmed, +3 to resist being read or thrown by an opponent’s technique, and you may parry Force-assisted melee at no penalty.
Trade-off — Unarmed only; no benefit with weapons or in heavy armour.

Stava Combat Crouch

Mixed · Noghri (Stava martial art)
Unseen, then upon them.
Stance — Low, explosive stance: +3 to hit on a charge or pounce from concealment, +2 to Stealth while holding the crouch, and the first such strike deals +1 per die.
Trade-off — -1 to ranged attacks; this is an ambush-and-close art.

Mandalorian Gunline

Blaster · Mandalorian (Cuy’val Dar drill)
Jetpack up, rain down, never stand still.
Stance — While using a blaster and taking a Move maneuver each turn, ignore the -2 for moving when you make a single aimed shot, and gain +1 to hit and +1 to defenses on any turn you both move and fire.
Trade-off — Lose all benefit if you take an Aim maneuver or stand still two turns running; this is a run-and-gun style only.

Republic Trooper Volley

Blaster · Republic Army (line-infantry drill)
Discipline of the firing line.
Stance — When you and an ally both ready blasters at the same foe, +2 to hit for coordinated fire; +1 more for each additional ally joining (no cap), and the volley ignores 1 point of cover.
Trade-off — The bonus needs the formation: if you move out of line with your squad, you lose it that turn.

Czerka Security Lockdown

Blaster · Czerka Corporation (corporate security)
Hold the chokepoint, charge the corporate rate.
Stance — From behind cover with a braced blaster, +2 to hit and +2 to your cover’s effective DR against return fire, and any foe entering your covered field is at -2 to hit you.
Trade-off — You may not leave the cover or change targets without losing the brace; setting up costs a Ready maneuver.

Bounty Hunter’s Mark

Blaster · Guild bounty hunters
Study the quarry, take the clean shot.
Stance — After one Evaluate against a single target, your next ranged attack on them ignores up to -4 of range or movement penalties and deals +1 per die — take them clean.
Trade-off — Works on one marked target at a time; switching marks wastes the Evaluate.

Death Watch Ambush

Blaster · Death Watch (Mandalorian splinter)
Strike from the dark, vanish before the answer.
Stance — Your first attack from surprise or concealment is at +3 to hit and deals +2 per die; if it drops the target, you may immediately attempt to fade back into concealment.
Trade-off — After that first turn, −1 to your own active defenses for the rest of the fight — the ambush spent your caution.

Verpine Marksman’s Stillness

Blaster · Verpine (precision shooters)
Breathe once. Fire once.
Stance — Add an extra +1 to a shot for every full turn spent in Aim, up to +5 total (above normal Aim bonuses), and your aimed shots ignore 1 point of the target’s cover.
Trade-off — Any movement, injury, or being attacked breaks the stillness and resets it entirely.

Trandoshan Hunt-Sight

Blaster · Trandoshan (Scorekeeper hunters)
The Scorekeeper rewards the kill, not the wound.
Stance — +2 to hit a fleeing or wounded target with a ranged weapon, and +1 per die of damage against any target already below full HP.
Trade-off — −1 to hit a fresh, healthy target — you are wired to chase weakness, not start the fight cleanly.

Echani Flowing Mirror

Melee · Echani (advanced mirror art)
Your move is already my move.
Stance — After successfully parrying a foe once, +1 to hit that same foe next turn — you have read their rhythm.
Trade-off — Against multiple attackers the read does not hold; the bonus only ever applies to one studied opponent.

Wookiee Berserker Surge

Melee · Kashyyyk (warrior tradition)
Let the rage carry the blade.
Stance — +3 to melee damage per attack while you make All-Out Attacks, and All-Out (Strong) adds a further +2 per die.
Trade-off — While surging you may take no active defenses at all — pure offense, exactly as All-Out Attack demands, and you cannot stop until the foe falls or flees.

Teräs Käsi Still Water

Melee · Followers of Palawa (Teräs Käsi)
The empty hand turns even the blade of light.
Stance — You may attempt an unarmed Parry against melee weapons (including lightsabers) at no penalty, and on a parry that beats the attack by 3+ you may immediately grapple or redirect the blade.
Trade-off — Requires both hands free and full focus; you cannot use this while carrying or wielding anything.

Nikto Blade-Dance

Melee · Nikto (Kajidic enforcers)
Two knives, no pause.
Stance — When fighting with a weapon in each hand, reduce the off-hand penalty by 4 (to zero for most fighters), and you may make one extra off-hand attack per turn at -2.
Trade-off — You forgo any shield or free-hand parry; both hands are committed to attack.

Zabrak Iron Resolve

Melee · Iridonia (Zabrak warrior caste)
Pain is information, not instruction.
Stance — Ignore the first 2 points of shock penalty from any wound each turn, and you do not suffer the usual penalty to stay conscious from the first major wound.
Trade-off — −1 to Stealth and social rolls while held — the bearing reads as openly aggressive.

Dathomiri Hunter’s Crouch

Melee · Dathomir (Nightbrother rites)
Low to the earth, fast off it.
Stance — From a crouch, +2 to hit on a melee attack made as part of a charge or pounce, and that strike deals +1 per die and may knock the target prone.
Trade-off — While crouched your Move is halved and ranged defenders get +1 against your slower approach.

Selkath Riptide Guard

Melee · Manaan (Selkath blade-wards)
Yield like water, return like the tide.
Stance — +2 to Parry; a successful retreat-parry grants +2 to your next attack on that foe and a free Step, and you may retreat-parry without the usual once-per-turn limit.
Trade-off — The style is defensive-first: you take −1 to hit on any turn you did not parry the previous turn.

Kissai Ritual Guard

Melee · Sith (Kissai temple-warden drill)
The blade guards the rite.
Stance — +2 to all active defenses while you stand your ground over a fixed point, and any foe attacking you across that threshold is at -1 to hit.
Trade-off — Step away from the spot you are guarding and the bonus is gone until you set a new ground and spend a turn.

Mandalorian Supercommando

Mixed · Mandalorian (elite supercommando)
Master of every range.
Stance — Switching between a readied blaster and a melee weapon costs no penalty, you may Fast-Draw the second weapon as a free action, and you suffer no penalty fighting at any range for the turn you transition.
Trade-off — Requires both weapons readied and a free Fast-Draw; against a foe who closes faster than you react, you take the normal switch penalties.

Sith Trooper Drill

Mixed · Sith Empire (trooper conditioning)
The unit is the weapon.
Stance — While within three metres of an ally also using this drill, +2 to your Will against fear and intimidation, +1 to hit a foe an ally also targets, and you may rally a shaken ally as a free action.
Trade-off — Alone, the conditioning works against you: −1 to Will when no allied trooper is near.

Jedi Sentinel’s Readiness

Mixed · Jedi Order (Sentinel field-training, non-Force)
Ready for the threat you did not expect.
Stance — +2 to defenses on the first turn of any fight you did not start, you act as though you have Combat Reflexes for that turn (never surprised), no Force required.
Trade-off — Only the first turn; once the fight is joined you fight at your normal level with no ongoing edge.

Pirate’s Deck Brawl

Mixed · Outer Rim corsairs
Whatever’s in reach is a weapon.
Stance — +2 to hit with improvised weapons and in cramped quarters, improvised weapons in your hands deal +1 per die, and you ignore 1 point of DR with a well-placed bludgeon.
Trade-off — In open ground with proper weapons you have no edge, and −1 to hit at any range beyond close.

Gamorrean Bull-Rush

Mixed · Gamorr (war-clan tradition)
Put your whole weight behind it.
Stance — +3 to Slam and shove attacks and to knock a foe back or down, your Slam counts your ST as 4 higher for damage and knockback, and a successful Slam may stun a smaller foe.
Trade-off — Committing your mass leaves you off-balance: −1 to active defenses for the rest of that turn.

Iktotchi Foresight Guard

Mixed · Iktotchi (seer-court guard drill)
The blow is seen before it is thrown.
Stance — Precognitive defense: +2 to all active defenses, you are never at the −4 penalty for defending against an attack you could not see coming, and once per fight you may declare a defense after seeing the attack roll’s result.
Trade-off — The foresight needs a clear mind: −2 to all defenses for the rest of any turn in which you are mentally stunned, surprised or afflicted.

Iktotchi Windreader’s Aim

Blaster · Iktotchi (windhide hunter craft)
Read the wind and the moment to come.
Stance — After one turn of Aim or Evaluate, your next ranged attack ignores up to −3 of range, movement, or weather penalties, and a miss by exactly 1 may be re-rolled once per fight.
Trade-off — The read breaks if you are struck, or forced to move more than a step before firing.

Melee — Swing & Thrust as Separate Modes

In v15 of the character sheet, every melee weapon now displays its Swing and Thrust modes as separate lines with their own damage, armour divisor, and damage type — matching the canonical melee tables in Documents I and Part X. The sheet's wpnDamage() resolver was updated to support per-mode fixed, mod, ad, and dtype overrides, and to evaluate "thr+1d"-style shorthand expressions against the wielder's current ST.

WeaponSwing (Sw)Thrust (Th)
Standard lightsaber8d+8 (5) burn4d+4 (10) imp+burn
— Hilt configurations of the standard saber (grip slot, one per saber — Doc IX components) —
w/ Shoto hilt (shoto / short saber)6d+6 (5) burn3d+3 (10) imp+burn
w/ Curved hilt (Makashi duelling saber)8d+8 (5) burn4d+4 (10) imp+burn
w/ Dual-grip hilt (saberstaff / double-blade)8d+8 (5) burn (both ends)4d+4 (10) imp+burn
w/ Extended hilt (great lightsaber)9d+9 (5) burn4d+6 (10) imp+burn
w/ Duelling foil hilt (lightfoil)7d+7 (5) burn3d+5 (10) imp+burn
— Distinct light-blade weapons (not configurations) —
Lightwhip5d+5 (5) burn— (flexible, no thrust)
Training sabersw cr (stun only)—
— Vibroblades (sw cut / th imp, AD 2 both) —
Vibrobladesw+1 (2) cutthr+1 (2) imp
Vibroswordsw+2 (2) cutthr+1 (2) imp
Techbladesw+1 (2) cutthr+1 (2) imp
Dire vibrobladesw+3 (2) cutthr+2 (2) imp
War blade (Sith)sw+2 (2) cutthr+2 (2) imp
— Mundane swords (sw cut / th imp split) —
Short swordsw cutthr imp
War swordsw+1 cutthr imp
Dire swordsw+2 cutthr+1 imp
Shyarnsw cutthr imp
Firasw+1 (2) cutthr imp
— Polearms & staves —
Electrostaffsw+2 (2) cr + 1d burn (both ends); stun— (no thrust)
Shockstaffsw+1 (2) cr + 1d burn (both ends); stun— (no thrust)
Techstaffsw+2 crthr+1 cr (both ends)
Force pike— (thrust-only)thr+3 (2) imp + 1d burn
Stun baton— (thrust-only)thr cr + HT-3 aff (stun)
— Knives (thrust-only) —
Combat knife—thr cut/imp
Shiv / dirk—thr−1 imp
Vibroknife—thr+1d (2) cut/imp
Electroknife—thr+1d (2) cut + 1d burn

Why this matters in play. The split lets a player target between two different defenses on the same weapon — a swing is pure burning against a refined-beskar wearer; a thrust is impaling+burning at twice the armour divisor and double-wound, perfect for a chink-of-armour called shot. Before the split, the sheet flattened both modes into a single damage line and made the iconic Jedi tactic — "swing to wear them down, thrust to finish" — impossible to execute mechanically.

Overriding on the sheet. Players can still type any value into the per-weapon override pane (Equipment → Modifications → Direct stat edits) to homebrew their own canon. The catalogue values above are the defaults the sheet applies on load.

Why Feint Appears Twice

Players new to GURPS 4e flipping between Part II (Maneuvers) and Part III (Attack Modifiers) routinely ask: "Why is Feint listed twice?" It's the same Quick Contest rule, but the compendium documents it twice because there are two distinct delivery slots for that contest. This part pulls them onto one page.

FormDelivery slotQuick ContestCostPayoff
Feint (maneuver)
Part II, line 1966 / 2010		Your whole turnBest Melee/Unarmed/Cloak vs target's best of same OR target's DXYou don't strike this turn. Step permitted. Weapon must be ready.Margin → penalty to ONE active defense vs your NEXT attack on that target, next turn.
Defensive Feint (maneuver variant)
Part II, line 2010		Your whole turnSame QC as aboveSame as Feint; declare Defensive Feint instead.Margin → penalty to THEIR next ATTACK ON YOU. Use when you don't plan to swing at them next turn.
AoA (Feint)
Part III, line 2042 / 1979		Same turn as the strikeFree Feint QC fires first; then one attackAll-Out — NO active defenses this turn.Free-Feint margin lowers ONE defense vs THIS attack. Single same-target strike.
Double (with Feint sub)
Part III, line 2040		Same turn as the strikesSubstitute ONE of your two attacks with a Feint QCSame as the Double maneuver (no special cost vs vanilla Double).Feint margin lowers defense vs your other attack the SAME turn. Melee only.
Beat
Part III, line 2102		Inside the Feint maneuverYour weapon skill using ST vs target's weapon skill using ST or DXRequires Feint maneuver. Needs a prior parry/block exchange OR a grappled foe.Margin penalises ONE chosen defense vs ALL attackers until end of your next turn. Physical guard-batter. No damage.
Ruse
Part III, line 2104		Inside the Feint maneuverYour weapon skill using IQ vs target's Per-based skill (wary), DX-based skill (reset guard), or TacticsRequires Feint maneuver. Verbal/gesture misdirection.Margin penalises ONE defense vs your next attack. Eligible for Defensive Feint conversion.

The point. Same QC math, three different costs:

  1. Whole turn now, payoff next turn → it's a Maneuver (Feint / Defensive Feint).
  2. Same turn as the swing, at the cost of all defenses → it's AoA (Feint), an Attack-type Modifier listed in Part III.
  3. Same turn, no extra cost beyond a maneuver that already chains attacks → substitute a Feint inside Double, Combination, or Rapid Strike.

Beat and Ruse are not separate maneuvers — they're flavour variants of the Feint maneuver's contest, swapping the attribute (Beat = ST, Ruse = IQ) for a different result (Beat = single defense vs all attackers; Ruse = same as Feint but resolves vs Per/Tactics).

Restrictions that catch new players.

  • Ranged attacks cannot Feint at all — neither maneuver nor modifier (Part III line 1994). RoF and Aim are the ranged equivalents.
  • All-Out Attack (Strong) can't carry Defensive Feint redirection or Riposte; they need defensive capacity you've given up.
  • Multiple Feints in the same maneuver only the most recent applies. You don't stack feints, you replace.
  • Resisting Feint uses your best weapon skill — even when holding an unfamiliar weapon. The compendium calls this out twice (2010 and 2106) because it's the most-missed sub-rule.
  • Riposte (Part II, line 2219) is its own thing — it consumes your Parry score, not your maneuver. It stacks WITH prior Feint but is mutually exclusive with Deceptive Attack on the riposte strike.

In short. Feint isn't two rules — it's one Quick Contest with three different invoices. The compendium prints it in both indexes so the player flipping to "what maneuver do I pick this turn?" and the player flipping to "what tricks can I bolt onto my attack?" both find it. They are the same tool with different turn-economy costs.

Document II

Ranged Combat

Aim · Range · Rate of Fire · Cover · Bolt Deflection

Overview

What this covers. Everything about shooting — Accuracy and Aim, range and recoil, rate of fire and bursts, cover, and defending against incoming fire (including saber deflection).

How to use it. Pair it with the Combat document; the turn structure is shared. The Ranged Playbook at the end is a quick action menu for the table.

Part I

The Ranged Attack

What’s different · the to-hit calculation · what ranged forbids

A ranged attack uses a weapon that throws, fires, projects, or launches a projectile, beam, or wave at a target you can perceive at distance. The attack roll is your ranged weapon skill modified by your weapon’s Accuracy, the target’s Size Modifier, the range penalty from the Speed/Range Table, and any situational modifiers — posture, cover, light, target motion. Bullets, bolts, arrows, throwing knives, grenades, lassos, force-projected lightning, blaster fire, and shoulder-fired rockets all resolve here.

Component Source What It Does
SkillCharacter sheetYour trained ability with this weapon family. Beam Weapons (Pistol), Guns (Rifle), Bow, Thrown Weapon, etc.
Accuracy (Acc)Weapon statBonus from Aiming. Added on top of your skill when you spend a turn (or more) Aiming first.
Size Modifier (SM)Target stat block+ for larger targets, − for smaller. Human = 0, speeder = +3, AT-ST = +5, mouse droid = −3.
