The SentinelCode of Honor + a worldly skill disciplineBalanced access; the only Path that may also climb a grey tradition without penalty.

Living Path capstones, by tier

ReachCrossing capstoneMastery capstones
II · KnightThe First Restraint — in play, choose mercy or protection at real personal cost when the dark, fast option was right there.(1) Heal another to full from the brink of death in the field.
III · MasterThe Tested Heart — face a personal temptation to the dark (revenge, a loved one in danger) and refuse it, on screen.(1) Resolve a deadly conflict without violence where violence was expected. (2) Sustain a Master-tier working under fire.
IV · GrandmasterThe Vow Made Flesh — formally take your Code/Path before witnesses and a teacher (or the Force itself), and immediately honour it under pressure.(1) Cleanse a corrupted person or place. (2) Best a dark-sider without falling. (3) A season of teaching or guardianship.
V · LegendThe Self Given — a single great sacrifice for others, expecting nothing, that the whole table recognises as the act of a life.Legendary powers are not trained but earned by the Crossing; the deed is the mastery.

These powers belong to those who would heal, defend, and guide. They are slow where the dark is fast, deliberate where the dark is hungry. Their cost is patience — most require concentration, touch, or the willingness to make oneself smaller so that another might be made whole. The Order trains its initiates in these techniques across decades. A Jedi who masters them does not master the Force; the Force has taught itself to flow through them.

Attribute alignment: if a power’s governing attribute matches your primary Force attribute, you learn it in half the normal training time.

Contests: when a target is Force-sensitive, they may substitute their Force skill for the listed resistance attribute (Will, HT, or ST) if it is higher. Ties always go to the defender.

Scalability: powers marked Scalable allow extra FP for stronger effects or resistance penalties on the target.

I · INITIATE

Initiate

Foundational abilities. Skill bonus: +2. Low cost and reliable — the building blocks everything else rests on. Powers marked Scalable allow extra FP to be spent for stronger effects or resistance penalties on the target.

✦ Light Side Powers

Force Guidance

+1 per FP to a single non-combat skill roll.
AffinityWillActionInstantRangeSelf or a touched/nearby ally (within 10 hexes)Defence— (a buff; never resisted)FP1 per +1 (max = ½ the assisted skill’s attribute)ReactionNo

Effect. +1 per FP to a single non-combat skill roll. Must be applied before the roll. Maximum bonus = HALF the attribute that skill is based on, round down (a DX-based skill caps at ½ DX; an IQ-based skill at ½ IQ; a Will- or HT-based skill at ½ that attribute). The guidance can only sharpen a skill as far as the body or mind underlying it could ever be pushed. Cannot be applied to attack or defense rolls. / Scalable: +2 FP allows the bonus to also apply to the next roll of the same skill within the same encounter — the guidance lingers briefly. / Note: activation always uses YOUR Force attribute; only the cap scales with the assisted skill’s attribute.

BODY. Bodiless intuition guiding the hands already in use — no separate gesture; it sharpens whatever the body is doing.

A warmth passes between you and the target — not visible, but the target's posture shifts subtly, muscles unlocking as confidence settles in.

Cannot exceed half the assisted skill’s governing attribute as a bonus. Cannot apply to combat rolls.

Force Soothing

Reduce a target's fear or emotional distress by 1 severity level per FP spent.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)Range3 hexes, line-of-sight (Touch drops LoS and costs 1 less)DefenceWilling targets none; an unwilling target resists with WillFP1 per level reducedReactionNo

Effect. Reduce a target's fear or emotional distress by 1 severity level per FP spent. Touch removes the LoS requirement and reduces cost by 1 FP (minimum 1). Cannot reduce a Frenzied or Berserk target below Frightened in one casting. / Scalable: +3 FP applies −2 to an unwilling target's Will resistance roll.

BODY. The VOICE and a calm open-handed gesture; the target must perceive one or the other. Hands need not be empty.

A slow warmth radiates from your hands or from the space between you. Shoulders drop. Fists unclench. The sharp edge of what they were feeling blunts.

Unwilling targets resist with Will. Cannot leap levels — distress is reduced one step at a time.

Animal Bond

Calm a wild beast or sense its current emotional intent.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn), up to 1 minRange8 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceResisted by the animal’s HT (provocation lets it re-roll at +2); droids/synthetics immuneFP2ReactionNo

Effect. Calm a wild beast or sense its current emotional intent. On success, the animal will not attack for the duration unless directly provoked. Provocation (attack, threat to young) allows HT roll at +2 to break the bond.

Your body language changes completely — no sudden moves, eyes cast slightly down, breathing matched to the animal's rhythm. A soft resonance pulses between you.

Only works on naturally occurring creatures — droids, cyborgs, and synthetic beings are immune. Cannot compel the animal to act.

Truth-Sense

Detect intentional deception. On success, you know when a statement was a deliberate lie (not an honest mistake).
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn), up to 5 minRange8 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceResisted by Will (Will 14+ or trained liars +2); your failure isn’t revealed to the targetFP2ReactionNo

Effect. Detect intentional deception. On success, you know when a statement was a deliberate lie (not an honest mistake). On a tie, no reading either way. Highly trained liars or those with Will 14+ add +2 to resist. / Scalable: +3 FP on a successful detection also reveals the dominant emotional weight behind the lie — desperation, calculation, fear, loyalty — giving context without giving words.

BODY. Bodiless — attention on the speaker. No gesture; works in conversation with hands occupied.

You become very still, head tilted, eyes reading something invisible in the space around the target.

Perception Meditation (30 min): extend to a sustained read across a whole conversation, and detect not just lies but deliberate omissions and the shape of what is being hidden. [SUSTAINED — you must stay in the meditative trance the whole time the effect runs (sit apart, let others act); if the meditation breaks, the whole enhanced effect ends, not just the bonus.]

Does not work on droids, the Force-dead, or those who genuinely believe their own lies. Tells you deception occurred — not the truth underneath it.

Force Empathy

Read the precise emotional state of one target — joy, fear, anger, grief, calm, deception, longing.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn), then maintainedRange8 hexes, line-of-sightDefencePassive on an open target; a guarded one resists with Will (Mind Block, Quick Contest)FP1, then 1 per minuteReactionNo

Effect. Read the precise emotional state of one target — joy, fear, anger, grief, calm, deception, longing. Cannot read thoughts, only the texture of feeling beneath them. Maintained at 1 FP/min while held; loses target on LoS break.

A warmth opens between you and them. You feel what they feel as if it were faintly your own — distant, but unmistakable.

Suppressed by Mind Block (Quick Contest). Strong hostile emotion may force a Will roll on you or leave you Shaken. Cannot read droids or Force-dead.

Cure Poison

Neutralise one ingested poison, venom, or biological toxin in the target.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)RangeTouchDefencevs the poison’s own HT (GM sets); the patient is willingFP4ReactionNo

Effect. Neutralise one ingested poison, venom, or biological toxin in the target. Existing toxin-damage recovery rolls gain +2. Does not address Sith alchemy or technological contaminants (cybernetic toxins, nanite swarms).

Both hands rest on the afflicted area. The target's veins darken briefly, then clear — a current passing through and out.

Cannot work post-mortem. Disease-specific cleansing is a separate power (Cure Disease, Tier 2). One poison at a time.

II · KNIGHT

Knight

Reliable combat and utility powers. Skill bonus: +3. Average cost 3−8 FP once scaled. Scaling options increase effect, extend duration, lower resistance, or expand area — invest more FP for meaningfully harder-to-resist results.

✦ Light Side Powers

Force Heal

Restore 3 HP per 2 FP. External and surface wounds only — cannot address internal injuries or lost organs.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)RangeTouchDefence— (a willing patient; an interruption wastes it, a critical failure deals 1 HP)FP2 per 3 HP (cap 12 HP per cast)ReactionNo

Effect. Restore 3 HP per 2 FP. External and surface wounds only — cannot address internal injuries or lost organs. Cannot heal your own wounds. Base maximum: 12 HP per casting (8 FP). / Scalable: +4 FP raises the cap to 18 HP (up to 12 FP total for healing). / +4 FP separate option: also addresses internal injuries and structural damage (broken bones, internal bleeding) — does not stack with the cap increase.

BODY. Through TOUCH — one or both free hands laid on the wound. At least one hand must contact the patient; two hands speed deep healing.

Your hands press to skin and glow — a warm, living light that pulses slowly, the colour of late afternoon sun. Where it touches, bruises fade at the edges.

Healing Meditation (1 hr): double HP restored per FP, and a meditated healing can knit damaged organs and recent crippling injuries that a battlefield cast cannot reach. [SUSTAINED — you must stay in the meditative trance the whole time the effect runs (sit apart, let others act); if the meditation breaks, the whole enhanced effect ends, not just the bonus.]

Any interruption (being hit, forced movement) loses FP and heals nothing.

Affect Mind

+1 per FP to your Influence skill rolls (Persuasion, Fast-Talk, Intimidate) against one target.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn), up to 5 minRange3 hexes; the target must see or hear youDefenceResisted by Will (fails entirely vs an active Force Sense); never dodgedFP1 per +1 (max = ½ IQ)ReactionNo

Effect. +1 per FP to your Influence skill rolls (Persuasion, Fast-Talk, Intimidate) against one target. Maximum bonus = ½ IQ. / Subtle — the target does not perceive the nudge on a failed resistance. Fails entirely against Force-sensitive targets actively using Force Sense. / Scalable: +3 FP applies −2 to the target's Will resistance roll.

BODY. A small gesture of one hand near the face (the classic wave) plus the VOICE carrying the suggestion — both focus it; without the gesture, resistance worsens by 1. Target must hear or see the caster.

Your voice and eyes carry something extra — a resonance that shouldn't be there. The words arrive slightly pre-approved.

Extended Exposure: a mind worked on repeatedly yields more each time. Each successful use on the SAME individual within a span of days lowers their effective resistance by a cumulative −1 (max −4) and lets a suggestion sink one step deeper toward genuine belief. Sustained influence over weeks can shade into lasting conviction the target no longer questions — the slow art of turning a person rather than nudging them.

If a Perception roll reveals something feels off, the target gets a free Will roll to break the effect.

Force Resolve

+1 per FP to resist fear, mind-affecting powers, or pain penalties while maintained.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn), then maintainedRangeSelf, or willing allies within 4 hexes (Touch halves cost)Defence— (a buff; willing only)FP1 per +1 per turn (max = ½ Will)ReactionNo

Effect. +1 per FP to resist fear, mind-affecting powers, or pain penalties while maintained. Maximum bonus = HALF your Will, round down (this is a steeling of the mind, so it scales with Will rather than IQ). If you take damage while maintaining, make a Will roll or the effect ends. / Scalable: +2 FP extends the benefit to one additional adjacent willing target at the same bonus level. / Note: activation uses your Force attribute as normal.

The target's posture straightens almost imperceptibly. A faint gilded shimmer traces their shoulders and integrates, becoming an internal warmth they can feel but not see.

Cannot exceed ½ Will as a bonus. Touch halves cost. Target must be willing.

Force Shelter

Reduce environmental harm (heat, cold, radiation, toxic atmosphere, vacuum) by 1 point per FP per turn.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn), then maintainedRangeTouch — the target must stay in contactDefence— (environmental protection; not vs attacks)FP1 per point of harm reduced, per turnReactionNo

Effect. Reduce environmental harm (heat, cold, radiation, toxic atmosphere, vacuum) by 1 point per FP per turn. Target must maintain physical contact throughout. Does not protect against weapons or targeted attacks.

A luminous membrane — the colour of moonlight on still water — wraps the target in a barely-visible skin. Heat distorts around it.

Target must remain touching you. Dropping contact immediately ends the effect.

Ionize

Disable droids or electronic devices for 1d turns.
AffinityWillActionAttackRange8 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceThe device resists with HT−2 (large systems +2, small −4); biologicals immuneFP3ReactionNo

Effect. Disable droids or electronic devices for 1d turns. Larger/more complex systems add +2 to HT. Small devices have HT−4. Does not permanently damage the device. / Scalable: +3 FP increases duration to 2d turns. / +3 FP (separate option): the discharge also affects one additional adjacent device simultaneously, each making its own HT roll.

Your fingertips spark with a web of pale blue-white light — finer than lightning, more like circuitry made visible. The discharge enters the device as a silent pulse.

Cannot be used against biological targets. Against cyborgs or partially organic targets, only electronic components are affected.

Force Valor

Aura of resolve and clarity. All willing allies within 4 hexes (including self) gain +1 to ST, DX, IQ, and HT for the…
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn), 10 minRangewilling allies within a 4-hex radius (incl. you)Defence— (a buff; willing only)FP6ReactionNo

Effect. Aura of resolve and clarity. All willing allies within 4 hexes (including self) gain +1 to ST, DX, IQ, and HT for the duration. Scalable: +3 FP extends to 30 minutes; +4 FP extends the radius to 8 hexes.

A pale gold haze rises from your shoulders and outward. Allies feel themselves steadier, sharper, more present in their own bodies.

Effect drops if you fall unconscious. Does not stack with other Force buffs or chemical stimulants. Visible to Force Sense at 50 hexes.

Force Aura

A protective shell of Force-energy around you.
AffinityWillActionInstant, then maintainedRangeSelf (a 1-hex aura also shields nearby allies)Defence— (a defensive buff)FP1 per minute (scales for more DR)ReactionNo

Effect. A protective shell of Force-energy around you. +6 DR against energy and kinetic damage for yourself; allies within 1 hex gain +3 DR. Scalable: each additional +2 FP/min adds +2 DR to you and +1 to nearby allies, to a maximum of +12 DR (allies +6).

A faintly luminous shell flows around your skin, just under your clothing — gold and white, barely visible except when struck.

All-Out Attack drops the aura immediately for that turn. Does not stack with armour's listed DR. Visible to Force Sense.

Cure Disease

Cure one disease in the touched target — mundane illness, Force-derived plague, or parasitic infection.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (5 min)RangeTouchDefencevs the disease’s own HT (GM sets); the patient is willingFP8ReactionNo

Effect. Cure one disease in the touched target — mundane illness, Force-derived plague, or parasitic infection. Restores 1d HP of damage caused by the disease. Cannot remove genetic conditions, Sith alchemical alterations, or biotechnical infections.

Both hands cup the afflicted area in soft white light. The sickness becomes briefly visible as a darker thread, then dissipates into the air.

Five minutes of focused concentration — any interruption wastes the FP. Critical failure: you contract the disease yourself (HT roll to resist).

III · MASTER

Master

Complex techniques of high focus. Skill bonus: +3. Average cost 5−15 FP once scaled. At this tier, scaling resistance penalties matters most — peer Force-users have high resistance rolls, and every −2 you can apply changes the outcome.

✦ Light Side Powers

Force Stasis

Freeze one target in a stasis field for 1d turns.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)Range8 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceResisted by Will (or Force skill if higher); any damage breaks it; droids/Force-dead immuneFP5ReactionNo

Effect. Freeze one target in a stasis field for 1d turns. Target is completely immobilised. Any damage to the target instantly breaks stasis. Droids and Force-dead beings are immune. / Scalable: +3 FP applies −2 to the target's Will resistance roll. / +4 FP increases duration to 2d turns.

GRIP & BODY. The Force grips the target's whole body and holds it still — full envelopment. One extended hand, fingers spread and frozen. Envelopment-mode weight ceiling (half the shove gates).

The target freezes mid-motion with an almost audible click — suspended perfectly, as if someone pressed pause on their existence. A faint shell of pale blue-white light traces their outline.

Focus Meditation (30 min): double the duration and raise the resistance penalty — a meditated stasis can hold a far stronger target far longer. [SUSTAINED — you must stay in the meditative trance the whole time the effect runs (sit apart, let others act); if the meditation breaks, the whole enhanced effect ends, not just the bonus.]

Breaks on any damage. Targets with Will 14+ add +2. No ongoing LoS required after firing.

Electric Judgment

1d burn damage (cyan-blue lightning) per 2 FP.
AffinityWillActionAttackRange6 hexes, line-of-sight (full cover blocks it)DefenceDodge, or a Lightsaber Parry (a successful saber-parry forms a Lightning Lock, Doc I §Saber Lock) — targets in the open make two defence rolls; full cover = immuneFP2 per 1d (non-lethal; can’t drop below 1 HP)ReactionNo

Effect. 1d burn damage (cyan-blue lightning) per 2 FP. Non-lethal — cannot reduce target below 1 HP. Targets in the open must make two defense rolls. Metallic armour or wet targets: +1 to attack roll. / Scalable: +2 FP forces even targets behind partial cover to make two defense rolls (normally they only need one).

BODY. The light-side current — one or two free, empty hands, same somatic rule as Force Lightning. Hands must hold nothing. Two-finger saber-hand variant at skill 22+ for half output.

Cyan-blue lightning — brighter and cleaner than Sith lightning, with none of the purple-black edge — arcs from your outstretched hand. It crackles like a live wire and smells of ozone and rain.

Cannot kill. Targets behind full cover are entirely immune. The light side does not give freely the way the dark does — Electric Judgment is non-lethal by nature and costs twice the FP per die of Force Lightning. This is the price of a power that leaves no corruption behind: it cannot be turned to killing, and it never makes the wielder hungry for more.

Revitalize

Instantly wake an unconscious ally at 0 HP regardless of current injury state.
AffinityWillActionInstantRangeTouchDefence— (a willing ally; critical failure wakes them at −1d HP)FP10ReactionNo

Effect. Instantly wake an unconscious ally at 0 HP regardless of current injury state. Does not heal wounds. The target is Shaken (−2 all actions) for 1 turn after waking. / Scalable: +5 FP upgrades the effect — the target wakes at 1 HP instead of 0 and without the Shaken penalty (the surge is more controlled).

BODY. A free hand on the subject (or self), channelling recovery through contact.

You slam your palm to their sternum. A pulse of sharp white light radiates from the contact point — one sudden, clean flash. Their chest heaves. Their eyes open, wide and disoriented.

Healing Meditation (30 min): double the FP/fatigue restored and clear one level of affliction (poison-shock, exhaustion, pain) alongside the recovery. [SUSTAINED — you must stay in the meditative trance the whole time the effect runs (sit apart, let others act); if the meditation breaks, the whole enhanced effect ends, not just the bonus.]

Critical failure: the power misfires and the target wakes at −1d HP.

Force Light

A burst of pure light-side energy.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)Range8-hex radius burst, line-of-sightDefenceDark-siders resist with Force skill, dark creatures with HT; bypasses standard DR; light/neutral unharmedFP8 (3d; +4 per +1d, max 6d)ReactionNo

Effect. A burst of pure light-side energy. Dark-side users and dark-corrupted creatures within 8 hexes take 3d burning damage that bypasses standard DR. Sith holocrons and dark-imbued items treat the burst as a ST 18 attack. Light and neutral users feel only warmth. Force-dead unaffected. Scalable: +4 FP per +1d damage (max 6d at 16 FP).

You raise both hands palm up. A burst of clean white-gold light pulses outward in a sphere — beautiful, brief, terrifying to those whose nature it scours.

Only damages dark-aligned beings/items. Sith with active Dark Aura take +1d. Critical failure: 2d FP self-strain. Visible to all Force-sensitives within 50 hexes.

Force Barrier

Raise a translucent wall of Force-energy 3 hexes wide × 2 hexes tall at any visible point within 5 hexes.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)Rangean anchored point within 5 hexes; a wall 3 hexes wide × 2 tallDefence— (a physical wall: DR 15, HP 40; allies fire through, enemies can’t)FP8ReactionNo

Effect. Raise a translucent wall of Force-energy 3 hexes wide × 2 hexes tall at any visible point within 5 hexes. DR 15, HP 40. Allies can shoot or cast through it from their side; enemies cannot. Scalable: +4 FP extends to 5 turns; +4 FP additional makes the barrier two-sided opaque (full visual blocker, not just defensive).

You set your hand toward the chosen point. A wall of pale blue-white light raises itself — visible like rippling water, unmistakably solid where it matters.

Cannot be raised as a reaction. Allies wishing to pass must spend a maneuver and a Force skill roll. The barrier emits a soft hum audible at 10 hexes.

IV · GRANDMASTER

Grandmaster

Pinnacle abilities. Near once-per-fight at Knight level. Scaling at this tier is decisive — the base costs are already high, so scaling investments represent serious commitment. Use only when the moment demands it.

✦ Light Side Powers

Stasis Field

All enemies in a 5-hex radius who fail Will are frozen in stasis for 3 turns.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn), then sustainedRange5-hex radius, line-of-sight to its centreDefenceEach enemy resists with Will independently (Will 14+ +2); any damage frees that one; allies unaffectedFP8ReactionNo

Effect. All enemies in a 5-hex radius who fail Will are frozen in stasis for 3 turns. Allies are unaffected. Any damage to a frozen target breaks their stasis instantly. Being hit while concentrating forces a Will roll or the entire field collapses. / Scalable: +3 FP extends the radius to 7 hexes. / +4 FP applies −2 to all targets' Will resistance rolls.

GRIP & BODY. Both hands held out and apart, freezing an area — the Force grips everything within the field. Two free hands; area-mode effective space.

You spread both hands outward and freeze. Every enemy caught locks into place simultaneously — a sudden still-life, expressions caught mid-snarl, weapons half-raised.

Will 14+ targets add +2. The field requires continuous concentration to sustain.

Force Enlightenment

For 1 minute, three pre-declared known Force powers are automatic successes without rolling.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn), 1 minRangeSelfDefence— (declare three known powers; they then auto-succeed)FP10ReactionNo

Effect. For 1 minute, three pre-declared known Force powers are automatic successes without rolling. FP costs still apply normally. Must declare which three powers before activating. If Stunned or Incapacitated during the minute, the effect ends. / Scalable: +5 FP extends the duration to 2 minutes.

You close your eyes and go still. When they open, your irises are filled with a steady, luminous white-gold light — present and unwavering. Every motion thereafter is precise and inevitable.

Powers still cost FP. Must pre-declare all three.

Wall of Light

Erect a barrier of living Force energy that blocks and punishes Dark Side users.
AffinityWillActionRitual (20 min; solo or cooperative — more casters = larger, stronger)Rangeanchored to a central point; radius scales with participants (see effect); no line-of-sightDefence— (an anti-dark ward; dark-siders inside are weakened and repelled)FP15 per participantReactionNo

Effect. Erect a barrier of living Force energy that blocks and punishes Dark Side users. Effect scales with participant count. / Solo (1 user, 15 FP): 10-metre radius. The barrier halves the Force skill of any Dark-sider inside and inflicts 1 point of physical discomfort per turn (not damaging, but deeply unpleasant — Will rolls to maintain concentration at −2). Dark-side powers activated inside automatically fail on a roll of 14+ regardless of skill. / 2 participants (30 FP total): 25-metre radius. Dark-siders inside suffer −3 to Force skill. Dark-side powers fail on 13+. Any Dark-sider attempting to cross makes a Force skill roll at −4 or is physically repelled 2 hexes. / 3+ participants (45+ FP total): 50-metre radius. Dark-siders inside suffer −4 to Force skill. Dark-side powers fail on 12+. Crossing requires winning a Force skill contest vs the combined Force skill of all participants. / The ritual takes 20 uninterrupted minutes regardless of participant count. Ritual takes 20 uninterrupted minutes regardless of participant count. / Dispelling requires a dark-side ritual spending FP equal to or greater than what was spent to create it.

The participants (or lone practitioner) press both palms together at the ritual's centre. A column of brilliant white light rises from their hands, then expands outward in a slow, unstoppable wave — a wall of living Force energy that hums at a frequency Dark-side users describe as unbearable.

Requires 20 uninterrupted minutes — any interruption aborts the ritual and wastes all FP. Solo use is possible but produces a smaller, weaker effect. The barrier is visible to Force Sense as a constant, powerful presence. Cannot be used in active combat.

Force Cleanse

Remove all active negative Force effects, mental influences, compulsions, fear-states, mind-affecting damage, and…
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (2 turns)RangeTouchDefencevs each affliction’s original caster Will; the ally is willingFP10ReactionNo

Effect. Remove all active negative Force effects, mental influences, compulsions, fear-states, mind-affecting damage, and dark-corruptive marks from one ally. Returns them to baseline mental and emotional state. Does not heal HP or FP. Scalable: +4 FP applies −2 to caster contest on each affliction; +5 FP additional includes removing one Force Bond (with both parties' willingness only).

Your palm rests against their brow. A slow, deliberate white pulse passes from your hand into their head, then chest. Their eyes clear. Their breath returns.

Cannot remove permanent traits (Force Horror trauma requires Force Restoration). Cannot break a Force Bond against either party's will. Cannot remove physical/biological damage.

Force Sacrifice

Pour every drop of your Force into healing one ally.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)RangeTouchDefence— (a willing ally; you drop to 1 HP, Stunned 1 min, no powers 1 hr)FPall your FP (heals 2 HP each)ReactionNo

Effect. Pour every drop of your Force into healing one ally. You drop to 0 FP and 1 HP. Ally regains HP equal to your transferred FP at 2:1 ratio (each FP heals 2 HP) AND has all mental/Force afflictions removed. You are Stunned (DX/IQ −4) for 1 minute and cannot use Force powers for 1 hour.

You place both palms against them and pour yourself out — visibly. Pale gold light streams from your body into theirs. When it ends, you are kneeling, breathing, alive but spent. They stand.

You drop to 1 HP — a single attack can kill you in this state. Allies witnessing react +3 to you afterward. Cannot be performed on yourself. Cannot be partial — all or nothing.

V · LEGEND

Legend

Mythic. Training: Special / Lifetime. These powers define situations and leave permanent marks. Scaling on legendary powers amplifies already-enormous effects — the cost reflects the consequence.

✦ Light Side — Legendary

Joining the Force

At the moment of death, consciously dissolve your physical form into the Force and become a Force ghost — able to…
AffinityWillActionRitual — at the moment of natural death, chosen freelyRangeSelfDefence— (requires genuine, unforced acceptance; desperation makes a tormented echo instead)FPall remainingReactionNo

Effect. At the moment of death, consciously dissolve your physical form into the Force and become a Force ghost — able to manifest, speak, and guide indefinitely. Requires genuine acceptance of death. Attempted in desperation or to escape consequences, you become a tormented echo instead — present but without peace or purpose.

Your body does not so much die as dissolve — slowly, over seconds, growing translucent until you are light, and then nothing visible. A warmth fills the space you occupied. Then it, too, is gone. But something remains.

Cannot be used to escape consequences or avoid a killing blow — requires genuine unforced acceptance at the actual moment of natural death. The choice must be made before the last breath, not because of it.

Force Restoration

Permanently restore the Force connection of an individual whose connection was severed — by injury, the dark side,…
AffinityWillActionRitual (30 min, sustained touch)RangeTouchDefence— (works on severances up to 10 years old; the subject rolls HT or is briefly incapacitated)FP20ReactionNo

Effect. Permanently restore the Force connection of an individual whose connection was severed — by injury, the dark side, trauma, or an external power. The connection is fully restored, not diminished. / Works on severances up to 10 years old. Beyond 10 years, the wound has calcified and this power cannot reach it. / The restored connection floods back all at once — overwhelming and often physically painful. The subject makes a HT roll or is briefly incapacitated by the returning sensation.

BODY. Both hands, usually framing the head or the injury — a deep ritual touch. Two free hands.

You sit with them for half an hour, both hands resting on their shoulders or forehead. The glow that builds is slow and deep — not the quick pulse of Force Heal, but something reaching into something foundational. The subject sometimes weeps without knowing why. Sometimes they gasp, as if surfacing from very cold water.

Healing Meditation (several hrs): a fully meditated Restoration can address deep, old, or Force-derived damage — trauma, lingering corruption-marks, and crippling that ordinary healing has long since set wrong. [SUSTAINED — you must stay in the meditative trance the whole time the effect runs (sit apart, let others act); if the meditation breaks, the whole enhanced effect ends, not just the bonus.]

Cannot restore connections severed by the Thought Bomb or equivalent mythic severance — those wounds are beyond this power's reach. The 30 minutes requires total stillness and silence from both parties. On a critical failure, the ritual fails and cannot be attempted on this subject again for 1 year.

The Unbroken Light

Cleanse a dark-side nexus, corrupted location, or powerful Sith artefact of its dark-side taint.
AffinityWillActionRitual (1 hr, cooperative — min. 3 Light-side casters)Rangea dark nexus, corrupted site, or Sith artefact (site-scale, no line-of-sight)Defence— (drives out the dark; dark powers nearby at −6 for 1d hours after)FP25 per participantReactionNo

Effect. Cleanse a dark-side nexus, corrupted location, or powerful Sith artefact of its dark-side taint. The ritual drives out the dark-side presence entirely — the location becomes Force-neutral, the artefact becomes inert, the nexus collapses. / Any dark-side entity or power sustained by the nexus is immediately disrupted — Force skill rolls at −6 for any dark-side power active in the area for 1d hours after. / The cleansing is not permanent if the original source of corruption returns — but without active reinvestment, the taint does not naturally return. / All participants are Exhausted (−4 all actions) for 1 hour after and permanently lose 1 HP each from the strain.

Three or more Force-users kneel around the nexus or artefact and breathe together. A slow pulse of warm white light builds from each of them — not dramatic, not explosive, just steady and inexorable. The dark-side presence does not go quietly. Those present describe the sensation of something vast and cold trying to hold on. Then it simply — isn't there anymore.

All participants must succeed their Force skill roll. A single failure collapses the ritual — all FP spent, no effect. Each participant loses 1 HP permanently. Minimum 3 users. Cannot cleanse a dark-side user — only locations and objects.

Convocation of Light

Performed in concert by 3+ Light-side practitioners, this ritual establishes a permanent Light Nexus at the prepared…
AffinityWillActionRitual (24 hr, cooperative — min. 3 Light-side casters)Rangea prepared site (site-scale, no line-of-sight)Defence— (one contest per pre-existing Dark presence)FP30 per participantReactionNo

Effect. Performed in concert by 3+ Light-side practitioners, this ritual establishes a permanent Light Nexus at the prepared site. Within: natural recovery rates double, fear/intimidation effects automatically fail, dark-aligned beings suffer −3 to all Force rolls. Lasts indefinitely unless dispelled by an equivalent dark-side ritual or weathered by abandonment over years.

Three or more practitioners kneel and join hands or touch the central focal point for a day and a night. The light builds slowly — not dramatic, just steady — until the place itself answers and the Force settles deeper there than anywhere else.

30 FP per participant is a major sacrifice — most spend a week recovering. The Nexus is detectable by any Force-user within 10km. Cannot be performed atop a Dark Nexus (requires Unbroken Light cleansing first).

BOOK II

THE COMMON FORCE

Neutral Powers
The foundational abilities that belong to no side. Telekinesis, sense, leap, the breath beneath all paths.
AVAILABLE TO ALL FORCE USERS

The Common Force

Foundational abilities available to any tradition

The Common Force — the Shared Art

The abilities that belong to no path — the shared tongue every tradition speaks.

Alignment req.None — open to all. No path bars these, and learning them shifts no one on the meter.
Force skillEach art uses its own skill (mostly IQ- or Will-based) — given on the power.
TiersI Initiate · II Knight · III Master · IV Grandmaster · V Legend — but with no gate of its own.
Capstone & CrossingNone. The Common Force has no capstones and no crossing — it rises with your Primary tradition.
To buyNo tier buy-in; each power is bought individually — 4 / 8 / 14 / 20 / 28 by its tier.

Scaling with your Primary. Your Common tier equals your Primary tradition’s tier, capped there — advance your Primary and your access to Common powers of that tier opens with it (you still pay each power’s point cost and brief learning time, but there are no capstones and no gate). Walk more than one path and your Common tier follows your highest. A raw Force-sensitive with no tradition may still learn Common powers up to Tier II on instinct, then stalls until they commit to a path.

These are the abilities that belong to no path. A Sith and a Jedi feel the same gravity when they push. A Mandalorian Force-sensitive and a Sith Inquisitor leap with the same arc. The Living Force is not partisan — it lends itself to anyone with the discipline to ask. These powers, listed here in the same tier structure as Light and Dark, form the shared vocabulary of every Force-using tradition.

The Order teaches them first. The Sith Academy also teaches them first. The Concordance considers them clean of doctrine and therefore safe to study early. Most independent Force-users learn them on instinct before any teacher names them.

Meditation Enhancement — A General Rule. Almost any Force ability can be deepened through meditation. By spending uninterrupted time in focused meditation on a specific power before using it — typically 10 minutes to 1 hour, occasionally longer for the grandest effects — a Force-user may produce a markedly stronger, more focused version of that ability than they could invoke in the moment.

As a default, a meditated working may roughly double its key output (range, duration, lifting capacity, healing, area, or potency — whichever defines the power) or sharpen it into a more precise, harder-to-resist form. The meditation must be uninterrupted; being struck, forced to move, or losing concentration wastes the time. The longer the meditation, the greater the enhancement — trading time for power on a sliding scale at the GM’s discretion.

Sustained vs. Prepared — and what happens if meditation breaks. Meditation enhancements come in two modes, and each rider says which it is. Sustained (the default for sensing, holding, and ongoing powers, e.g. Truth-Sense, Force Sense, Force Stasis): the practitioner must remain in the meditative trance for the whole time the effect runs — they typically sit apart and let someone else act (conduct the interrogation, fight the fight) while they hold the heightened working open. If the meditation ends — the practitioner is struck, moves, speaks at length, or simply stops — the entire enhanced effect ends immediately, not merely the bonus. Prepared (for instantaneous bursts, throws, and single discharges, e.g. Force Push, Force Lightning, a meditated heavy lift): the practitioner meditates beforehand to “charge” the working, then releases it in a normal action; the charge holds until used or until their next full rest, and once released the effect resolves on its own. As a rule of thumb: if the power does something over time, meditation is sustained and fragile; if it happens in an instant, meditation is prepared and fire-and-forget.

Specific powers throughout this Codex carry their own named Meditation Enhancement riders where the effect is iconic or mechanically distinct (Telekinesis Meditation, Healing Meditation, and so on). Where a power has no named rider, the general rule above applies. For holocron-adepts such as practitioners of the Inscribed Voice, a Ritual Enhancement (see Book IV) can substitute ritual intensity for meditation time — achieving the same deepening faster, or pushing further given both.

Extended Exposure — A General Rule. Some powers grow stronger not through preparation but through repetition on the same victim. Where meditation is the wrong fit — mostly powers that work on a mind or body over time, such as Affect Mind, Force Menace, Force Pain, and similar — the power instead carries an Extended Exposure rider (marked ⌗). Each repeated success on the same individual within a span of days lowers their resistance cumulatively (typically −1 per use, to a −4 maximum) and lets the effect sink deeper, until what began as a nudge becomes a standing condition. A mind worked on long enough does not merely yield — it changes.

Casting Always Uses Your Force Attribute. However a power scales its effect — against a target’s attribute, against the attribute of an assisted skill, against a cap of ½ this-or-that — the activation and any Force contest are always rolled against your own main Force attribute (the one listed in the power’s stat block, usually Will or IQ). Effect-scaling and cap-scaling are about how far the power reaches; the casting roll itself is always your connection to the Force, and nothing else.

The Telekinetic Load System — Basic Lift, Hands & Focus. Telekinesis does not let you fling a starship with a flick of will. It is lifting, done with the mind instead of the back, and it obeys the same limits. Your telekinetic Strength equals your governing Force attribute (one of ST, DX, Will or HT — your choice). From it derives your Telekinetic Basic Lift exactly as in GURPS:

Telekinetic BL = (Force Attribute × Force Attribute) ÷ 5, in kg

Everything you move, hold, hurl, or crush is measured against that BL, through three gates of effort:

Effort GateWeight CeilingWhat It Costs You
One hand, freeup to 1× BL1 FP. A free hand, gesture only. You may still act, move, fight, hold a lit saber in the other hand.
One hand, full reachup to 2× BL2 FP. A free hand, committed — this is your one-handed lift ceiling. No other action with that hand this turn.
Two hands + focusup to 8× BL4 FP. BOTH hands free and extended, full concentration: lightsaber unignited / holstered, no attack, no defense bonus, no movement beyond a step. This is the heaviest a snap invocation can manage.
Beyond 8× BLmore than two hands can holdImpossible as a snap action. Requires Meditation (below) or a holocron Ritual Enhancement. You simply cannot grab it cold.

Worked example (Force attribute 36): BL = (36×36)÷5 = 259 kg. One free hand moves up to 118 kg; one committed hand lifts up to ~235 kg; two hands with full focus heave up to ~940 kg — a speeder bike, a heavy door, a single armoured warrior thrown like a sack. A landspeeder (~2 t) or a starfighter (~10 t) is simply out of reach without meditation, no matter how much FP is spent. FP buys exertion, never tonnage — you cannot pay your way past your own Strength.

Hurling as a weapon. An object thrown telekinetically strikes for crushing damage by its weight bracket relative to your BL: a load up to 1× BL does thrust-based damage as an improvised missile; a 2× load does +2 per die; an 8× two-handed heave is a genuine vehicle-scale impact (GM sets dice by mass, but treat a fully-focused two-handed hurl as the equivalent of a small-vehicle collision). You can only throw what you could lift — so a starship-as-weapon is, again, meditation territory.

Telekinesis Meditation — Lifting Beyond Two Hands. To move what your hands cannot, you must meditate, and meditation doubles your effective Force Strength for every tier of time invested (BL rises with the square of Strength, so each doubling of ST multiplies your lift fourfold):

MeditationEffective STTwo-Hand Lift (at base ST 36)Reaches
None (snap)×1~940 kgA warrior, a door, a speeder bike
10 minutes×2~3.7 tonnesA landspeeder, a mounted gun emplacement
30 minutes×4~15 tonnesA starfighter, a small dwelling’s roof
1 hour×8~60 tonnesA light freighter, a bridge span
3 hours / ritual×16+~240+ tonnesA capital-ship turret, a collapsing tower — the stuff of legend

The meditation must be uninterrupted, and the lifted mass can be held or hurled only at the end of it — a meditated lift is a prepared feat, not a reaction. A holocron Ritual Enhancement (Book V) substitutes ritual intensity for meditation time, reaching the same multipliers faster, or further given both. The figures above scale from a base ST of 36 for illustration; recompute BL from each character’s own Force attribute.

Surface vs. envelopment. Powers that shove a surface (Push, Pull) act on one face of the target and use the gates above directly. Powers that envelop a whole mass (Grip, Stasis, Choke) hold every part at once and so halve the weight ceiling at each gate (the Force is doing twice the work). Each power’s entry states which it is.

I · INITIATE

Initiate

Foundational abilities. Skill bonus: +2. Low cost and reliable — the building blocks everything else rests on. Powers marked Scalable allow extra FP to be spent for stronger effects or resistance penalties on the target.

Force Sense

Sense nearby living beings, strong emotions, active Force use, or impending danger within 10 hexes; gives direction and…
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)Range10-hex radius, no line-of-sightDefenceNone — passive (Force Stealth can hide from it)FP2, then 1 to maintainReactionNo

Effect. Sense nearby living beings, strong emotions, active Force use, or impending danger within 10 hexes; gives direction and rough intensity. Scalable: +2 FP widens the radius by 5 hexes (to a maximum of 20 at 6 FP). +4 FP more: on a success, read the dominant emotional state of each sensed being — fear, hostility, grief, calm — not just their presence.

BODY. Bodiless — a turning of attention. Often eyes-closed for focus, but needs no gesture or free limb.

Eyes close. Awareness spreads outward in slow rings. Presences register as warmth — those in fear or pain a sharper note.

Perception Meditation (30 min): double the range and resolution — read a whole settlement’s emotional weather, or pinpoint a single hidden mind at distances raw sensing cannot reach. [SUSTAINED — stay in the trance the whole time the effect runs; if it breaks, the whole enhanced effect ends.]

Direction and intensity only — not identity or specific intent. Fails against Force Stealth (Quick Contest). The emotional read gives broad category only.

Force Leap

Leap up to 1 hex per FP in any direction, ignoring normal jump limits.
AffinityDXActionInstantRangeSelfDefence— (affects only you)FP1 per hex leaptReactionNo

Effect. Leap up to 1 hex per FP in any direction, ignoring normal jump limits. Can chain into an attack on arrival.

BODY. The whole body coils and launches — legs drive, the Force amplifies the jump. No hands required; usable mid-duel. Landing wants at least one limb free to steady.

A drop into a half-crouch, then an explosion upward or forward trailing a barely-visible shimmer of kinetic Force energy. Dust scatters from the launch point.

Movement Meditation (10 min): double your leap distance and land without a roll; a meditated leap clears a building or crosses a chasm a snap-leap could not. [PREPARED — charge it beforehand, then release in a normal action; the charge holds until used or your next rest.]

Cannot be used while Prone or Grappled. Landing on difficult terrain needs a DX roll or you stumble. An arrival attack suffers −1 unless Acrobatics succeeds.

Force Push

Shove one target or loose object 1 hex per FP.
AffinitySTActionAttackRange8 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceDodge if aware; a caught or braced target resists with ST instead (margin = hexes shoved)FP1 per hex pushedReactionYes — uses your Reaction; also an Attack/Quick Action on your turn

Effect. Shove one target or loose object 1 hex per FP. Optional: 1 flat crushing damage per 2 FP. Driving a target into a solid obstacle deals collision damage (1d per 2 hexes of momentum lost). Scalable: +3 FP applies −2 to the target’s ST resistance.

BODY. A free open hand thrust toward the target — the Force forms a broad palm-shaped plate of pressure against a single surface (a chest, a door, a wall of bodies), shoving rather than enveloping; it strikes one face, not the whole. Weight ceiling per the Telekinetic Load System (one hand = up to your Basic Lift; one hand committed = 2×BL; two hands + full focus = 8×BL). A free hand is required; at Force skill 20+, two extended fingers from the saber hand suffice (the blade stays lit).

The hand extends, fingers spread. A visible compression wave distorts the air — a pulse of transparent pressure that slams outward with a sharp crack.

Telekinesis Meditation: each tier of meditation DOUBLES your effective Force-ST for this working (10 min ×2, 30 min ×4, 1 hr ×8…), and Basic Lift rises with the square of ST — so 30 minutes’ focus turns a 940 kg heave into a 15-tonne one. Lifting beyond your two-handed ceiling REQUIRES this; FP never buys tonnage on its own. [PREPARED — charge it beforehand, then release in a normal action; the charge holds until used or your next rest.]

Large or braced targets (SM +1 or higher) add +2 to ST per SM level. Affects allies in the path of thrown objects.

Force Pull

Pull a target or object toward you 1 hex per FP.
AffinitySTActionAttackRange8 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceResisted by ST — margin = hexes hauled; braced or anchored targets +3FP1 per hex pulledReactionNo

Effect. Pull a target or object toward you 1 hex per FP. Optional: 1 flat crushing damage per 2 FP. / Scalable: +3 FP flat applies −2 to the target's ST resistance roll.

GRIP & BODY. A free hand reaching out and drawing back, hooking the Force around a graspable feature (a limb, a strap, an edge) and hauling. Like Push it grips a surface, not the whole mass — pulling a being by the chest drags them face-first. Weight ceiling by the hand/focus gates of the Telekinetic Load System. Free hand required; two-finger saber-hand variant at skill 20+.

A hand snaps toward you, palm open. The target lurches forward as if yanked by an invisible cable, air whistling around them.

Telekinesis Meditation: each meditation tier DOUBLES effective Force-ST (10 min ×2, 30 min ×4, 1 hr ×8…); Basic Lift scales with ST squared. This is the only way to pull something heavier than your two-handed lift ceiling — a mounted gun, a landing strut, a pinned ally under rubble. [PREPARED — meditate beforehand to charge it, then release in a normal action; the charge holds until used or your next rest, and resolves on its own once loosed.]

Braced or anchored targets gain +3 to resist. Cannot pull objects heavier than 10× your ST in kg. Pulled objects travel in a straight line — anything in the path must dodge.

Force Focus

+1 per FP to a single Will or mental resistance roll.
AffinityWillActionInstantRangeSelfDefence— (self buff)FP1 per +1 (max = ½ Will)ReactionNo — Instant; applies even out of turn

Effect. +1 per FP to a single Will or mental resistance roll. Must be declared before the roll is made. Maximum bonus = HALF your Will, round down — focus can only marshal the discipline you already possess. (Activation uses your Force attribute as normal.)

Eyes sharpen. A faint luminescence gathers briefly in the irises — gone in a blink, like a shutter opening and closing.

Cannot be retroactively applied after a roll is declared.

Force Brace

+1 per FP to resist knockback, sweep attacks, or forced movement until your next turn.
AffinitySTActionReactionRangeSelfDefence— (self buff)FP1 per +1ReactionYes — uses your Reaction

Effect. +1 per FP to resist knockback, sweep attacks, or forced movement until your next turn. Triggered as a reaction when knockback is about to occur.

BODY. The whole body roots — stance and core, the Force anchoring the feet. No hands needed; can brace while wielding.

Feet plant. A subtle golden haze traces up the legs and spine, then vanishes — still as carved stone for a moment.

Must be triggered before the knockback roll resolves. Grants no bonus against gravity (falls) or established grapples.

Force Object Sense

Sense recent emotional or Force-significant impressions left on an object within the past day.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)RangeTouch, or an object in line-of-sightDefenceNone — passive read (margin sets clarity & how far back)FP2ReactionNo

Effect. Sense recent emotional or Force-significant impressions left on an object within the past day. Impressions are emotional — not verbal. A critical failure returns a deliberately distorted impression.

BODY. Bodiless reach of attention toward a thing; a free hand laid on or pointed at the object sharpens it (+1) but is not required.

Both hands cup the object. Eyes go unfocused. Faint traces of colour seem to bleed from the object into the air around it — visible only at the edge of perception.

Mundane or heavily-handled objects give muddled signals. Impressions fade significantly beyond 24 hours.

Force Balance Walk

Ignore all penalties from unstable, slippery, or hazardous footing while maintained.
AffinityDXActionInstant, then maintainedRangeSelfDefence— (self buff)FP1 per turnReactionNo

Effect. Ignore all penalties from unstable, slippery, or hazardous footing while maintained.

BODY. Whole-body poise — the feet and inner ear, guided by the Force. Hands free for anything.

Movement becomes unnaturally fluid — each step lands exactly, weight shifting mid-stride with eerie precision.

Ends immediately on being knocked prone or stunned. Does not help against deliberately induced knockdown.

Force Stealth

Suppress your Force signature entirely.
AffinityDXActionConcentrate (1 turn)RangeSelf (1-hex aura)Defence— a searcher beats it with a Quick Contest of Force SenseFP2 per minuteReactionNo

Effect. Suppress your Force signature entirely. You are immune to passive Force Sense. Active searchers must win a Quick Contest of Force skills to detect you.

Nothing visible. Your Force presence simply ceases — like a candle being cupped. Those with active Force Sense describe the sensation as a warm presence going cold and quiet.

Concealment Meditation (30 min): the suppression holds even while using low-tier powers, and extends to mask one adjacent ally as well as yourself. [SUSTAINED — you must stay in the meditative trance the whole time the effect runs (sit apart, let others act); if the meditation breaks, the whole enhanced effect ends, not just the bonus.]

Does not hide you physically. Using a Tier 2+ Force power while Stealthed automatically breaks it for that turn.

Force Echo

Read the residue an event leaves in the Force.
AffinityIQ or Per (best)ActionConcentrate (1 turn)RangeTouch a place/object, or within 8 hexes of an event-siteDefenceNone — passive readFP2 (scalable)ReactionNo

Effect. Read the residue an event leaves in the Force. Strong moments — death, terror, triumph, ritual, the passage of a powerful Force-user — soak into a place or object and linger as an echo. Touching the site (or standing in it) lets you replay the strongest echo: who was here, what they felt, roughly when, and the shape of what happened. Belongs to no alignment — light, dark, and grey traditions all read echoes; only what they DO with the knowledge differs. Scalable: +2 FP reads progressively older or fainter echoes; +3 FP reads a sequence of events rather than just the loudest one.

You go still and let the place speak. The Force holds what happened here the way a room holds a smell after the fire is out — and if you listen with the right kind of quiet, the echo plays back behind your eyes.

Violent or atrocity-scale echoes may force a Will roll or leave you Shaken (1d turns). Heavily-trafficked sites blur into noise. A site deliberately wiped (see The Hollowing, Book IV) reads as a wrong, silent absence — itself a clue.

Veil of the Quiet Self

Suppress not just your Force signature but its alignment-colour as well.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)RangeSelfDefence— a searcher beats it with a Quick Contest (and learns only that someone is there)FP2, then 1 per minuteReactionNo

Effect. Suppress not just your Force signature but its alignment-colour as well. Passive Force Sense reads nothing; active searchers must win a Quick Contest of Force skills to detect you, and even on success they learn only that someone is there — not whether you are light, dark, or neutral. Where plain Force Stealth cups the candle, this hides what colour the flame was. Scalable: +2 FP applies −2 to searchers' detection contest.

You let your sense of yourself go still and grey. Not absent — absent draws attention. Just unremarkable, unreadable, the texture of empty air. Even your alignment, the thing every Force-user wears like a scent, simply isn't there to read.

Does not hide you physically. Using a Tier 2+ Force power while veiled breaks it for that turn. Sustained hostile scrutiny (a dedicated Force-interrogator) may force repeated contests. Cannot mask the signature of an active lightsaber or ongoing loud working.

II · KNIGHT

Knight

Reliable combat and utility powers. Skill bonus: +3. Average cost 3−8 FP once scaled. Scaling options increase effect, extend duration, lower resistance, or expand area — invest more FP for meaningfully harder-to-resist results.

Force Speed

+1 Move per FP/turn (max +2), or +1 Dodge/Parry per 2 FP/turn (max +2) while maintained.
AffinityDXActionConcentrate (1 turn), then maintainedRangeSelfDefence— (self buff)FP1 per turnReactionNo

Effect. +1 Move per FP/turn (max +2), or +1 Dodge/Parry per 2 FP/turn (max +2) while maintained. / Effects can be mixed. Attacking while maintaining costs 1 extra FP that turn.

BODY. A whole-body enhancement — no gesture, no hands. Runs, leaps, and strikes all accelerate. Fully compatible with a two-handed saber grip.

You become a smear of motion — not invisible, but trailing a faint after-image delay. Footfalls barely register.

Movement Meditation (10 min): double the duration and add an extra +1 to Move steps beyond the base; sustained meditated speed lets you cross a battlefield before the enemy reacts. [PREPARED — meditate beforehand to charge it, then release in a normal action; the charge holds until used or your next rest, and resolves on its own once loosed.]

Being struck forces a Will roll — failure drops the speed bonus by 1 step. Activating costs the concentrate turn.

Force Grip

Immobilise a target in place. Optional: 1 flat crushing damage per 2 FP while gripping.
AffinitySTActionConcentrate (1 turn), then maintainedRange8 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceResisted by ST, re-rolled each turn at cumulative +1; losing line-of-sight ends itFP2, then 2 per turnReactionNo

Effect. Immobilise a target in place. Optional: 1 flat crushing damage per 2 FP while gripping. Can drag (1 hex/turn, 1 extra FP). Target re-rolls ST each turn at +1 cumulative. / Scalable: +3 FP upfront applies −2 to the target's initial ST resistance roll. / +1 FP/turn increases drag speed to 2 hexes/turn.

GRIP & BODY. The Force closes around the WHOLE target like an invisible fist — this is true envelopment, not a surface shove, which is why it can lift, hold aloft, and crush. A free hand clenches slowly in the air; the degree of closure maps to the squeeze. Lift ceiling by the hand/focus gates — but HALVED at each gate because Grip envelops the whole mass (true lifting/holding/crushing). Two hands + focus tops out near 4×BL enveloped. Free hand required.

One hand raises, fingers half-closed. The target freezes mid-motion. Visible pressure dimples their clothing around the chest or limbs.

Telekinesis Meditation: each tier DOUBLES effective Force-ST (10 min ×2, 30 min ×4…). Note Grip ENVELOPS, so its weight ceiling is HALF a shove's at every gate — but with meditation even a halved ceiling reaches landspeeder mass and beyond. Required to lift or crush anything past your two-handed limit. [SUSTAINED — you must stay in the meditative trance the whole time the effect runs (sit apart, let others act); if the meditation breaks, the whole enhanced effect ends, not just the bonus.]

Losing LoS ends the grip immediately. Cannot use other concentration powers simultaneously. Large targets (SM+2) start first resistance at +3.

Force Step

Move 1 hex per FP without triggering free attacks or reactions.
AffinityDXActionInstantRangeSelf — blink up to 5 hexesDefence— (affects only you)FP1 per hexReactionNo

Effect. Move 1 hex per FP without triggering free attacks or reactions. Does not consume your normal step.

BODY. A blink of the whole body — a breath and a flex of will, no hands needed. Anything held travels with her.

You move and leave nothing — a glide that skips friction entirely. No footfall sound.

Movement Meditation (10 min): double the range of the blink and, on arrival, you are not winded — may act immediately at no penalty. [PREPARED — meditate beforehand to charge it, then release in a normal action; the charge holds until used or your next rest, and resolves on its own once loosed.]

Cannot be used while Grappled or Pinned.

Tutaminis (Energy Absorption)

The discipline of catching energy in the bare hand — a blaster bolt, a gout of Force Lightning, a wash of flame drunk…
RollForce (Tutaminis — Very Hard)ActionActive defense (out of turn)RangeSelf — bare hand; you must perceive the attackDefenceIt IS the defence — Tutaminis Parry = 3 + Skill/2, in place of a Dodge or saber-parryFPbolts 1 → free at skill +2; heavy energy 1 per 4 damage (see table)ReactionNo

Effect. The discipline of catching energy in the bare hand — a blaster bolt, a gout of Force Lightning, a wash of flame drunk into the body and the Force rather than left to burn. It is a Parry, not a contest: roll against your Tutaminis Parry (3 + Skill/2) exactly as you would parry a blade, and a success catches the attack whole — it deals no damage. It counts as your active defense for that attack, so you may Tutaminis in place of a Dodge or a saber-parry-and-resist. You must see it coming; a blindside cannot be turned, just as no parry answers an unseen blow. It works only on energy — blaster bolts, Force Lightning, plasma, flame, ion and the like — never on slugs, shrapnel, vibroblades, or anything with mass behind it.

BODY. An open hand raised palm-out, fingers slightly spread, the body utterly still in the instant of contact. The energy seems to vanish into the palm. Masters stand in a stream of Lightning and let it crawl harmlessly across their skin.

Your hand does not burn. The galaxy insists it should, and you decline.

Energy only — blaster bolts, Lightning, plasma, flame, ion; never slugs, shrapnel, blades, or anything with mass behind it. You must see the attack coming; a blindside cannot be caught. It is your active defence for that blow, so you cannot also Dodge or saber-parry the same attack.

What it turns, and what it costs. Pay the FP after a successful Parry. Bolts are cheap and soon free; the heavy energies always exact a toll scaled to their fury.

What you turnSkillFP
Bolts & light energy — blaster, ion, stun, a single small flameTutaminis known1 per deflection
Bolts & light energyTutaminis +2 over ForceFree
Heavy / streamed energy — Force Lightning, plasma jets, flamethrower gouts, heavy cannonTutaminis +2 over Force1 per 4 points of damage turned (round up)

The +2 line. Tutaminis is Very Hard, so reaching +2 over your Force attribute is real, dedicated study. Below it, every bolt you catch costs 1 FP. At +2 and beyond, ordinary bolts come free — and only then can you begin turning the heavy stuff.

Heavy energy — pay per point. Force Lightning and its kin never come free: you pay 1 FP for every 4 points of damage you turn aside (round up). A 12-point Lightning arc costs 3 FP; a 20-point torrent, 5. The greater the storm, the more it drains you — this is what lets a master stand in a Sith’s lightning and answer with an open hand instead of a saber or a desperate dive.

If you fail. A failed Parry means the energy lands normally — you take the hit. On a critical failure against heavy energy, the hand and arm take the blow in full, with the usual crippling consequences.

Force Bellow

Concussive shout. All targets in a 3-hex forward cone who fail ST are knocked back 1 hex and make a DX roll or fall…
AffinitySTActionAttackRange3-hex forward cone, no line-of-sight (+2 FP → 5 hexes)DefenceResisted by ST — those who fail are knocked back and must make DX or fall; solid cover blocks itFP3ReactionNo

Effect. Concussive shout. All targets in a 3-hex forward cone who fail ST are knocked back 1 hex and make a DX roll or fall prone. Affects allies in the cone too. / Scalable: +2 FP extends the cone to 5 hexes. / +2 FP increases knockback to 2 hexes. Stackable with range upgrade.

BODY. Projected through the VOICE and lungs — a shout amplified by the Force. Requires the ability to vocalise (gagged or silenced casters cannot use it); hands free.

Your mouth opens and the sound that comes out is bigger than a human body should produce — a visible pressure ring warping the air outward.

You must brace or are also pushed 1 hex backwards. Targets behind solid cover are unaffected.

Mind Shard

Deal 1d+2 mental (Fatigue) damage directly to the target's FP pool.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)Range8 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceResisted by Will or Mind Block; never dodgedFP3ReactionNo

Effect. Deal 1d+2 mental (Fatigue) damage directly to the target's FP pool. Target cannot make quick or instant actions until they succeed on a HT roll each turn. Directly interrupts any active concentration. On a critical failure by you, the backlash deals 1d Fatigue to yourself. / Scalable: +3 FP applies −2 to the target's Will resistance roll. / +3 FP extends the incapacitation duration to 2d turns.

BODY. A sharp pointing gesture of one hand toward the target's head — focuses the spike of pain. One hand, need not be empty.

You stare at the target with sudden, alarming focus. There is no visible effect — but the target staggers, blinking hard, one hand going to their head.

Targets with Mental Resistance (IQ 14+) add +2 to Will. Force-dead targets are immune.

Force Whirlwind

Trap a target in a kinetic vortex.
AffinitySTActionConcentrate (1 turn), then maintainedRange4-hex radius, line-of-sightDefenceResisted by ST, re-rolled each turn at cumulative +1; losing line-of-sight ends itFP3, then 2 per turnReactionNo

Effect. Trap a target in a kinetic vortex. Target is immobilised and suffers −2 to all actions. Losing LoS or using another concentration power simultaneously ends it immediately. / Scalable: +4 FP upfront applies −2 to the target's initial ST resistance roll. / +2 FP/turn adds 1 flat crushing damage to the target each turn they remain trapped.

GRIP & BODY. A circling gesture of one or both hands — the Force forms a rotating column that grips loose objects and light bodies around their whole mass and flings them centrifugally. What the column can hoist is bounded by your Basic Lift; loose light objects and bodies up to your one-hand ceiling are flung freely. At least one free hand.

The target is suddenly spinning — buffeted by a column of Force energy manifesting as a tight spiral of displaced air, dust, and debris.

Telekinesis Meditation: double the vortex radius; and each meditation tier doubles the effective ST governing what it can hoist, so a meditated whirlwind lifts and flings bodies and loose mass it could otherwise only batter. [SUSTAINED — you must stay in the meditative trance the whole time the effect runs (sit apart, let others act); if the meditation breaks, the whole enhanced effect ends, not just the bonus.]

Cumulative +1 to target's ST resistance each turn. Cannot use other concentration powers simultaneously.

III · MASTER

Master

Complex techniques of high focus. Skill bonus: +3. Average cost 5−15 FP once scaled. At this tier, scaling resistance penalties matters most — peer Force-users have high resistance rolls, and every −2 you can apply changes the outcome.

Psychometry

See the recent history of an object or location.
AffinityWillActionRitual (20 min, uninterrupted)RangeTouchDefenceNone — passive read (margin sets how far back)FP5ReactionNo

Effect. See the recent history of an object or location. Margin of success determines timeframe: base = past month. Margin 3+: past 6 months. Critical success: past 5 years. Any interruption loses all FP and yields nothing. / Scalable: +3 FP extends the base timeframe to 6 months (adjusts all margins up accordingly). / +5 FP on top: base becomes 2 years, critical success reaches decades.

BODY. Through TOUCH — a free hand laid on the object, reading its history through the skin. One hand must contact the item.

Both hands cup the object and you go still. Impressions play across the air above it — fragmented, overlapping — visible only if watching your hands directly.

Perception Meditation (1 hr): read far deeper into an object's history — not just the last strong impression but a sequence of events, with names and faces where the impressions were strong enough. [SUSTAINED — you must stay in the meditative trance the whole time the effect runs (sit apart, let others act); if the meditation breaks, the whole enhanced effect ends, not just the bonus.]

Full 20-minute concentration required. Traumatic visions may leave you Shaken (HT roll to avoid).

Malacia

Target suffers severe dizziness: −4 to DX and Move for up to 5 turns.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)Range8 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceResisted by HT, re-rolled each turn; never dodged (droids & beings with no inner ear are immune)FP4ReactionNo

Effect. Target suffers severe dizziness: −4 to DX and Move for up to 5 turns. Each turn the target rolls HT — success reduces penalty by 1. Droids and creatures without inner ears are immune. / Scalable: +2 FP applies −2 to the target's initial HT resistance roll. / +2 FP extends the effect to one additional adjacent target who makes their own HT roll.

You make a slow pressing motion. The target pales. Their eyes lose focus. The world for them has begun to spin.

Droids are immune. Targets with HT 14+ start first resistance at +2. No ongoing LoS required once applied.

Force Wave

360° kinetic burst. All targets within 4 hexes who fail ST are pushed 2 hexes and take 2 flat crushing damage.
AffinitySTActionAttackRange4-hex radius burst around you (360°, no line-of-sight)DefenceDodge if aware; else resisted by ST — failure = pushed 2 hexes + 2 crushingFP5ReactionNo

Effect. 360° kinetic burst. All targets within 4 hexes who fail ST are pushed 2 hexes and take 2 flat crushing damage. You must brace or are pushed back 1 hex yourself. Affects allies. / Scalable: +3 FP increases push distance to 3 hexes. / +4 FP increases damage to 3 flat crushing. Both options stack.

GRIP & BODY. Both hands sweep outward from the centre of the body — the Force radiates as an expanding spherical shell of force from the caster, gripping every surface it passes across at once. The wave grips every surface in its radius at once; what it can actually MOVE within that radius is bounded by your Basic Lift per the hand/focus gates. Best with two free hands; one hand halves radius.

You drop both arms downward with sudden force, then release — a visible ring of compressed Force energy blasts outward from your entire body in a perfect disc.

Telekinesis Meditation: double the radius and knockback; and since the wave grips every surface it crosses, meditation's ST-doubling lets it move heavier bodies and debris within that radius — a meditated wave topples walls and scatters a squad rather than merely shoving them. [PREPARED — meditate beforehand to charge it, then release in a normal action; the charge holds until used or your next rest, and resolves on its own once loosed.]

Cannot be aimed at specific targets. You are also pushed back if you do not brace.

False Aspect

Project a deliberately false alignment to the Force.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 min), then maintained at −2 to all elseRangeSelfDefence— an observer pierces it only with a Quick Contest of Force skill and reason to doubtFP10, then 3 per minuteReactionNo

Effect. Project a deliberately false alignment to the Force. A dark-side practitioner can read as luminously light; a Jedi can read as a creature of the dark; a neutral can wear either colour. Other Force-users sensing you receive the false reading unless they win a Quick Contest of Force skill against you AND have specific reason to doubt. This is far more than concealment — it is an active lie told to the Force itself, and the Force resists being lied to. Requires heavy, ongoing concentration; you act at −2 to all other rolls while maintaining it.

You build the mask deliberately, layer by layer, until the Force itself reports you as something you are not. To the searching mind you are exactly what you wish to seem — and nothing about you reads false, because the falseness goes all the way down to the signature.

Sustaining a facade against your true nature is a strain. A LIGHT practitioner masquerading as DARK risks corruption: each 10 continuous minutes, roll Will or take 1 Corruption, as wearing the dark too convincingly invites it inward. (A dark practitioner feigning light suffers no such risk — the light does not creep in uninvited, it merely fails to take hold.) Dropping the facade is a Free action. Critical failure on the maintaining roll: the mask inverts and briefly broadcasts your TRUE alignment amplified — unmistakable to every Force-user in range for 1 turn.

IV · GRANDMASTER

Grandmaster

Pinnacle abilities. Near once-per-fight at Knight level. Scaling at this tier is decisive — the base costs are already high, so scaling investments represent serious commitment. Use only when the moment demands it.

Doppelganger

Project a semi-corporeal duplicate of yourself before a chosen individual.
AffinityWillActionRitual (deep meditation; you are defenceless throughout)RangeProjected before a chosen, Force-connected individual (no line-of-sight)DefenceNone — observers may roll IQ to notice the signature is thin and remoteFP10ReactionNo

Effect. Project a semi-corporeal duplicate of yourself before a chosen individual. It can speak and appear physically present but cannot interact with anything material. You are completely defenceless while projecting — any damage ends it immediately.

Your body goes very still and your eyes defocus entirely. Elsewhere — sometimes far away — a figure assembles itself from nothing: first an outline, then mass, then your face, bearing, and voice.

Any Force-user observing the duplicate can roll IQ to notice the Force signature is thin and remote.

Shatterpoint Sense

Perceive structural and physical weaknesses in a target.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)Rangea target within 20 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceNone — you read the target; the +10 rides your next attack only (critical failure inverts it to −5)FP8ReactionNo

Effect. Perceive structural and physical weaknesses in a target. +10 to hit on your very next successful attack roll. The bonus applies to the next attack only, regardless of when it occurs. If interrupted before you attack, the effect is lost.

BODY. Bodiless — a focusing of sight upon the target; the weaknesses overlay the vision. No hands.

Your eyes go distant. For a moment you are seeing something overlaid on the target — stress lines, fault points — like a diagram of light rendered over reality.

Perception Meditation (1 hr): perceive not just one weakness but the entire web of consequence around a person, object, or decision — the Force shows you which thread, if cut, unravels the most. [SUSTAINED — you must stay in the meditative trance the whole time the effect runs (sit apart, let others act); if the meditation breaks, the whole enhanced effect ends, not just the bonus.]

Critical failure inverts the bonus to −5.

Battle Meditation

A rare centre-of-the-Force discipline.
AffinityWillActionRitual (1 hr to enter; you sit helpless while it holds)RangeLocalized 6–12 hexes, scaling to Extended / Unleashed — see modesDefenceNone for allies; a hostile Force-user may contest Will to disruptFP20, then 2 per minute (4 when Unleashed)ReactionNoPrereqWill-based Force-user, IQ 14+

Effect. A rare centre-of-the-Force discipline. The meditator becomes the still axis around which an entire battle turns — not commanding allies, but letting the Force flow through their own ordering mind into everyone fighting alongside them. Requires a Will-based Force-user with a keen intellect (IQ 14+); a being who fights on instinct cannot hold the lattice of a battle in their head.

CORE EFFECT (all modes). Every ally within the active radius gains +6 to all combat skills, +3 to all active defenses (Dodge, Parry, Block), and immunity to fright checks and being caught by surprise. More than the numbers: their combative minds are linked through the meditator, so tactics merge — flanks hold, fire is concentrated without a word spoken, no one strays or breaks formation. In large-scale combat this is decisive: a Battle-Meditated force does not splinter, rout, or lose cohesion, because the Force itself is choreographing them as one body.

THREE MODES OF REACH (scalable by time and ritual strength):

• LOCALIZED (base, 1-hour ritual): radius 6−12 hexes around the meditator. The standard battlefield form.

• EXTENDED (2-hour ritual, or 1 hour for a master with a holocron Ritual Enhancement): radius expands to a full engagement zone — hundreds of metres, an entire battlefield or a besieged compound.

• UNLEASHED (4+ hour ritual at a Force Nexus, or a Ritual-Enhanced working for a skilled adept): influence spans an entire star system — every allied ship and soldier in the theatre. Maintaining Unleashed is exhausting (4 FP/min) and leaves the meditator helpless and immobile throughout.

SCALABILITY. Range is bought with time or ritual strength: more reach demands a longer ritual, OR a stronger ritual enhancement (see Ritual Enhancement, Inscribement). A field caster trades hours for reach; a holocron-adept can trade ritual intensity for the same expansion in less time, or push further given both.

She sits at the centre and goes utterly still, and the battle arranges itself around that stillness. Every soldier feels a quiet certainty arrive — where to step, when to fire, who to cover. None of them could tell you why they suddenly fight like one organism. The meditator is not giving orders. She is the place the Force passes through on its way to all of them.

The meditator cannot move or fight while sustaining it — leaving the meditative seat collapses the working instantly. Disrupting concentration (a hit, a forced move, a successful hostile Will contest) ends it. Allies who turn hostile during the effect instead suffer −3 (the lattice rejects them). Hostile Force-users who sense the meditation may attempt Will contests to pinpoint and disrupt the meditator — and the meditator is an obvious, high-value, defenceless target for the duration. RITUAL-ENHANCED VARIANT (deep mind-link): a Ritual-Enhanced Battle Meditation forges a far deeper connection — the meditator can see through the eyes of any linked ally and channel Force powers through them, from their position and perspective. But this depth makes forming a permanent Force Bond with any linked ally almost effortless and largely involuntary — bonds may form whether or not either party intends it, with all the risk, vulnerability, and lasting consequence that Force Bonds carry (see Force Bonds). The deeper the link, the harder it is to keep separate minds separate.

V · LEGEND

Legend

Mythic workings of the shared Force — abilities no single Order owns, because they belong to the living current itself. Training: Special / Lifetime. A Force-user reaches these only after a lifetime inside the common disciplines; the cost reflects the consequence. Scaling here amplifies effects that are already enormous.

Force Confluence

You braid your own Force-sense together with that of every willing Force-sensitive within reach into a single shared…
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn) to open; willing participants onlyRange10 hexes (scalable), no line-of-sight — Force-sense sufficesDefenceNone — the unwilling are simply not joinedFP10, then 2 per minuteReactionNoPrereqTier IV Common Force, IQ 13+

Effect. You braid your own Force-sense together with that of every willing Force-sensitive within reach into a single shared awareness. While the confluence holds, joined minds perceive as one: each sees through the others' senses, no one can be flanked or surprised, and any single member may channel a power they personally know from another member's position, paying its FP themselves.
