A circle of three Knights becomes, for a few minutes, something closer to a single Master split across three bodies.

SCALABILITY. +2 FP doubles the radius; +5 FP lets the confluence reach across a Force Bond regardless of distance, joining a bonded ally on the far side of a battlefield or a ship.

For a breath, the wall between you dissolves. You feel your friend's pulse, the weight of the blade in a hand that is not yours, the fear behind you that you cannot see. You stop being three frightened people in a corridor and become one calm thing wearing three faces.

Anything that strikes one mind hard — a fright check failure, a stunning blow, a successful hostile Will assault — ripples to all joined members for that turn. A member who turns on the circle, or falls to the dark in the moment, can lash the whole confluence; opening this working to someone you do not trust is how Legends die in pairs.

Sphere of Suspension

You seize a volume of space — a sphere up to two hexes across — and hold everything inside it suspended in the current.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn) to raise, then total concentrationRangecentred within 12 hexes; line-of-sight to place, not to holdDefenceBeings inside resist with ST/Will at −2 (no single direction to push)FP12, then 3 per turnReactionNoPrereqTier IV Common Force; Force Grip or Force Wave

Effect. You seize a volume of space — a sphere up to two hexes across — and hold everything inside it suspended in the current. Loose objects hang motionless. Beings within are held as if gripped from every direction at once (treat as a Force Grip they must beat your skill to move against, at −2 because there is no single direction to push). Most strikingly, things in flight stop: a thrown grenade, a blaster bolt, a falling body all arrest mid-arc and simply wait. Bolts already loosed hang glowing until you release the sphere or it ends, whereupon they resume their original path unless redirected.

SCALABILITY. +4 FP widens the sphere by one hex of radius; +6 FP lets you, on release, fling everything suspended in a direction of your choice at the speed it entered (a held volley turned back on its owners).

You spread your fingers and the world inside the circle forgets how to fall. The grenade hangs at eye height, fuse hissing into a silence that does nothing. A man caught mid-leap drifts, kicking at air that has become as patient as stone.

Holding a sphere is total concentration — you cannot move from your hex or take other actions while it stands, and any hit that breaks your concentration drops everything at once, all at the velocity it held. Anything too massive to grip (a vehicle, a collapsing wall) is not suspended, merely slowed.

The Far Seeing

The oldest and most dangerous of the shared gifts: you cast your perception loose from your body to behold a person you…
AffinityWillActionRitual (minutes of stillness; vulnerable throughout)RangeUnlimited — a known person, place, or near-future moment; never needs line-of-sightDefenceNone — you witness, you do not act (critical failure shows a convincing falsehood)FP8ReactionNoPrereqTier IV Common Force; any precognitive or sensing power

Effect. The oldest and most dangerous of the shared gifts: you cast your perception loose from your body to behold a person you know, a place you have stood or can name truly, or a moment of the near future that turns on a choice now being made. A clean success shows the scene as it is or will likely be; a critical success may answer a question you did not think to ask. The vision is sight and feeling, not control — you witness, you do not act.

SCALABILITY. +4 FP holds the vision steady enough to follow a moving subject for a full scene; spending a Force Point (Destiny) lets you ask the Force one specific question of the moment and receive a true, if oblique, answer.

Your body stays in the chamber but your seeing goes out past the walls, past the world, until it settles on a face half a galaxy away — and for a moment you are there, unseen, learning what you should perhaps have left unknown.

The future is a river, not a road: visions of what is to come are likely, never certain, and acting to prevent one has more than once been the very thing that caused it. A critical failure shows a convincing falsehood — a true-feeling vision the dark side, or your own fear, has dressed up to lead you. Far Seeing toward a powerful dark adept can draw their notice back along the same thread.

BOOK III

THE CONCORDANCE

of Feathers
Kreia’s Way. The Force read not for victory but for consequence. The Grey path of the philosopher.
TIERS I–V · INITIATE TO ARBITER

The Concordance of Feathers

Kreia’s tutelage · The path of consequence

The Concordance of Feathers — the Grey Path of Consequence

Kreia’s tutelage: clarity, restraint, and ownership of every consequence.

Alignment req.Grey (light-leaning) — gated not by a band but by discipline held still: keep the Concordance Code the whole climb. The dark is barred absolutely — one deliberate reach for it shatters the tradition.
Force skillWill-based; your own Mind Block answers anything that targets the Will.
TiersI Initiate · II Adept · III Sentinel · IV Shadow · V Arbiter.
Capstone & CrossingGated by a self-authored Code and proven consequence-sight — each tier-up is a choice made by consequence alone, refusing both the light and dark framings (full conditions and deeds below).
To buyTier costs 8 / 16 / 30 / 50 / 80; single powers priced individually by tier.

Capstones, Conditions & the Code

This is the strictest ladder of all, and the conditions are specific by design — the Concordance cannot be stumbled into. It is gated by discipline held still: you must keep to the Concordance Code — balance, restraint, the refusal of cruelty — for the entire climb. Break the Code and your progress freezes until you have returned to it.

The Specific Conditions of the Concordance
  1. You must hold a personal Code of Honor of your own authorship — not inherited, not the Order’s, not a master’s. The Concordance is the path of deciding your own consequence; a borrowed code disqualifies you. (A model code is given at 2.5a.)
  2. You must have received teaching, even partial, from a Concordance bearer (Kreia’s line) or reconstructed the philosophy yourself from a primary source and had it recognised. There is no Concordance academy; it passes hand to hand, or not at all.
  3. You must keep the Code. Each tier requires a sustained stretch (a full arc of play) holding to the Concordance Code. Breaking it pauses the climb; it does not reset it.
  4. You must demonstrate consequence-sight — make a hard choice purely on its consequences, refusing both the ”light” and ”dark” framings offered to you, and own the result.
There is no destiny
There is choice
There is no light or dark
Only consequence
The force does not guide
It compels
Power without reflection is entrapment
Through choice unbound
Life endures — A model Concordance Code of Honor · 2.5a · a partial teaching of Kreia’s line, made one’s own
2.5b · The Light-Lean — how a Concordant walks the Living Path

The Concordance is a grey tradition that leans toward the light. Its clarity, restraint, and refusal of cruelty read to the light-side powers as kin — so a Concordant may study and climb the Living Path while keeping their Code, never needing the devotion the Living Path normally demands (their adherence to the Code stands in its place). This is the ruling that lets a Concordant wield healing and light beside their grey workings.

The light cannot outrun the grey, and it has a ceiling. A Concordant’s Living-Path tier climbs one tier behind their Concordance tier AND is hard-capped at Tier III (Master) — no Concordant ever reaches Grandmaster or Legend of the light. The Living Path’s two highest tiers demand a wholehearted devotion to the light that the grey discipline, by its nature, withholds: a Concordant gives the light their clarity and their restraint, but never the surrender of self that Grandmaster and Legend require. (Concordance Adept → Light Initiate; Sentinel → Light Knight; Shadow → Light Master; Arbiter → still Light Master, the ceiling holds.) They complete the Living Path’s own capstones for each light tier they take, minus the band requirement.

The dark is barred — absolutely. A Concordant can never learn or hold a single Dark Side or Hollowing power. They are not on the sheet; the philosophy that grants the light forbids the dark in the same breath. Should a Concordant ever deliberately reach for the dark — the ”guarantee success” temptation, a dark-fuelled boost, any Unchained or Hollowing working — it does not merely taint them: it shatters the Concordance. They break the Code instantly, all Concordance progress freezes, and the tradition is sealed to them until they re-earn its standing from the beginning — a new Code, the long climb back to it, the consequence-sight proven again. The grey path is a discipline of not reaching; reach once, and it is gone.

ReachCrossing capstoneMastery capstones
II · AdeptThe Code Authored — write and declare your own Code of Honor, and keep it through one situation that punished you for it.(1) Read a being’s true intent with the grey sight and act on the reading, not the appearance.
III · SentinelThe Unmoved Choice — stand in a charged moral moment where both light and dark scream at you, and choose by consequence alone, unmoved by either pull.(1) Sever or deny another’s Force working through clarity, not force. (2) Hold to the Code through a full arc that tried to break you toward one side.
IV · ShadowThe Long View Accepted — foresee a grave consequence of your own path and choose to walk it anyway, eyes open, because it is yours to choose.(1) Take The Long View on a campaign-level question and act on it. (2) Break a destiny laid on you or another. (3) Stand serene in a Dark Nexus, unsteered. (Strict: the Concordance demands all three.)
V · ArbiterChoice Unbound — at a true crossroads, make the choice that defines you against every external pull, and in doing so cease to be pulled at all: you are moved only by your own will. (See Becoming, Codex Book III.)The Becoming is the mastery; there is nothing left to prove to anyone but yourself.

The dead-eyed witch who taught these workings cared nothing for Light or Dark. She cared for clarity — for the moment when a choice is made because it is the right choice, not because some external authority has blessed it. Her teaching is uncomfortable. It demands the practitioner own every consequence. It is the path of the philosopher, not the warrior; the question mark, not the answer.

Most who attempt it abandon it within a year. Those who endure find their Force skill has become something neither the temple nor the academy recognises.

Force skill is Will-based. Mind Block protects in return — anything targeting the Will faces that wall first. Key combination: Force Denial first (reduces their Force resistance), then a reading or disruption power through the gap. The signature strategy against high-Will opponents.

I · INITIATE

Initiate

Your foundational abilities — perception, interruption, and subtle environmental control. Against opponents at your tier (800pt, Will 35+), plan to spend 9−16 FP per meaningful use once scaled. These are most effective as setup tools for your Tier 2−3 powers.

Force Sense (Grey)

Sense living beings, Force use, and danger within 15 hexes.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn), then maintainedRange15-hex radius, no line-of-sightDefenceNone — passive (Force Stealth can hide from it via Quick Contest)FP2, then 1 to maintainReactionNo

Effect. Sense living beings, Force use, and danger within 15 hexes. You also detect the alignment of active Force effects as a distinct quality — Light, Dark, or neither. No other Force Sense reads alignment directly.

Your eyes close. Awareness spreads outward in slow rings from where you stand. Each alignment texture registers differently — you have learned to read the difference between what the light demands and what the dark takes.

Fails against Force Stealth (Quick Contest). Gives direction and rough intensity — not identity or specific intent.

Force Equilibrium

End one active Tier 1 Force effect on any target.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn) — costs your actionRangea Tier-1 effect on any target you can perceiveDefenceContest your Force skill vs the original caster’s Force skill (or ST/HT if higher)FP4 (−2 per +4 FP, max −6)ReactionNo

Effect. End one active Tier 1 Force effect on any target. You do not need to know what it is — only that something is running. Costs your full action. Scalable: each additional 4 FP applies −2 to the caster's resistance roll (8 FP = −2, 12 FP = −4, max −6 at 16 FP).

Two fingers extend. You exhale. Whatever was running stops — a note cut off mid-phrase. More FP behind it, the quieter their resistance becomes.

Tier 1 effects only. Critical failure: the effect intensifies for 1 turn. Cannot end the same caster's effect more than once per 10 minutes — they adapt. Maximum contest penalty: −6. / Tier 1 only. Against peer opponents (Force skill ~36), plan to spend 12−16 FP to have a real contest chance.

Fracture

Break the target's concentration for 1 turn.
AffinityWillActionAttackRange6 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceResisted by Will or Mind Block; never dodgedFP3 (−2 per +3 FP to −6; +4 FP = 2 turns & auto-drop)ReactionNo

Effect. Break the target's concentration for 1 turn. Any Concentrate action they were performing fails without FP refund. Their maintained powers make an immediate Will roll or drop. Scalable: +3 FP per −2 to their resistance (6 FP = Will−2, 9 FP = Will−4, max Will−6 at 12 FP). Alternatively, +4 FP beyond base = 2-turn disruption and maintained powers drop automatically without a roll. Both scale options stack: 16 FP total = 2 turns, auto-drop, at Will−6.

Your gaze sharpens to a needle point. Something crosses between you and lands behind their eyes — a crack in whatever quiet they were holding. More FP, deeper the crack.

Against Will 35+, the −6 scaling is your minimum for meaningful effect — budget 9 FP at least. At 16 FP, this is your hardest disruption: 2-turn auto-drop at Will−6. Does not damage, does not last beyond the disruption window.

Weight of Action

Immediately after any significant action resolves within 10 hexes — an attack landing, a Force power firing, a choice…
AffinityWillActionReaction — right after an action resolvesRangewithin 8 hexesDefenceNone — passive read (margin sets clarity; critical failure inverts it)FP2ReactionYes — uses your Reaction

Effect. Immediately after any significant action resolves within 10 hexes — an attack landing, a Force power firing, a choice under pressure — read its ripple in the Force. Not the future. The consequence already in motion. One impression: what has this just set into play that isn't visible yet?

Your eyes go briefly distant. The action hangs for a moment before dispersing — and in that moment, you read where its weight is falling.

One impression per action. The same action's ripple cannot be read twice. Usable on different actions across the same fight. Critical failure: the impression is inverted. / Consequences are not punishments. They are simply what happens next.

Force Denial

One Force-sensitive target within 2 hexes suffers −2 to their Force skill when resisting any incoming effect this turn.
AffinityWillActionAttackRangeone Force-sensitive within 2 hexesDefenceNone — it saps their Force-skill resistance (−2, scaling to −6); no effect if they resist with raw Will or HTFP3 (−2; +3 FP per −2 more, max −6)ReactionNo

Effect. One Force-sensitive target within 2 hexes suffers −2 to their Force skill when resisting any incoming effect this turn. Scalable: +3 FP per −2 further (6 FP = −4, 9 FP = −6, maximum −6). Applies only when they use Force skill as their resistance — if they resist with raw Will or HT instead, this has no effect.

You exhale sharply and press your awareness outward. The Force in the immediate radius becomes briefly resistive — dragging, like moving through thick water. They can still act. They just find it slightly harder to hold.

This is primarily a setup power. Apply Force Denial (−6) first, then follow with Surface Read, Inversion, or Mindbreak Veil through the gap. At −6, their Force resistance of 36 becomes 30 — now within your reach. Maximum scaling: −6.

II · ADEPT

Adept

Your active toolkit — masking your presence, reading surfaces, inverting attacks, cancelling equal-tier effects. Effective spend at your level: 5−18 FP per use depending on opposition. Resonance Strike is your best single-target disruption against Will-primary opponents — it contests HT 17 rather than Force skill, making it dramatically easier to land.

Force Veil

Your Force alignment becomes unreadable.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn), then maintainedRangeSelf (or touched)Defence— a searcher pierces it with a Quick Contest of Force Sense; violence breaks it for that turnFP2, then 2 per turnReactionNo

Effect. Your Force alignment becomes unreadable. Force Sense perceives you as an undifferentiated presence — neither Light nor Dark, simply there. You appear as nothing.

Your presence becomes deliberately featureless — still water with no current. Those probing find nothing to grip.

Violence breaks the Veil for that turn — any attack made or received allows a free Force Sense roll to pierce it. Does not hide you physically. 2 FP/turn is 36 FP for 3 minutes — manage duration deliberately.

Force Rebuke

When a Tier 2 Force power targets you or an ally within 4 hexes, cancel it.
AffinityWillActionReaction — when a Tier-2 power targets you or an ally within 4 hexesRange4 hexesDefenceContest your Force skill vs the attacker’s (Tier 2 only)FP5ReactionYes — uses your Reaction

Effect. When a Tier 2 Force power targets you or an ally within 4 hexes, cancel it. The power is unmade — no damage to the attacker, no residue. Tier 1 attacks are handled by Force Equilibrium. Tier 3+ require Force Mirror.

A percussive pulse of colourless energy meets the incoming power mid-air. It dies on contact. Like a candle being cupped.

Tier 2 only. One Reaction per turn. Against a peer attacker (Force skill ~36), this is a roughly even contest — the 5 FP cost is fair for a fair chance. Failure: FP spent and the attack lands.

Surface Read

Read the surface of a target's current conscious thoughts.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn); 5 min out of combatRange8 hexes, line-of-sight (eye contact)DefenceResisted by Will+2 or Mind Block+2; never dodgedFP5 (−2 per +3 FP to −6)ReactionNo

Effect. Read the surface of a target's current conscious thoughts. Base (5 FP): one yes/no question you hold in mind before concentrating — answered truthfully. Scaled (9 FP, +4 FP): the GM describes what they are most focused on right now in their own terms. Further scaling: +3 FP per −2 to their resistance (12 FP = −2, 15 FP = −4, max −6 at 18 FP). Out of combat (5 minutes) gives richer, more textured responses.

You hold their gaze a moment longer than feels natural. Something passes — invisible, brief, one-way. They may feel faintly exposed without knowing why.

Against Will 36 the base contest is Will+2 = 38. Without resistance scaling you are unlikely to succeed. Use Force Denial (−6) first to bring effective resistance to 32, then Surface Read. Cannot be used more than once per hour on the same target.

Inversion

When a Force power targets you, turn it back on the caster for one turn.
AffinityWillActionReaction — when a Force power targets youRange6 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceContest your Force skill vs the attacker’s (−1 per tier above 1; buy off +3 FP/tier); can’t invert area powersFP7ReactionYes — uses your Reaction

Effect. When a Force power targets you, turn it back on the caster for one turn. They experience what they tried to inflict — a Slow affects them, a Choke, a Fear. After one turn it dissipates entirely. Scalable: spend +3 FP to remove 1 tier of contest penalty (10 FP total = no penalty vs Tier 3; 13 FP = no penalty vs Tier 4).

You meet the incoming power with open hands — not blocking but redirecting. The energy folds back toward the mirror's other side. For one turn, they are inside their own attack.

Against a Tier 3 attacker at Force skill 36: base Inversion is at −2, contesting their 34. Spend 10 FP total (7 + 3) for a clean 36 vs 36 contest. Cannot invert area powers. One-turn effect only.

III · SENTINEL

Sentinel

Your most tactically significant powers. Severance Mark at 7 FP is your best value investment — the +5 counter bonus and free second Reaction reshape fights regardless of the opponent's CP level. Equilibrium Extended scales up to 20 FP to reliably end effects held by Force skill 36 characters.

Still Point

For 2 turns, no Force power can affect you at all — Light, Dark, or Grey.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn) — declared at turn start, before anyone actsRangeSelf (a 2-hex radius of Force-silence)Defence— (no Force power can touch you for 2 turns; you’re Force-blind too; physical attacks still land)FP10ReactionNo

Effect. For 2 turns, no Force power can affect you at all — Light, Dark, or Grey. Physical attacks still land. You also cannot use Force powers during this time. A complete Force-silence around you alone.

You plant your feet and exhale completely. Force powers arrive and find nothing — not resistance, but a stillness so total there is nothing to push against.

Must be declared at the start of your turn before anyone else acts. You cannot trigger this reactively. You are Force-blind for the duration. At 10 FP this is once per fight for you — use it when you know a major Force effect is about to land.

Force Equilibrium (Extended)

End one active Tier 2 or Tier 3 Force effect.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn) — costs your actionRangea Tier-2 or -3 effect on any target you can perceiveDefenceContest your Force skill vs the caster’s at −2 (or ST/HT if higher)FP8 (−2 per +4 FP, max −8 at 20 FP)ReactionNo

Effect. End one active Tier 2 or Tier 3 Force effect. Costs your full action. Scalable: +4 FP per −2 further on the caster's resistance (12 FP = −4 total, 16 FP = −6, max −8 at 20 FP). At 20 FP this is your strongest counter to held effects.

The same gesture as Equilibrium — but the effort shows. Your arm shakes slightly against something larger before it closes. More FP, less resistance.

Tier 2−3 only. At 20 FP you are applying −8 to Force skill 36 — they roll against 28, which you can reliably beat. Critical failure: you take 1d mental fatigue and the effect persists. Cannot end Tier 4+ powers.

Force Mirror

Reflect a Tier 3 Force power back at its caster using your Force skill for the reflected effect.
AffinityWillActionReaction — when a Tier-3 power targets youRange8 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceContest your Force skill vs the attacker’s (Tier 3 only; the caster’s own resistances apply to the reflection)FP8ReactionYes — uses your Reaction

Effect. Reflect a Tier 3 Force power back at its caster using your Force skill for the reflected effect. Tier 1−2 are handled by Equilibrium and Rebuke. Tier 4+ cannot be mirrored — though Tier 3−4 can be cancelled (not reflected) by Greater Rebuke, at its Fatigue price.

Your hand rises between you and the incoming power. It reverses at your palm — same shape, same speed — and returns to its origin.

Tier 3 only. The caster's own resistances apply to the reflected version. Against a peer caster this is a 36 vs 36 contest — 8 FP for an even chance at a full reflection. One Reaction per turn. Failure: FP spent and the attack hits.

Severance Mark

Brand one specific Force power the target has used as intimately known to you.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn), lasts 10 minRangeTouch, or a target within 4 hexes you’ve sensed using the powerDefenceNone — a brand/read (margin: success marks one power, crit two)FP7ReactionNo

Effect. Brand one specific Force power the target has used as intimately known to you. For 10 minutes, three things apply: (1) Whenever the marked power activates within 20 hexes, you know instantly — even without LoS. (2) If it requires Concentrate (1 turn), you feel the buildup a full turn before it fires — giving you a proactive turn rather than a reactive one. (3) Any counter attempt you make against that specific power (Equilibrium, Rebuke, Greater Rebuke, Mirror, Anchor) gains +5. If you have already used your Reaction when the marked power fires, you may use a second Reaction specifically to counter it.

You close your eyes after witnessing it. The power's signature stays — a thread kept taut between you and that ability. When the thread pulls again, you are already turning. Not reacting. Anticipating.

One specific power only — not the target generally. Different powers give no warning. Critical failure: you mark the wrong power (GM substitutes). The advance warning on Concentrate powers is the real value here at any opponent CP level — it converts reaction into anticipation.

Unmaking

Strip the Force-derived properties from an enhanced object — a Force-imbued weapon, a lightsaber crystal's alignment,…
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn), lasts 10 minRange4 hexes, line-of-sight (the object)DefenceContest your Force skill vs the creator’s Force skill or IQ (10 if unknown); unopposed on mundane itemsFP7ReactionNo

Effect. Strip the Force-derived properties from an enhanced object — a Force-imbued weapon, a lightsaber crystal's alignment, Sith alchemy-treated armour. The object's physical properties remain intact. The enhancement returns after 10 minutes.

Two fingers extend toward the object. Something in its structure untunes — the particular alignment that gave it its edge releases with a faint harmonic resonance, then silence.

Affects Force-enhanced properties only. You must have seen or sensed the enhancement first. The 10-minute window is significant — this can reshape a prolonged fight or negotiation.

Force Anchor

Sever an active mental domination, compulsion, or possession acting on a target.
AffinityWillActionReactionRangea target within a 10-hex tether (Touch needs no contest)DefenceContest your Force skill vs the dominating caster’s Will (or Force skill if higher)FP8ReactionYes — uses your Reaction

Effect. Sever an active mental domination, compulsion, or possession acting on a target. Breaks Force Domination, Force Compel, or equivalent effects. The target is returned to full autonomy instantly.

You reach toward the dominated target through the Force. Something wraps around what is holding their mind and pulls it loose. They blink as if surfacing from deep water.

Active ongoing effects only — cannot undo something already fully resolved. Costs 8 FP regardless of the dominating power's tier. Failure: FP spent, domination persists. Tie: the domination weakens (−2 to all actions for its caster next turn).

IV · SHADOW

Shadow

Your rarest active powers — Force Void, deep disruption, Force Concordance, and the tier’s reactive answer, Greater Rebuke. Force Void at 9 FP creates a genuine sanctuary for allies that forces even elite Force-users to win two contests. Greater Rebuke is the only Reaction in the galaxy that stops a Tier 4 working cold — a straight contest, but the Fatigue bill (4 + half the working’s cost, win or lose) means you stop the one that matters, not all of them. Mindbreak Veil and Resonance Strike work best after Force Denial. At 73 FP, each power here is 12−20% of your pool — use deliberately.

Force Void

Create a dead zone where the Force is actively suppressed.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn), then maintainedRangea 4-hex-radius dead zone (you stay outside); no line-of-sightDefenceTwo-stage — a power must beat the Void (your Force skill), then the target’s own resistance; Concentrate powers are impossible insideFP9, then 3 per turn (+3 FP per +1 hex radius)ReactionNo

Effect. Create a dead zone where the Force is actively suppressed. Three rules inside the Void: (1) Concentrate powers are completely impossible — no roll, no attempt. (2) Instantaneous powers may be attempted but require a Force skill contest vs the Void (your Force skill) before firing — fail means FP spent and nothing happens. (3) Any power projected from outside onto someone inside must first contest the Void, then separately contest the target's resistance. Two contests to reach anyone inside. Scalable: +3 FP at casting = +1 hex radius (12 FP = 5-hex, 15 FP = 6-hex). Maintenance stays 3 FP/turn.

The air over the zone flattens and dims. Force-sensitives approaching the edge feel a cold resistance — an absence so complete that reaching for the Force inside is like reaching into still water and finding no reflection.

You can use your own abilities in and out of the area. Collapses instantly if your concentration breaks. The two-contest requirement makes anyone inside a sanctuary from Force attacks, but a peer Force-user (Force skill ~36) can still pierce instantaneous powers through a clean contest against your Force skill.

Greater Rebuke

Cancel a Tier 3 or 4 power outright — at a price scaled to what you turn aside.
AffinityWillActionReaction — when a Tier-3 or Tier-4 power targets you or an ally within 4 hexesRange4 hexesDefenceContest your Force skill vs the caster's — a straight contest, no tier penaltyFP4 + half the incoming working's FP cost (round up) — paid win or loseReactionThis is one

Effect. When a Tier 3 or Tier 4 power fires at you or an ally within 4 hexes, you may slap it out of the air: contest your Force skill vs the caster's — a clean contest, with no penalty for the working's weight. The weight is paid in Fatigue instead: 4 FP plus half the incoming working's FP cost (round up), paid whether you win or lose — you carry what you turn aside. Win: the power is cancelled outright, the caster's FP wasted. Lose: it lands as cast, and you are out the Fatigue regardless. Limits: cannot touch Tier 5 workings or anything RITUAL-tagged mid-execution — only Force Equilibrium (Grand) reaches those. A power with no listed FP cost (constant effects, item-bound or holocron-drawn workings) is surcharged as if it cost 8. Severance Mark's +5 and free second Reaction apply to Greater Rebuke as to any counter.

Both palms rise this time. The incoming working breaks across them like a wave against a cliff — and the cliff pays for every ton of water it stops. What flows back into the Force is not violence. It is the bill.

Force Scar (Read)

Read a Force scar — a dead zone, a severed connection, the aftermath of Force mass-destruction.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (5 min, uninterrupted contact)RangeTouch, or within 3 hexes of the scarDefenceNone — a read (margin: one answer; crit a second; 13 FP reads two)FP8ReactionNo

Effect. Read a Force scar — a dead zone, a severed connection, the aftermath of Force mass-destruction. Choose one aspect before rolling: what happened, who caused it, or what it cost. Scaled (13 FP, +5 FP): read two aspects in the same casting. 5 minutes of uninterrupted contact required.

You reach toward the emptiness. Where others feel only void, you find a shape. The wound has a history. You read a page of it.

5 minutes — any interruption wastes all FP. One answer per base casting; two at 13 FP. Critical failure: 1d mental fatigue from the echo. Cannot read naturally Force-dead regions. / Not every wound can be healed. But every wound can be understood.

Resonance Strike

Charge the target's Force signature against itself.
AffinityWillActionAttack (melee)RangeMelee / blade reachDefenceContest your Force skill vs the target’s HT (or Force skill if higher)FP10 (15 FP triggers both effects)ReactionNo

Effect. Charge the target's Force signature against itself. Their next Force power either costs double FP or misfires (power fails, target takes 1d mental fatigue) — you choose which when you see them activate. Scaled (15 FP, +5 FP): both effects trigger simultaneously — double cost AND HT roll or misfire. Expires after 10 minutes if unused.

A subtle, precise gesture — less an attack, more pressing a finger against a tuning fork. Their Force signature brightens slightly wrong, charged against itself.

This contests HT, not Force skill. Your Will 36 opponents have HT 17 — this is dramatically easier to land than any of your other powers against Will-primary Force-users. At 10 FP it is your most FP-efficient disruption against them. One Resonance Strike per target at a time.

Mindbreak Veil

Force the target to doubt their most recent clear memory for 1 hour.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn), lasts 1 hourRange8 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceResisted by Will or Mind Block; never dodgedFP10ReactionNo

Effect. Force the target to doubt their most recent clear memory for 1 hour. They know what they remember. They cannot be certain it is real — every decision that relies on it requires a Will roll first.

You meet their gaze and breathe once, deliberately. Something in their certainty loosens — not erased, not replaced, just slightly unmoored from conviction.

Will 36 targets resist at +2 — effective resistance 38. Use Force Denial (−6) first to bring that to 32 before attempting. Targets the most recent clear memory only — you and the GM agree which based on what they last perceived clearly.

Force Concordance

For 4 turns, all your Force powers cost half FP (rounded up, minimum 2).
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (2 turns, unbroken & calm)RangeSelfDefence— (for 4 turns your powers cost half; your alignment can’t be shifted)FP15ReactionNo

Effect. For 4 turns, all your Force powers cost half FP (rounded up, minimum 2). Your alignment cannot be shifted by any means for the duration.

Force energy — light and dark, neither dominant — draws toward you over two full turns. When you exhale, it integrates. For 4 turns, you are entirely and impossibly balanced.

15 FP is ~20% of your pool. The 2-turn wind-up leaves you fully exposed — you need cover from an ally or a safe moment. Any interruption aborts and wastes all FP. Cannot activate in strong emotion. After it ends, −2 to all actions for 1 turn. Minimum 2 FP per power during Concordance. / Not a weapon. A state of being.

V · ARBITER

Arbiter

These are the powers at the edge of what you are. Grand Equilibrium can end anything, but at 20−32 FP it is most of your pool in one action. Force Schism cuts a specific power out of an opponent's reach entirely — at 26−30 FP against elite opponents, it is your most resource-intensive tool. The Long View is once per month. Becoming is not a power to plan for.

Force Equilibrium (Grand)

End any Force effect of any tier, including active Tier 4 powers and rituals mid-execution.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn) — costs your actionRangeany Force effect on a target you can perceive (no tier ceiling)DefenceContest your Force skill vs the caster’s (−3 vs Tier 4+; scales to −9 at 32 FP)FP20ReactionNo

Effect. End any Force effect of any tier, including active Tier 4 powers and rituals mid-execution. No ceiling. Scalable: +4 FP per −2 further on the caster's resistance, on top of the base −3 for Tier 4+ (24 FP = −5, 28 FP = −7, max −9 at 32 FP). Against the highest-tier opponents (Force skill 60+, e.g. a Grandmaster Sith Lord of the era), spending 32 FP drops their effective resistance to 51 − now genuinely contested.

One hand rises. The effect closes. The space it occupied is clean. More FP behind it, and even the largest power goes quiet.

20 FP base plus your full action. Critical failure: the effect rebounds, you take 2d mental fatigue. Cannot undo already-discharged powers. Maximum penalty: −9 at 32 FP. At your current pool (73 FP), 32 FP is nearly half — this is your most expensive single action. / Balance, fully realised.

Deep Read

Reach past the surface into genuine memory or deep intent.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (2 turns); 10 min out of combat (willing or restrained)RangeTouch, or 4 hexes, sustained eye contactDefenceResisted by Will or Mind Block; never dodgedFP18ReactionNo

Effect. Reach past the surface into genuine memory or deep intent. Combat (2 turns): one concrete true fact — a name, a location, a yes/no of real weight. Out of combat (10 minutes, willing or restrained): one full coherent memory or true motive — not just the fact but the shape of why.

You maintain eye contact throughout. They feel the pressure — not painful, but exposing. Something not meant to be found is found.

Combat: they break it by looking away or acting. Any interruption wastes all FP. Critical failure: you are Shaken for 1d turns and cannot immediately act on what you found. Out of combat requires genuine cooperation or full physical restraint — not usable on a free hostile target. / One fact. Not a biography.

Force Schism

Cut one specific Force power the target has used this encounter out of their reach entirely.
AffinityWillActionAttackRange6 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceResisted by Will (or Force skill if higher); you must have seen them use the powerFP18 (−2 per +4 FP, max −6)ReactionNo

Effect. Cut one specific Force power the target has used this encounter out of their reach entirely. For 10 minutes in combat or 1 hour outside it, they cannot access it — not weakened, not expensive, just absent. Scalable: +4 FP per −2 to their resistance (22 FP = −2, 26 FP = −4, max −6 at 30 FP). You must have witnessed them use the power.

One deliberate, quiet gesture — almost dismissive. Something in their Force vocabulary goes silent. Not taken. Not destroyed. Simply no longer answering when they reach for it.

Against Force skill 36+, budget 26−30 FP for a reliable contest. The target knows something is missing but may not immediately know which power. Critical failure: you lose access to one of your own powers (GM chooses) for 1d turns as backlash. / The Force is not a crutch — and now, for this fight, neither is that ability.

The Long View

Ask the Force one specific question about the most likely consequence of a current path — not what will happen, but…
AffinityWillActionRitual (1 hr meditation)RangeSelfDefenceNone — a reading (margin: one true probability; crit a second); 30-day cooldownFP18ReactionNo

Effect. Ask the Force one specific question about the most likely consequence of a current path — not what will happen, but what the Force reads as most probable if nothing changes. One answer per casting.

You dissolve into the Force for an hour. You float in possibility, reading currents rather than fixed points. When you return, you carry one true thing about what is likely coming.

One answer per casting. Critical success gives a second unprompted. Critical failure: the answer comes from a possible future that has since closed — plausible, detailed, and wrong. 30-day cooldown. / The Force does not tell you what to do. It shows you what is.

Becoming

The last freedom of the path of consequence.
AffinityWillActionRitual — at the moment of natural death, chosen freelyRangeSelfDefence— (requires genuine acceptance that every choice was yours; desperation fragments the self instead)FPall remainingReactionNo

Effect. The last freedom of the path of consequence. At the moment of your natural death — and only if you have truly owned every choice that brought you here — you neither linger as ghost or echo nor dissolve into the Force’s balance. You accept the death as yours, accept that the choices were yours alone, release the life that was, and are returned to the wheel: reborn — another time, another place, perhaps long before this one — as a wholly new being, with no memory of who you were. At most a faint echo crosses over: an unaccountable pull toward a certain kind of choice, a déjà vu in a place you have never stood, a skill the new hands seem to already know. Not transcendence, not victory — a continuation bought by surrendering the self that was for the self that will be.

No visible dissolution. Your body empties. Those present share a brief, wordless impression — not of a presence joining the Force, but of a thread quietly let go here and drawn taut somewhere else, somewhen else, in hands not yet born.

Cannot be used to escape consequences or cheat a killing blow — it asks the opposite, that you own them completely. It requires genuine, unforced acceptance at the actual moment of natural death; the choice is made before the last breath, not because of it. Attempted in desperation, the self fragments instead of crossing over — a Force disturbance for 1d years, and no rebirth. The reborn life is the GM’s to shape; the player does not carry the old sheet across. (Choice Unbound — the final ownership of choice.)

BOOK IV

THE HOLLOWING

The Path of Hunger
The dark mirror of the Concordance. Where Kreia’s way lets the Force pass through, this way kills it, tears it loose, and devours it. The road that made the Lord of Hunger.
TIERS I–V · THE FIRST HUNGER TO THE DEVOURING ABYSS

The Hollowing

The devouring path · the dark mirror of the Concordance

The Hollowing — the Devouring Path

The dark mirror of the Concordance: it does not let the Force pass through — it stops, seizes, and swallows it.

Alignment req.Dark (devouring) — serves no master, not even the Sith’s dark side; it serves the hunger. Walked to its end it makes not a Dark Lord but a wound in the Force that walks.
Force skillWill-based — but Mind Block offers no shelter here; there is no stillness to retreat into, only appetite. Workings grow stronger the more death surrounds you.
TiersI The First Hunger · II The Widening Mouth · III The Devouring Will · IV The Lord of Hunger · V The Devouring Abyss.
Capstone & CrossingEach crossing is an act of feeding — owned without being consumed by it; the fifth tier is not mastery but surrender (full deeds below).
To buyTier costs 8 / 16 / 30 / 50 / 80; single powers priced individually by tier.

The price. The Hollowing runs hotter than any clean tradition and costs more than the dark side does — its feeding is faintly addictive, each use harder to refuse than the last, and its end-states slowly eat the wielder. Every feeding accrues Hunger — see below.

Capstones, Conditions & the Hunger

The Hollowing runs five tiers, and its capstones are the most specific and the most ruinous, because each is an act of feeding that deepens the hunger itself. You do not master the Hollowing; you are progressively consumed by it, trading more of your self for more of its appetite. The fifth tier — the Devouring Abyss — is not a higher mastery but a final surrender: the hunger stops being something you have and becomes the only thing you are, culminating in the Last Silence, an act of consumption turned against the living Force itself. It requires falling (Touched or worse) and accrues the hunger track.

The Specific Conditions of the Hollowing
  1. You must have fed. Each tier’s Crossing is a specific act of consumption, escalating in scale — and once fed at a tier’s scale, the hunger for it never fully leaves (a permanent +1 to that tier’s hunger baseline).
  2. You must be marked by emptiness. The Hollowing reads as a cold absence, not a dark flare; each tier deepens it. By the Devouring Will, sensitives feel you as a wrong, hungry silence.
  3. There is no teacher. The path is self-derived from the act of feeding; it cannot be taught, only fallen into. The only ”text” is the hunger’s own logic.
2.6b · The Dark-Lean — the antithesis of the Concordance

The Hollowing is the Concordance’s mirror: a grey tradition that leans toward the dark. Where the Concordant’s clarity opens the light, the Hollower’s appetite opens the dark — and seals the light away. A Hollower may study and climb the Unchained Path on the strength of their hunger track, which stands in place of the devotion the dark normally demands (exactly as the Concordance’s Code stands in place of the light’s).

The dark cannot outrun the hunger, and it has a ceiling. A Hollower’s Unchained tier climbs one tier behind their Hollowing tier AND is hard-capped at Tier III (Master) — no Hollower ever becomes a Dark Grandmaster or a Sith Lord of the Unchained Path. The dark’s highest tiers demand the building of a self into a throne (Megalomania, dominion, an apprentice to rule); the hunger is the opposite impulse — it un-makes the self rather than enthroning it, and so the deepest Sith arts curdle in its grip. (Widening Mouth → Dark Initiate; Devouring Will → Dark Knight; Lord of Hunger → Dark Master, the ceiling holds; and the Devouring Abyss reaches no higher — the hunger that un-makes the self can never build it into a throne.) They complete the Unchained capstones — including its mandatory Disadvantages — for each dark tier taken.

The light is barred — absolutely. A Hollower can never learn or hold a single Light Side power. The light is a giving-away of self; the hunger is its opposite, and cannot perform it — Force Heal, Restoration, Cleanse and their kin simply will not answer a creature of appetite. There is no breach clause here, because there is no temptation to breach: a Hollower reaching for the light would feel only emptiness where the power should be. The hunger does not fall to the light; it starves away from it.

ReachCrossing capstone (the Feeding)Mastery capstones
II · The Widening MouthThe First Taste Kept — drain a living being to death and like it; refuse to stop when you could have.(1) Feed mid-combat to turn a losing fight.
III · The Devouring WillThe Shared Famine — feed on many at once (a Hollow Radius over a crowd, a squad, a room) and walk away stronger from their lessening.(1) Hold open a wound that feeds you across a whole fight. (2) Survive the Sated Hour’s crash and feed again rather than recover.
IV · The Lord of HungerThe Feast of Worlds Begun — consume on a scale that leaves a place dead to the Force; take the Eclipse of the Self and come back still able to speak your name.The Lord of Hunger’s powers are the reward of the Crossing; to feed at this scale and remain a person at all is the mastery — and most do not.
V · the Devouring AbyssThe Last Silence Spoken — open a Silence that eats the Force itself and let it take living connections within reach, completing the un-making of who you were. This is the dark Crossing in its purest form: not an act of power, but the death of every spark near you, yourself included if you hold it too long.The Abyss is not trained; it is surrendered to. The deed — feeding on the Force as such — is the mastery, and there is nothing of the self left over to call a reward.

The Concordance of Feathers and the Hollowing are sisters born of the same question — what is the Force, beneath light and dark? — and they answer it in opposite directions. Where Kreia’s way teaches the practitioner to let the Force pass through them, muted and observed and unowned, the Hollowing teaches them to stop it, seize it, and swallow it. It is not the Sith path of passion and dominance; it is colder than that, and hungrier. The Sith use the dark side. The Hollowing eats the Force itself.

It is a neutral tradition only in the sense that it serves no master — not the light, not even the dark side as the Sith understand it. It serves the hunger. Walked to its end, it does not produce a Dark Lord. It produces a wound in the Force that walks: a Nihilus, a thing that consumes worlds because it can no longer do anything else.

Force skill is Will-based. Unlike the Concordance, Mind Block offers the practitioner no shelter here — this path has no stillness to retreat into, only appetite. Its workings grow stronger the more death surrounds the user, which makes a Hollowing adept most dangerous exactly where they should be weakest: a losing battle, a massacre, a dying world.

Power & the Hunger. The Hollowing runs hotter than any clean tradition, and it costs more than even the Sith’s dark side. Its price is the Hunger — a mark accrued with every feeding, tracking how deep the appetite has rooted in you — and it compounds: the feeding workings are faintly addictive (each use makes the next harder to refuse), and the path’s end-states — the Sated Hour’s crash, Eclipse of the Self, the Wound That Walks — describe a wielder being slowly eaten by their own appetite. The power is real and it is great. So is the price. (The Hunger track and its thresholds are detailed in a later section.)

I · THE FIRST HUNGER

The First Hunger

Where the Concordance teaches stillness, the Hollowing teaches appetite. The first lessons are small thefts — a sip of another's strength, a taste of the life in a room. Force skill is Will-based, but unlike Kreia's way, Mind Block does not shelter the practitioner here: the Hollowing offers no peace, only feeding.

Taste of Life

Draw a thread of living energy from a target into yourself.
AffinityWillActionAttackRange2 hexesDefenceResisted by HT; never dodgedFP2 (+2 FP per +1d)ReactionNo

Effect. Draw a thread of living energy from a target into yourself. Drain 1d FP (or 1d HP if the target has no Force); you absorb half as FP for yourself. Unlike a Sith Drain, this leaves a faint COLDNESS in the target — a momentary sense of having been touched by something empty. Scalable: +2 FP per +1d.

BODY. An indrawn breath through a slightly-parted mouth, eyes half-closed — the hunger reaches out along the line of sight and the breath pulls the energy IN. No hands; this is consumption, not gesture.

You inhale, and something more than air comes with it. The target shivers as if a door opened on a cold place. You feel, for a moment, slightly more real.

Faintly addictive — repeated use in a day imposes a cumulative −1 Will to resist using it again. Sensed by other Force-users as a wrongness, not a dark-side flare.

Null Step

Briefly hollow your own presence — you become a void in the Force rather than a signature within it.
AffinityWillActionQuick (free)RangeSelfDefence— (you become a void: Force-sensing slides off, Force-guided attacks vs you at −3; cuts both ways)FP3ReactionNo

Effect. Briefly hollow your own presence — you become a void in the Force rather than a signature within it. Force Sense and precognition slide off you; danger-sense and Force-guided attacks against you suffer −3. Unlike Force Stealth (which hides the candle), this snuffs it: you read as nothing, which is itself unnerving to those who notice the hole where a person should be.

BODY. A turning-inward — the practitioner stops radiating. No gesture; the whole self goes quiet and cold.

You let yourself go empty. Not hidden — absent. The Force flows around the place where you stand the way water rounds a stone, finding nothing to grip.

While Null-Stepped you cannot benefit from your own precognition or Force-defenses either — the void cuts both ways. Animals and children often sense the wrongness and react with unease.

Cold Reading

Read a target not by sensing their living Force, but by tasting the EMPTINESS where you have already begun to thin it.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)RangeTouch or 2 hexesDefenceResisted by Will; automatic & unresisted on someone you’ve fed onFP1ReactionNo

Effect. Read a target not by sensing their living Force, but by tasting the EMPTINESS where you have already begun to thin it. You learn their current FP/HP state, their strongest fear, and whether they are Force-sensitive. Against a target you have fed on before (Taste of Life, Sever Feeding) this is automatic and unresisted — your hunger remembers the shape of what it ate.

BODY. A half-second of eye contact and a slow exhale — you are not reaching toward them so much as noticing the cold spot you left. No gesture.

You look at them and feel, faintly, the place where you took something once. The wound remembers the knife. Through it, you read them like a draft through a cracked door.

Only as good as the emptiness present — a wholly untouched, full-strength target gives only vague impressions. Force-users may feel the cold attention and resist.

Starve the Spark

Smother a target's access to the Force for a few breaths — not by tearing it (that comes later) but by standing between…
AffinityWillActionAttackRange3 hexesDefenceResisted by Will; Force-users onlyFP3ReactionNo

Effect. Smother a target's access to the Force for a few breaths — not by tearing it (that comes later) but by standing between them and it, a cold mouth drinking the trickle before it reaches them. On a win, the target's next 1d Force activations cost double FP and suffer −2 skill. Mundane targets unaffected.

BODY. A slow closing of one hand into a loose, patient fist held toward the target — not crushing, just covering, like a palm over a candle.

You don't take their power. You simply stand in the doorway it comes through, and feed on the draft, and they reach for the Force and find you got there first.

Brief and partial — a determined Force-user pushes through at the doubled cost. Sensed as a smothering cold. Cumulative addiction as Taste of Life.

Ashtongue

Speak with the cold authority of something that has eaten part of the world.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)Range10 hexesDefenceResisted by Will or Mind Block; Fearless/Iron Will are immune to the freezeFP2ReactionNo

Effect. Speak with the cold authority of something that has eaten part of the world. Your words carry an unnatural weight — gain +3 to Intimidation, and a target who fails the contest cannot bring themselves to attack you for 1 turn (a primal sense that you are not safe to touch). This is not the Sith's domineering Force-presence; it is the quiet of prey near a predator that does not hurry.

BODY. The VOICE — low, unhurried, certain. The target must hear you. No gesture; the hunger is in the tone.

You speak, and some animal part of them recognises what you are becoming, and goes still the way a field goes still when the hawk's shadow crosses it.

Fearless or Iron Will targets are immune to the freeze (but still feel the wrongness). Repeated use on the same target loses force as they steel themselves (+2 to resist each time).

Gnawing Touch

Roll your Force attribute vs HT. Drain 1d FP (1d HP if the target has no Force); absorb half as FP.
AffinityWillActionAttackRangeTouchDefenceResisted by HT; never dodgedFP2ReactionNo

Effect. Roll your Force attribute vs HT. Drain 1d FP (1d HP if the target has no Force); absorb half as FP.

BODY. Fingertips on bare skin and a small breath drawn through the nose; skin is the channel, the breath the pull.

A small cold mouth opens where you touch.

Faintly addictive — repeated draws in a day impose a cumulative −1 Will to resist reaching for it again.

Scent of Life

Roll your Force attribute. Sense the size and rough location of every living thing within 20 hexes.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)Range20-hex radiusDefenceNone — passive senseFP1ReactionNo

Effect. Roll your Force attribute. Sense the size and rough location of every living thing within 20 hexes.

BODY. A slow inhalation through a parted mouth, eyes half-closed; no hands, but the turn of breathing-in is needed to taste the room.

Some glow full, and your hunger turns toward them.

Living things only — droids, synthetics, and the Force-dead do not register. Gives size and rough position, not identity, health, or intent.

II · THE WIDENING MOUTH

The Widening Mouth

The appetite grows. At this stage the practitioner learns to take not just energy but ABILITY — to strip the Force from another's grasp, to feed in a radius, to leave a place quieter than they found it.

Sever Feeding

Reach into a Force-user's connection and tear a piece loose into yourself.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)Range5 hexesDefenceResisted by Will (or Force skill if higher); Force-users only (theft, not suppression)FP6ReactionNo

Effect. Reach into a Force-user's connection and tear a piece loose into yourself. On a win, the target's Force skill drops by 1d for 1 minute AND you gain that many points as a temporary bonus to YOUR Force skill for the same duration. This is theft, not suppression — what they lose, you wear. Mundane targets are immune (nothing to take).

BODY. A clawing gesture of one hand, fingers hooking and pulling back toward the chest — the Force-connection is dragged bodily out of the target and into the practitioner. One hand, need not be empty.

You hook your fingers into the bright thing inside them and PULL. It comes away like wet cloth tearing, and for a minute it is yours, and they are less.

The target knows, instantly and viscerally, that something was taken. Stolen skill is borrowed, not kept — it bleeds away as the minute ends, and cannot be banked.

Hollow Radius

Open your hunger in all directions at once.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)Range4-hex radiusDefenceResisted by HT; indiscriminate — hits allies in the radius tooFP8ReactionNo

Effect. Open your hunger in all directions at once. Every living thing in a 4-hex radius takes 2d fatigue as a thread of their vitality is drawn toward you; you absorb FP equal to the number of targets affected (max = your Will/3). Plants in the radius wilt; small vermin may die. The area is left subtly LESSER — a faint deadness that lingers for hours.

GRIP & BODY. Arms open from the body, palms turned up, head tilted back — the hunger radiates outward through the whole radius and draws inward. Both arms; the openness is the mechanism.

You open yourself like a wound and the room pours into it. Everything alive within reach dims by a degree — and you are warmer for it, briefly, terribly.

Indiscriminate — affects allies in the radius. A faint dead-zone lingers and is readable by Force Echo for hours. Cumulative addiction risk as Taste of Life.

Hollow the Wound

When you wound a living target in melee (saber, blade, or bare hand), pour your hunger into the wound to keep it from…
AffinityWillActionQuick (free) — the instant you wound a target in meleeRangeTouch / saber reachDefenceResisted by HTFP4ReactionNo

Effect. When you wound a living target in melee (saber, blade, or bare hand), pour your hunger into the wound to keep it from closing. The injury cannot be healed by Force means for 1d hours and bleeds at +1 HP/turn beyond its normal rate. You absorb the bled vitality: regain 1 FP per turn it bleeds. A signature of the path — your hunger turns every cut you land into a slow feeding straw.

GRIP & BODY. A free hand splayed toward the wound you just made (or the saber-hand's two fingers at skill 20+) — the hunger reaches into the opening and holds it wide.

The cut you gave them should be closing. Instead it gapes, and through it you drink, slow and steady, while they wonder why the bleeding will not stop.

Requires you to have dealt the wound this turn or last. Cortosis-caused or Force-immune wounds cannot be hollowed. Each use: cumulative addiction, and the act is unmistakably this tradition to any witness who knows it.

Feed the Blade

Charge your weapon (lightsaber or vibroblade) with stored hunger.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn) to chargeRangeSelf (weapon)Defence— (next melee hit: +1d & drains 1d FP)FP5ReactionNo

Effect. Charge your weapon (lightsaber or vibroblade) with stored hunger. Your next melee hit deals +1d damage AND drains 1d FP from the struck target into you. With a lightsaber, the blade briefly dims to a sick, lightless grey at the moment of the strike — the hunger eats even the blade's own glow for an instant.

GRIP & BODY. A slow draw of the free hand down the length of the weapon (or along the saber's blade-path, not touching it) — feeding the hunger into the edge. One free hand to charge; the strike itself is normal.

You run your hand down the blade and it drinks what you give it. When it lands, it takes more than a cut should — and for a heartbeat the light goes out of it, like a candle in a swallowed breath.

Holds one charge at a time; charging again before striking wastes the first. Mundane targets give the +1d but no FP drain. Addiction risk on the drain.

Rend the Thread

Roll your Force attribute vs Will. Tear 2d FP from a Force-user in 6 hexes; keep half.
AffinityWillActionAttackRange6 hexesDefenceResisted by Will (or Force skill if higher); Force-users onlyFP6ReactionNo

Effect. Roll your Force attribute vs Will. Tear 2d FP from a Force-user in 6 hexes; keep half.

BODY. A clawed hand snatched back through the air toward you, as if tearing cloth — one sharp gesture at range.

A piece of them comes loose in your hand.

Felt by other Force-users as a wrongness in the Current, not a bright dark-side flare.

Hollow Step

Roll your Force attribute (reaction). Until your next turn gain +3 to defenses vs Force powers and pass once through a Force barrier.
AffinityWillActionReactionRangeSelfDefence— (+3 to defences vs Force powers this turn; pass once through a Force barrier)FP4ReactionYes — uses your Reaction

Effect. Roll your Force attribute (reaction). Until your next turn gain +3 to defenses vs Force powers and pass once through a Force barrier.

BODY. A single backward step into your own shadow; no hands — a reflexive un-being, declared as the attack comes.

For an instant you are a hole the Force pours around.

The +3 applies only against Force powers — not blasters, blades, or other physical attacks. The barrier pass is a single one-way slip for you alone, not an opening for anyone else. Declared as the attack comes, like any defence.

Starving Aura

Roll your Force attribute; targets resist HT each turn.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn), 3 turnsRange2-hex radiusDefenceResisted by HT each turn; indiscriminate — allies tooFP5ReactionNo

Effect. Roll your Force attribute; targets resist HT each turn. Living things within 2 hexes lose 1 FP/turn to you for 3 turns.

BODY. A held breath and a stillness that thins the air; the aura lasts while you keep that breath and concentration.

Standing near you grows tiring, then frightening.

Indiscriminate within reach — allies standing too close are fed upon as well.

III · THE DEVOURING WILL

The Devouring Will

Here the tradition becomes terrible. The practitioner can consume the Force itself from the world around them, drink the life from the dying, and turn a battlefield's deaths into their own strength. This is the territory the galaxy fears — the road that, walked to its end, produced the Lord of Hunger.

Drink the Fallen

When any living being dies within 10 hexes, you may catch the energy of its passing as it returns to the Force — and…
AffinityWillActionReaction — when a being dies within rangeRange10 hexesDefenceNone — the dead don’t resist (every Force-user feels the theft)FP0 — it feeds youReactionYes — uses your Reaction

Effect. When any living being dies within 10 hexes, you may catch the energy of its passing as it returns to the Force — and divert it into yourself instead. Regain FP equal to the creature's SM+5 (a humanoid ≈ 5 FP; a large beast more). Multiple deaths in the same turn each feed you. This is the engine of the tradition: in a large battle, the practitioner only grows stronger as others fall.

BODY. A sharp inhalation at the instant of a nearby death — instinctive, almost involuntary once learned. No gesture; the hunger simply takes what the dying release.

Someone dies near you, and where their light should rejoin the Force, you intercept it. It does not go home. It comes to you. You are sick with how good it feels.

Every Force-user within range FEELS a death be stolen rather than returned — it is unmistakable and deeply alarming, the signature act of this path. Each soul taken: 1 Hunger, and a cumulative −1 to resist Drinking the next one.

Unmake the Bright

Attack a Force-user's connection itself, not their body.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (2 turns)Range5 hexesDefenceResisted by Will (or Force skill); Force-users onlyFP12ReactionNo

Effect. Attack a Force-user's connection itself, not their body. On a win by margin, you can SEVER a target's Force connection — temporarily (margin 1−3: they lose all Force use for 1d hours) or, on a margin of 5+ against an already-weakened target, threaten it permanently (a Force-user reduced to nothing — the cruellest thing this tradition can do to a fellow adept). You absorb a surge of FP (2d) from what you tear away.

GRIP & BODY. Both hands rise and then close into fists as if crushing something out of the air between them — the target's connection is gripped and snuffed. Two free hands.

You take hold of the thing that makes them what they are and you close your hands. The light in them gutters. For a while — or forever — they are just a person, and they will never stop feeling the absence.

Among the most feared acts in the galaxy; any witness who understands it will treat the practitioner as an existential threat. Permanent severance: 1d Hunger and a permanent mark on the practitioner's own presence (other adepts sense what you have done). Critical failure: the connection snaps back and YOU lose Force use for 1d hours.

Devour the Working

Eat a Force power as it is cast.
AffinityWillActionReaction — as a Force power is cast within rangeRange8 hexesDefenceContest your Will vs the caster’s Force skill; only the moment of castingFP4ReactionYes — uses your Reaction

Effect. Eat a Force power as it is cast. As a reaction to any Force ability used within range (against you or another), contest the caster — on a win, the working is CONSUMED before it resolves (no effect) and you absorb its FP cost as your own FP. The grander the power you devour, the more you are fed. This is the tradition's answer to every other Force-user: their strength becomes your meal.

BODY. A sharp, open-mouthed inhalation aimed at the casting — instinctive once mastered. The hands may be occupied; the hunger does the work.

They cast, and the working leaps toward its purpose — and you open your hunger in its path and swallow it whole. It never happened. And you are fuller for it.

One devour per turn. Cannot eat a working already resolved, nor a sustained working already running (only the moment of casting). Failed contest: the power resolves normally and the caster knows what you tried. Devouring light-side workings carries +1 Hunger.

Wake the Hollow Dead

Raise the recently dead not as souls (you have no interest in souls — you ATE the Force that would animate them) but as…
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (2 turns), lasts 10 minRange5 hexesDefenceNone — raises 1d corpses as mindless husksFP10ReactionNo

Effect. Raise the recently dead not as souls (you have no interest in souls — you ATE the Force that would animate them) but as hollow husks driven by your hunger. Up to 1d corpses within range rise as mindless drones for 10 minutes: they shamble, grapple, and absorb blows for you (treat as weak combatants, ST 10, no Force, collapse at any serious wound). When one is destroyed, you regain 2 FP — even your own minions are food.

GRIP & BODY. Both hands lowered toward the bodies, fingers spread — you pour a thread of your hunger into them to stand them up. Two hands to raise; they move on their own after.

You give the dead a little of your hunger and they stand, empty-eyed, moving because emptiness wants to move. When they fall again, even that little comes back to you.

Husks are mindless and slow — tools, not soldiers. Obvious necromancy-adjacent horror; any witness treats you accordingly. Cannot raise the long-dead, the burned, or the Force-immune. 1 Hunger per casting.

Consume the Spark

Roll your Force attribute (reaction to a death within 10 hexes).
AffinityWillActionReaction — when a being dies within rangeRange10 hexesDefenceNone — feeds you (2d FP and a bonus to your next Force roll)FP8ReactionYes — uses your Reaction

Effect. Roll your Force attribute (reaction to a death within 10 hexes). Devour the escaping Force: gain 2d FP and +1 to your next Force roll.

BODY. A sharp inhalation turned toward the dying, mouth slightly open; no hands, only the timed breath as the spark flees.

Their last breath is warm, and brief.

Deeply addictive — the warmth of a stolen death is the hardest hunger to leave behind.

Maw of Silence

Roll your Force attribute vs Will. The target cannot use any Force power for 1d turns, re-rolling Will each turn to break free.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)Range8 hexesDefenceResisted by Will; re-roll each turn to break freeFP7ReactionNo

Effect. Roll your Force attribute vs Will. The target cannot use any Force power for 1d turns, re-rolling Will each turn to break free.

BODY. A slow fist closing before your own mouth; the closing fist sets it, and you must hold concentration to keep the throat shut.

Their power goes quiet, like a throat held shut.

Resisted by Will, re-rolled each turn to break free. Requires unbroken concentration — drop focus and the throat opens. It silences Force use only; the target may still move, fight, and act normally. One target at a time.

Feed the Wound

Roll your Force attribute immediately after you wound a living target in melee.
AffinityWillActionQuick (free) — right after you wound a target in meleeRangeSelfDefenceNone — converts up to 2d of your own fresh melee injury into FPFP6ReactionNo

Effect. Roll your Force attribute immediately after you wound a living target in melee. Convert up to 2d of that injury into FP for yourself.

BODY. A breath drawn through the teeth as your blow lands — no extra hand, it rides the strike you already made.

The blade takes the body; you take what spills out.

Only your own freshly-dealt melee injury feeds you; a companion's kill is no meal.

IV · THE LORD OF HUNGER

The Lord of Hunger

The final lessons are not survivable as a person. To reach them is to become mostly appetite — a wound in the Force that walks. These are the workings attributed to Nihilus and the few like him: the consumption of whole worlds, the hunger that cannot be sated and so devours until nothing remains, including, eventually, the self.

Feast of Worlds

The signature horror of the tradition: consume the living Force of an entire region, settlement, or — for a true Lord…
AffinityWillActionRitual (hours), or instinctive at a death-rich siteRangeRegion to planetaryDefenceMass HT / GM narrativeFPcatastrophic — a narrative event, never casualReactionNo

Effect. The signature horror of the tradition: consume the living Force of an entire region, settlement, or — for a true Lord of Hunger — a whole world. All life in the area is drawn into the practitioner at once; the place is left a dead echo, silent in the Force forever. The practitioner is restored utterly and gains temporary power beyond their normal ceiling. This is less a 'power' than a narrative event — the GM adjudicates scale, and it should never be casual.

BODY. The whole self becomes the mouth. There is no gesture left — the practitioner has become the hunger, and merely ceasing to hold it back is the act.

You stop holding it back. The world pours into the hole where you used to be. When it is done there is silence in the Force for light-years, and you are full, and it is already not enough.

An atrocity without equal. Permanently scars the practitioner's presence into a recognised horror; the Force itself recoils. Massive Hunger accrual; the GM should treat this as a campaign-defining event with consequences to match. The hunger is never sated — it always returns, worse.

The Wound That Walks

The end-state of the path. The practitioner ceases to be a person who consumes the Force and becomes a VOID in it that…
Roll—ActionPassive (permanent end-state)RangeSelf / auraDefence— (you no longer register as alive; living things wither near you; you must feed or starve inward)FPthe selfReactionNo

Effect. The end-state of the path. The practitioner ceases to be a person who consumes the Force and becomes a VOID in it that consumes by existing. Permanent traits: you no longer register as alive to the Force (immune to most Force-sensing and Force-attacks that target a living mind); living things and plants wither slowly in your prolonged presence; you must feed (Drink the Fallen / Hollow Radius) regularly or suffer escalating FP loss as the hunger turns inward. You can no longer be healed by light-side means, nor form ordinary Force Bonds.

BODY. There is no body-rule left to give. The practitioner IS the working.

There is no line anymore between you and the hunger. You do not use it. You are it. People feel cold when you pass, and do not know why, and are right to hurry on.

This is character-ending in all but name — a transformation into something that is no longer truly a player character but a force of nature. Presented for completeness and for NPCs like the Lord of Hunger. A PC who reaches here has, in every meaningful sense, lost themselves.

The Sated Hour

For one hour after a major feeding (Hollow Radius on a crowd, Drink the Fallen on several deaths, Feast of Worlds),…
Roll—ActionQuick (free) — after a large feedingRangeSelfDefence— (+3 Force skill, FP max doubled, powers cost half for 1 hour; then the crash)FPfree (spends stored hunger)ReactionNo

Effect. For one hour after a major feeding (Hollow Radius on a crowd, Drink the Fallen on several deaths, Feast of Worlds), ride the surplus. Your Force skill rises by +3, your FP maximum is temporarily doubled, and Force powers cost half (round up). This is the terrible efficiency of the path: a fed Hollowing adept is, briefly, one of the most powerful beings on a battlefield — which is exactly why they seek the slaughter that feeds them.

BODY. No gesture — a stillness, almost serene, as the taken life settles into you. For this hour you do not look hungry. You look full, and content, and that is worse.

For one hour you are not starving. The power sits easy in you, vast and warm, and you understand exactly why the ones who walk this road never stop walking it.

When the hour ends, the crash: −3 Force skill and FP regeneration halted for the next hour as the hunger returns redoubled. Requires a genuine large feeding to trigger — it cannot be faked. The contrast between the Sated Hour and the starving that follows is what hollows the practitioner's mind over time.

Eclipse of the Self

Become, for a time, a true void in the Force — a small foretaste of the Wound That Walks without its permanence.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 min), then maintainedRangeSelf (6-hex withering aura)Defence— (a true void: immune to Force-sensing & mind-targeting; living within 6 hexes lose 1 FP/turn)FP15, then 4/minReactionNo

Effect. Become, for a time, a true void in the Force — a small foretaste of the Wound That Walks without its permanence. While active: you are immune to all Force-sensing and to any Force power that targets a living mind (they find no living mind to target); living things within 6 hexes wither slightly and lose 1 FP/turn (drawn into you); and your own dark workings cost no Hunger for the duration (you are briefly beyond alignment, simply hunger). The deepest reach a practitioner can make and still come back a person.

BODY. The whole self goes cold and absent — there is no gesture because there is, for the moment, almost no 'you' left to gesture. Maintaining it is the act.

You let yourself go out, like a star collapsing — and what is left is a cold mouth in the shape of a person. The Force cannot find you because there is nothing alive here to find. Only the appetite, wearing your name.

Maintaining Eclipse is ruinous: each minute, roll Will or take 1d FP burn as the void turns inward. While Eclipsed you cannot be healed by any means and cannot receive help through Force Bonds (nothing reaches in). Stay too long (GM's call, typically minutes) and the state threatens to become permanent — the first step toward the Wound That Walks. Every Force-user for a great distance feels the hole where you were.

Devouring Field

Roll your Force attribute; targets resist HT each turn.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn), 3 turnsRange4-hex radiusDefenceResisted by HT each turn; you stand at its centre, unsparedFP10ReactionNo

Effect. Roll your Force attribute; targets resist HT each turn. All living within 4 hexes lose 1d FP/turn for 3 turns; you gain a third of the total.

BODY. Arms spread and a long, rattling exhale; the field opens around you and holds while you keep arms wide and concentration unbroken.

The grass browns and you feel full.

You stand at its centre and are not spared the unease — though never the drain.

Crown's Compulsion

Roll your Force attribute vs Will, at −2 to targets at or below 1/3 FP or HP.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)Range8 hexesDefenceResisted by Will (−2 if target is at or below 1/3 FP or HP); only the already-weakened can be takenFP12ReactionNo

Effect. Roll your Force attribute vs Will, at −2 to targets at or below 1/3 FP or HP. Dominate the weakened target for 1 minute.

BODY. A slow beckoning hand and one named command, held in concentration; break focus and the leash falls.

The empty have no walls left; you take the chair.

Only the already-emptied can be taken — a target above a third of its strength simply refuses.

Unmake the Living

Roll your Force attribute; target resists HT. On a failure they take 3d injury that ignores all DR as their life is eaten.
AffinityWillActionAttackRange6 hexesDefenceResisted by HT; the injury ignores all DRFP10ReactionNo

Effect. Roll your Force attribute; target resists HT. On a failure they take 3d injury that ignores all DR as their life is eaten.

BODY. A slow closing of the fist at range, knuckles whitening — one terrible gesture, no upkeep.

You decide that less of them should exist.

Eats the life-thread directly: no armour, ward, or DR stands between it and the body.

V · THE DEVOURING ABYSS

The Devouring Abyss

The end of the road the First Hunger began. The Lord of Hunger ate to grow strong; the Abyss does not eat to grow — it eats because eating is now all it is. Training: Special / Lifetime, and the price is the self. These workings are not learned so much as surrendered to; each one spends a measure of who you were that you will not get back. No one walks this tier and comes back whole, and most who reach it no longer want to.

Maw of the Endless

You stop being a person who hungers and become, for one terrible instant, the mouth itself.
AffinityWillActionAttack — you open, and the hunger pours outRange8-hex cone, line-of-sight (scales to a 10-hex circle)DefenceContest Will vs your Force skill, each living thing in the cone; allies tooFP12 (or pay HP)ReactionNoPrereqTier IV · Feast of Worlds

Effect. You stop being a person who hungers and become, for one terrible instant, the mouth itself. A cone of pure absence opens before you and draws the life out of everything inside it. Each being in the area that loses the Will contest takes draining damage that you keep — FP and HP both flow into you, healing your wounds and refilling your reserves past their normal maximum (the overflow fades after the scene). Plants wither, small animals drop, lights dim. What you take this way cannot be restored by ordinary rest, kolto, or healing trance; only time, or another's deliberate Force-gift, undoes it.

SCALABILITY. +6 FP widens the cone to a 10-hex circle around you; spending HP instead of FP (1 HP = 2 FP toward the cost) lets you cast it even when your reserves are empty — the Abyss is always willing to be fed from the inside.

There is no light, no sound, no push — only a direction, toward you, that everything alive suddenly obeys. For a heartbeat the room exhales into your chest and goes grey, and you have never in your life felt so full or so cold.

Every use of the Maw is a Monstrous act — among the darkest a Hollower can commit. Allies caught in the cone are drained exactly as enemies are — the hunger does not know friends. A Force-user who survives the draining knows, forever, exactly what fed on them.
