Critical failure: the fold misaligns — passengers scatter 2d hexes around the destination holocron, prone, and the carried holocron’s link is severed (requiring re-ritual).

V  ·  CLAN MOTHER

The Final Inscriptions

Once-per-arc · ritual scale · 15–30+ FP · use them when they are the moment

Rare uses. Each requires either deep ritual preparation, near-maximum FP, or specific narrative conditions. These are the workings you carry but does not casually invoke. Some cannot be face-bolstered at all — they consume their own resources or burn the bonds that hold them.

Ghostfire Horror

Inflict a permanent psychological trauma on the target.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)Range5 hexes — requires eye contactDefenceResisted by Will or Mind Block; never dodgedFP20 (30 bolstered)ReactionNo

Effect. Inflict a permanent psychological trauma on the target. Target gains a −10 to −15 point Mental Disadvantage chosen by you at casting (Phobia, Delusion, Bad Temper, Paranoia, etc.) — or, on margin ≥5, paralysing insanity for 1d days followed by the same permanent disadvantage. Bolstered: two related mental disadvantages totalling −20 to −30 points; paralysing-insanity duration extends to 1d weeks.

Your saber rises to chest height. Teal mist coils around its full length like a slow snake. The target’s gaze meets yours and finds something inside it that should not be inside any human gaze — and breaks against it. They will not remember what they saw, only that they cannot ever stop seeing it.

Among the cruellest workings you retained. NPCs aware of the casting treat you as a serious threat regardless of alignment claims. Cannot be undone by Force healing — the trauma is real, not compulsion. Critical failure: you suffer 1 hour of the same paralysing insanity yourself; the target sees through to your vulnerability and gains Enemy at +0 cost if they survive.

Soulforge

Forge or bind a permanent Force property into a single item — a vibroblade gains Crystal Edge permanently, a comlink…
AffinityWillActionGrand Ritual (3 days at a prepared forge)RangeTouch (the item, throughout)DefenceThe bound soul resists with its living Will vs your Will +4FP40 across the 3 daysReactionNoHolocrons1 small holocron (destroyed) + 1 tether (released)Sacrificethe holocron is gone for good; you age a cosmetic month

Effect. Forge or bind a permanent Force property into a single item — a vibroblade gains Crystal Edge permanently, a comlink can transmit holocron-stored memories, armour resists one specific Force power type (Lightning, Fear, Choke). The bound soul-tether is consumed (released cleanly into the Force as part of the act; you may choose this dedication intentionally). One Soulforged item active at a time. To Soulforge another: either re-absorb the previous (3-day reverse ritual, recovering the holocron but not the tether or your aged month) or destroy the item.

The forge — any sufficient fire — burns teal continuously for three days and nights. You take the saber apart at the start: emitter, power cell, harmonic guides set at the cardinal runes around the forge, the hilt-shell laid open beside the anvil. The ghostfire crystal hangs on a thin chain at the geometric centre, directly above the working item. You hold the small holocron in one hand and the item in the other for hours at a stretch, alternating with brief meditation but never leaving the ring of runes. By the third dawn the holocron is cracking visibly along its edges, the tether-soul is whispering its release, the crystal is glowing bronze-teal through the chain, and the item is no longer entirely what it was. At completion the small holocron splits with a soft sound and disintegrates into bronze dust. The crystal cools. You reassemble the saber by hand — the components remember themselves to one another. You are yourself, but a month older around the eyes.

Requires genuine intent — cannot be used to fool yourself about the soul’s release. The 3-day ritual cannot be interrupted; you cannot leave the inscribed circle, cannot sleep more than 2 hours in any 24, and must keep the forge fed continuously. Failure during the ritual (sleep, leaving the circle, attack that breaks your focus) ruins the working and consumes the holocron and tether anyway. Critical failure: item ruined; soul fragments — does not return to the Force cleanly (1d months of Force disturbance in the area); you take 3d mental fatigue and loses a permanent point of Will until reconciliation (1 month of meditation).

Curse of the Lingering

Mark the target with a slow-burning curse.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (3 turns), saber litRange10 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceResisted by Will or Mind Block; never dodgedFP15 (22 bolstered)ReactionNoHolocrons1 pre-inscribed face + a free Soul Bind slot

Effect. Mark the target with a slow-burning curse. They suffer no immediate effect. But — if the target dies by any cause within the duration — their soul does not depart cleanly into the Force. Instead, it lingers, anchored by your saber’s harmonic, for up to 24 hours after death, available to you for Soul Bind regardless of where you are or whether you are present. You know the moment they die (subtle internal chime, even across the galaxy). You have 24 hours to reach the body, or to send a Crystal Phantom or Bound Voice messenger to collect the tether on your behalf. Bolstered: duration 30 days; the soul lingers for 72 hours after death; you may bind it remotely (within 1 system) without physically reaching the body, paying only the Soul Bind FP cost.

For three turns you hold the saber vertical and meets the target’s gaze. Teal mist drifts from your palm, crosses the distance unhurried, and settles around their throat like a noose nobody else can see. The target may feel cold, or hear a single bell. You have placed a debt on their death.

Extended Exposure: a soul you have cursed before answers your harmonic more readily. If you have previously bound, read, or cursed this individual, a fresh Curse takes hold at −2 to their resistance, and the lingering window after death lengthens by half (the soul already knows the shape of your call). Repeated cursings of the same person across a campaign make them, in effect, permanently tethered-in-waiting.

Unmistakeable to other Force-sensitives at the moment of casting (it is a long, obvious working). Cannot be cast secretly. Critical failure: the curse adheres to you instead — if you die within the duration, the next person who touches your body becomes able to Soul Bind you.

The Last Voice

You speak one sentence of 10 words or fewer, in any language you know.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (3 turns), saber litRange100 m radiusDefenceResisted per declaration — Will/Mind Block at −6 (Compulsion), −4 (Revelation), −3 (Severance)FPall remaining (min 40) + 1 permanent WillReactionNoHolocronsspends every Soul Bind tether you hold

Effect. You speak one sentence of 10 words or fewer, in any language you know. The bound souls you carry are spent — every tether in your Soul Bind holocron dissolves into the Force, gone permanently. In their dissolution, the words you speak land on every listener within 100m as if uttered with the weight of the Force itself. The declaration you choose takes one of three forms, declared at activation:

(a) A Compulsion. “Drop your weapons.” “Sleep.” “Run.” “Tell me where you went.” Every listener must roll Will or Mind Block at −6 (Force-sensitives roll their Force skill at −6, best applies) or obey the compulsion for the next minute. After the minute they remember being compelled but cannot resist for the duration. Listeners may attempt one re-roll per minute thereafter at the same −6. Compulsions that require sustained complex action (“hunt down X and kill you”) fade after 24 hours regardless; instant compulsions (“sleep”) resolve once.

(b) A Revelation. “He is the traitor.” “The bomb is in the third locker.” “Master Sirran killed your son.” Every listener within range knows this statement as objectively true for the next 24 hours — not as an opinion you imposed, but as truth they have always known and only just remembered. Force-sensitives may roll Will or Mind Block at −4 to recognise it as imposed (success: they know it was the Last Voice but the conviction remains for the 24 hours; critical success: they shake it off entirely). The declaration does not have to be true — you may speak lies as easily as truths. After 24 hours, the imposed conviction fades; listeners may reconsider on their own, though evidence built during the conviction window often remains influential.

(c) A Severance. “You are no longer my brother.” “This place is not your home.” “You never knew me.” The declaration severs a specific relational truth from listeners’ minds. Targets within range named or referenced lose the named bond, memory, or belonging — not their factual memory of events, but the emotional weight that made the bond real to them. A severed brother remembers their sibling exists but feels no warmth or kinship. A severed home no longer feels like home. Force-sensitives resist with Will or Mind Block at −3. The severance lasts permanently unless restored by another Force working of comparable scale or by a year of deliberate relational repair. Use this when the moment is irrevocable; it cannot be casually undone.

Range and Witnesses. Affects every listener within 100m who can hear or sense the declaration (deaf Force-sensitives still register it through the Force; Force-deafened individuals are immune). The declaration is openly perceptible — everyone within 1km feels something Force-significant happened, even if they did not hear the words.

You raise the saber overhead. The blade goes from its usual invisible pulse to fully visible — teal, sharp-edged, unmistakable. Every soul you have carried answers at once and lets go. Your ten words leave your mouth and arrive in every otyour mouth at the same time, true.

Once-per-arc at most. Every tether is gone — the Soul Bind holocron is empty but intact. You lose 1 permanent point of Will until you have bound at least one new tether and meditated for 24 hours uninterrupted — the empty holocron is a wound until refilled. Critical failure: the souls release violently — 5d FP damage to you, unconsciousness, 1d minor Force disturbances locally (broken glass, wind, fear in animals), and the words you spoke land on you instead of on the listeners (a Compulsion compels you; a Revelation imposes the “truth” on your own mind; a Severance severs you from whatever you named).

Mother’s Calling

SUMMONING   A 7-day preparation at a chosen site, followed by a 4-hour invocation at its centre.
AffinityWillActionGrand Ritual (7 days prep + 4 hr invocation)RangeBonded dead from your holocron; bonded living pulled from any distanceDefenceLiving bonded may refuse with a Will roll; the dead obeyFP50 + 5 per entityReactionNoHolocrons2 large or 4 small at the cardinals + the Soul Bind at centreSacrificeevery dead tether is released; the site is spent for 1 year

Effect. SUMMONING   A 7-day preparation at a chosen site, followed by a 4-hour invocation at its centre. At the end of the invocation, the working calls every bond you have woven — the bonded dead from your Soul Bind holocron and the living tethered to you by Force bond (see Force Bonds sidebar).

(a) The Bonded Dead. Each bound soul you draw (up to all 4 in your Soul Bind holocron) manifests at the site as a fully visible teal-and-bone phantom of who they were — armed and armoured as they died, free of the mindless silence that normally afflicts the bound. They obey you, fight on your turn (each as a 200-point combatant using their living statlines, capped), and may speak briefly. After 1 hour they dissolve permanently into the Force — the tethers are consumed and cannot be re-bound. The Soul Bind holocron is empty after Mother’s Calling.

(b) The Bonded Living. Each living Force-bonded companion you call is instantly pulled to the site — appearing within 2 hexes of your location in a teal mist-step, regardless of distance (galactic or otherwise). The personal token you have kept for each bonded since their bond formed is what allows the summoning to find them; without the token, that bond cannot be called. They arrive with whatever they were holding and conscious of being summoned. They may attempt a Will roll to resist; failing, they arrive; succeeding, they remain where they were and the FP cost is wasted on them.

Site Binding. You must remain at the centre of the inscribed circle throughout the 4-hour invocation. If you leave the circle for any reason — physically pushed, knocked out, voluntarily stepping aside — the ritual collapses immediately: all 50 FP is spent, the 7 days of preparation are wasted, the holocron-runes burn out, the tokens are not consumed, and any summoning in progress fails (dead souls return to tethers; living summoned mid-arrival are returned to where they started). Damage taken during the invocation that does not move you does not break the ritual but requires a Will roll per 5 HP lost to maintain concentration.

Site Decay. After completion (successful or failed), the inscribed runes burn out completely. The site cannot host another Mother’s Calling for 1 year while it recovers; attempts within the year fail at activation and waste components.

You choose the place a week before. You walk its perimeter at dawn each morning, inscribing rune after rune by hand, in chalk that turns to ash, in lines you draw with your own blood. On the seventh day at sundown you enter the centre and does not leave. You take the saber apart with reverence and arranges its components across the inscribed geometry — emitter at the north rune, power cell at the south, harmonic guides at east and west, hilt-shell opened and laid before you, secondary couplings at the four inter-cardinal points. The ghostfire crystal you suspend on a thin chain at the very centre, level with your own chest. For four hours the crystal stands suspended between your hands, the Soul Bind holocron pressed to your chest, the cardinal braziers burning teal. Teal mist rolls outward in a slow tide — you can see it crossing rooms, drifting through walls. Where it touches your dead, they rise as themselves, briefly, before they go. Where it touches your living — wherever they are in the galaxy — the mist finds them, knows them by the tokens you have carried for years, and they are simply here, blinking, beside you.

A defining act, not a tactic. After the invocation: HT or be exhausted (¼ FP and Move) for 72 hours. Living bonded who succeed on their Will roll feel the attempt clearly and know you called them — this damages relationships if used coercively. Cannot be used in Force Voids, on warships in hyperspace, or anywhere lacking open sky. Critical failure: bonds slip — you take 5d mental fatigue, all bonded (living and dead) feel your vulnerability for 1 month, one randomly determined living bond is severed permanently, all dead tethers are released anyway (the worst of both outcomes).

Inscribed Future

Inscribe one Tier I–IV working onto a holocron face along with a specific triggering condition (“if my heart stops”,…
AffinityWillActionBrief Ritual (1 hr); then auto-fires on its triggerRangeAs the inscribed working (at trigger)DefenceAs the inscribed workingFP15 to inscribeReactionNo — pre-inscribed; auto-fires on its triggerHolocrons1 face (one Inscribed Future per holocron)

Effect. Inscribe one Tier I–IV working onto a holocron face along with a specific triggering condition (“if my heart stops”, “when this door is opened by an enemy”, “if [named being] enters this room”, “if I say the word ‘enough’ three times”). When the condition is met, the inscribed working resolves automatically with your stats and Will at the moment of inscription. The face is consumed. Limit: one Inscribed Future per holocron at a time.

You kneel for an hour with the saber across your knees and the holocron in your hands. The trigger condition is built into the inscription as a phrase, an image, a felt-sense. When the moment arrives — however far away you are, however unconscious or absent — the face flares teal, the working resolves, and something happens that you planned for long ago.

One condition only — ambiguity defaults to “trigger immediately” (wasted). Cannot trigger from your own actions while awake (the system reads your intent). Critical failure on inscription burns the face and reveals the planned trigger to any Force-sensitive who handles the holocron afterward — a major secrecy break.

Appendix — Quick Reference & Strategy

Tier Summary · Combat Priorities · The Cost of What You carry

Tier-by-Tier Strategy Summary

Tier I (Initiate). Cheap, repeatable, scene-setting. Phantom Stride is reposition; Witchsight reveals the field and the bonds running through it; Hex of Wandering seeds long-term misfortune on a target; Resonant Touch lets allies benefit briefly from your work; Coldfinger is the nasty close-contact tool when a touch is enough.

Tier II (Apprentice). Reliable mainstays for moment-to-moment encounters. Spirit Sever for solo neutralisation (close range, gesture-arm); Crystal Tether for control at distance (6 hexes); Verdant Surge for area denial; Weighing of Hearts for tactical awareness in dense or confused fights. Wither stands apart from the rest of the tier — it is a Major Ritual, not an in-combat tool, and turns an entire area against its inhabitants over an 8-hour working. Plan its use days in advance.

Tier III (Witch). The territory where Nightsister inheritance shows through. Soul Bind is the cornerstone — it powers four other workings and one Tier V. Holocron Tracing turns one-shot holocrons into recurring resources. Echo Shriek at the new 2-turn build is heavy but devastating in tight quarters. Etched Counter is the best free-trigger investment against known opposing tactics.

Tier IV (High Priestess). Major fights only. Crystal Phantom for sustained presence; Holocron Sentry for prepared defences; Coffin Mist for single-target lockdown that other observers do not even realise is happening; The Borrowed Face for infiltration and impersonation. Bound Voice gives the resonance bonus that makes high-stakes contests tractable.

Tier V (Clan Mother). Grand-scale narrative workings. Force Horror burns relationships even on success. Soulforge is the permanent-item path — a 3-day ritual that consumes a small holocron and a soul. Curse of the Lingering is the long game — cast it on a major threat days in advance and you have an option no matter where the death happens. The Last Voice spends every tether for a single 10-word declaration that compels, reveals, or severs. Mother’s Calling is a 7-day prepared site that summons every bond — living from across the galaxy, dead from your holocron, all to one place you cannot leave during the invocation. None of these are tactics; they are commitments.

Holocron Loadout Strategy

Because each small holocron can only hold one type of working across all its faces, loadout is a strategic choice rather than a mixed inventory. A small holocron dedicated to a frequently-used Tier I or Tier II working gives three bolstered uses per ritual cycle — a meaningful tactical resource. A large holocron’s 7 faces may each hold a different working, making it the principal toolkit-carrier; the small holocrons act as specialist reserves.

Common loadout patterns.

The Combat Reserve. One small holocron dedicated to Phantom Stride (three bolstered teleports for a heavy engagement) or Crystal Tether (three sustained binds across a long fight).

The Counter-Ward. One small holocron dedicated to Etched Counter, inscribed in advance with the working the practitioner most expects to face (Force Lightning, Choke, Mind Trick). Three free triggers across a session.

The Greater Toolkit. The large holocron loaded with a Tier I–IV working on each face — e.g. Witchsight + Coffin Mist + The Borrowed Face + Spirit Sever + Verdant Surge + Resonant Touch + Crystal Phantom. Each available once before requiring re-inscription.

Soul Bind Holocron. Always separate. 4 tether-slots. Tethers are inventory for the necromantic workings — not all four need be filled at once; you may carry one or two with the remainder open for future bindings.

Re-inscription rhythm. A small holocron’s three faces inscribed all to the same working takes 3 hours of meditation (1 hour per face). A large holocron loaded with seven distinct workings is 7 hours. Practitioners typically re-inscribe during downtime between major encounters — planning the next loadout based on intelligence about what comes next.

Combat Priority Chain — Elite Opponents

Stage 0: Prepare in advance. Etched Counter inscribed on relevant counter-targets days before engagement. Holocron faces bolstered. Curse of the Lingering on the priority target if you can get close enough days ahead. If the encounter location is known and you have the time, a full Wither ritual at the site before the enemy arrives. If a Force Nexus is available and accepting, perform whatever rituals are possible there — halved cost means twice the bolstered face capacity at half the FP drain.

Stage 1: Read the field. Witchsight (2 FP) at the start of any major engagement reveals bonds, residues, and Force-Stealth-cloaked combatants. Combined with Weighing of Hearts (4 FP), the practitioner knows what each opponent intends before they act.

Stage 2: Hex the priority targets. Hex of Wandering on the most-mobile or most-precise opponent (a pilot, a sniper, a force-user mid-incantation). They will fail something critical within minutes.

Stage 3: Lock down the leader. Coffin Mist (bolstered, 12 FP) on the highest-priority single target removes them from the encounter for 3–5 turns without other observers noticing. While they are buried alive in their own head, the rest of the encounter happens around them.

Stage 4: Bound resources. Bound Voice (bolstered, 10 FP) for +2 Force skill via a relevant tether. Crystal Phantom if the fight is large enough to need a second body.

Reserve. Hold Soul Bind for opponents reduced to 0 FP — turn a defeat into a tether. Keep at least 15 FP for Etched Counter triggers.

On the Cost of What You carry

These workings are yours by re-derivation, not inheritance. Soul Bind, Force Horror, Holocron Sentry, Coffin Mist — all remain off-putting regardless of the saber’s teal pulse and the holocron’s clean architecture. The conversion changed what they are — they are no longer dark — but it did not change what they look like to others, and it did not undo what they cost the bound to be bound.

Bind sparingly. Release when you can. The holocron is not a prison; it is a page you can erase. Treat it accordingly.

— The Concordance of Feathers, marginalia

BOOK VI

THE UNCHAINED

Path
Peace is a lie, there is only passion.
Through passion, I gain strength.
Through strength, I gain power.
Through power, I gain victory.
Through victory, my chains are broken.
The Force shall free me.
— The Sith Code
TIERS I · II · III · IV · LEGENDARY

The Dark Side

A path of passion, dominance, and inevitable corruption

The Dark Side — the Unchained Path

Passion, dominance, and inevitable corruption. What the dark gives, it takes back with interest.

Alignment req.The dark side. The Unchained Path — open to any who will pay its price; barred to none by virtue, only by unwillingness to fall.
Force skillPer working — Will for drains, dominations and curses; ST for telekinetic crushing; Per or IQ where noted. Each power names its Roll.
TiersI Initiate · II Knight · III Master · IV Grandmaster · V Legend.
Capstone & CrossingEach Crossing is a Fall — a deed of escalating cruelty that forces a mandatory Disadvantage and deepens the corruption (full table below).
To buyTier costs 8 / 16 / 30 / 50 / 80; single powers priced individually by tier.

The price. These workings run hotter than their light and neutral counterparts by design — and every meaningful use accrues Corruption, the slow toll that reshapes personality and invites disadvantageous traits. The power is a loan against who you are.

Capstones, Crossings & Corruption

The dark asks the opposite price. You do not become worthy of it; you give it pieces of yourself in exchange for power, and it changes you to fit. Every dark tier-up forces a new Disadvantage, and the deeper you go, the more the body and presence corrupt to match the soul.

The Price of Each Crossing — mandatory Disadvantages

On each Crossing into a new dark tier, take the listed Disadvantage (GM helps choose to fit your conduct). These are not optional flavour — they are the toll the dark exacts, and they stay with you for the rest of your life.

  • Crossing to II (Knight): a −5pt dark trait — Bad Temper, Bully, or Callous.
  • Crossing to III (Master): a −10pt trait — Bloodlust, Greed (severe), Intolerance, or Paranoia. First physical mark appears (eyes, pallor, a coldness others feel).
  • Crossing to IV (Grandmaster): a −10/−15pt trait — Sadism, Megalomania, or Obsession. Visible corruption (Sith eyes, greyed/cracked skin, a presence that frightens animals).
  • Crossing to V (Legend / Dark Lord): Megalomania plus the surrender of a defining human bond. The corruption is now who you are; you may no longer pass for unfallen.

Unchained Path capstones, by tier

ReachCrossing capstone (the Fall)Mastery capstones
II · KnightFirst Blood Willingly Spilled — use the dark to harm when you did not have to, and feel the rush of it. Take your −5pt trait.(1) Win a fight you were losing by reaching for the dark.
III · MasterThe Severed Tie — betray, sacrifice, or destroy something/someone you cared for, for power or victory. Take your −10pt trait + first mark.(1) Break a stronger foe through Lightning or Crush. (2) Dominate a mind and make it serve.
IV · GrandmasterThe Throne of Self — commit an atrocity that serves only your power or pride, with relish. Take your −10/−15pt trait + visible corruption.(1) Raise or command lesser dark-siders/creatures. (2) Survive your own apprentice’s betrayal-attempt (the Rule of Two in miniature). (3) A working of Grave Weight.
V · Legend / Dark LordThe Last Humanity Spent — a Monstrous act (a Force Storm on the helpless, a step toward a Thought Bomb) that ends who you were.The Legendary dark powers are the reward of the Crossing; the atrocity is the mastery.

What the dark gives, it takes back with interest. These powers do not require patience — they require hunger. They reward investment immediately and demand payment later. The user is changed by each casting; the changes accumulate. There is no ”responsible” use of the dark. There is only use, and the marks of use, and what those marks attract.

Most who walk this path believe they will be the exception. Most are not. The Sith Academy teaches these techniques openly; independent dark-siders learn them by example, by accident, or by surrender.

Corruption. Many powers in this book accrue Corruption with use — some automatically, some by failed Will roll. The GM tracks corruption per GURPS Star Wars conventions. Use is rarely consequence-free.

Power & Corruption — The Design Contract. The powers in this book and in the Hollowing run hotter than their light and neutral counterparts — more damage, more efficiency, more reach, fewer restrictions. This is deliberate, and it is paid for. The light side offers nothing for free either, but what it withholds is raw power; what the dark withholds is the wielder’s self.

Every meaningful use here moves a counter the light-sider never touches: Corruption, the slow accrual that reshapes personality, invites disadvantageous traits, and bends the character toward the very hunger they are wielding. A Force Lightning that out-damages Electric Judgement two-to-one is not a free lunch — it is a loan against who you are. (The full corruption mechanics — thresholds, traits gained, the road to falling — are detailed in a later section.)

So when comparing a dark or Hollowing working to its clean counterpart and finding it stronger: that is the system working as intended. The price is simply paid somewhere the character sheet does not show until it is too late.

I · INITIATE

Initiate

Foundational abilities. Skill bonus: +2. Low cost and reliable. Powers marked Scalable allow extra FP for stronger effects, extended duration, or resistance penalties against the target.

✦ Dark Side Powers

Force Jolt

1 flat burning nerve damage per 2 FP.
AffinityWillActionAttackRange6 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceResisted by HT; bypasses armour, never dodgedFP2 per 1 damage (max 5 this tier)ReactionNo

Effect. 1 flat burning nerve damage per 2 FP. Bypasses physical armour. Causes severe pain — target suffers −1 to all actions next turn per point of damage dealt. Maximum 5 damage per casting at this tier. Stackable. / Scalable: +3 FP applies −2 to the target's HT resistance roll. / +3 FP extends the pain penalty duration to 2 turns instead of 1.

BODY. A snap of one free hand, fingers flicking forward — a brief arc. The hand must be empty at the instant of release, but may immediately re-grip afterward (a duelist can jolt between saber exchanges).

You flick your fingers or point two fingers at the target. Dark purple-red lightning arcs along their skin for a fraction of a second, leaving a smell of ozone and scorched nerve endings.

Targets with High Pain Threshold halve the action penalty. LoS must be unobstructed. No effect through shields or cover.

Force Slow

Target suffers −1 to Move and −1 to Dodge for up to 3 turns.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)Range8 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceResisted by HT, re-rolled each turn at cumulative +1 (droids +2); never dodgedFP2ReactionNo

Effect. Target suffers −1 to Move and −1 to Dodge for up to 3 turns. Each turn the target rolls HT — success reduces the penalty by 1. Droids resist at HT+2. Ineffective against SM+3 or larger. / Scalable: +3 FP applies −2 to the target's HT resistance roll. / +2 FP increases the penalty to −2 Move and −2 Dodge.

GRIP & BODY. A flattened, slowly-lowering hand — the Force thickens around the target's whole body like setting resin, gripping every joint. One free hand; envelopment mode (half-ceiling), per the Telekinetic Load System.

You make a slow pressing motion with one hand. The target's movements stutter — dragging against invisible resistance.

Cumulative +1 to target's HT resistance each turn. Droids at HT+2.

Force Rage (Initiate)

+3 to ST-based damage rolls per die for 1 turn.
AffinitySTActionQuick (free) — selfRangeSelfDefenceNone — a self-buff (Winded after; Will roll vs compulsion)FP3ReactionNo

Effect. +3 to ST-based damage rolls per die for 1 turn. Afterward, you are Winded (−2 all actions for 1 turn) and must succeed on a Will roll or feel compelled to attack the nearest living target. Each additional use this encounter increases the Will roll penalty by 1. Stackable. / Scalable: +2 FP reduces the Will roll penalty by 1 for this casting (safer use at higher cost). / +2 FP extends the damage bonus to 2 turns instead of 1 (Winded still applies after).

Your eyes go flat and dark. Veins surface across your temples. The air immediately around you vibrates at a frequency that makes nearby objects hum.

Will roll required after each use. Risk of attacking allies increases with repeated use.

Drain Life

Deal 2d damage bypassing armour.
AffinityWillActionAttackRange4 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceResisted by HT; never dodged; no droids or Force-deadFP3ReactionNo

Effect. Deal 2d damage bypassing armour. You heal HP equal to half the damage dealt AND recover FP equal to the other half — a strike that fuels you even as it wounds them. Cannot be used on droids or Force-dead. Dark-side pressure if used on non-combatants. / Scalable: +4 FP increases healing to full damage dealt as HP (FP recovery stays at half). / +4 FP (separate) applies −2 to the target's HT resistance roll. / +3 FP (separate) lets you split the drain across two adjacent targets.

BODY. An extended free hand, fingers curled toward the target — the Force draws the life across the gap to the palm. One free hand; closer range without it.

Your hand stretches toward them. A pulse of dark-violet energy crosses the gap — visible to Force-sensitives, a faint flicker to others. You feel briefly more alive. They feel briefly less.

Wrath Meditation (30 min): double the HP drained and the fraction you reclaim; a meditated drain can leave a victim a husk and restore you to full. (+1 Corruption on completion.) [PREPARED — meditate beforehand to charge it, then release in a normal action; the charge holds until used or your next rest, and resolves on its own once loosed.]

Each use risks a Will roll vs Dark Side compulsion (cumulative −1 per use this encounter). Repeated draining of the same target within 1 hour costs +1 FP per repeat.

Fear

Inject raw terror into the target's mind.
AffinityWillActionAttackRange8 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceResisted by Will or Mind Block; Fearless/Iron Will immune; never dodgedFP3ReactionNo

Effect. Inject raw terror into the target's mind. Failure: target gains Frightened condition — must use available actions to flee, −2 to attack and defence rolls. Fearless or Iron Will: immune. High Bravery: +2 to resist. Scalable: +3 FP applies −2 to Will; +3 FP additional extends duration to 1 minute.

Your eyes meet theirs and they see something inside them they were not meant to see. Their face whitens. Their breath shortens. The choice they were about to make becomes "run."

Extended Exposure: terror compounds with familiarity. Each time you successfully frighten the SAME individual, the next attempt lands a cumulative −1 to their resistance (max −4), and the duration of their panic grows — a mind taught to dread you stops being able to steady itself in your presence at all.

Failed contest: no effect, but the target knows you tried. Force-sensitive targets may identify the technique. 1 Dark Side compulsion roll on use.

II · KNIGHT

Knight

Reliable combat and control powers. Skill bonus: +3. Average cost 3−10 FP once scaled. Scaling options add resistance penalties, extend duration, increase damage, or widen area. The dark side rewards investment.

✦ Dark Side Powers

Force Disrupt

1 flat damage per 2 FP, bypassing armour.
AffinityDXActionAttackRange8 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceResisted by HT (stun on a failure); bypasses armour, never dodgedFP2 per 1 damage (max 4 this tier)ReactionNo

Effect. 1 flat damage per 2 FP, bypassing armour. Target fails HT: Stunned for 1 turn. Maximum 4 damage at this tier. Does not re-stun an already-stunned target. / Scalable: +3 FP extends the stun duration to 2 turns on a failed HT roll. / +3 FP applies −2 to the target's HT resistance roll.

BODY. Two fingers of one hand snapped toward the target — a focused pulse. The hand need not be empty.

You jab two fingers at the target. A compressed bolt of dark kinetic energy strikes them in the centre of mass, resonating through the nervous system.

Maximum 4 damage (8 FP) at this tier.

Force Menace

−1 per FP to target's morale, Will rolls, and social actions while maintained.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn), then maintainedRange8 hexes, line-of-sight (losing it ends the working)DefenceResisted by Will or Mind Block, re-rolled each turn at +1; never dodgedFP1 per −1, per turn (max −4)ReactionNo

Effect. −1 per FP to target's morale, Will rolls, and social actions while maintained. Losing LoS ends it. Stackable up to −4 total. Targets who succeed Will break free and are immune for the rest of the encounter. / Scalable: +3 FP applies −2 to the target's initial Will resistance roll. / +2 FP extends the menacing aura to affect all targets within 2 hexes of the primary target at half the penalty (round down, minimum −1).

BODY. Projected through PRESENCE — bearing, eyes, and an optional looming gesture. No free hand strictly required, but a raised hand deepens the effect.

You do nothing dramatic. You simply look at the target with a stillness so absolute it becomes threatening — and the Force-pressure of your presence bears down.

Extended Exposure: fear compounds. Each time you successfully cow the SAME individual, your menace lands a cumulative −1 to their resistance against you (max −4), and they begin to flinch from you on sight. Break them often enough and the dread becomes a standing condition — they cannot easily steel themselves against you again.

Cumulative +1 to resistance each turn. Maximum −4 total.

Force Pain

−1 per FP to all DX and IQ actions for 2 turns.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)Range8 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceResisted by HT; High Pain Threshold halves it; never dodgedFP1 per −1 (max −4; degrades over 2 turns)ReactionNo

Effect. −1 per FP to all DX and IQ actions for 2 turns. Penalty degrades by 1 each turn. High Pain Threshold halves penalties. Maximum −4 per casting. Stackable up to −6 total. / Scalable: +3 FP applies −2 to the target's HT resistance roll. / +3 FP extends the duration to 3 turns instead of 2 before degrading.

BODY. A slow clenching of one hand — the Force wrings the target's nerves. One hand, need not be empty.

You close your fist slowly. The target's face goes tight — a gasp cut short. The pain is internal and sourceless.

Extended Exposure: a body taught pain remembers it. Repeated use on the SAME individual builds a cumulative −1 to their resistance (max −4) as their nerves learn to anticipate the agony; eventually the mere intent, telegraphed, can stagger them before the power fully lands.

High Pain Threshold halves penalties. Maximum −6 total from stacking.

Force Choke

1d+1 crushing to the throat per turn (the dark closes hard).
AffinitySTActionConcentrate (1 turn), then maintainedRange12 hexes, unbroken line-of-sightDefenceResisted by ST, re-rolled each turn at +1; never dodgedFP3/turnReactionNo

Effect. 1d+1 crushing to the throat per turn (the dark closes hard). Target cannot speak or breathe — suffocating (HT each turn or lose 1d HP). Armoured gorget or sealed helmet: DR applies to damage, not suffocation. Cumulative +1 to ST resistance each turn. Stackable. / Scalable: +3 FP upfront applies −2 to the target's initial ST resistance. / +2 FP/turn raises damage to 2d per turn.

GRIP & BODY. The Force closes around the target's throat specifically — a fine, localized envelopment, which is why it throttles rather than crushes wholesale. A free hand makes a slow clutching or pinching gesture; clenching tighter constricts harder. One free hand required; two-finger saber-hand variant at skill 20+.

You raise one hand, fingers half-curled. The target's hands fly to their throat. Their face reddens. Their feet may leave the ground.

Wrath Meditation (30 min): double the reach and the squeeze — lift and throttle at range, against a tougher target, for longer. (+1 Corruption on completion.) [PREPARED — meditate beforehand to charge it, then release in a normal action; the charge holds until used or your next rest, and resolves on its own once loosed.]

Requires completely unbroken LoS — any cover risks dropping the choke. Cannot use other concentration powers simultaneously.

Force Affliction

Target takes 1 fatigue damage per turn for 10 turns automatically.
AffinityWillActionAttackRange8 hexes, line-of-sight (none needed once it fires)DefenceResisted by HT; droids immune; cleansable by Light powers; never dodgedFP4ReactionNo

Effect. Target takes 1 fatigue damage per turn for 10 turns automatically. No ongoing LoS required. Force-using targets can cleanse with a T2+ Light power or Force Focus (Will vs original Force skill). Droids are immune. Stackable. / Scalable: +3 FP applies −2 to the target's HT resistance roll. / +4 FP increases damage to 2 fatigue per turn instead of 1.

BODY. A pointing gesture of one hand sets the sickness; need not be empty.

You point one finger at the target and exhale a syllable under your breath. A tendril of dark energy — visible only as a brief shadow — enters their body and vanishes.

Extended Exposure: the sickness takes deeper root with each visitation. Re-afflicting the SAME individual before they have fully recovered stacks the damage and applies a cumulative −1 to their HT resistance (max −4) — the body, never given time to mend, fails faster each time.

No ongoing LoS needed once fired. Cleansable by Light-side powers.

Drain Force

Siphon 2d FP from a Force-sensitive target.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)Range8 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceResisted by Will (or Force skill if higher); only bites Force-sensitives; never dodgedFP2 (recouped on success)ReactionNo

Effect. Siphon 2d FP from a Force-sensitive target. You recover half the FP drained (rounded up). Mundane targets are not affected — the power finds no Force to drain. Scalable: +3 FP for +1d drain (max 4d at 8 FP).

You reach toward them through the Force. Their connection visibly thins for an instant — they breathe wrong, they stumble. Your own connection brightens.

Failed contest: the target knows. Critical failure: drain backfires, you lose 2d FP instead. Force-users resist with Force skill if higher than Will.

Wound

Cause a sourceless wound to open in the target.
AffinityWillActionAttackRange6 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceResisted by HT; no cortosis; never dodgedFP4ReactionNo

Effect. Cause a sourceless wound to open in the target. 2d burn damage immediately, plus 1 HP/turn bleed for 3 turns (HT each turn to staunch, or any First Aid maneuver staunches automatically). Stackable on the same target.

A simple gesture — closing your fist. A wound opens in their flesh where nothing struck them. Their face goes from confusion to pain.

Cannot wound through cortosis-weave. Heavy Dark Side compulsion on use. The wound is identifiably Force-derived to medics.

Force Scream

Release a scream that cracks reality.
AffinityHTActionConcentrate (1 turn)Range5-hex radius (no LoS needed)DefenceResisted by HT (sonic; cover & hearing protection halve it); hits alliesFP6ReactionNo

Effect. Release a scream that cracks reality. All within 5-hex radius take 2d fatigue damage and HT or be Stunned 1 turn. Brittle materials shatter. Cover halves damage. Scalable: +3 FP per +1d; +3 FP extends radius +2 hexes.

BODY. Torn from the VOICE — a scream amplified into a weapon. Requires vocalising; the mouth must be unobstructed. Hands free.

Your mouth opens and what comes out is not human. A scream made of grief and rage, amplified into a weapon. Air ripples outward from you in visible waves.

Affects allies. Hearing-protection halves damage. Identifiable as Sith technique to those who have heard it. Heavy Dark Side compulsion.

III · MASTER

Master

Complex techniques of high focus. Skill bonus: +3. Average cost 5−16 FP once scaled. Force Lightning and Force Plague do the heavy lifting here — both have significant scaling options that change how they interact with the battlefield.

✦ Dark Side Powers

Force Lightning

1d burning per FP. Targets in the open must succeed on two defense rolls or take full damage.
AffinityWillActionAttack, or maintainedRange6 hexes, line-of-sight (cover blocks it)DefenceTwo defence rolls in the open — Dodge; Tutaminis (bare hand); or a Lightsaber Parry, where a successful saber-parry forms a Lightning Lock (Doc I §Saber Lock); full cover = immune; metal armour −2 to defendFP1 per 1dReactionNo

Effect. 1d burning per FP. Targets in the open must succeed on two defense rolls or take full damage. Full cover: immune. Wet targets: +1d. Metallic armour: −2 to defense rolls. Insulated: +1 to defense rolls. Sustained version: any hit on you forces a Will roll or the chain breaks. / Scalable: +4 FP increases damage by +1d for the entire casting or that turn of sustained use (the dark gives generously to those who pay its price). / +3 FP allows the bolt to arc to one additional target within 1 hex of the primary, dealing −1d to the secondary; this may chain repeatedly, each arc −1d from the last.

BODY. Channelled through ONE or TWO free hands — fingers spread, energy leaping from the fingertips. The hands MUST be empty; nothing may be held (a lightsaber, a blaster, even a gripped object blocks the discharge). Two hands doubles output but commits both. At Force skill 22+, a single charged fingertip can arc from the saber hand without dropping the blade, at half output.

Dark violet-purple lightning erupts from your outstretched hands — jagged, hungry arcs that crack and split the air with an electric roar. The light it casts is wrong: cold and flickering.

Wrath Meditation (30 min): double the damage dice OR the number of arcing targets; a meditated storm is the difference between hurting one foe and dropping a line of them. (Heightened corruption risk — meditating on the dark deepens its hold: +1 Corruption on completion.) [PREPARED — meditate beforehand to charge it, then release in a normal action; the charge holds until used or your next rest, and resolves on its own once loosed.]

Wet targets +1d. Metal armour −2 to defenses. Full cover = immune.

Force Pressure

Reduce target's maximum movement by 1 hex per FP.
AffinitySTActionConcentrate (1 turn), then maintainedRange6 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceResisted by ST, re-rolled each turn at +1; never dodgedFP1 per −1 hex of Move, per turnReactionNo

Effect. Reduce target's maximum movement by 1 hex per FP. Target re-rolls ST each turn with cumulative +1. Cannot reduce Move below 0. SM+2 or larger add +2 to initial ST. / Scalable: +3 FP applies −2 to the target's initial ST resistance roll. / +2 FP/turn adds −1 to target's DX on top of the movement reduction (the pressure disrupts coordination).

GRIP & BODY. A slow downward press of an open palm — the Force bears down on the whole target from above like a rising gravity, not a localized grab. Envelopment mode: weight ceiling is half the shove gates (Grip-class holding). One free hand.

You extend a hand toward the target, palm facing down and pressing slowly. They strain against invisible weight — muscles working against something that isn't there.

Losing LoS ends it. Cumulative +1 to resistance each turn.

Force Dominance

Overwhelm the target's mind. On failure: they lose their next action entirely.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)Range8 hexes — eye contactDefenceResisted by Will or Mind Block; never dodged; once per 10 min per targetFP6ReactionNo

Effect. Overwhelm the target's mind. On failure: they lose their next action entirely. On a tie: they lose their next free action (can still defend but cannot initiate). Once per 10 minutes per target. / Scalable: +3 FP applies −2 to the target's Will resistance roll. / +4 FP upgrades the effect — on a failure, the target loses their next 2 actions instead of 1.

BODY. Projected through the eyes and an extended open hand, palm toward the target — the will reaches out along the line of the gesture. The hand need not be empty of the Force, but a pointing or open-palm gesture focuses it; without the gesture, the resistance penalty worsens by 2.

Your gaze sharpens to a point. For one heartbeat, the Force wraps around their mind like a fist. The target simply — stops. Mid-word, mid-step.

Extended Exposure: domination is reinforced by repetition. Each successful imposition on the SAME individual makes the next easier (cumulative −1, max −4) and lengthens how long they remain pliant — a will bent often enough begins to stay bent.

Once per 10 minutes per target. Targets who critically succeed gain +3 to future Will rolls against you for the rest of the session.

Drain Life

Deal 2d damage bypassing armour.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)Range4 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceResisted by HT; never dodged; no droids or Force-deadFP5ReactionNo

Effect. Deal 2d damage bypassing armour. You heal HP equal to half the damage dealt AND recover FP equal to the other half — a strike that fuels you even as it wounds them. Cannot be used on droids or Force-dead. Dark-side pressure if used on non-combatants. / Scalable: +4 FP increases healing to full damage dealt as HP (FP recovery stays at half). / +4 FP (separate) applies −2 to the target's HT resistance roll. / +3 FP (separate) lets you split the drain across two adjacent targets.

BODY. An extended free hand, fingers curled toward the target — the Force draws the life across the gap to the palm. One free hand; closer range without it.

You reach toward the target, palm open. A thin dark ribbon of reddish energy pulls from their body and flows to your palm. The target looks immediately, visibly, older.

Wrath Meditation (30 min): double the HP drained and the fraction you reclaim; a meditated drain can leave a victim a husk and restore you to full. (+1 Corruption on completion.) [PREPARED — meditate beforehand to charge it, then release in a normal action; the charge holds until used or your next rest, and resolves on its own once loosed.]

Cannot target droids or Force-dead. Dark-side pressure if used on non-combatants.

Force Plague

All targets within 6 hexes who fail HT take −4 to all attributes for 5 turns.
AffinityWillActionAttackRange6-hex radius (spreads by contact; no LoS after firing)DefenceEach target resists with HT; droids immune; hits alliesFP6ReactionNo

Effect. All targets within 6 hexes who fail HT take −4 to all attributes for 5 turns. No LoS needed after firing. Affects allies. HT 13+ targets may shake it off early (HT−2 each turn from turn 3). Droids immune. / Scalable: +4 FP applies −2 to all targets' HT resistance rolls. / +4 FP extends the radius to 8 hexes.

You exhale and a barely-visible dark mist expands outward — more felt than seen. Targets within it feel their extremities go heavy and cold, like illness arriving all at once.

Affects allies in range. Droids immune.

Drain Knowledge

Steal a specific memory, skill-fact, or piece of information from the target's mind.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (3 turns) — sustained gazeRange2 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceResisted by Will or Mind Block; never dodgedFP10ReactionNo

Effect. Steal a specific memory, skill-fact, or piece of information from the target's mind. You gain temporary access (1 hour) to one skill at the target's level, OR one concrete memory in full detail. The target loses access to that knowledge for the same duration. They may or may not realise something has been taken.

You hold their gaze through three slow seconds. A bright thread crosses between your eyes and theirs. When it ends, what they knew, you know — and they look briefly blank, as if they cannot remember what they were about to say.

Critical failure: you absorb their worst trauma into your own mind as an intrusive vision. Repeated use of this power leaves permanent psychological damage (GM tracks). Heavy Dark Side compulsion.

Insanity

Inflict screaming terror on all targets in a 4-hex radius.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)Range4-hex radiusDefenceEach resists with Will or Mind Block; Fearless immune; hits allies; never dodgedFP10ReactionNo

Effect. Inflict screaming terror on all targets in a 4-hex radius. Each must roll Will or be reduced to gibbering panic for 1 minute — flees, attacks random targets, drops weapons (GM determines per target). Will 14+: +2 to resist. Fearless: immune. Scalable: +5 FP applies −2 to all resistance rolls.

You spread your arms and project nothing. The space between you and the targets fills with something visible to them and them alone. Some collapse. Some attack their friends.

Affects allies unless Fearless or Will save. Heavy Dark Side compulsion. Critical failure: you suffer the effect yourself for 1 turn.

Force Corruption

Roll your Force attribute; the target resists HT.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)Range8 hexesDefenceResisted by HT each turn (a success ends it early); bypasses all DR; never dodgedFP8ReactionNo

Effect. Roll your Force attribute; the target resists HT. Rot blooms from within: 1d toxic injury per turn for 3 turns, bypassing all DR. A new HT success on any turn ends it early.

BODY. A clawing gesture toward the target and one hissed, rotten word; the gesture-and-word seeds the rot, which then needs no upkeep.

You do not strike them — you tell the body to fail.

Rots from within — DR cannot stop it; only the target's own HT throws it off.

IV · GRANDMASTER

Grandmaster

Pinnacle abilities. Near once-per-fight at Knight level. Several powers at this tier have permanent consequences — Force Storm (Barrage), Force Rend, Force Horror, Death Field. Scaling amplifies those consequences. Use with intent.

✦ Dark Side Powers

Force Rend

Target takes 5d internal damage bypassing armour.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)Range8 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceResisted by HT (failure = permanent 1d3 HT loss); bypasses armour, never dodgedFP10ReactionNo

Effect. Target takes 5d internal damage bypassing armour. A failed HT roll causes permanent HT loss of 1d3 and a dark-side taint. HT loss is irreversible without Light-side ritual intervention. Force-dead targets take damage but no HT loss. / Scalable: +4 FP applies −2 to the target's HT resistance roll. / +5 FP additionally cripples a chosen limb — target makes an additional HT roll or the designated arm or leg is crippled for 1d days.

GRIP & BODY. Both hands seize and then wrench apart — the Force grips two points of a single object or body and tears them in opposite directions. Requires TWO free hands (the tearing is bilateral); cannot be done one-handed or with the saber hand. Two-hand envelopment by definition (the bilateral tear), so it uses the two-hands-plus-focus gate — 8×BL halved for envelopment, ~4×BL.

You drive one hand forward in a tearing motion. Nothing exits the body. But the target drops as if their structure has been compromised from within.

Permanent HT loss on failed HT check. Irreversible without Light-side intervention.

Force Storm (Barrage)

Sustained electrical storm dealing 3d burning damage to all in a 6-hex radius each turn.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn), then maintainedRange6-hex radius (no LoS needed)DefenceEach target resists with HT each turn; hits allies; never dodgedFP5/turnReactionNo

Effect. Sustained electrical storm dealing 3d burning damage to all in a 6-hex radius each turn. Cannot distinguish allies from enemies. Wet environments or metallic armour: +1d to all targets. Each turn you maintain, make a Will roll at cumulative −1 or begin to feel the dark-side pull. / Scalable: +5 FP/turn increases damage by +1d (4d at +5 FP/turn, 5d at +10 FP/turn). / +5 FP/turn extends the radius by 2 hexes (8-hex at +5, 10-hex at +10). / Both options can be combined — pay both costs per turn.

The sky above you darkens unnaturally, crackling with dark energy you feed continuously. Lightning hammers down across the radius — not targeted, not precise, just relentless.

Affects allies. Will rolls each turn at cumulative −1.

Death Field

Siphon life-energy in a 4-hex radius.
AffinityWillActionAttackRange4-hex radius (no LoS needed)DefenceEach living target resists with HT; droids immune; hits allies; never dodgedFP10ReactionNo

Effect. Siphon life-energy in a 4-hex radius. All living targets take 3d damage. You heal HP equal to 60% of total damage dealt across all targets — in a crowd, a single casting can restore you completely. Cannot distinguish allies. Droids immune. Each use increases dark-side compulsion Will penalty by 1. / Scalable: +5 FP extends the radius to 6 hexes. / +5 FP (separate) raises healing efficiency to 80% of total damage dealt.

GRIP & BODY. Arms spread from the body — the Force reaches out through the whole radius and draws life inward to the caster. Both hands spread; area-mode reach.

You spread both arms wide and tilt your head back. A pulse of dark reddish-black energy erupts outward from your entire body simultaneously — and then draws back, pulling something with it.

Affects allies. Droids immune. Dark-side pressure accumulates with each use.

Deadly Sight

Your sustained gaze inflicts 1d mental/internal damage per turn to each target within LoS.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn), then maintained (you can't move)Range10 hexes — sustained gaze, line-of-sight per targetDefenceResisted by HT each turn; breaking LoS stops it; never dodgedFP6/turnReactionNo

Effect. Your sustained gaze inflicts 1d mental/internal damage per turn to each target within LoS. Targets make HT rolls or develop internal injuries. Breaking LoS stops their damage. Cannot move or take other actions while maintaining. / Scalable: +3 FP/turn increases damage to 1d+2 per turn for all current targets. / +3 FP/turn allows you to affect one additional target simultaneously (each subsequent target costs +3 FP/turn on top of all other costs).

BODY. Channelled through the EYES, not the hands — a sustained hostile gaze fixed on the target. Hands are free to fight; breaking line of sight or closing the eyes ends it. The cost is paid in the strain of the stare.

The whites of your eyes darken slightly at the edges. The target feels it as pressure — a gaze that has weight, that enters the skull and takes root.

Cannot move or act while maintaining. Breaking LoS with a target stops their damage.

Force Horror

Project a vision of absolute terror.
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (1 turn)Range8 hexes — eye contactDefenceResisted by Will or Mind Block (margin sets severity; 4+ permanent); Force-dead immune; never dodgedFP8ReactionNo

Effect. Project a vision of absolute terror. Target fails Will: gains a permanent phobia, night terror, or hysterical condition. Inflicts immediate Shock and Hysterical response. Margin 1−3: severe temporary trauma (1d days). Margin 4+: permanent. Will 14+ add +3. Force-dead immune. / Scalable: +4 FP applies −2 to the target's Will resistance roll. / +4 FP extends the effect to all targets within 2 hexes who can see the primary target — witnessing someone else's terror is contagious (secondary targets resist at Will+2).

BODY. Through the EYES — sustained eye contact carries the vision. Hands free; breaking gaze ends the casting.

You reach toward their face without touching it, expression blank. The target's eyes go wide. Their face cycles through expressions of extreme terror — seeing something only they can see — before they collapse or bolt.

Extended Exposure: a mind shown horror again and again frays. Repeated use on the SAME individual deepens the permanent disadvantage gained and adds a cumulative −1 to resist (max −4); sustained exposure can break a sanity that a single vision only cracked.

Permanent on margin of failure 4+. Irreversible without treatment.

Force Crush

Lift one target into the air via Force-grip and crush them inward.
AffinitySTActionConcentrate (1 turn)Range8 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceResisted by ST, re-rolled each turn at +1; no cortosis; never dodgedFP12ReactionNo

Effect. Lift one target into the air via Force-grip and crush them inward. Target takes 3d crushing immediately and 2d each subsequent turn (2 turns total). They are immobilised — cannot act except attempt Break Free at ST −3. Cannot exceed SM +2. Scalable: +4 FP applies −2 to ST contest.

You raise one hand, fingers curling into a closed fist. The target lifts from the ground, screaming. Their armour compresses inward audibly. Bones snap. They cannot breathe.

Cumulative +1 to target's ST resistance each turn. Cannot crush through cortosis. 1 Corruption per use, automatic. Critical failure: the inversion strikes you (3d crush self).

Memory Walk

Force the target to relive a traumatic memory of your choice (if you know one) or their worst memory (if you do not).
AffinityWillActionConcentrate (3 turns) — eye contact throughoutRange8 hexes, line-of-sightDefenceResisted by Will; never dodged; helpless for the durationFP12ReactionNo

Effect. Force the target to relive a traumatic memory of your choice (if you know one) or their worst memory (if you do not). They are paralysed for the duration, mentally trapped — treat as helpless. You may extract one true fact from their reliving (GM determined). They wake knowing what you did.

You hold their gaze. They cease to see the room. Their face cycles through expressions of remembered pain. You stand at the edge of their dream and read what they cannot stop seeing.

Heavy Dark Side compulsion. The memory you forced them to relive may now haunt you too (1d nightmares). Cannot be used on Force-dead or those without significant trauma.

V · LEGEND

Legend

Mythic. Training: Special / Lifetime. History-defining events, not tactical options. Force Storm, Thought Bomb, and Transfer Essence are dark-side exclusives. Scaling these powers amplifies already-world-altering effects — the cost is always proportionate to the consequence.

✦ Dark Side — Legendary

Force Storm

Tear a hole in hyperspace to consume entire fleets.
AffinityWillActionRitual (hours)RangeRegional — affects hyperspace itself, no LoSDefenceNo personal defence; only an opposing ritual of equal power or the caster's death ends itFP50+ReactionNo

Effect. Tear a hole in hyperspace to consume entire fleets. Every participant permanently loses 1d HT. The Force wound is perceptible across a sector. / Scalable: every additional 20 FP increases the storm's radius by one order of magnitude. / The HT loss to participants scales with extra power (+1 permanent HT per 20 extra FP per participant).

The sky tears. Hyperspace bleeds through the wound — a purple-black maelstrom visible from orbit, consuming everything drawn toward it.

Irreversible without opposing ritual or caster death. All participants lose 1d HT permanently regardless of outcome.

Transfer Essence

Transfer your soul into a new vessel at the moment of death.
AffinityWillActionReaction — cast at the moment of your deathRangeTouch / near-touch (the vessel)DefenceThe vessel resists with Will; ties go to the vessel; failure destroys your essenceFPall remainingReactionYes — uses your Reaction

Effect. Transfer your soul into a new vessel at the moment of death. On failure, your essence is destroyed. The new vessel loses most Force skills for 1 month while adjusting. The original soul is displaced. / The original soul is displaced. / Scalable: spend up to 20 extra FP before death to apply −2 per 5 FP to the vessel's Will resistance roll — the more power behind it, the harder it is for the vessel to reject you.

BODY. Both hands and the full focus of the self — a ritual of touch or near-touch on the vessel. Two free hands.

As your body fails, a wash of pale luminescence pours from you — arcing into the target vessel. The body left behind goes cold instantly.

Dark-side act. On failure, your essence is destroyed — there is no second attempt.

Thought Bomb

Total annihilation of all Force-sensitives in radius.
AffinityWillActionRitual (cooperative — min 3 casters)RangePlanetary — radius by power spent, no LoSDefenceNone — no resistance, no escape; every Force-sensitive in radius (the casters included) is annihilatedFP100+ReactionNo

Effect. Total annihilation of all Force-sensitives in radius. Destroys the casters too. There is no escape clause. Minimum 3 participants, all destroyed.

A sphere of absolute dark expands from the casting point — silent, not explosive. Inside it, every Force-sensitive is simply gone. The sphere collapses back on itself.

No survivors. No exceptions. The casters are destroyed alongside every Force-sensitive in the radius.

Dark Transfer

Resurrect a recently dead target.
AffinityWillActionRitual (the target dead under 1 hour)RangeTouchDefenceA contest against death itself (GM sets the difficulty by cause of death)FP60+ReactionNo

Effect. Resurrect a recently dead target. You permanently lose 2d HP. All injuries healed but the target carries a permanent dark-side taint and a psychological scar. The caster is never entirely the same. / Scalable: +10 FP reduces the caster's permanent HP loss from 2d to 1d (the ritual is controlled more carefully, at greater power cost). / +10 FP (separate option) extends the target death window from 1 hour to 6 hours — reaching someone longer gone.

BODY. Both hands on the corpse or dying body — a ritual touch. Two free hands.

You press both hands to the body and pour yourself into the act — your own vitality visibly dimming as the dead one draws breath. The resurrection looks wrong: too sudden, colour returning like paint applied from the inside.

Caster permanently loses 2d HP. Target gains dark-side taint and psychological scar.

Ravage

Channel concentrated dark-side energy into a sustained 10-hex line attack.
AffinityWillActionRitual (1 hr prep), then 3 turns of outputRange10-hex line (fixed once committed)DefenceEach target in the line resists with HT each turn; hits allies; never dodgedFP30ReactionNo

Effect. Channel concentrated dark-side energy into a sustained 10-hex line attack. Each turn for 3 turns, all in the line take 5d burning (lightning), plus 1d unhealable wounds (treat as max-HP reduction until 1 month of full rest). Cannot turn the line once committed — it follows the original trajectory only.

You point. Lightning braids itself into a sustained beam — not a strike but a river — and burns a path through anything in front of you. The line glows for hours after.

Affects allies in line. 1 Corruption per turn (3 total automatic). Use in inhabited areas: +1d Corruption and ongoing Force disturbance for 1d months. The path remains as a visible Force scar for years.

Sith Sorcery

Establish a Sith illusion across a 1-km region.
AffinityWillActionRitual (3 hrs at a Dark Nexus, or 1 hr with Sith artefacts)Range1-km illusion region, no LoSDefenceEach visitor resists with Will (a critical success sees through); never dodgedFP25ReactionNo

Effect. Establish a Sith illusion across a 1-km region. Terrain looks wrong, paths lead nowhere, voices come from the wrong direction. Each visitor rolls Will to perceive truth; failure means they wander the illusion until rescued or until they roll critical success on subsequent Will rolls (one per hour). Cumulative +1 Will resistance per subsequent visit.

You inscribe runes in the air, in blood, in stone. The land itself reshapes its appearance to those who enter. They will spend hours believing they are walking forward when they are circling.

Permanent until ritually broken or the caster dies. Detectable as a dark presence by any Force-user within 10 km. 2 Corruption on completion. Cannot affect Force-dead or beings of equal Force skill on critical Will success.

BOOK III·5

MINOR TRADITIONS

The Smaller Books
Iktotchi foresight, Baran Do sky-craft, and Zeltron empathy — the small species and regional disciplines, gathered into one conglomerate Book.
TIERS I–V · DABBLER TO KEEPER OF THE SMALL WAYS

Minor Traditions — The Smaller Books

Minor Traditions — The Smaller Books

Not every discipline is a great Order — species gifts and bloodline arts, gathered into one Book.

Alignment req.Grey (light-lean) — honest foresight, sky-craft and empathy serve no side; only Zeltron coercion pulls toward the dark.
Force skillEach sub-tradition rolls its own — Per, Will, or IQ, given on every ability.
TiersI–V, gated by tier like any Book. Enable the Book and all sub-traditions open at once.
Capstone & CrossingNone of its own — each sub-tradition’s Tier V ability is its capstone; you climb by tier and points, not a crossing deed.
To buyAbilities bought individually by tier — 4 / 8 / 14 / 20 / 28 (T1–T5).

Reading these. The smaller arts are written compactly: each lists its attribute · FP · tier, with range, timing and any reaction noted in the text. Most are personal abilities — foresight and empathy worked on yourself — and several can be used reactively; the description says which.

Not every Force discipline is a great Order with a cathedral and a thousand years of doctrine. Scattered across the galaxy are smaller traditions — species gifts, regional schools, a handful of techniques passed down a single bloodline. Minor Traditions gathers these into a single conglomerate Book: enable it and you have access to all of its sub-traditions at once, gated by tier like any Book. It is Grey (light-lean) — honest use of foresight, sky-craft, and empathy serves no side, though Zeltron coercion can pull a user toward the dark. The three founding sub-traditions below are a starting set; a GM can fold further small disciplines into the same Book.

Iktotchi Premonition — Foresight

The horned Iktotchi are born seeing a heartbeat ahead. Powers roll against Per unless noted. A reading that fails by 5+ is a false vision — the GM may feed misleading information.

Sense of the Moment. SW Per · 1 FP · T1. Passive when active. You are never surprised and never flat-footed; add +1 to Initiative (+2 with margin 5+). You may act normally in the first turn of any ambush.

Flash of Warning. SW Per · 2 FP · T1. The signature defensive vision. Roll as a free action at the start of any turn you expect danger, or reactively the instant you are attacked. On a success you foresee the incoming attacks and halve the penalty (round in your favour) on your active defenses against them this turn — this turns surprise, darkness, attacks from the flank/rear, and Deceptive Attack penalties from full into half. The number of separate attacks you can so defend equals 1 + (margin ÷ 3): margin 0–2 covers one attack, 3–5 covers two, 6–8 covers three, and so on. On a margin of 10+ those defenses ignore the penalty entirely. Crit success: also grants a single free Retreat that protects against all of them.

Read the Currents. SW Per · 3 FP · T2. Short-term precognition held for up to (margin) turns, minimum 1. While it lasts you gain +1 to all active defenses, rising to +2 at margin 5+ and +3 at margin 10+, and you may take a Retreat against ranged attacks as if they were melee. Stacks with Flash of Warning.

Visions of the Branch. SW IQ · 5 FP · T3. One minute of meditation yields a vision of the most likely outcome of a chosen course of action within the next day. The GM answers one yes/no or short question truthfully; each 2 full points of margin earns one additional question or a layer of detail. Usable once per question per scene.

Premonition of the Fixed Point. SW IQ · 8 FP · T4. You glimpse a major event that will come to pass — a death, a betrayal, a disaster — though not always its exact time or cause. Once per session, when that foreseen event begins, you and all allies who can act gain +2 to all rolls for one turn (you saw it coming). Margin 5+: the bonus lasts two turns, or extends to a second related event.

Walk the Unwritten. SW Will · 10 FP · T5. The capstone. Once per session you may re-roll any single roll — yours or one you witnessed — after seeing the result, as though you had stepped back and chosen the better branch. On a success the new roll stands; margin 5+ lets you take the better of the two rolls automatically. A crit failure locks the original (the future would not be denied).

Baran Do Sages — Sky-Craft

The Kel Dor sages read and shape the air. Powers roll against Will unless noted; outdoor or open-air use is at +2, sealed interiors at −3 (there is little air to command).

Breath of the Sage. SW HT · 1 FP · T1. You read the currents of air, temperature, and pressure around you. Gain +2 Per outdoors (+3 at margin 5+) for one scene, detect approaching weather and unseen movement that disturbs the air, and ignore wind penalties to your own ranged attacks.

Whisper on the Wind. SW Per · 2 FP · T1. The air carries sound to you, or your words away. Eavesdrop on a conversation up to (margin × 10) metres away through open air, or send a short whispered message the same distance to one chosen listener. Walls, vacuum, and sealed rooms block it.

Call the Squall. SW Will · 4 FP · T2. You stir wind and dust across a (margin × 2)-metre radius for (margin) turns. Everyone inside (you may exclude yourself) suffers −2 to ranged attacks and Per/Vision rolls, −3 at margin 5+, and the area counts as light cover/concealment. Loose objects and small drones may be knocked about.

Storm-Sending. SW Will · 6 FP · T3. Over one minute you summon a localized storm — rain, wind, dust, or static — across roughly (margin × 20) metres, lasting (margin) minutes. Imposes −4 to ranged attacks and movement-based skills within, may force HT rolls vs the elements, and grounds light repulsorcraft on a failed Piloting roll. You and chosen allies are unaffected.

The Distant Eye. SW IQ · 6 FP · T3. Farseeing. After a minute’s meditation you view a remote place you have been to, or that lies within (margin) kilometres, for (margin) minutes. You perceive sight and sound as if present but cannot act. Familiar places are at +2; warded or Force-shrouded places resist with a Quick Contest.

Eye of the Storm. SW Will · 10 FP · T4. A grand working. You command a full tempest over (margin × 100) metres: −6 to ranged fire, hazardous movement, lightning you may loosely direct (3d–6d burn, see GM), and near-total concealment. Sustaining costs 2 FP per minute. At the storm’s heart you and your allies stand in an untouched calm.

The Sky Made Answer. SW Will · 12 FP · T5. The capstone — the gift the eldest Baran Do are remembered for. After an hour seated under open sky, you do not merely read the weather but ask it a question and are answered: choose one — (a) foresight on the wind, a clear true vision of a coming storm, fleet, or upheaval anywhere the sky touches that region, days before it arrives; or (b) the great stilling, calm an existing tempest (natural or Force-raised) across kilometres to a flat, windless hush for the scene; or (c) the long squall, raise weather of regional scale — a wall of dust to hide an army's march, a cyclone over a single valley — that persists for hours at 2 FP per ten minutes. Sealed indoors or off-world from any sky, this power simply will not answer.

Zeltron Empathic Arts — Feeling

Zeltron empathy is pheromone-aided and works only on living minds that can feel emotion (immune: droids, the will-less, those in sealed suits for the pheromone powers). Each power is a Quick Contest of your skill vs the target’s Will unless noted. Coercive use — forcing rather than nudging — is a dark-side act; flagged powers are dark-side acts.

Empathic Read. SW Per · 1 FP · T1. Read one target’s current emotional state and surface intent. Win the Quick Contest vs Will to learn their dominant emotion; margin 3+ reveals whether they are lying, margin 5+ their immediate intention. Not a dark-side act — this only listens.

Soothing Presence. SW IQ · 2 FP · T1. Calm and reassure those around you. Grants +1 (margin 5+: +2) to your Influence reactions for the scene, and lets a target re-roll a Fright Check or shake a fear/rage effect. Calming someone is not coercion.

Project Feeling. SW Will · 3 FP · T2. Push one chosen emotion (calm, doubt, affection, unease) onto a target. Quick Contest vs Will: on a win they feel it at −1 per point of margin to act against it for (margin) minutes. Forcing a strong or harmful emotion is a dark-side act.

Pheromonic Allure. SW HT · 3 FP · T2. Pheromone-aided warmth. Everyone within a few metres who can smell you reacts at +2 (margin 5+: +3), and one focused target gives a further +1. Sealed helmets and air-filters negate it. Using it to manipulate someone into acting against their interest is a dark-side act.

Wave of Dread. SW Will · 5 FP · T3. Project raw fear across a (margin × 2)-metre radius. Each target makes a Fright Check at −(margin); those who fail are shaken (−2 to all actions) for (margin) turns, and those who fail badly flee. Inflicting terror is a dark-side act, the worse for breaking innocents.

Bind the Heart. SW Will · 8 FP · T4. A grand working, and the darkest below the capstone. A sustained emotional hold over one mind: win a Quick Contest vs Will and the target treats you as a trusted intimate, resisting orders that obviously harm them at +(their Will), for (margin) hours. Sustaining costs 2 FP per hour; a fresh Will roll is allowed whenever you ask something against their nature. Binding a heart against its will is a grave dark-side act, worse if exploited.

Heart of the Multitude. SW Will · 12 FP · T5. The capstone of the empathic arts — to move not one heart but a whole room of them. With a minute's open presence among a crowd, project a single emotion onto everyone able to perceive you and feel (immune as ever: droids, the will-less, sealed suits): win a Quick Contest vs the highest Will present and the entire gathering tilts as one — a mob calmed to stillness, a wary council warmed to your word, a steady line broken by sudden dread. The swing is real but blunt (one broad feeling, not fine commands), lasts (margin) minutes, and decays as individuals notice the wrongness of feeling what they did not choose. Swaying a crowd toward fear, hatred, or against their own clear interest is a dark-side act scaling with the harm done.

BOOK VII

THE NINE

Forms
Lightsaber combat philosophies. Each Form is a complete approach to the blade — a way of thinking, breathing, and ending a fight.
FORMS I–IX · INITIATE TO BATTLE MASTER

The Nine Forms

Nine complete answers to: what is a lightsaber for?

The Nine Forms — the blade arts

Nine complete answers to one question: what is a lightsaber for? Each Form is a philosophy of motion, not just a set of strikes.

Alignment req.None — the blade arts are not Force-gated; a Jedi and a Sith can both master Soresu. Juyo is the exception: a forbidden dark variant.
UsesLightsaber skill for attacks. Each Form grants a Core Buff, carries a permanent Flaw mastery never erases, and unlocks named Techniques at its tiers.
The FormsI Shii-Cho · II Makashi · III Soresu · IV Ataru · V Shien · VI Niman · VII Juyo · VII Vaapad · VIII Jar'Kai · IX Saberstaff.
TiersInitiate · Knight · Master · Grandmaster — with Battle Master the cross-Form capstone (and Dark Warrior the terminal tier of forbidden Juyo).
To advanceA Lightsaber-skill threshold per tier (≈14/16/18/20/22+), the Form’s Flaw accepted, and a signature demonstration in real combat (full table below).

Advanced & Battle Master. Jar’Kai and Saberstaff are advanced disciplines, each requiring Grandmaster mastery of a single-blade Form first. Battle Master is the rarest title: Grandmaster in every disciplined Form, with seamless mid-combat transitions. Technique action costs are tagged inline (legend below).

Capstones & Advancement — the Saber Tracks

The blade arts are not Force-alignment-gated — a Sith and a Jedi can both master Soresu. They use a lighter capstone structure: skill thresholds + a signature demonstration, with training time per Part 2.

TrackAdvancement gateCapstone style
Saber Forms (Initiate→Battle Master)Lightsaber skill threshold per tier (e.g. 14/16/18/20/22+), plus the Form’s permanent Flaw accepted.One signature demonstration per tier — perform the tier’s named technique successfully in real combat. Battle Master requires a signature from every disciplined Form.
Form-Neutral Techniques (Saber Throw, Bind, Trakata, etc.)Learned individually; each lists its own prerequisite (Part 2).No tier ladder — each technique is a single learnable unit. Trakata is the exception: forbidden, taught by almost no one, and self-discovery is brutal (Part 2).
The Bonded Blade signatures (Torrent/Vessel/Velocity/Tempered)Keyed to your Force primary; available once the blade is bonded.The bond itself is the capstone — a single deep meditation with the completed saber. One per character, by primary attribute.

The Forms are the complete answers to the question: what is a lightsaber for? Each is a philosophy of motion as much as a fighting technique.
