Time and Economics

Build time is the longest single component. Teams in parallel can finish a ship in roughly the build time of its slowest part.

Fit/install time is sequential — one shipwright works through every part in turn. Multiple shipwrights divide this by their count (rounded up).

Refit & repair: swapping a part costs the new part's fit-hours plus 1d hours of disassembly. Repair (post-combat) is roughly fit-hours × 1/4 per quarter of damage, requiring Mechanic (or Engineer) rolls.

Quality grades: at GM discretion any part may be ordered at Fine (×2 cost, ×1.5 fit time, +1 effective tier-stat) or Masterwork (×4 cost, ×3 build time, +2). These are rare in mass production and almost always custom.

Ship Damage — Per-Part Effects

Every starship on the sheet tracks a Hull HP pool and an inventory of parts. Damage comes off the pool; parts break at integrity thresholds or under a called shot, not on every hit. Each part is ● Intact, ◐ Damaged, or ✕ Destroyed; damaged/destroyed parts apply the effects below to the vessel's stats. The sheet routes hits and computes effects automatically (the ⚔ Incoming Hit tool on each fleet card) — the tables here are the canon for the GM.

Hull Integrity — the HP Pool

Resolving a hit. ① Shields soak first — subtract from the shield SP pool until it empties (ion counts double vs shields; kinetic warheads at half vs shields). ② Hull DR reduces what's left, 1:1 — no dividing; a weapon's AP rating ignores that many points of DR (subtract AP from DR first). ③ The remainder comes off the Hull HP pool. A plain centre-of-mass hit does nothing else — it just whittles the pool. Systems break only two ways: at the integrity thresholds below, or because a gunner spent the Aim penalty to call the shot (Ship Hit Locations, Part IX) — a called shot lands its penetrating damage on the named part instead of the pool (◐ on any penetration, ✕ if it beats the part's outward DR twice over).

Integrity thresholds (the strain ladder). A wounded ship sheds systems as it comes apart — but on thresholds, not on every hit. The first time the pool falls to ½, and again at ¼, roll 3d on the Systems Strain table: one random part goes ◐ Damaged (at ¼, an already-damaged part may worsen to ✕). At ¼ the ship is wallowing — −1 to all crew rolls. This is the smoke-in-the-cockpit middle of a fight: the warning before the kill, and the window to break off, divert power, or board.

3dSystem strained → ◐3dSystem strained → ◐
3–4Weapon mount11–13Sensors
5–7Sublight engines14–15Manoeuvring (−1 Hnd)
8–10Shield generator (regen cut)16–18Reactor (fire) / Hyperdrive

Hull HP by class. Like Hnd, Move and SM, the pool is set by hull class — every catalogue ship reads its HP from here (no per-ship bookkeeping; the sheet auto-fills it). Roughly Hull DR × 5–7 — heavier hulls carry proportionally more structure behind the plate. (The Part III and Part IV class overviews now list a representative Hull HP per subclass; this table gives the definitive by-class bands.)

Vessel classHull HPFeel
Speeders, swoops, walkers15–25One vehicle-cannon hit is a real wound.
Starfighters, interceptors, bombers25–40Fragile once naked — a clean cannon hit or two ends it. Fighters live by the Dodge, not the hull.
Shuttles, light freighters, transports, gunships90–2006–10 cannon hits of buffer — the do-everything hull can take a beating.
Corvettes, patrol craft280–4205–7 turbolaser hits; fighter guns only sting exposed mounts.
Frigates & cruisers750–1,100A slugfest — bring batteries and torpedoes.
Dreadnoughts & capitals1,600–2,400Dozens of hits, coordinated volleys, or a boarding party.

How ships die. Hull HP 0 = Crippled — adrift and helpless: shields collapse, drive and weapons go dead, but the hull is intact and salvageable. Board it, capture it, or finish it (a great many prizes are taken exactly here). Hull HP below −½ its maximum = Destroyed: structural breakup or reactor breach, 1d rounds for the crew to reach the pods. A Reactor ✕ or Hull ✕ from a called shot ends a ship the same way, whatever the pool reads.

Net DR, and the old DT. All hull DR values are quoted net — the old armour-divisor baseline (÷5) is already baked in, so play is 1:1: damage − (DR − AP) off the pool. The Damage Threshold (DT) stat is retired; toughness is the Hull HP pool above. If you meet a legacy stat block with huge DR (hundreds for a fighter, thousands for a capital), divide it by 5 once and write it down.

Ship-to-Ship Defence & Scale

Vehicle Dodge (canonical). Dodge = 3 + (Pilot ÷ 3, round down) + Hnd. Roll against each incoming attack the crew can see coming. An ace (Pilot 16) in an interceptor (Hnd +2) dodges on 10; a cruiser helm (Hnd −5) effectively cannot dodge — capital defence is shields, armour and point-defence, not agility. Canonical Hnd by class: interceptor/swoop +2 · light fighter/speeder bike +1 · heavy fighter/bomber 0 · gunship/shuttle/walker −1 · freighter −2 · corvette −3 · frigate −4 · cruiser −5 (the sheet uses these defaults; individual hulls may override).

Evasive action. Declare it on your turn: +2 Dodge, −2 to your own attacks until your next turn. The default state of any sane fighter pilot under battery fire.

Size Modifier (SM). Added to the attacker's roll to hit: speeder/walker +2 · starfighter +4 · gunship/shuttle +5 · freighter +6 · corvette +8 · frigate +9 · cruiser +10. Hitting a capital ship is trivial — surviving its reply is the problem.

Capital batteries vs small craft. Turbolasers and other capital-grade batteries track slowly: −6 to hit targets of SM +5 or less (fighters, speeders, gunships). Point-defence weapons are exempt — that is their job. If a battery DOES connect with a fighter, the penetration is dozens of times its Hull HP: it is simply gone (see Vaporisation).

Point defence. Each PD mount rolls Gunnery once per turn against an inbound missile or strafing fighter at close range. Success: a missile is destroyed outright; a fighter takes a light-laser hit (6d×2 burn (4)).

Missiles & locks. Warheads need a sensor lock (Electronics Operation [Sensors], one turn). Dodging a guided warhead is at −2; countermeasures (chaff, decoys, ECM) force the warhead to re-roll its lock or give +2 to the Dodge, per system.

One-shot kills (vaporisation). If a single hit's damage, after shields and full hull DR (after AP), is ≥ the target's entire Hull HP in one blow, the vessel is destroyed outright — no strain roll, nothing to salvage. This is what a turbolaser does to a starfighter, and why fighters never let a capital battery connect. Ion damage never vaporises and never touches Hull or Armour: it counts double against shields, arcs over structure into systems, and leaves the hull intact — the boarder's and bounty hunter's tool.

Cross-scale fire (the only time AD appears). A ship-scale weapon striking a personal-scale target (people, droids, battle armour, SM ≤ +2) treats the target's DR as ÷5 — armour barely matters against a cannon built to crack hulls. A personal-scale weapon striking a ship resolves 1:1 but almost never reaches the vessel's Hull HP (the deliberate scale wall). Within the same scale, nobody divides anything.

Shield regeneration. Out of direct fire, shields regenerate 10% of capacity per turn (round up). Emitters ◐ Damaged: half rate; generator ✕ Destroyed: none. The sheet's ⟳ button on the shield bar applies one turn of regen.

Exposed mounts. Engines, weapon mounts and sensor arrays sit proud of the hull: they take only 40% of hull DR. This is how a fighter wing hurts a frigate it can never blow through — burn the engines off, blind it, then leave or board it.

Designer's yardstick (simulated): fighter-vs-fighter ≈ 3–4 meaningful hits · fighter wing vs freighter ≈ 3–6 · torpedo run vs corvette ≈ 6 · bomber squadron vs frigate ≈ 5 turns of concentrated runs · capital duel ≈ 4–11 volleys · fighter guns vs a frigate's hull: never (go for the exposed mounts). If your fight is dragging past these numbers, something is mis-statted.

Part TypeDamaged (◐)Destroyed (✕)
HullHull integrity compromised. −25% max HP.Hull breach. Atmosphere bleeding; −50% HP cap; all repair rolls at −4.
SublightSublight engines limp. −25% Move and −1 to Piloting evasions.Engines dead. Move 0 (drift); cannot maneuver.
HyperdriveHyperdrive misaligned. Astrogation at −4; safe jumps take double time.Hyperdrive destroyed. Cannot jump to hyperspace.
ShieldsShield emitters intermittent. Cap −50%; regen cut.Shield generator destroyed. SP 0; cannot raise shields.
ReactorReactor wounded. Weapons fire every second turn; Move −25%.Reactor offline. Weapons & Move dead. Life support failing — suit-up.
ArmourHull plating spalled. DR −25%.Armour gone. DR 0 for affected facing.
SensorsSensors degraded. −2 to all detect/scan rolls.Sensors dead. Eyeball-only; −6 detect; no auto-targeting.
AstrogationNavicomp glitching. Astrogation at −2.Navicomp destroyed. Astrogation at −8; manual plotting only.
TargetingTargeting computer wobbly. −1 to all attacks.Targeting offline. −4 attacks; no missile lock.
WeaponWeapon mount damaged. −1 to hit, RoF −25%.Weapon mount destroyed. Lose that weapon entirely.
CrewCrew quarters compromised. No long-rest recovery aboard.Crew section vented. 1d crew lost; no quarters.
CargoCargo hold damaged. −50% cargo cap; contents at risk.Cargo hold gone. No cargo capacity; contents lost.
ECMECM intermittent. Lose +Defense bonus.ECM destroyed. No jamming, no decoy launches.
Life SupportLife support degraded. 1 hour of air; CO₂ climbing.Life support failed. 5 min air; vac-suit required.

Default parts manifest. When a ship is added from the catalogue, the sheet auto-populates a sensible parts list based on the vessel category (fighters get hull/sublight/reactor/shields/sensors/targeting/life-support/navicomp/hyperdrive/main-guns; capital ships add ECM, secondary batteries, armour plating, crew decks, etc.). The list is fully editable.

Cross-Document References

For personal combat mechanics underlying ship-aboard fights, see Docs I-II. For damage mechanics (DR, wound types, critical hits) at the personal scale used when boarding actions move from ship-vs-ship to person-vs-person, see Doc III. For Force powers that apply to piloting (precognitive evasion, telekinetic ship handling, Force healing aboard ship), see Doc IV. For specific ship and vehicle stat blocks with named models, weapons, and pricing, see the Equipment Compendium (Doc VII).

Document V · Added Reference

Starship Stat Blocks — All Scales

Snubfighter to dreadnought · with the skills to crew each

Representative craft from the snubfighter to the dreadnought, each with the stats a scene needs and the skills required to crew it. Stat conventions follow this book: SM Size Modifier, Hnd/SR Handling / Stability Rating, HT Health (reliability), Move top space Move (atmospheric where noted), DR hull armour, Occ crew + passengers, Range endurance. Costs are in credits, consistent with the rest of the economy (a blaster rifle is ~2,000 cr; a scrap of beskar, a fortune). All flying of a craft is a Piloting roll (personal jetpacks and rocketpacks use Free Fall instead — they are worn, not piloted); gunnery is Gunner; the jump itself is Astrogation.

Personal & Starfighter Scale
Speeder Bike Vehicle · Personal repulsorlift
Open one-seat repulsor scout/courier — fast, fragile, lethal in a fall ≈ 4,000 cr
SM +2
Hnd/SR +2/2
HT 11
Move 120 (atmos only)
DR 8 · Occ 1 · Range 500 km
Armament — usually none (rider’s personal weapons); some mount a light blaster
Skills to crew — Driving (Speeder Bike)
Forest-moon chases. A crash at speed is a real wound, not a fender-bender.
Airspeeder Vehicle · Atmospheric flyer
Enclosed civilian/military air-car or combat speeder ≈ 25,000–90,000 cr
SM +3
Hnd/SR +1/3
HT 11
Move 260 (atmos only)
DR 12 · Occ 1–4 · Range 1,000 km
Armament — civilian: none; combat: 1–2 light blaster cannons
Skills to crew — Piloting (Airspeeder); combat models add Gunner
No hyperdrive, no space flight. The workhorse of any inhabited sky.
Snubfighter (e.g. light interceptor) Vehicle · Starfighter
Single-seat war-craft; the dogfighter of the era ≈ 150,000 cr
SM +4
Hnd/SR +1/3
HT 12
Move Move 8 space / 1,100 atmos
DR 25 · Occ 1 (+ astromech socket) · Range short; needs a tender or hyperdrive ring
Armament — 2–4 laser cannons (linked); some carry a proton-torpedo pair
Skills to crew — Piloting (Starfighter) to fly; Gunner (Beams) to shoot; Astrogation (or an astromech) to jump
Many lack an organic hyperdrive and rely on an astromech’s nav-core or a jump ring.
Heavy Fighter / Bomber Vehicle · Starfighter (assault)
Two-crew strike craft — heavier shields, ordnance bay ≈ 320,000 cr
SM +5
Hnd/SR 0/3
HT 12
Move Move 7 space / 950 atmos
DR 40 · Occ 1–2 · Range self-jump capable (short range)
Armament — 2 laser cannons + proton-torpedo / concussion-missile launchers
Skills to crew — Piloting (Starfighter); gunner uses Gunner (Beams) and Gunner (Missiles); Astrogation for the jump
A bomber wants a gunner; flying and firing the ordnance solo is at −2 each.
Transport & Light Freighter Scale
Shuttle Vehicle · Light transport
Short-haul passenger/cargo hauler, often un-armed ≈ 240,000 cr
SM +6
Hnd/SR 0/11
HT 11
Move Move 6 space / 800 atmos
DR 30 · Occ 2 crew + 6–20 · Range hyperdrive-capable, modest
Armament — often none; armed customs/patrol shuttles carry 1–2 light cannons
Skills to crew — Piloting (Space Transport); Astrogation; Spacer keeps her running well
The galaxy’s bus. Boarding-action favourite.
Light Freighter Vehicle · Space transport
The iconic tramp freighter — modular, modifiable, beloved ≈ 100,000 cr stock (heavily modified in play)
SM +6
Hnd/SR +1 (if souped-up)/11
HT 12
Move Move 6 space / 850 atmos
DR 45 · Occ 2 crew + 4–6 + cargo · Range long-range hyperdrive
Armament — stock: 1 light quad-laser turret; smugglers add concealed cannons, missiles
Skills to crew — Piloting (Space Transport) to fly; Gunner (turret); Astrogation; Mechanic (Hyperdrive) to keep the jumps clean
A turret fired from the cockpit is at −2; a dedicated gunner removes it. The most upgraded ship in any campaign.
Gunship / Dropship Vehicle · Assault transport
Armoured troop-carrier and close-support craft ≈ 500,000 cr
SM +6
Hnd/SR 0/12
HT 12
Move Move 6 space / 800 atmos
DR 70 · Occ 2–4 crew + 20–40 troops · Range hyperdrive-capable
Armament — multiple laser cannons, missile pods; door guns
Skills to crew — Piloting (Space Transport); door- and turret-gunners use Gunner; Astrogation
Built to deliver soldiers into fire and survive the trip back.
Capital Scale
Corvette Capital (light) · Patrol / escort warship
The smallest true warship — fast, lightly armed, hard-running ≈ 3,500,000 cr
SM +9
Hnd/SR 0/12
HT 12
Move Move 5 space
DR 120 · Occ 8–30 crew + troops · Range very long-range hyperdrive
Armament — multiple turbolaser and laser-cannon batteries; some carry missiles
Skills to crew — helm: Piloting (Capital Ship); fought via Shiphandling; batteries crewed by Gunner (Beams)/Gunner (Missiles); Astrogation; Electronics Operation (Sensors)
Steered by Piloting (Capital Ship); fought by Shiphandling. Needs a real crew.
Frigate / Light Cruiser Capital · Line warship
Mid-weight warship — the backbone of a fleet ≈ 25,000,000 cr
SM +11
Hnd/SR −1/13
HT 13
Move Move 4 space
DR 200 · Occ hundreds of crew · Range fleet-range hyperdrive
Armament — heavy turbolaser batteries, ion cannons, point-defense, fighter complement
Skills to crew — command: Shiphandling; helm: Piloting (Capital Ship); gunnery stations: Gunner & Artillery; Astrogation; Electronics Operation (Sensors)
No one person runs her. Capital combat is resolved at fleet scale (Doc VI).
Heavy Cruiser / Battlecruiser Capital (heavy) · Capital warship
A war-ending hammer — kilometres long, a town’s population aboard ≈ 150,000,000+ cr
SM +13
Hnd/SR −2/13
HT 13
Move Move 3 space
DR 350 · Occ thousands of crew + fighter wings + ground forces · Range strategic hyperdrive
Armament — massed turbolaser and ion batteries, heavy launchers, full fighter complement
Skills to crew — command: Shiphandling; helm crews: Piloting (Capital Ship); gun directors: Artillery & Gunner; navigation: Astrogation; sensors/EW: Electronics Operation (Sensors)
A mobile fortress. Indirect and battery fire uses Artillery; her presence decides systems.
Dreadnought / Battleship Capital (super) · Strategic warship
The largest mobile thing most beings will ever see ≈ 1,000,000,000+ cr
SM +15 to +18
Hnd/SR −3/14
HT 14
Move Move 2–3 space
DR 500+ · Occ tens of thousands · Range strategic hyperdrive
Armament — overwhelming massed batteries, heavy ion armament, vast fighter and troop complement
Skills to crew — command staff: Shiphandling; many Piloting (Capital Ship) helm crews; Artillery gun directors; Astrogation; Electronics Operation (Sensors)
Beyond personal-scale rules entirely — fought in the fleet-scale layer (Doc VI). Shown for context and for the day the players steal one.
Document VII

Equipment Compendium

Weapons · armour · gear · cybernetics · materials · the damage-scaling system

Overview

What this covers. Every weapon, armour, shield, grenade, gear item, cybernetic, material, and modification — rules and stat tables up front, full prose descriptions in the Codex behind them.

How to use it. Read Part 7.1 (the Scaling System) once — it decodes every stat line in the book — then use the navigation table to jump straight to the gear you need.

Contents

How to Navigate
This Compendium

Front matter · the catalogue · the codex · the appendices

The Shape of the Book. This compendium has four movements. The Reference (front matter) decodes every abbreviation and notation — read it first if a stat line ever puzzles you. The Catalogue (Parts I-XI) holds the rules and the stat tables for every item. The Codex describes every item in full prose. The Appendices at the back hold the at-a-glance playbook — counter-loadouts and faction kits — kept separate so the rules stay clean, the way GURPS gathers its templates at the end.

SectionContentsFind it for…

Reference

Jargon Index

Every abbreviation, notation, and piece of math used in this document — read first

Everything cryptic on a stat line, decoded — and placed at the front so you never have to hunt for it. Stat-block abbreviations, damage-type codes, the math behind armour divisors and falloff, and the campaign shorthand. If a symbol on any table in this book puzzles you, it is defined here.

Stat-Block Abbreviations

Abbr.MeaningNotes
DamageDamage dealt on a hitIn GURPS dice; see damage-type codes below.
(N)Armour DivisorDivides target DR by N before subtracting. (2) halves DR; (0.5) doubles it; (10) near-ignores.
AccAccuracyBonus to hit when you Aim (Doc II). +2 = integral scope.
1/2DHalf-Damage range (m)Beyond this range, damage is halved.
MaxMaximum range (m)The weapon cannot reach beyond this.
RoFRate of FireShots per turn. ”×9” = a cone/spread of 9 pellets. Higher RoF gives a to-hit bonus (Doc II).
ShotsCapacityShots per core/magazine. capacity(reload-seconds) where shown.
STStrength requirementMin ST to use without penalty. Below it: −1 to hit per point short, plus extra recoil.
BulkBulkinessPenalty when concealing or using in close quarters; more negative = bigger.
RclRecoilHow much follow-up shots scatter; lower is better for rapid fire.
WtWeight (kg)Mass of the item.
CostPrice (credits)Catalogue benchmark; campaign-adjustable.
LCLegality ClassSee Legality codes below.
ReachMelee reach (hexes)C = close (grappling). 1 = adjacent. 2,3 = polearm range. * = unready after attack.
ParryParry modifierBonus/penalty to Parry with the weapon. F = fencing (retreat bonus). U = unbalanced (can’t parry the turn after attacking).
DRDamage ResistanceArmour value subtracted from damage. Shown energy/kinetic when split.
+STPowered strength bonusServo-assisted armour adds this to ST.
SRShield RatingEnergy reduction from a personal energy shield (energy only).
★Master damage bonusWeapon Master / Trained by a Master: +1 per die (skill DX+1) or +2/die (DX+2+).
†Two-handed weaponRequires both hands; may double ST bonus to damage.
BBraced / bipodNeeds bracing or a bipod to fire accurately.

Damage-Type Codes

CodeTypeBehaviour
burnBurning (energy)Blaster/laser energy. Reduced by energy shields & energy-weave armour. Usually AD 2-3; the lightsaber swing is burning at AD 5.
impImpalingDeep-penetrating thrust; ×2 wound. Spears, thrusts, the lightsaber thrust (with burn).
pi-Small piercingNeedles, small darts. ×0.5 wound. Ignores energy shields (kinetic).
piPiercingStandard slug. ×1 wound. Kinetic — ignores shields.
pi+Large piercingHeavy slug, shrapnel. ×1.5 wound. Kinetic.
pi++Huge piercingAnti-materiel rounds. ×2 wound. Kinetic.
cutCuttingSlashing blades. ×1.5 wound. Kinetic — ignores shields.
crCrushingBlunt impact, concussion, most explosions. ×1 wound. Kinetic.
sonSonicCompressed sound. Bends around lightsaber Deflect; (0.5) divisor (doubled DR). Energy for shield purposes.
ionIonAnti-machine. AD 5 vs droids/vehicles/cyborgs; near-0 vs flesh.
corCorrosive / disruptorMolecular breakdown. AD 10, ×4 wound. Disruptors; acid (per turn).
toxToxicPoison. Resisted by HT; bypasses physical DR if delivered (dart, gas, contact).
fatFatigueStun/exhaustion damage to FP, not HP. Stun settings, some sonic.
affAfflictionHT-based effect (stun, blind, sleep). Written HT−N; failure inflicts the effect.
exExplosionArea effect with hex falloff (Part VI). Carries a base type (cr/burn/etc.).
fragFragmentationShrapnel follow-up, [in brackets]. pi, kinetic, ignores shields.

Math & Dice Notation

NotationMeaningWorked Example
NdRoll N six-sided dice, sum them3d = roll 3 dice (3-18, avg 10.5).
Nd+MN dice plus a flat modifier8d+8 = 8 dice +8 (16-56, avg 36).
Nd×MN dice, total multiplied by M6d×3 = roll 6 dice, ×3 (18-108, avg 63).
swSwing damage (from ST, Doc III)”sw+2” = your swing damage +2. ST 11 sw = 1d+1, so sw+2 = 1d+3.
thrThrust damage (from ST, Doc III)”thr+6” = your thrust +6. ST 11 thr = 1d+1, so thr+6 = 1d+7.
(N) divisor mathEffective DR = DR ÷ N6d(2) vs DR 18 → effective DR 9 → ~12 through.
(0.5) divisor mathEffective DR = DR × 23d(0.5) vs DR 10 → effective DR 20 → bounces.
Falloff mathDamage = centre ÷ (3 × hexes)63 centre at 2 hexes → 63 ÷ 6 ≈ 10.
Wound multiplierPenetrating damage × type multiplier10 pi+ → ×1.5 → 15 injury. Applied after DR.
HT−NRoll HT at −N to resist”HT−4 aff” = roll HT at −4 or suffer the effect.
×N recoveryHealing rate multiplierKolto tank ×6 = six times natural healing speed (Doc III).
ST×1.5Throwing range (m)Thrown weapon 1/2D range = wielder’s ST × 1.5 metres.
1 hex1 metreThe universal grid unit (Doc I).

Legality, Era & Tag Codes

CodeMeaning
— (LC)Unrestricted / mundane — freely owned (knives, clothing, tools).
LicLicensed — legal with a permit in most jurisdictions.
ResRestricted — controlled; civilian ownership limited.
MilMilitary — military-grade; illegal for most civilians.
IllegalContraband — banned in most of the galaxy (disruptors, poison gas).
RareScarcity, not legality — found, earned, or commissioned, never stocked (cortosis, templated & beskar gear — cortosis dearest of all).
SWStar Wars — setting-specific item or mechanic.
HOUSEHouse Rule — a campaign ruling supplementing or modifying GURPS RAW.
RAWRules As Written — standard GURPS 4e (Basic Set, High-Tech, Ultra-Tech).
TbaMTrained by a Master (advantage). Required to wield a lightsaber at all.
WMWeapon Master (advantage). Grants the ★ master damage bonus.
FP / HPFatigue Points / Hit Points (Doc III).
DX / IQ / HTDexterity / Intelligence / Health attributes (GURPS).
Class 1/2/3Cybernetic install tier (Part IX): chip / minor surgery / major surgery.
Part I

The Scaling
System

Armour divisors · split DR by damage type · how lightsabers, blasters & blades each defeat different armour

The problem with a naïve stat block is that a lightsaber deals 8d+8 and battle armour has DR 11 — so either the armour is useless or the saber is. Real Old Republic combat is rock-paper-scissors: energy-dispersing mesh shrugs off a blaster but parts like cloth before a vibroblade; beskar laughs at a saber’s slash and shrugs off the thrust alike, yielding only to a strike through the seams; a slugthrower ignores the energy shield that stops every blaster in the room. This is achieved with two tools borrowed from GURPS High-Tech and Ultra-Tech: the armour divisor and split DR.

Tool 1 — Armour Divisor (AD)

What It Does. An armour divisor, written (N) after damage, divides the target’s DR before subtracting it. A blaster doing 6d(2) against DR 12 treats that armour as DR 6. A weapon at (0.5) faces doubled DR (a weakness). A damage line with no parenthesis has no divisor (the old “AD 1”) and faces the target’s full DR. The divisor is how some weapons punch above their dice — and how some armour is rendered nearly irrelevant to the right attack.

The Divisor Ladder. A weapon shows a divisor in parentheses only when it is not 1 — a bare damage line (no parenthesis) faces the target’s full DR. Values in this document: (0.5) reduced penetration, armour counts double (some sonic, less-lethal); (2) standard blaster bolts, vibro vs the kinetic side; (3) heavy/military blasters and disruptor pistols; (5) ion vs droids/cyborgs, anti-materiel, and the lightsaber swing; (10) the lightsaber thrust and disruptor rifles. The higher the divisor, the more armour the attack ignores.

The Lightsaber, Properly. SW Earlier drafts gave the lightsaber “AD ∞,” which breaks the math against beskar. The campaign rule splits it into two attacks. A swing is the wide, overwhelming cut — 8d+8 burning, AD 5 (×1 wound): it obliterates ordinary armour by sheer energy (DR 20 plate becomes effective DR 4). A thrust concentrates the blade to a point — 4d+4 impaling+burning, AD 10 (×2 wound): less raw damage, but the same injury on a clean hit (18×2 = 36 = the swing’s 36) and twice the armour penetration, so the thrust is the bore-through-anything strike and the killer against vitals. Both modes shred normal armour; against true beskar’s massive DR and Damage Divisor they finally meet their match (Part X). A saber cuts through almost everything — it simply isn’t literally infinite, which lets the one legendary material resist it.

Tool 1b — Damage Divisor (DD)

What It Does. A damage divisor is the mirror of the armour divisor, and it belongs to the armour rather than the weapon. Written ÷N on an armour entry, it divides the incoming damage (after DR is subtracted) for the damage type the armour is built to resist. Where an Armour Divisor (AD) makes a weapon ignore some of the target’s DR, a Damage Divisor (DD) makes the armour shrug off part of a hit it does not fully stop.

How To Read It. AD and DD are resolved in order: first apply the weapon’s AD to the armour’s DR, subtract DR from the rolled damage, then divide whatever penetrates by the armour’s DD for that damage type. Example: Neo-Crusader plate is DR 40, ÷2 vs cutting. A vibroblade rolls 7d+6 cutting (say 30) at AD 1 against a covered location: full DR 40 stops it outright. Against a heavier cut that beats DR, only half of the penetrating damage is applied. A DD only ever applies to the damage type listed; all other types are resisted by DR alone.

Notation In This Document. Armour entries now carry both figures where relevant: AD (any armour-divisor the suit itself imposes — rare, mostly powered/Mandalorian) and DD (the damage divisor and the type it applies to, e.g. ÷2 vs cut). Most armour has neither and relies on DR alone; the columns simply read “—”. The companion character sheet shows the same AD and DD columns on every armour piece so the table and the sheet agree.

Tool 1c — Energy-Dispersing Armour (½ energy AD) SW

What It Does. Energy-dispersing armour — the fibre / mesh / matrix weave family, and reflec — doesn’t merely have high energy DR; the weave actively scatters a bolt’s coherent energy, which blunts the armour divisor of energy attacks as well as the damage. Against any energy/blaster attack, this armour halves the attacker’s armour divisor (round down, minimum AD 1) before applying it to the armour’s energy DR. Kinetic attacks are unaffected — the weave does nothing against a slug or a blade.

Why It Matters. Without this, a heavy blaster’s AD 3 would carve straight through the very armour built to stop blasters, and the rock-paper-scissors collapses (energy-weave is supposed to beat blasters). With it, the matchup holds: a heavy blaster (AD 3) facing mesh becomes AD 1 (3 ÷ 2 = 1.5, rounded down); a Mandalorian rifle (AD 2) becomes AD 1 and meets the weave’s full energy DR. So energy-weave defeats energy guns — even the heavy, divisor-carrying ones — but still folds to kinetic slugs and blades, exactly as the three-way is meant to work. The lightsaber is the deliberate exception: its huge damage overwhelms the weave even at halved AD.

Tier Parity. A higher-tier blaster can still punch through a lower-tier weave — but within any given price/role tier there is always an energy-weave suit rated to stop that tier’s blasters. Pay up, and the wall keeps pace; out-tier your opponent’s armour with a heavier gun, and you get through. The armour entries flag this with ½eAD.

Tool 2 — Split DR by Damage Type

The Notation. Armour in this document lists DR as energy / kinetic where the two differ, e.g. DR 18/4 means DR 18 against energy attacks (blasters, lasers, sonic, lightsaber-burn) and DR 4 against kinetic (slugs, shrapnel, vibroblades, fists, falls). A single number means the armour resists both equally. This single mechanic is why armour choice matters.

Why It Works. Energy-dispersing weave armour channels and scatters energy attacks — superb against blasters, useless against a knife. Plate stops everything moderately. Reflec mirrors blaster bolts almost perfectly (DR 30+ vs energy) but offers nothing against a thrown rock (DR 0). The attacker’s job is to bring the damage type the target’s armour doesn’t cover.

The Asterisk. A * after split DR (e.g. 18/6*) marks flexible armour — it suffers blunt trauma (Doc III): even when it stops a kinetic attack, some force transmits as crushing damage to the wearer. Rigid plate has no asterisk and transmits no blunt trauma.

Reading a Weapon Stat Block

Format. Name · Damage(AD) type · Acc · Range 1/2D/Max (m) · RoF · Shots · ST · Bulk · Rcl · Wt · Cost · LC. Damage uses GURPS dice (Doc III). Type: burn (energy), imp (impaling), pi-/pi/pi+/pi++ (piercing classes), cut, cr (crushing), son (sonic), ion, cor (corrosive/disruptor), tox, fat (fatigue/stun), aff (affliction).

Damage-Type Shorthand. SW burn = blaster/laser energy, usually AD 2-3. son = sonic, bends around lightsaber Deflect, partial DR-bypass. ion = anti-droid/cyborg, AD 5 vs mechanical, near-0 vs flesh. cor = disruptor molecular breakdown, AD 10, ×4 wound. imp+burn AD 10 = lightsaber.

The Master Damage-Type vs Armour Matrix

Attack Type Typical AD vs Energy-Weave Armour vs Plate / Battle Armour vs Reflec vs Refined Beskar vs Energy Shield
Blaster bolt (burn)2Poor — high energy DRFair — halved by ADUseless — DR 30+UselessReduced by SR
Heavy blaster (burn)3PoorGood — AD 3UselessPoorReduced by SR
Slugthrower / needler (pi)1Excellent — low kinetic DRFairExcellent — DR 0 kineticPoorIgnores shield
Vibroblade (cut/imp)1-2Excellent — low kinetic DRFairExcellentPoorIgnores shield
Sonic (son)1 (½ DR)FairFairGoodPoorReduced by SR; bypasses saber Deflect
Ion (vs droid/cyborg)5n/a vs fleshn/a vs fleshn/an/aReduced by SR
Lightsaber (imp+burn)10Cuts throughCuts throughCuts throughResisted (slash); thrust/slow-cut onlyEnergy portion reduced; blade cuts
Disruptor rifle (cor)10Cuts throughCuts throughCuts throughPoor (but rare enough to matter)Reduced by SR

Using the Matrix. This single table is the strategic heart of the compendium. Bring blasters against the unarmoured and the lightly-plated; bring slugs and blades against energy-weave and reflec; bring ion against droids; bring a lightsaber against almost anyone — except a Mandalorian in true beskar, against whom you had better have a thrust, a slow-cut, time, or a very large gun.

”Just That Tanky.” SW Some armour cannot be meaningfully broken by personal weapons at all. Refined beskar against a blaster (DR 55+ vs an AD-2 bolt = effective DR 27, vs 6d ≈ 21 damage) takes nothing. Heavy powered armour against a hold-out pistol, the same. The right answer against an un-defeatable target is a different weapon entirely — a disruptor, a heavy weapon, a lightsaber thrust, or simply not fighting them head-on. The compendium is honest about this: a few things are nearly impervious, and they are correspondingly rare and precious.

Ammunition, Reloads & the Blaster-Core Recharge SW

Two Power Systems. Ranged weapons in this era run on one of two systems. Blaster cores (energy cells / power packs) store charges for energy weapons — blasters, ion, sonic, disruptors, electro-melee. Ammunition magazines feed kinetic weapons — slugthrowers, needlers, rippers, scatterguns, dart guns. The two reload very differently, which matters in a firefight.

The Shots Stat. Each ranged weapon lists Shots as capacity(reload-time) where relevant — e.g. 50(3) means 50 shots per core and 3 seconds to swap. Every weapon in the catalogues now lists this explicitly — e.g. 50(3s) for a blaster (50 shots, 3-second cell swap) or 1(10s) for a missile tube. Energy weapons swap a power cell, kinetic weapons swap a magazine, and single-shot heavy weapons reload per round (shown as 2/rd).

Blaster-Core Recharge. SW Blaster cores slowly recover charge when not being fired — bleed-back from the weapon’s capacitor. A core regains +4 charges at the end of any turn in which the weapon was not fired. Stand off for a few seconds and a near-empty pistol tops itself back up; sustained fire drains faster than the trickle can replace. This rewards disciplined, aimed shooting over spray-and-pray, and means a blaster wielder is rarely truly empty — only temporarily tapped out. The recharge caps at the core’s listed capacity.

Swapping a Core. SW A wielder may instead slam in a fresh full core as a Ready maneuver (3 seconds standard; 1 second with the Fast-Draw (Ammo) skill on a success). This instantly restores full capacity — the choice between waiting out the recharge or burning a spare core is a real tactical decision. Spare cores are cheap (~10-25 cr) and light; professionals carry several. A core left to recharge in a belt pouch refills fully over about a minute out of the weapon.

Charge Time — Recharging a Drained Cell. SW A fully drained power cell or blaster core does not refill instantly. Slotted into a charging port — a ship’s power grid, a barracks rack, a portable recharger — an empty cell reaches full charge in the times below. A cell left in the weapon recovers only via the slow combat trickle (+4/turn when not fired); deliberate charging is far faster. This is why professionals carry spare cells and rotate them: one in the gun, two charging, none ever truly empty.

Cell / Core TypePowersFull Charge TimeCharged At
Standard power cellPistols, carbines, most sidearms~2 hoursAny port, recharger, or ship grid
Heavy blaster coreRifles, heavy pistols, repeaters~4 hoursShip grid or barracks rack
Weapon capacitor (in-gun)The cell left inside the weapon~1 minute (trickle, out of fight)Self — the +4/turn bleed-back
Crew/emplaced coreCannons, emplaced repeaters~8 hoursDedicated power feed only
Rapid field recharger (Part XI)Any cell, in the field½ the above timesCarried unit; draws its own charge
Tibanna-fed mains chargerAny cell, fixed installation×¼ (fastest)Armoury, capital ship, depot

A spare cell costs ~10–25 cr (Equipment), so the practical limit is how many a wielder carries, not charge time — in a long siege or a stranded crew, though, charge time and a working power source suddenly matter a great deal. Kinetic weapons have no charge time; they reload from carried magazines (the reload seconds in each weapon’s Shots stat) and are limited only by how much ammunition the wielder hauls.

Overcharge & Drain. SW Firing rapid-fire (RoF 5+) or using an overcharger (Part XI) drains multiple charges per shot and suppresses the recharge that turn. A weapon fired every turn never benefits from the +4 trickle; the recharge only helps the wielder who pauses. Heavy and military weapons (heavy blaster rifle, cannon) draw larger cores with the same +4 trickle — proportionally a smaller top-up.

Kinetic Reloads. Slugthrowers, needlers, rippers, and scatterguns reload by magazine or clip — a Ready maneuver (3 seconds; 1 second with Fast-Draw (Ammo)). No trickle-recharge: once a magazine is dry it is dry until swapped. Magazines are cheap (~20-50 cr) and the spent brass is recoverable (and forensically traceable). Belt-fed heavy weapons (heavy repeating slug, assault cannon) reload far slower (10+ seconds) and are usually crew-served or braced.

Specialty Ammunition. SW Carbonite rifles use carbonite cartridges (~50 cr); flame projectors use fuel canisters; launchers load individual rockets/grenades. These reload one unit at a time as a Ready maneuver and carry no recharge. Specialty rounds (poison darts, armour-piercing slugs, tracer) are noted on the relevant weapon.

Power SystemReload MethodReload TimeRecharge?Spare CostUsed By
Blaster core (energy cell)Swap core (Ready) or wait out recharge3s swap (1s w/ Fast-Draw)+4/turn if not fired10-25 crBlasters, ion, sonic, disruptor, electro-melee
Slug magazine / clipSwap magazine (Ready)3s (1s w/ Fast-Draw)No20-50 crSlugthrowers, autopistols, carbines
Needle / dart clipSwap clip (Ready)3sNo60 crNeedlers, rippers, dart guns
Shotgun/scatter tubeLoad shells (1-2/Ready) or swap tube1-2s per shellNo90 crScatterguns, projectile shotguns
Belt feedSwap belt/drum (slow)10s+No100+ crHeavy repeaters, assault cannon, emplaced
Cartridge / canisterLoad one unit (Ready)3sNo150 crCarbonite rifle, flame projector
Single-load munitionLoad rocket/grenade (Ready)3s per roundNoper munitionLaunchers, missile tubes

The +4/turn blaster-core recharge applies only on turns the weapon is NOT fired, and never exceeds listed capacity. A wielder always has the option to swap a full core (Ready maneuver) instead of waiting. See per-weapon Shots stats for capacity.

Part II

Melee Weapons

Vibroweapons · techblades · electrostaves · swords · exotic melee · lightsabers & crystals

Melee thrives in this era. Personal shields turn aside blaster bolts but not blades; lightsaber duelists must be met edge-to-edge. Vibroweapons — ultrasonic edges that shear armour — are the standard sidearm, carried by Sith warriors, Mandalorian commandos, Exchange enforcers. Techblades and techstaves arm the non-Force soldier. Electrostaves stun and shock. And above them all, the lightsaber and its kin. Melee damage is kinetic (cut/imp) — it ignores energy shields and bites deep into energy-dispersing armour, the great equaliser against blaster-proofed foes.

— Knives & Light Blades —

† Two-handed. * Reach varies / unready after attack. (N) = armour divisor. sw/thr scale with ST (Doc III). Vibroweapons need a power cell (negligible cost; ~20 hrs use). ★ Master damage: a wielder with Weapon Master or Trained by a Master (for the relevant skill) adds +1 per die at skill DX+1, or +2 per die at skill DX+2 or higher to that weapon’s damage (GURPS Martial Arts). The ★ line shows the master bonus at full mastery. Thrown weapons (vibro-pike, and any knife/spear thrown): range ST×1.5 m for 1/2D (GURPS thrown-weapon ruling where the weapon has a published range stat; use that instead).

Why Melee Matters — the Kinetic Advantage SW

Blades Ignore Shields. Every melee weapon here deals kinetic damage (cut/imp/cr). Personal energy shields (Part X) reduce only energy — a vibrosword passes through a shield’s SR untouched and strikes the body. Against a shield-equipped soldier who feels invulnerable to your squad’s blasters, the answer is to close and cut.

Blades Beat Energy-Weave. Energy-dispersing armour (fiber, mesh, reflec — Part VII) carries enormous energy DR and feeble kinetic DR. A vibroblade at AD 2 against mesh’s kinetic DR 4 effectively faces DR 2 — it bites deep where a blaster merely scorches. The vibroblade is the great counter to blaster-proofed foes.

The Vibro Divisor. Vibroweapons carry AD 2 because the ultrasonic edge shears through material. Plain blades (short sword, gaffi) are AD 1. This is why a vibroblade meaningfully out-penetrates a steel sword of the same size against any rigid armour.

Lightsabers & Variants SW

A lightsaber is a platform, not a fixed weapon: one hilt, a handful of component slots — crystal, grip, emitter & lens, power cell. The blades the galaxy calls “variants” are hilt configurations: one grip-slot component each, fitted to the same standard weapon (Class 2 install). Build the saber once; configure it forever.

Base weaponDamage(AD)ReachParryWtCostNotes
Standard lightsaberSw 8d+8(5) burn
Th 4d+4(10) imp+burn		1,20F0.5build*Cuts nearly all DR; cauterises (Doc III). Can Deflect blaster bolts (Doc II). Accepts every component below.
LightwhipSw 5d+5(5) burn (no thrust — flexible)1-4*−2F0.6build*Flexible plasma lash — a genuinely different weapon, not a hilt configuration. Hard to parry, hard to use. Rare Sith weapon.
Training sabersw cr (stun only)1011,200Practice tool. Equivalently: fit a Training emitter (Class 1 swap) to any saber.

Hilt Configurations — slot: grip (one per saber, Class 2 install)

ConfigurationBecomesDamage(AD)ReachParryPrereqCostNotes
Shoto hilt (compact)Shoto / short saberSw 6d+6(5) burn
Th 3d+3(10) imp+burn		10F—2,400Off-hand companion blade (Jar’Kai): +1 to dual-wield off-hand use. Easily concealed; easier in tight spaces.
Curved hiltDuelling saberSw 8d+8(5) burn
Th 4d+4(10) imp+burn		1,2+1F—1,600Makashi duelling grip: +1 Parry; −1 to retain (slips on Disarm).
Duelling foil hiltLightfoilSw 7d+7(5) burn
Th 3d+5(10) imp+burn		1+1F—4,600Elegant Tapani salon blade: −1 damage die, +1 Parry, −2 min ST. May count as a Small weapon. One-handed only.
Dual-grip hilt (saberstaff)Saberstaff / double-bladed saberSw 8d+8(5) burn
Th 4d+4(10) imp+burn (both ends)		1,20FSaberstaff form training (Doc VI)4,200Double weapon, two crystals: 2 attacks per turn at −2 each. Sith Assassins, Jedi Shadows.
Extended hilt (great saber)Great lightsaberSw 9d+9(5) burn
Th 4d+6(10) imp+burn		1,2,30FST 11+5,200Oversized two-handed blade: +1 damage die and +1 hex reach — in exchange for +2 min ST and an unwieldy hilt in confined spaces. Heavy-assault Sith.

One grip component per saber — fitting a new configuration replaces the old. Effects shown are the resulting blade; the character sheet computes them automatically when the component is socketed. Thrust profiles follow the saber convention: thrust dice = half the swing dice (8d→4d+4 · 9d→4d+6 · 7d→3d+5 · 6d→3d+3).

* Lightsabers are built, not bought: components (~2,000-6,000 cr) plus a focusing crystal, assembled by the wielder (Doc IV). F = fencing parry (retreat bonus). Each saber lists two attacks — Sw (swing, burning, AD 5) and Th (thrust, impaling+burning, AD 10, half dice but ×2 wound); see Part I & Part X. No ★ master line: unlike other melee weapons, a lightsaber requires Trained by a Master simply to wield proficiently at all (without it, see the untrained-lightsaber danger rules, Doc I) — so the damage shown already assumes a trained wielder. There is no ”untrained baseline” version of a lightsaber.

The 8d+8 Question, Answered. SW A standard lightsaber swing rolls 8d+8 (avg 36) burning at AD 5. Against DR 20 plate that’s effective DR 4 — it cuts through for ~32. A thrust rolls 4d+4 (avg 18) impaling+burning at AD 10, ×2 wound: against the same plate, effective DR 2 → 16 ×2 = 32 — equal damage, but it bores through far heavier plate where the swing would only score it. Against refined beskar DR 60, even the thrust’s AD 10 gives effective DR 6, leaking ~12 past the plate — but beskar’s Damage Divisor ÷10 then cuts that to ~1, and a weak roll to nothing. That ÷10 is what makes beskar meaningful (Part X): the saber’s answer to beskar isn’t a harder hit, it’s a thrust through a seam.

Cauterisation. Lightsaber wounds cauterise (Doc III): no bleeding, but cripple/sever is permanent. A swing is pure burning; a thrust is impaling+burning, so against a thrust both energy and impaling defenses are relevant (Part X mixed-damage rule).

Lightsaber Crystals SW

The Focusing Crystal. A lightsaber’s crystal sets blade colour and tunes performance. The primary crystal determines colour; some hilts hold secondary tuning crystals. Crystals are found, won, or grown — never simply bought. Detailed crystal-attunement and Force-bonding mechanics live in Doc IV; the gear-level effects are here.

Combat Crystals. Firkrann (+ion/energy disruption, bonus vs droids/electronics) · Phond (+1 per die damage) · Rubat (+1 weapon skill) · Jenruax (+1 Parry) · Opila (crits read one row worse for victim) · Sigil (balanced +1 attack/+1 damage, rare) · Damind (+follow-up shock) · Eralam (cracked-and-refused: +1 dmg/+1 skill) · Nextor (unstable: crits easier) · Sapith (+attack speed, extra Rapid-Strike).

Aligned Crystals. SW Solari — pure light-side, only a strongly light-aligned wielder benefits; inert/harmful to dark-siders. Sith synth-crystals (“bleeding” crystals) — dark-side-forged red crystals, power at the cost of stability, usable only by dark-siders. These carry a corruption cost (Doc IV).

Natural vs Synthetic. Natural crystals (Adegan, Ilum kyber) are Jedi-traditional and stable. Synthetic crystals are forged in furnaces (Sith method) and can be pushed to higher output. The colour and ”personality” of a blade reflect its crystal and its maker.

Crystals as components. Every lightsaber carries exactly one primary focusing crystal in its crystal slot. Swapping a crystal is a Class 2 install (workshop, hours, an Armoury or saber-construction roll — Doc IV for attunement). Crystals are never stocked: found, won, or grown. Tier marks rarity and how tightly the galaxy controls it, not a shop shelf.

Focusing Crystal Catalogue — slot: crystal (one per saber)

ComponentTierEffectsWt (kg)CostPrereqNotes
Crystal focus (saber)Civilian+1 dmg—1,800—+1 dmg OR +1 Parry — pick at activation.
Bondar crystalPolice——4,800—Concussive corona: on any critical hit the victim also checks HT−2 or is stunned.
Damind crystalPolice——5,000—Searing follow-up: targets struck suffer −1 shock penalty extra next turn.
Firkrann crystalPolice——5,200—Ion-charged lattice: +2 damage vs droids, electronics and energy shields.
Ilum kyber (standard)Police——found—The Jedi baseline. Stable, attuned, no modifiers — the blade the catalogue assumes.
Jenruax crystalPolice+1 Parry—5,400—+1 Parry — the blade moves to guard almost before you ask.
Phond crystalPolice+1 dmg per die—6,200—+1 damage per die (an 8d blade gains +8). Hot, hungry edge.
Rubat crystalPolice+1 skill—5,600—+1 weapon skill — the blade feels lighter, more present in the hand.
Dueling crystalMilitary-1 dmg · +1 Parry—5,400—+1 Parry vs sabers; -1 dmg vs droids/objects.
Eralam crystalMilitary+1 dmg · +1 skill—8,800—Cracked then re-fused; +1 dmg and +1 skill. Sought by duellists.
Nextor crystalMilitary-1 Parry—9,200—Unstable matrix: critical success threshold improves by 1 (a 5 always crits); −1 Parry.
Opila crystalMilitary+1 dmg—8,400—+1 dmg; your criticals read one row worse for the victim on the crit table.
Sapith crystalMilitary——11,000—Fluid arcs: one Rapid Strike per turn at −3/−3 instead of −6/−6.
Sigil crystalMilitary+1 dmg · +1 skill—13,500—Perfectly balanced: +1 skill, +1 dmg. Rare and prized.
Vented saber crystalMilitary+1 dmg · -1 Parry—6,600—+1 dmg; -1 Parry (unbalanced corona).
Hurrikaine crystalExotic——18,000—Phase-shifted blade: ignores energy-dispersing weave (no ½-energy AD vs this saber).
Krayt dragon pearlExotic+1 dmg · +1 skill · +1 Parry—24,000—Not a crystal at all — a pearl ground in a dragon’s gullet. +1 dmg/skill/Parry.
Sith corrupted crystalExotic+2 dmg—25,000—+2 dmg; -2 to Light-side Force rolls while wielded. Corrupts.
Solari crystalExotic+2 dmg · +1 skill—foundStrongly light-aligned wielderPure light: +2 dmg, +1 skill for a light-side champion; inert for the unaligned, −2 skill in a darkened hand.
Synth crystalExotic+1 dmg—17,000—+1d dmg; non-attuned (any Force user can wield). Sith use these.
Ultima PearlExotic+2 dmg · +1 skill—30,000—A flawless krayt pearl. +1 skill, +2 dmg. Hutts have started wars over one.
Upari crystalExotic+2 dmg—16,000—+2 dmg. One of the most powerful natural focusing gems known.
Ghost-fire crystalUniqueMode: Unseen blade—foundAdaptive & unique — it chooses wielders who are strange in the Force (GM)Pale spectral flame. Adapts to its bearer: counts as matched affinity for the wielder’s Force attribute. Toggle Unseen blade below. [Unseen blade: Blade and sound vanish utterly — invisible, silent. −2 damage dice; defenders Parry at −3 against what they cannot see or hear.]
Heart of the GuardianUnique+2 dmg · +1 Parry—foundUnique — one exists (GM)Bronze-gold blade of legend: +2 dmg, +1 Parry. It remembers every hand that held it.
Mantle of the ForceUnique+1 skill—foundUnique — one exists (GM)Cyan-white clarity: +1 skill, and Force powers channelled through the blade cost −1 FP (min 1).

Unique crystals. Unique-tier stones are singular artefacts: no price, no replica, and they tend to choose their wielders. The Ghost-fire crystal is adaptive — it counts as matched affinity for whichever Force attribute its bearer leads with, and it favours those who are strange in the Force. Its Unseen blade mode drops the blade to 6d+6 (Th 3d+3) but renders it utterly invisible and totally silent; defenders parry it at −3.

Part III

Slugthrowers &
Kinetic Ranged

Shield-defeating projectiles · needlers · rippers · scatterguns · the kinetic arsenal

Kinetic ranged weapons are the shield-breakers. Every blaster in the galaxy is stopped by a personal energy shield and scattered by energy-dispersing weave — but a slug, a dart, a flechette, a needle ignores the shield entirely and bites through energy-armour’s feeble kinetic DR. These are the weapons you bring when the enemy has invested in stopping energy. They are also quieter than the propaganda suggests, traceable by their casings, and beloved of assassins.

WeaponDamage(AD)AccRange 1/2D/MaxRoFShotsSTBulkCostLC
— Needle & Dart Weapons (defeat shields; poison delivery) —
Dart pistol1d pi- + tox130/20016(3s)7−12,400Lic
Needle rifle2d pi-4150/1,000320(3s)9−45,400Res
Needler pistol1d+1 pi-250/300310(3s)8−23,900Lic
Toxin dartgun1 pi- + tox (HT-2+)240/25011(3s)6−27,200Illegal
Wrist dart launcher1d pi- + tox120/15014(2s)—04,800Res
— Flechette & Shrapnel (defeat shields; armour-shredding) —
Flechette launcher1d+2(0.5) pi (×9 cone)225/1501×95(3s)10−45,400Res
Heavy scattergun4d(0.5) pi (cone)225/2001×98(3s)10−56,600Res
Ripper pistol2d pi+250/400310(3s)9−24,500Lic
Scattergun3d(0.5) pi (cone)220/1501×96(3s)11−44,200Lic
— Conventional Slugthrowers —
Anti-materiel slug rifle8d(2) pi++61,500/6,00015(4s)13B†−830,000Mil
Heavy repeating slug6d pi+4500/3,50012100(5s)12B†−721,000Mil
Heavy slug pistol3d-1 pi+2120/1,80038(5s)10−22,700Lic
Projectile shotgun1d+1(0.5) pi (×9)335/7002×98(5s)11−52,100Lic
Slug autopistol2d+1 pi2120/1,8001020(3s)9−23,000Res
Slug sniper rifle6d+1 pi+6800/4,500110(4s)11†−612,000Mil
Slugthrower carbine4d pi4200/2,500830(3s)9†−43,600Res
Slugthrower hold-out1d+2 pi150/70036(3s)8−11,500Lic
Slugthrower pistol2d pi2100/1,500312(3s)9−21,800Lic
Slugthrower rifle5d pi5500/3,500320(3s)10†−53,000Lic

† Two-handed. B = bipod/braced. (0.5) = cone weapons face doubled DR but spread 9 pellets. Ammunition: ~20-50 cr per reload. Kinetic weapons reload by magazine (Ready maneuver, 3s; 1s with Fast-Draw) — no recharge (Part I). Slugs & needles ignore energy shields entirely.

The Shield-Defeat Doctrine. SW Against a shielded foe, kinetic is king. The needler and ripper are purpose-built — light damage, but it lands where blasters cannot. Against energy-weave armour, the same kinetic damage faces only the armour’s tiny kinetic DR. A squad expecting shielded or weave-armoured enemies carries slug sidearms as a matter of course.

Cone Weapons. Scatterguns and flechette launchers fire a spreading cone (RoF ×9). They’re devastating at point-blank, falling off fast. The (0.5) divisor means rigid armour resists them well — but against the unarmoured or lightly-clad they shred. The close-quarters boarding weapon of choice (Doc VI).

Poison Delivery. SW Dart and needle weapons can carry contact or injected toxins (Doc III Part VIII). The GenoHaradan and Exchange favour them — a shielded, armoured target is still vulnerable to a single poisoned needle through the gaps. Tox damage is separate from the dart’s pi- and follows poison rules.

Traceability. Slugthrowers leave casings and recoverable slugs — forensic evidence blaster fire doesn’t produce. Subsonic ammunition plus a suppressor (Part XI) makes a slug pistol the quietest killing tool in the galaxy. The assassin’s paradox: louder than a blaster, but deniable.

Part IV

Blaster Weapons

Pistols · carbines · rifles · sniper · cannons · the galaxy’s standard armament

The blaster is the galaxy’s default weapon — tibanna-gas energy bolts, reliable, rechargeable, deadly. The Republic, the Empire, the corporations, the bounty hunters all run on blasters. They deal burning energy at AD 2 (military and heavy variants AD 3), which makes them excellent against the unarmoured and the lightly-plated but poor against energy-dispersing weave and useless against reflec and beskar. They are also stopped cold by a personal energy shield — the era’s great blaster weakness.

Repeating blaster (emplaced)6d+2(3) burn61,000/4,00020500(10s)—−954,000Mil Heavy Impact.

† Two-handed. B = braced/bipod. +2 Acc = integral scope. Stun setting deals fatigue (Doc III), no armour divisor. Blaster cores recharge +4 charges per turn not fired (Part I); or swap a full core as a Ready maneuver (~10-25 cr/core). ”Shots” = capacity per core.

Blaster Damage by Tier. SW The progression: hold-out 1d+2 → pistol 3d → heavy pistol 4d → carbine 4d+1 → rifle 5d → heavy rifle 6d → cannon 6d×2+. Standard bolts are AD 2; heavy and military bolts AD 3; vehicle-grade cannon AD 5. The divisor matters more than the dice: a 6d(3) heavy rifle out-penetrates a 5d×2(2) weapon against any real armour.

Against Armour. A 5d blaster rifle (avg 17-18) at AD 1 versus DR 11 battle armour = full DR 11, ~6 through — a real but survivable wound; the armour is doing its job. The same rifle versus mesh’s energy DR 30 (Part VII) = effective DR 15, nothing through. Versus a vibroblade-vulnerable reflec suit (energy DR 40) = bounces. The blaster is a generalist that the right armour shuts down completely.

Stun Settings. Most military blasters toggle to stun — a wide low-energy bolt dealing fatigue (Doc III), no divisor, knocking targets out without lasting harm. The legal default for captures. Sealed/hardened armour and nerve-reinforcement implants (Part IX) resist it.

The Shield Problem. SW Every blaster on this page is reduced by a personal energy shield’s rating (Part X), and the shield faces no armour divisor. Against a foe behind even a basic SR 50 screen, a 5d rifle (avg 17) — or a heavy blaster, or a cannon bolt — does nothing to the wearer until the shield overloads or its charges deplete. This is why no professional relies on blasters alone in an era of shields: they carry a slug sidearm or a blade, because kinetic passes straight through an energy screen.

Part V

Heavy, Exotic &
Specialist Ranged

Sonic · ion · disruptor · carbonite · flame · bowcaster · launchers · the niche arsenal

Beyond the blaster lies the specialist’s arsenal — each weapon a key cut for one lock. Sonic weapons bend around lightsabers. Ion weapons fry droids and cyborgs. Disruptors atomise through any armour. Carbonite freezes. Flame ignores cover. The bowcaster blends energy and kinetic. And the launchers reach into vehicle-scale (Doc VI). When the standard blaster fails, one of these is the answer.

WeaponDamage(AD)AccRange 1/2D/MaxRoFShotsCostLCDefining Trait
— Sonic Weapons (bend around lightsabers; can’t be Deflected) —
Aurial blaster3d(0.5) son250/500150(3s)15,000ResSW Systech — deafens (−5 Per) on strong hit.
Heavy sonic pistol2d+2(0.5) son240/600350(3s)7,500LicSW More damage, less range.
Sonic disruptor2d+2(0.5) son, disint.230/3001/2t10(3s)6,000IllegalSW Atomises; −5 to victim’s Major Wound threshold.
Sonic pistol2d+1(0.5) son250/8003100(3s)5,400LicSW Anti-Jedi sidearm.
Sonic rifle3d(0.5) son4150/1,200350(3s)5,400ResSW Rifle-range sonic.
Sonic stunnerHT-3 aff (stun)220/100150(3s)4,200LicSW Non-lethal sonic; bends around saber.
— Ion Weapons (devastate droids/cyborgs; near-0 vs flesh) —
Ion cannon (portable)6d(5) ion5500/2,500120(5s)21,000MilSW Disables vehicles & war droids.
Ion carbine3d(5) ion3100/1,0001030(3s)4,800ResSW Autofire droid-killer.
Ion pistol2d+2(5) ion250/600330(3s)3,600ResSW Anti-droid sidearm.
Ion rifle4d(5) ion5300/2,000340(3s)7,200MilSW Heavy anti-droid / anti-vehicle (light).
— Disruptors (molecular breakdown; defeat nearly all armour) —
Disruptor carbine4d×2(10) cor, ×4 wound340/350315(3s)19,000IllegalSW Rapid disruptor; black-market.
Disruptor pistol3d×2(10) cor, ×4 wound230/300110(3s)15,000IllegalSW Horrific. Mostly outlawed.
Disruptor rifle6d×2(10) cor, ×4 wound450/400110(5s)24,000IllegalSW Atomises armour and flesh alike.
— Specialist Energy & Projectile —
Bowcaster5d(2) burn+pi+3200/1,800120(4s)6,000ResSW Wookiee. Mixed energy+kinetic (Part X).
Carbonite rifle1d cr + freeze350/500120(3s)7,200LicSW HT or immobilised 1d turns.
Cryoban projector2d cr + slow340/400320(3s)8,400ResSW Supercold spray; halves Move on strong hit.
Flame projector3d burn (cone) + follow-up—20/50110(5s)9,000MilSW Ignores cover; sets fires. Mandalorian wrist-mount.
Pulse-wave pistol2d+2(2) burn (+4 point-blank)130/3003100(3s)1,200LicSW Archaic. Point-blank specialist.
Pulse-wave rifle2d+3(2) burn (+5 point-blank)250/500350(3s)3,300ResSW Pre-blaster tech.
Sith lanvarok3d cr (discs)130/20015(3s)24,000RareSW Wrist disc-launcher; hands-free.
Wrist flamethrower2d+2 burn (cone)—15/4015(5s)7,200MilSW Integrated Mandalorian armament.
Wrist rocket (Mandalorian)4d×2(2) cr ex2100/1,00012(4s)12,000MilSW Mini-missile, armour-mounted. Heavy Impact.
— Launchers & Anti-Vehicle (bridge to Doc VI) —
Grenade launcherby grenade (Part VI)3150/60016(2/rd)7,200MilSW Lobs grenades to range.
Missile tube (guided)6d×4(2) cr ex41,000/4,00011(10s)30,000MilSW Sensor-locked anti-vehicle (Doc VI). Heavy Impact.
Plasma lance6d×2(5) burn4200/1,200110(5s)27,000MilSW Anti-armour energy lance. Heavy Impact.
Rocket launcher6d×3(2) cr ex3500/2,00011(10s)15,000MilSW Anti-vehicle. Disposable variants exist. Heavy Impact.

1/2t = once per two turns. ex = explosive (Doc III area rules & Part VI falloff). Cone weapons spread. Sonic at (0.5) faces doubled DR but ignores lightsaber Deflect & partially bypasses (see Part X). Energy weapons recharge +4/turn not fired or swap a core; carbonite/flame load cartridges; launchers load one munition per Ready (Part I).

Sonic — the Anti-Jedi Key SW

Bends Around the Blade. Sonic weapons cannot be stopped by a lightsaber’s Deflect (Doc II) — the sound wraps past the blade. This is their entire reason for existing. The trade-off is the (0.5) divisor: sonic faces doubled physical DR, so heavy armour resists it well. Against an unarmoured or lightly-clad Force-user, though, sonic is the great equaliser. The disruptor variant additionally atomises and lowers the Major Wound threshold — the most feared anti-Jedi weapon, and outlawed for it.

Ion — the Anti-Machine Key SW

AD 5 vs Mechanical. Ion weapons carry AD 5 against droids, vehicles, and cyborgs — they shred electronics and seize servos (Doc III droid rules). Against pure flesh they do almost nothing (treat as minor fatigue). The prevalence of war droids and HK-series assassins makes ion essential. Note: a heavily cyberneticised character (Part IX) is vulnerable to ion as if partly a droid.

Disruptor — the Universal Key SW

AD 10, ×4 Wound. Disruptors break matter at the molecular level — AD 10 defeats nearly all armour, ×4 wound multiplier makes any penetration catastrophic. They are the answer to the un-defeatable target: heavy armour, even (given the rarity caveat) beskar. The cost is universal illegality and horror — disruptor wounds don’t heal, they erase. Carrying one marks you.

Mixed-Type: the Bowcaster SW

Energy + Kinetic. The Wookiee bowcaster fires a quarrel wrapped in energy — dealing burn AND pi+ simultaneously (Part X mixed-damage rule). It can be Deflected as energy, dispersed by energy-weave as energy — yet also drives a physical bolt. A versatile weapon that hedges against single-type defenses.

Part VI

Grenades &
Explosives

Frag · plasma · ion · cryoban · gas · adhesive · thermal · breaching · mines

Area weapons clear what direct fire cannot. Each grenade is a key for a situation: frag for soft targets, ion for droids, cryoban for runners, adhesive for captures, thermal detonators for everything. Thrown (Throwing skill, Doc II) or launched (Part V), they deal area damage falling off with distance (Doc III explosion rules).

ExplosiveDamage(AD) / EffectWtCostLCNotes
Adhesive grenadeImmobilise (ST contest)0.51,600ResSW Glues targets 3 turns. Non-lethal.
Cryoban grenade3d cr + slow0.51,800ResSW Freezing; halves Move on strong hit.
Detonite charge (det tape)varies by massvariesper kgMilSW Mouldable explosive. Cutting & breaching.
EMP grenadeHT-4 aff (electronics/droids)0.51,800ResSW Shuts down electronics & droids; no HP damage.
Flash-bang (flashbang)HT-4 aff (blind+deaf 1d turns)0.5600ResSW Stun/disorient; non-lethal breaching.
Fragmentation grenade6d cr ex + [2d] frag0.51,200ResStandard anti-personnel. Shrapnel ignores energy shields.
Gas grenade (acid/corrosive)2d cor/turn cloud0.52,400IllegalSW Eats armour & flesh over time.
Gas grenade (poison)tox (HT-3+/min)0.53,000IllegalSW Lethal aerosol. Sealed armour negates.
Gas grenade (sleep)HT-4 aff (fatigue/sleep)0.51,500ResSW Sealed armour negates (Doc III).
Glop grenadeImmobilise + armour-foul0.52,100ResSW Expanding foam; fouls servos & joints.
Incendiary grenade3d burn ex + fire field0.51,500MilSW Sets persistent fires.
Ion grenade6d(5) ion ex (droids/cyborgs)0.51,500ResSW Disables droids in radius.
Magnetic limpet mine6d×3(3) burn ex1.53,600MilSW Sticks to hull/vehicle; timed (Doc VI).
Plasma mine (anti-droid)6d(5) ion ex12,700ResSW Buried droid-killer.
Plasma/concussion grenade6d(2) burn ex0.51,800MilSW Energy blast; concussive.
Plasma/thermal charge (placed)6d×4(3) burn ex1-23,000MilSW Demolition/breaching (Doc VI boarding).
Proximity mine6d×2(2) cr ex12,400MilSW Triggered area-denial.
Smoke grenadeConcealment cloud0.5300LicVisual cover; some block IR.
Sonic grenade4d(0.5) son ex + HT-3 stun0.52,400ResSW Bypasses energy shields partly; deafens.
Stun grenadeHT-5 aff (stun, radius)0.51,200LicSW Non-lethal area knockout.
Thermal detonator6d×3(2) burn ex0.512,000IllegalSW Devastating. Mandalorian-carried; tightly controlled.

ex = explosion (Doc III: full at centre, falls off). [frag] = fragmentation follow-up. Most grenades come in thrown or launcher-compatible variants.

Grenade Damage Types Matter Too. SW Frag is kinetic (ignores energy shields, shredded by plate). Plasma/concussion is energy (stopped by energy-weave). Ion hits only machines. Choose by what the enemy’s armour doesn’t cover — the same logic as direct fire.

Thermal Detonators. SW 6d×3 at centre will gut a room. So feared that brandishing one is itself a threat. Mandalorians carry them as standard; most others treat them as last resort. Tightly controlled — possession alone is a serious charge.

Non-Lethal Options. Flash-bang, stun, gas-sleep, adhesive, and glop grenades capture rather than kill — essential for arrests, extractions, and anyone who needs the target alive. Sealed armour (Part VII) defeats gas; nerve-reinforcement implants (Part IX) resist concussive stun.

Faction Arsenals & Cultural Weapons SW

Every power signs its work. The Republic arms a hundred thousand garrisons with the same dependable pattern; the Selkath enforce Ahto City's peace with sound; the Hutts arm whoever is cheapest and loudest; the Chiss bring charric masers nobody else can copy; the Echani duel; Czerka sells to all of the above. These arsenals appear on the sheet under their own faction categories. All standard component slots, archetype fits and Bulk rules apply.

The Republic — pattern-issue, rugged, everywhere

The Chiss Ascendancy — charric masers (mixed burn + kinetic punch; full line under Blasters, now grouped as a faction)

Charric pistol, carbine, rifle and heavy repeater appear in the sheet under Chiss Ascendancy (charric). Charric bolts deliver burning and concussive kinetic transfer — see the charric entries and the Chiss commando armour in the armour tables.

WeaponDamage(AD)AccRange ½D/MaxRoFShotsSTBulkCostLCNotes

Mandalorian arms & faction armour additions

ItemProfileWtCostLCNotes
Wrist rocket (Mandalorian)4d×2(2) cr ex · Acc 2 · 100/1,000 · RoF 1 · 2(4s) · Bulk −11.512,000MilArmour-mounted mini-missile; Heavy Impact (knockdown check regardless of damage).
Republic marine assault armourDR 16/14 (E/K)1838,000MilBoarding-action rig: sealed, mag-boots, +2 vs knockdown.
Kolto-weave diving suitDR 6/6821,000LicSelkath aquatic suit: sealed underwater; kolto micro-reservoir — +2 to HT recovery rolls, heals 2 HP once per day.
Hutt enforcer plateDR 12/12169,800LicGaudy gilded plate; −1 Stealth (it gleams), +1 Intimidation in Hutt space.
Gamorrean heavy harnessDR 6/10122,400—Crude iron and boar-leather. Smells exactly as expected.
Echani light battle armourDR 10/8526,000LicHalf-weight Echani lattice, no DX penalty; fragile — half normal item HP.

Lightwhip (Sw 5d+5(5) burn, reach 1-4*, Parry −2F) is restored to the sheet's saber catalogue as its own weapon — see Lightsabers & Variants, Part II.

Area-of-Effect & Explosion Falloff — Hex by Hex

The Falloff Rule. An explosion deals its full listed damage at the centre hex (hex 0), then drops as the blast spreads. The GURPS convention: divide the centre damage by (3 × distance in hexes). So at 1 hex away the target takes centre ÷ 3; at 2 hexes, ÷ 6; at 3 hexes, ÷ 9; and so on. We pre-compute this on the table below so you don’t roll then divide mid-fight — just read across to the victim’s hex distance.

Reading the Falloff Table. Find the grenade’s centre damage in the left column, then read the average damage delivered at each hex range. Anyone in hex 0 (the hex the grenade lands in, or an adjacent body it’s pressed against) takes the full roll. Apply armour divisor and DR after falloff — explosive concussion is usually cr (kinetic) so plate resists it; frag and shrapnel are pi (kinetic, shield-ignoring); plasma/thermal is burn (energy).

Damage Type Per Explosive. SW Each AoE weapon’s blast carries a type: cr ex (frag, concussion, thermal-detonator overpressure — kinetic, plate resists, shields don’t), burn ex (plasma/thermal/incendiary energy — energy-weave resists, shields reduce), ion ex (anti-droid only), pi frag (shrapnel follow-up — kinetic, ignores shields), son ex (sonic, bypasses shields partly), or an affliction (flash-bang, stun, gas — resisted by HT, negated by sealed armour, no falloff division on the affliction roll but the radius is fixed).

Blast Radius. SW Most hand grenades have an effective radius of 3 hexes (damage trails off to near-nothing by hex 3-4). Thermal detonators and demolition charges reach 5-6 hexes. Gas, smoke, and affliction clouds fill a fixed radius (2-4 hexes) at uniform effect rather than falling off — everyone in the cloud rolls against the full effect. Mines and limpets follow their parent munition’s profile.

Diving for Cover. A target who sees the grenade may attempt to Dodge (Doc I) to dive one or more hexes away before detonation — each hex of successful movement steps them down the falloff column. Diving prone in the same hex doesn’t reduce damage but may put cover (a wall, a body) between victim and blast for partial DR. Cover between the centre and a target blocks line-of-blast: subtract the cover’s DR from the post-falloff damage.

Contained Blasts. SW In a sealed corridor or small room (Doc VI boarding), the blast cannot dissipate outward — treat falloff as one step gentler (use the next-closer hex column) and everyone in the enclosed space takes at least the hex-2 value. This is why a thermal detonator ”guts a room”: indoors, the whole room is effectively within lethal range.

Centre DamageHex 0 (centre)Hex 1Hex 2Hex 3Hex 4Hex 5-6Used By
3d (avg 10)10 (full roll)~3 (÷3)~2 (÷6)~1 (÷9)——Cryoban, incendiary, sonic grenade
6d (avg 21)21 (full roll)~7 (÷3)~3 (÷6)~2 (÷9)~1—Frag, plasma/concussion, ion grenade
6d×2 (avg 42)42 (full roll)~14 (÷3)~7 (÷6)~5 (÷9)~3 (÷12)~2Proximity mine, wrist rocket
6d×3 (avg 63)63 (full roll)~21 (÷3)~10 (÷6)~7 (÷9)~5 (÷12)~4 (÷15)Thermal detonator, magnetic limpet, rocket launcher
6d×4 (avg 84)84 (full roll)~28 (÷3)~14 (÷6)~9 (÷9)~7 (÷12)~5 (÷15)Plasma/thermal charge, missile tube
— Fixed-radius effects (uniform within cloud; no falloff division) —
Affliction (HT roll)Full effect uniformly within radius: flash-bang & stun grenade ~2 hexes · gas (sleep/poison/acid) ~3 hexes · smoke ~4 hexes · adhesive/glop ~1-2 hexes. Sealed armour negates gas; HT resists stun/flash.Flash-bang, stun, gas, adhesive, glop, smoke

Falloff = centre ÷ (3 × hexes). Apply armour divisor & DR after falloff. Damage type per weapon (cr/burn/pi/ion/son) determines which armour and shields apply (Part I matrix). Indoors/contained: shift one hex gentler. 1 hex = 1 m (Doc I).

Part VII

Armour

Split DR by type · energy-weave · plate · powered · Mandalorian · reflec · sealed · shields

Armour is where the scaling system pays off. Every suit lists DR as energy / kinetic (Part I): the number that matters depends on what is shooting you. Energy-weave is a wall against blasters and tissue-paper against blades; plate is balanced; reflec mirrors energy and ignores matter; powered armour adds strength and life support; and saber-resistant metals — cortosis, phrik, beskar — do what nothing else can. The catalogue runs in five broad bands of cost and capability — from concealable civilian wear, through militia and frontier kit, standard line-infantry issue, special-operations and specialist suits, up to master-crafted modular war-plate — and every suit names the material it is built from. The Armour Materials reference below explains what each one does. Choose armour for the threat you expect; no single suit covers everything.
