A charge half the size, set at the right seam, beats a bigger one slapped on a blast door.

Triggers & Fuzing

HouseArming a Charge. The same warhead can hang off any of these triggers — this is how a thermal detonator becomes a timed demolition charge, a tripwire, or a sentry. The simple ones are part of assembly; the sensor and condition triggers want their own Electronics roll. Disarming is Explosives (or Traps for the mechanical ones) against the difficulty the armer set with their margin — a failure stalls, a critical failure detonates it. A demolitions sensor or lifeform scanner helps you find a planted device before you ever touch it.

TriggerFires when…Wire / setBeat it with
Impact / contactit strikes somethingtrivial—
Timed fuze (0–10 s)the count runs outReady to setthrow it back / clear the radius
Long timer (minutes–days)the clock expiresExplosivesread & stop the clock (Explosives/Traps)
Command-detonate (remote / comlink)you trigger itElectronicsjam the signal (ion, slicing, EMP)
Tripwire / pressure-platea wire is crossed or weight appliedTrapsspot (Per/Traps), then disarm
Proximity / motion sensormovement enters its fieldElectronics (Sensors)spot, then disarm at −2
Life-form sensora living target enters (ignores droids)Electronics (Sensors)as proximity; droids pass freely
Condition lock (light / heat / sound / signal / altitude)the set condition is metElectronics−4 to disarm without knowing the condition
Dead-man switchthe held switch is releasedExplosivescannot be disarmed while held — take the holder alive
Anti-handling / tamperanyone but the armer moves or opens itExplosives−4 (−6 if expertly set); a botch detonates it

Compounds, Agents & Components

Explosive Compound. The grey workhorse — mouldable detonite / det-tape stock. ¼ kg = one 6d block (above); sold by the kilo. A demolitionist's trap mats are mostly this. Concussion / non-lethal compound is the same stuff tuned for overpressure and stun rather than killing fragments — load it for capture and breaching work.

SWBaradium. The fusion compound that makes a thermal detonator a thermal detonator — ~4× the yield density of standard compound, unstable, and tightly controlled. A baradium core is what lets a thrown shell reach 6d×3; a baradium charge is what gets you into ship-killer territory. Possession alone is a serious charge.

Pulse Chips & Ion Regulators. The trigger-maker's bin. Pulse chips are the timing and firing components — one feeds the fuze, timer or sensor of a single device, so they are the limiting reagent when building in bulk. Ion regulators tune ion and EMP payloads (one per ion/EMP build) and harden a device's own electronics against being jammed or sliced. Both are cheap and Restricted.

Gas Canisters. An empty pressure canister is the body of any gas weapon; load it with an agent (below) and pick a disperser — a grenade shell for a thrown ~4-m cloud, or a wide-mouth dispersal head for a room-filling fog. The canister sets the cloud's size and persistence; the agent sets what it does.

Gasses, Nerve Agents & Poisons

HouseHow a Cloud Behaves. A gas weapon fills a fixed radius at uniform effect — no hex falloff; everyone inside resists the full agent. Most clouds linger ~1d turns then thin; "lingering" agents persist far longer (minutes). Clouds drift with the wind and are scrubbed fast by ventilation or open sky, and fill a sealed room fast. Sealed armour, Filter Lungs and Doesn't Breathe negate inhaled agents entirely; a breath/filter mask gives a strong bonus or outright immunity depending on the agent. Delivery is by grenade, by dispersal canister, by injection into a ventilation feed, or — for the contact-capable agents — smeared straight onto a surface, blade or door handle.

SWFEX-M3 Nerve Agent. Colourless, odourless and lethal to nearly every sentient species — the assassin's gas. On exposure (breath or skin) the victim rolls HT: failure takes the full tick damage — 6d+4 internal toxic per tick, ten ticks over ten seconds (~155 total; this kills) — while success halves each tick. Sealed armour / Filter Lungs / Doesn't Breathe are immune. Contact-capable: a dose can be painted onto an object, but a dose is a dose — enough to murder a room, never enough to build a bomb around. Atrocity-grade; illegal everywhere with laws.

Nerve Gas (military canister). The area-weapon cousin of FEX-M3 — a lethal aerosol filling its cloud: HT−4 or 4d toxic per turn while you breathe it, with one more HT each turn to clear it once you are out. Sealed armour negates. War-crime ordnance.

Paralytic Gas (lingering). A persistent neuro-agent: HT−4 or your voluntary muscles lock — paralysed and helpless for minutes (a fresh HT each minute to shake it). The cloud lingers, settling and staying active far longer than a normal grenade fog. Non-lethal, but being frozen in a firefight is its own death sentence.

Knockout Gas (silent). Slow, odourless, colourless and all but invisible — the infiltrator's gas. A victim may not notice it at all (Per−4 to catch the faint haze); each minute of exposure is HT−3 or sink into unconsciousness for 1d×10 minutes. Made for taking a room without a shot or a sound.

Caustic Irritant ("the nasty one"). A choking, blistering area-denial agent — 3d toxic per turn in the cloud, and anyone inside is also blinded and retching: −3 to everything and a powerful urge to get out. Not subtle, not clean; it simply makes a space unusable.

Strategic & Doomsday Ordnance

HouseWhen d6 Stop Mattering. Above the breaching-charge tier, stop rolling dice against people and switch to the structure/vehicle scale — a charge's job here is to cripple a ship's Hull-HP pool or destroy a section outright (Doc VI), not to roll a number against a torso. The questions become where, how is it armed, and what could set it off early.

SWThe Ship-Killer Charge. You do not out-blast a capital ship's hull — you go inside and hit the guts. A shaped baradium charge (25–50 kg) seated against a reactor housing, magazine, structural keel or hyperdrive node bypasses the armour the vessel paid millions for. Resolve it as a Stealth/infiltration problem to reach the node, then one Explosives roll to seat the charge — margin buys the shaped + contained multipliers, and a solid hit cripples the vessel's Hull-HP pool or destroys that section. A reactor breach is the ship. This is exactly the kind of bomb a Sith adept can build to gut a capital ship from within — modest in mass, lethal in placement.

Baradium Core Bomb. The thermal detonator scaled up and up — a satchel-to-drum of fusion compound. Vehicle- and building-scale yields; the upper end levels a city block. Stable enough to transport with care, devastating enough that owning one is an act of war.

SWNeutronium Bomb — Continent-Killer. A sliver of stabilised neutron-degenerate matter held in a containment field. It is not "armed" so much as barely restrained: the matter wants to re-expand the instant the field falters, and when it does it releases continental-scale energy — tens of kilometres levelled, the region cratered and glassed, seismic and fallout effects across a hemisphere. A weapon of legend and atrocity, not a grenade.

— Why It Is Hard to Use. The field is the whole danger. To deliberately detonate, you drop containment through a deliberate arming sequence: a string of Physics or Engineer rolls plus Explosives over several minutes, capped by a final Will or HT roll — the nerve to actually do it. Each failed step risks a premature partial release (the GM rolls; a partial release is still a catastrophe). It cannot be command-detonated like a thermal detonator — far too unstable for a simple trigger — and it cannot be safely stored: the field decays and must be re-charged, or the device becomes a countdown nobody set.

— What Sets It Off Early. Any of: the containment field losing power (an ion/EMP hit, a power failure, sabotage); a hard shock or impact (dropped, in a crash, struck by weapons fire — roll against a high target each time); extreme heat; a critical failure on any handling or transport check; or a nearby detonation setting it off sympathetically. Simply moving it calls for periodic handling rolls (Explosives or HT); fail badly and it is already too late. The thing is a plot, a threat and a clock at once — point the players at one and the question is never "how much damage" but "can we get it off-world before it goes."

— Beyond. Older and darker things are whispered of: phase-disruptor warheads that unzip a planet's crust, baradium seismic torpedoes that crack a world's mantle, the Rakatan and Sith superweapons of the deep past. These are not items on a shelf. They are stories the GM tells, and very occasionally lets the table live through.

Utility & Field Gear

Aratech. Advanced medpac — Top-grade auto-trauma pack; Aratech bacta patch — Premium slap-patch; Aratech diagnostic scanner — Handheld med-scanner: +3 to Diagnosis and First Aid, and reads a patient's injuries, toxin.

Corporate Arms. Macrobinoculars — Hold to the eyes: cancels the −2 to −5 ranged/Vision penalty for distance when spotting or; Sensor Scanner (handheld) — One sweep = 1 turn (Electronics Operation/Sensors): detects life, metal or energy in one d; Motion sensor (placed) — Set a tripwire field (Electronics Operation): pings your comlink the instant anything livi.

Czerka Corporation. Kolto patch — Slap-on field dressing; Standard med pack — The adventurer's field kit; Stim Pack — Combat stimulant; inject as a Ready action; Anti-Rad Tabs — Swallow one tab: +5 to HT rolls vs radiation for 1 hour; Antitox Injector — Inject as a Ready action: +3 to HT vs ingested/injected poison for 1 hour, AND one dose im; Medical Kit (field) — Reusable surgical/diagnostic kit: +2 to First Aid, Physician and Diagnosis rolls; Fusioncutter / Cutting Torch — Cuts a typical door or restraint in ~30 sec, a bulkhead in minutes (much slower vs DR 30+); Liquid Cable / Syntherope + Grapple — 20 m line plus a magnetic or barbed grapple head you can fire ~10 m to anchor; Breath Mask — Worn over the face: filters smoke, toxins and thin air — auto-passes routine suffocation a; Rebreather — With a sealed suit, supplies ~1 hour of breathing underwater or in vacuum; negates drownin; Fusion Lantern / Glowrod — Glowrod: cheap, disposable, lights a ~10 m radius and cancels darkness penalties, battery; Survival Field Kit — +2 to Survival rolls; packs shelter, water reclamation, a fire-starter and ~3 days of rati; Cold-Weather Gear — Worn: auto-passes routine cold checks down to Hoth/Csilla extremes, but it's bulky — −1 to; Power Cell (spare) — One reload for a blaster, powered tool or droid; Utility Belt — Six belt pouches and clips; Utility satchel — A simple shoulder satchel of tough synthcloth; Durasteel cargo case — A rigid lockable durasteel case; Czerka shipping crate (portable) — A corporate stackable crate with carry handles; Repulsor cargo sled — A floating repulsorlift platform that tows behind you; Bacta spray (field) — Aerosolised bacta; Ascension gun — Fires a cable + piton up to ~30 m vertical (or to a beam): climb or rappel at full Move,; Magnetic grip boots — Switch on: cling to any metal hull or deck — move at normal Move on vertical surfaces and; Aquata breather — Bite-down rebreather the size of a thumb: ~10 minutes of underwater air per charge, rechar.

Echani Sciences. Battle adrenal (Attack) — Inject: +4 to attack skills and +2 to weapon/melee damage for 1d turns, then a −2 FP crash; Fortitude adrenal — Inject: +4 DR and +2 to HT (and HT-based rolls); Surge adrenal — Inject: +3 Move and +4 to Stealth, Acrobatics and Jumping for 1d turns, then a −2 FP crash; Echani reflex stim — Inject: +4 to active defenses (Dodge/Parry/Block).

Independent / Civilian. Courier sling bag — A cross-body sling favoured by messengers; Spacer's duffel — A big soft duffel for hauling a whole kit between berths; Field rucksack — A framed military pack with a hip belt that shifts load onto the hips.

Industrial Automaton. Droid repair kit — Reusable kit: restore 2d+4 HP to a droid or vehicle with ~10 min work (Mechanic/Electronic; IA droid diagnostic kit — Droid-tech kit: +3 to Electronics Repair, Computer Programming and Mechanic when working o; IA restraining bolt — Slap onto a droid: an unwilling droid rolls Will at −4 or is forced to obey simple command.

Locris Securities. Security Kit (slicer) — +3 to Lockpicking and Computer Hacking against electronic locks, plus mechanical bypass pi; Slicer spike — One-use breaching spike: plug into a terminal for +4 to a single Computer Hacking or Elect; Locris lockbreaker — One-use cutting charge sized for a lock or hinge: opens most sealed doors in 1 turn with n.

Mandalorian. Mandalorian load harness — A beskar-buckled load-bearing harness worn over armour; Beskar strongbox — A small chest of refined beskar; Mandalorian field-mend kit — Mando battlefield trauma kit: heals 3d+6 HP over 3 turns (your FULL daily saturation).

Merr-Sonn. Bandolier rig — Loops and pouches across the chest for cells, grenades, and small kit; Merr-Sonn weapon maintenance kit — Field armoury kit: +3 to Armoury (small arms); a quick service clears a malfunction or jam; Merr-Sonn power pack (spare) — Bandolier of charged power cells: a Ready action reloads a blaster's depleted cell.

Republic. Military med pack — Issue-grade auto-injector trauma pack; Binders / Restraints — Cuff a captive: ST−6 or an Escape roll to break free; Kolto grenade — Thrown healing burst (~ST×3 m; goes off at the end of this turn cycle); Code cylinder — Encoded clearance rod: +3 to pass checkpoints, open military/corporate doors and bluff aut.

SoroSuub. Comlink — Pocket radio: clear voice/data to orbit, or dozens of km on the ground (planet-wide with a; Datapad — Pocket computer: data storage, runs Computer Operation tasks, hosts a slicer kit; Holoprojector / Recorder — Records hours of holo (sight + sound).

Underworld. Stealth Field Generator — Switch on: +6 to Stealth (Vision); Smuggler's concealment vest — A lined vest of hidden pockets; Sensor baffle (Stygian cloak) — Worn: −4 to others' sensor sweeps and life-form scans against you; Bothan slicer veil — Worn signal-cloak: +4 to Computer Hacking to stay undetected while slicing, and −4 to othe.

Verpine Hive. Verpine modular pack — Verpine-engineered load-bearing pack with active weight-distribution webbing; Verpine tech kit — Verpine precision toolkit: +3 to Armoury and Electronics Repair; with an hour's work it ca; Verpine sensor pack — Verpine micro-sensor suite: +3 to Electronics Operation (Sensors).

Cross-Reference — by Effect Type

Using this. House The same arsenal sorted by what it does rather than who makes it — the second filter on the sheet works the same way (pick a type, then narrow by faction, or leave faction on “any”).

Cryo / Stasis. Cryoban grenade — 4d(1) cr + slow (3 m); Carbonite grenade — 2d cr + encase (2 m).

Fragmentation. Fragmentation grenade — 6d(1) cr ex + [3d] frag (3 m); Proximity mine — 6d×2(2) cr ex (3 m); Concussion grenade — 5d cr ex (knockback) (3 m); Mandalorian shrapnel charge — 6d(1) cr ex + [3d] frag (3 m); Nar Shaddaa scrap-frag — 5d(1) cr ex + [2d] frag (3 m); Republic frag grenade — 6d(1) cr ex + [2d] frag (3 m).

Gas / Chemical. Gas grenade (acid/corrosive) — 3d cor/turn cloud (4 m); Bioplague grenade (rakghoul) — 3d tox/turn cloud + disease (4 m); Sith poison canister — 3d tox/turn cloud + fear (4 m); Spice-gas grenade (Hutt) — Knockout gas (HT-3 or sleep) (4 m); Knockout-gas grenade (Exchange) — Stun gas (HT-3 or stun) (4 m).

High Explosive. Plasma/concussion grenade — 6d(2) burn ex (3 m); Thermal detonator — 6d×3(2) burn ex (4 m); Plasma/thermal charge (placed) — 6d×4(3) burn ex (5 m); Magnetic limpet mine — 6d×3(3) burn ex (3 m); Plasma mine (anti-droid) — 6d(5) ion ex (3 m); Plasma grenade (Merr-Sonn) — 6d(3) burn ex (3 m); Conqueror breaching charge — 6d×3(3) cr ex (4 m).

Incendiary. Incendiary grenade — 4d burn ex + fire field (3 m); Incinerator firebomb (Exchange) — 4d burn ex + fire field (3 m).

Ion / EMP. Ion grenade — 6d(5) ion ex (droids/cyborgs) (3 m); EMP grenade — HT-6 vs electronics; drops shields (3 m); Pulse grenade (anti-shield) — 3d(5) ion + shield-collapse (3 m).

Sonic. Sonic grenade — 5d(0.5) son ex + HT-3 stun (4 m); Seismic charge — 6d×3(2) cr ex (huge AOE) (6 m); Sonic imploder (SoroSuub) — 5d(0.5) son ex + HT-4 stun (4 m); Sonic disruptor mine (SoroSuub) — 5d(0.5) son ex + stun (3 m).

Special / Utility. Adhesive grenade (glop) — Adhesive — root (no damage) (3 m); Smoke grenade — Concealment cloud (no damage) (4 m); Interdiction grenade (Sith) — 2d burn + Force-suppression (3 m).

Stun / Flash. Flash-bang (flashbang) — HT-4 aff (blind+deaf 1d turns) (4 m); Stun grenade — HT-4 aff (stun 1d turns) (3 m).

Field Gear — by Function

Comms. Comlink.

Gear. Binders / Restraints, Power Cell (spare), Magnetic grip boots, Merr-Sonn power pack (spare).

Medical. Kolto patch, Standard med pack, Military med pack, Stim Pack, Anti-Rad Tabs, Antitox Injector, Medical Kit (field), Advanced medpac, Bacta spray (field), Kolto grenade, Battle adrenal (Attack), Fortitude adrenal, Surge adrenal, Aratech bacta patch, Aratech diagnostic scanner, Echani reflex stim, Mandalorian field-mend kit.

Sensor. Macrobinoculars, Sensor Scanner (handheld), Motion sensor (placed), Verpine sensor pack.

Stealth. Stealth Field Generator, Sensor baffle (Stygian cloak), Bothan slicer veil.

Storage. Utility Belt, Utility satchel, Courier sling bag, Bandolier rig, Spacer's duffel, Field rucksack, Smuggler's concealment vest, Verpine modular pack, Mandalorian load harness, Durasteel cargo case, Czerka shipping crate (portable), Repulsor cargo sled, Beskar strongbox.

Survival. Breath Mask, Rebreather, Fusion Lantern / Glowrod, Survival Field Kit, Cold-Weather Gear, Aquata breather.

Tech. Datapad, Holoprojector / Recorder, Security Kit (slicer), Slicer spike, Droid repair kit, Code cylinder, IA droid diagnostic kit, IA restraining bolt, Verpine tech kit.

Tool. Fusioncutter / Cutting Torch, Liquid Cable / Syntherope + Grapple, Ascension gun, Merr-Sonn weapon maintenance kit, Locris lockbreaker.

Reading These. SW Each explosive lists a blast type and radius; cross-reference the falloff table in Part VI for damage at each hex. Remember the type matters: cr/frag is kinetic (shields don’t help, plate does), burn is energy (energy-weave helps, shields reduce), ion hits only machines, affliction is HT-resisted and negated by sealed armour.

Fragmentation Grenade. SW The standard anti-personnel grenade — a crushing overpressure blast (6d cr) plus a shrapnel follow-up (pi frag). Both are kinetic, so energy shields offer no protection; rigid plate does. 3-hex radius. The infantry mainstay.

Plasma/Concussion Grenade. SW An energy-burst grenade (6d burn) — concussive and incendiary. Energy-weave armour resists it; shields reduce it. 3-hex radius. The choice against unarmoured or kinetically-armoured foes.

Thermal Detonator. SW The feared one — 6d×3 burning at the centre, a 5-6 hex radius that guts a room (and indoors, the whole room is in lethal range). So dangerous that openly holding one is a threat in itself. Mandalorians carry them as standard; everyone else treats them as a last resort. Illegal; possession alone is a serious charge.

Ion Grenade. SW An area ion burst (6d ion, AD 5 vs machines) — disables every droid in the radius, harmless to flesh. The answer to a droid swarm.

EMP Grenade. SW Shuts down electronics and droids (HT−4 affliction vs machines) without HP damage — fries comms, sensors, and droid brains in the radius. Non-lethal to organics; devastating to the mechanical.

Cryoban Grenade. SW An area freeze (3d cr + Move-slow) — pins and slows everyone caught. Control, not kill.

Adhesive Grenade. SW Sprays fast-setting glue — targets contest ST or are stuck for 3 turns. No damage; pure capture. The non-lethal way to stop a runner or a charge.

Sonic Grenade. SW An area sonic blast (4d son + stun) that partly bypasses shields and deafens. Good against shielded clusters.

Smoke Grenade. Fills a 4-hex cloud with concealment; some block infrared. Cover for movement and disengagement.

Flash-Bang. SW Blinds and deafens (HT−4) in a 2-hex radius — the breacher’s stun, non-lethal, for entering a room without killing everyone in it.

Gas Grenades (Sleep / Poison / Acid). SW Fill a 3-hex cloud: sleep gas knocks out (HT−4 fatigue), poison gas kills over time (tox), acid gas eats armour and flesh (cor/turn). All negated by sealed armour. Poison and acid variants are illegal.

Incendiary Grenade. SW Sets a persistent fire field (3d burn + ongoing) — area denial and terror. Burns until it exhausts fuel.

Stun Grenade. SW An area knockout (HT−5 affliction) — non-lethal crowd control, the riot-squad’s friend.

Glop Grenade. SW Expanding foam that immobilises and fouls servos and joints — especially nasty to powered armour and droids. Capture and area-denial.

Plasma/Thermal Charge. SW A placed demolition charge (6d×4 burn) — for breaching walls, sabotaging reactors, and boarding actions (Doc VI). Set, timed, detonated.

Detonite Charge (Det Tape). SW Mouldable explosive sold by mass — shape it to cut a door, breach a hull, or rig a trap. Damage scales with how much you use.

Proximity Mine. SW A triggered area-denial mine (6d×2 cr) — buried or placed, detonates when something crosses it.

Magnetic Limpet Mine. SW Sticks to a hull or vehicle and detonates on a timer (6d×3 burn) — sabotage and boarding (Doc VI).

Plasma Mine (Anti-Droid). SW A buried ion mine (6d ion) — drops droids that cross it, ignores organics.

Codex VII

Armour
— in Detail

Civilian · weave · reflec · plate · powered · Mandalorian · faction

Civilian & Light

Heavy Clothing / Robes. Layered cloth — Jedi robes, traveller’s garb. DR 1: enough to turn a graze, nothing more. Worn for mobility and anonymity, not protection.

Armoured Flight Suit. The pilot’s and spacer’s standard — light, flexible, DR 4 with some life-support sealing. Protects against debris and glancing fire without hindering a cockpit.

Blast Vest. A concealable torso protector (DR 10/6) — slips under a jacket. The bodyguard’s and the cautious citizen’s hidden armour.

Combat Jumpsuit. Light security and mercenary kit — DR 6/5 over the body, flexible enough for a full day’s wear.

Energy-Dispersing Weave

Fiber Armour. SW Conductive fibres woven to channel and scatter energy attacks — DR 18 vs energy, only 4 vs kinetic. A wall against blasters, tissue-paper against a vibroblade. Once per fight, while defending, it can throw up +10 DR against a single energy attack. The duelist who expects blaster lines, not blades.

Weave Armour. SW A flexible energy-tuned suit (DR 16/5) favoured by those who need to move — gladiators, duelists. Less protective than mesh, far less restrictive.

Mesh Armour. SW Advanced energy-dispersing weave (DR 28/6) — superb against blasters, with a once-per-fight +20 DR energy reaction. The premier anti-blaster armour. Still helpless against a determined blade or slug.

Matrix Armour. SW Starship hull-plate bonded over heavy armour (DR 34/10) — tries to combine energy-dispersion with real kinetic protection, at great weight. Once-per-fight +30 DR energy reaction. The heavy energy-specialist’s choice.

Reflec

Reflec Armour. SW A mirror-coat that reflects energy weapons almost perfectly — DR 40 vs energy, zero vs kinetic. Near-immune to blasters, utterly helpless against a slug or blade. Wear it against a blaster line; never against a knife-fighter. Light and not obvious until the bolts start bouncing.

Reflec Battle Plate. SW Reflec coating over real plate (DR 45/12) — covers both bases, blaster-reflecting and kinetically protected. Heavy and very expensive; the elite operative’s answer to mixed threats.

Battle Armour

Light Battle Armour. Basic balanced plate (DR 12/12) — equal protection against energy and kinetic, for soldiers who can’t carry more. The militia and entry-level trooper standard.

Battle Armour. Standard military plate (DR 18/18) with helmet — the line infantry’s protection. Balanced against all threats, defeated by none of the era’s specialist counters. Against a 5d blaster it leaves you wounded but standing.

Heavy Battle Armour. Shock-trooper plate (DR 25/25), rigid and heavy — for those who walk into the worst of it. Slows you down; keeps you alive.

Republic / Sith Trooper Armour. SW Faction-issue military armour (DR 20/18), sealed helmet, standardised. The Republic and Sith line trooper’s kit — the difference is the paint and the insignia, not the protection.

Powered Armour

Light Powered Armour. Servo-assisted plate (DR 22/22, +ST 2), sealed life support, two upgrade slots. The servos carry the weight and add strength; the seal makes it vacuum-rated. Ion-vulnerable (the servos are electronic).

Powered Battle Armour. Heavy servo-assist (DR 30/30, +ST 3) with full sealed life support — a soldier becomes a juggernaut. Maintenance-hungry, ion-bait, but immensely protective.

Heavy Powered Armour. Near-exoskeleton (DR 40/40, +ST 5) — the heaviest personal protection short of beskar, and the strength to carry weapons no unarmoured soldier could lift. The walking fortress.

Mandalorian Armour

Mandalorian Iron Plate (Unrefined). SW Worked from raw beskar ore — sturdy, ”a bit better than durasteel” (DR 22/22), but with no saber-resistance: a lightsaber cuts it like any plate. Common among the clans for everyday war-gear. The bulk of what people loosely call ”Mandalorian armour.”

Neo-Crusader Armour (Base). SW Full Mandalorian war-plate (DR 28/26), sealed-ready, with the iconic helmet and HUD — the standardised armour of the Neo-Crusaders. Around 90,000 credits for the base suit, before advancements (vacuum seal, integrated weapons, jetpack). A warrior’s identity as much as their protection.

Refined Beskar Plate. SW Full-density beskar forged with closely-guarded Mandalorian metallurgy (DR 60/55) — divides all penetrating damage by 10 (DD ÷10) — repels sabers, shrugs off blasters, resists most explosives (Part X). The most coveted substance in the galaxy — effectively priceless. Clan-held and almost never sold; what little reaches outsiders does so for a fortune or a blood-debt. A clan treasure; acquiring it is a story, not a purchase. Near-impervious to personal weapons — only a committed saber thrust, a disruptor, sustained slow-cutting, or a heavy weapon threatens it. Its two weaknesses are crushing force and coverage: beskar resists cuts and bolts, not raw impact, so a Heavy-Impact cannon, a fall, or a crushing blow still drives concussion and knockback through the plate (Doc III); and beskar guards only the locations it covers, so a chink shot (−10) or a hit to an unarmoured spot ignores it. The wearer is effectively immortal against blasters and sabers, but can still be knocked flat, concussed, or picked apart at the seams.

Faction & Role

Jedi Combat Robes. SW Light armourweave under traditional robes (DR 8/3) — for the Jedi who expects blaster fire and wants a little protection without abandoning mobility or the Order’s aesthetic.

Chiss Commando Armour. SW Sealed Chiss combat armour in a colour-shifting, fractal-pattern weave (DR 14/10) that bends light to its surroundings — +3 Stealth, and −2 to any roll to spot or target the wearer at range. Rated for the cold and vacuum of the Unknown Regions; standard issue for the Expansionary Defense Fleet’s warrior corps. Almost never seen outside Chiss hands.

Sith Lord Battle Armour. SW Heavy armour (DR 38/36) often alchemically treated to resist the Force (Doc IV). The dark-side warlord’s panoply — protection backed by sorcery.

Czerka Security Armour. SW Corporate mass-produced plate (DR 14/14) — whatever Czerka is stamping out this quarter. Adequate, affordable, everywhere their security operates.

Exchange Light Armour. SW Concealable armour (DR 12/8) with a Stealth bonus — the criminal operative’s choice, protection that doesn’t announce itself.

Zeyd-Cloth / Armorweave Robes. SW Armoured cloth (DR 9/7) worn under robes — the discreet protection of those who must look unarmoured.

Personal Energy Shields

Energy Shield (DR 50+). SW A forearm or belt generator projecting a field that reduces incoming energy damage by its DR — and does nothing against kinetic. Because the field faces no armour divisor, shields are rated far higher than worn armour: from DR 50 (civilian) past DR 150 on the finest models (see the Named Shields table). Roughly 5 charges, raised as a quick action, once per encounter with overload lockout, then recharged. The defining defensive tech of the era — and the reason slugs and blades never went away.

Named Shields. SW The era’s notable shields (Part X table) climb from the basic generator through the Arkanian (adds heat/cold), Echani dueling, and Sith (adds sonic) models, to the two Mandalorian shields that uniquely blunt kinetic as well as energy — the melee shield against blades, the power shield broadly — and finally the Verpine prototype’s near-total energy coverage and the unique Yusanis’ dueling shield. The Mandalorian and Verpine shields are the prizes; everything kinetic still ignores all the rest.

Codex VIII

Adventuring Gear
— in Detail

Medical & kolto · sensors · stealth · survival · electronics

Medical & Kolto

Kolto Patches, Medpacks & Injectors. SW The three tiers of field healing — emergency patch (1d+2/turn × 3), standard medpack (2d+4/turn × 3), military injector (3d+6/turn × 3). Each heals over three turns from application, so you slap it on a downed ally and they recover as you fight on. The catch is the Kolto Saturation Rule (Part VIII): the body absorbs only one tier-worth per day, so each application is a real choice. Bacta patches are the slightly weaker, more widely available alternative.

Bacta & Kolto Tanks. SW Immersion tanks heal at ×5 (bacta) or ×6 (kolto) the natural rate (Doc III), working around the saturation cap by slow controlled absorption. The only way to heal major wounds quickly between fights. Kolto is Manaan-sourced and superior; bacta is galactic-standard.

Stim Packs & Battle Stims. SW Combat stimulants — a temporary ST/HT boost at the cost of a fatigue crash afterward. The stim pack is the mild version; the hyper-adrenal battle stim hits harder and crashes worse. The edge that gets you through one more fight.

Anti-Rad Tabs & Antitox. SW Consumable defenses — anti-rad tabs boost HT against radiation (an era staple), antitox counters poisons. The careful spacer’s and the wary diner’s insurance.

Medical & Surgical Kits. Reusable kits that boost First Aid, Physician, and Surgery rolls — the field medic’s bag and the portable surgical suite (which enables cybernetic installs at a penalty, Part IX). The med scanner diagnoses what’s wrong.

Sensors, Visors & Optics

Interface Visors. SW The era’s great equalisers — computer, mechanical, and medical visors each let an untrained character attempt expert-only tasks and add +2 to the relevant skill. The catch: each fills the head slot and can’t be worn with a helmet. The Mandalorian helmet with integrated visor functions sidesteps this, at a price.

Electrobinoculars & Macrobinoculars. Magnifying optics with rangefinders — see and identify at distance. The scout’s and sniper’s eyes.

Aural Amplifier / Demolitions Sensor / Motion Visor / Lifeform Scanner. SW Specialist sensors — hear distant conversations, find planted explosives, spot movers, detect lifeforms through walls. Each fills the head slot. The infiltrator’s and EOD tech’s tools.

Targeting Visor & Neural Band. SW The targeting visor adds +1 to hit with a linked weapon (the Mandalorian HUD); the neural band defends the mind against Force manipulation (+2 vs mental effects) at the cost of a worsened damage threshold.

Stealth, Survival & Field Kit

Stealth Field Generator & Cloaking Device. SW The stealth field grants concealment (not invisibility), ending the instant you attack, take damage, or use the Force — an infiltration tool, not a combat one. The rare personal cloaking device approaches true invisibility, briefly and power-hungrily. The Exchange and GenoHaradan favour both.

Vacuum Mask, Breath Mask, Environmental Bodyglove, Rebreather. SW The spacer’s survival chain — the vacuum mask gives ten minutes of emergency air, the breath mask filters toxins, the bodyglove and rebreather sustain you in vacuum or underwater. The difference between a hull breach being a scare and a death.

Jetpack, Grapple, Ascension Gun. SW Mobility kit — the jetpack (the Mandalorian icon) grants flight in short fuel-limited bursts; the magnetic grapple and ascension gun get you up walls and across gaps. Vertical movement the enemy didn’t plan for.

HouseJetpack Agility — Jink, Thrust-Step & Boost-Retreat. A flyer on the jets is a hard target. While airborne under thrust and free to defend, you jink: +2 to Dodge as a reaction — it applies to every attack, costs no maneuver and no extra fuel (it rides the burst you are already spending). The jink falls to +1 when the load is Heavy or Sluggish and to +0 at the limit; it stacks with a Retreat but not with Acrobatic Dodge (take the better). Your Step while aloft may be up to two hexes instead of one — it is still a step, taken with Attack, Aim, Ready and the like; you still cannot also take a Move maneuver. As your defence you may instead declare a boost-retreat against one attacker: a hard powered veer for the standard Retreat bonus (+3 Dodge versus that attacker this turn) that carries you up to your climb rate in hexes. This consumes your step, works once per turn against the first attacker you break from, and burns within the active burst. Overloaded or grounded, none of it applies. SW Control is Free Fall; a fumble aloft is still a very public crash.

Survival Gear. Glow rods, fusion lanterns, survival kits, all-temperature cloaks, liquid cable — the unglamorous gear that keeps you alive between fights and gets you where the fight is.

Electronics, Slicing & Tools

Datapads, Comlinks, Holocoms. SW The galaxy’s communications and computing — the ubiquitous datapad, the standard comlink, the encrypted comlink for secure traffic, the holoprojector for face-to-face across light-years.

Security Spikes & Slicer Kits. SW The slicer’s tools — bypass locks and security systems (+2 Computer), defeat the door between you and the objective. Code cylinders carry the credentials; electronic lockpicks handle the physical locks.

Restraints, Jammers, Disguise Matrices. SW The operative’s odds and ends — binder cuffs for prisoners, frequency jammers for electronic warfare, the rare holographic disguise matrix for changing your face. Restraining bolts control droids; holocrons (Force-locked) hold the secrets of the Jedi and Sith.

Codex IX

Cybernetics
— in Detail

Neural implants · sensory & organ · prosthetics

Beyond the catalogue. SW The full roster of standard implants — neural chips, sensory and organ replacements, prosthetic limbs — with their effects, costs, install classes, and the rules and rolls governing surgery and the costs of augmentation, all live in Part IX. This Codex does not restate them. What follows is the deep end: the internal, life-changing augmentations that rebuild the body itself, and the master cost table that prices the whole field.

Internal & Life-Changing Cybernetics

Beneath the skin. The implants above mostly replace what was lost or bolt capability onto the nervous system. The augmentations below rebuild the body itself — muscle, bone, and frame — into something stronger than flesh allows. These are the augmentations that change a life: they turn a baseline being into a juggernaut, and they exact the steepest tolls in the section that follows. All are internal — invisible until they move — and all are Class 3–4 (heavy augmentation), demanding a Biofeedback Regulator first.

Myomer Muscle-Weave. SW Synthetic muscle fibre — myomer — laced through and around the natural muscle. As it is layered, it adds raw ST: a light weave gives +1–2 ST, a heavy military weave +3–4 ST, and a full-body industrial lattice up to +6 ST (at which point the user can crush durasteel and tear a blast door). Stacks with a strong prosthetic. The body looks unchanged until it lifts a speeder. Drawback: woven muscle runs hot and hungry — heavy weaves impose Increased Consumption (more food, faster fatigue when pushed) and the deepest grades draw an extra point of the Force tax.

Skeletal Reinforcement (Bone-Lacing). SW The skeleton is infused or sheathed with a metal-ceramic weave (often a durasteel or, for the wealthy, a phrik lattice). It grants +1 to +3 DR on the torso and limbs from within, lets the frame bear the strain of heavy muscle-weave without shattering, and makes the bones themselves nearly unbreakable (no more crippling fractures from blunt trauma). The natural complement to myomer — reinforced bone is what lets a body wield borrowed strength. Drawback: heavy, raises body mass toward the Force-tax thresholds fast, and a phrik lattice costs like the rare metal it is.

Subdermal & Reflex Suite (Life-Changing Tier). SW The full enhancement package the elite assassins, Sith troopers, and corporate enforcers buy: subdermal armour weave (+2–4 DR everywhere), an integrated Reflex Booster (+1 Basic Speed & Dodge), a Cardio and Regenerative core (extra HP, 2× healing), and a Pain Suppressor. Installed together, they remake a person into a weapon. This is what “going cyborg” means — and at this point the user is typically 50%+ cybernetic, with all that implies below.

The Costs, at the Deep End

The four costs of augmentation — the Force tax (with its −1 / −2 / −3 mass thresholds), ion vulnerability, rejection-and-dependency, slicing, and the quieter cost to the self — are detailed with their rolls in Part IX. They bite hardest here: muscle-weave and bone-lacing count double toward the Force-tax thresholds and run hot and hungry, and a 50%+ cyborg is treated substantially as a droid by ion weapons. Going this deep means accepting all of it at once.

How Cybernetics Are Made & Fitted

A cybernetic passes through the same Maker’s Chain as any complex device (Skills, Part I·C and the Working-Hours Reference). The device is designed with Engineer (Cybernetics) and built with Cybernetics & Bioelectronics — circuitry by Electronics Repair (Medical), frame and servos by Machinist, exotic materials (phrik bone-lattice, masterwork limbs) refined by Metallurgy. The installation is surgery on a living patient: Surgery (the install half of Cybernetics & Bioelectronics), in a proper theatre, where a critical failure injures the patient. Internal augmentations — muscle-weave, bone-lacing, organ work — are major surgery (hours of work-time, see Part VII), and recovery is real. Arkanian and Czerka clinics do the finest, cleanest work (low Force tax, reliable integration); back-alley cyberdocs do it cheap, fast, and at a higher rejection and malfunction risk.

Cybernetics — Master Cost Table

Credit costs, consistent with the wider economy (a blaster rifle ~2,000 cr; an adrenal regulator ~6,000 cr; a scrap of beskar is effectively priceless). Clinic prices; back-alley work runs ~½ the cost at higher risk, masterwork and Arkanian work 2–5×. Points as well as credits: an implant is bought twice — in credits to obtain and install it, and in character points for the advantage it grants (a +2 ST muscle-weave costs the points of +2 ST, and so on). Its drawbacks are taken as the matching Disadvantages, which return points. The full implant point table is in the Point Ledger (Skills, Part XI).

ImplantClassEffect (brief)Cost (cr)
Biofeedback Regulator1Foundation — accept heavy augmentation12,000
Neural socket1Hosts one active neural implant / skill-chip15,000
Skill / Coordination chip1*A skill at fixed level; squad-link3,000–15,000
Sensory eye / ear (basic → multi-spectrum)2Low-light → IR/UV/thermal, HUD2,000–30,000
Respirator / synthetic organ / cyber-heart2–3Filter toxins, vacuum, resist shock/poison8,000–40,000
Reflex Booster3+1 Basic Speed & Dodge75,000
Adrenal Regulator2Once-per-fight ST/HT burst18,000
Regenerative / Cardio implant32× healing; extra HP & stamina18,000–30,000
Dermal weave (+2 DR) / subdermal plate (+4 DR)3Permanent built-in armour15,000–45,000
Myomer muscle-weave (+1→+6 ST)3–4Raw strength; runs hot & hungry30,000–250,000
Skeletal reinforcement (durasteel → phrik)3–4+1→+3 internal DR; unbreakable frame40,000–400,000
Prosthetic limb (clinic → military → masterwork)2–3Full function → +ST/Move, tools → flesh-like5,000–200,000
Full-body prosthesis4Immense power; heavy Force tax & ion risk500,000–2,000,000+
*A skill-chip is Class 1 only once a neural socket is installed. Multiply for masterwork / Arkanian work; halve (at risk) for back-alley cyberdocs. Installation surgery and recovery are extra (Part VII work-hours).
Codex X-XI

Materials, Ores &
Modifications — in Detail

Every metal & substance · accessories · manufacture templates

Saber-Resistant & Legendary Materials

Beskar (Refined / Mandalorian Iron). SW The legend. In refined, full-density form it is one of the most valuable substances in the galaxy (essentially priceless) — divides all penetrating damage by 10 (DD ÷10) — shrugs off sabers, blaster fire, and most explosives, and tolerates extreme heat and acid (DR 55-60, Part X). Found only on Mandalore and its moon Concordia; the smithing techniques are a closely-guarded Mandalorian secret passed down within the culture. Even a single small plate runs about a million credits. Brittle to other beskar weapons, and (per some Mandalorian sects) forbidden to forge into weapons at all. Heavy unless carbon-cured for lightness.

Unrefined Mandalorian Iron. SW Raw beskar ore, worked without the full Mandalorian metallurgical art — sturdy, ”a bit better than durasteel” (+2 DR), but with none of refined beskar’s saber-repelling magic. Common among the clans for everyday gear. Worthless as unworked ore to anyone lacking the technique; this is the ”Mandalorian iron” most outsiders actually encounter.

Cortosis. SW The single rarest and hardest-to-mine material in the entire galaxy — scarcer even than beskar. Woven into a weapon or armour it resists lightsabers (AD 1); in raw ore form it does something no other material can — it shorts out a lightsaber on contact, killing the blade for 1d turns. This single property makes it the most feared anti-Jedi substance there is, and the reason a cortosis-weave Echani blade is a treasured heirloom rather than standard kit. So rare that most warriors will never see a confirmed piece.

Phrik. SW A lightweight alloy that resists lightsabers (AD 1) and is considered nearly indestructible — it endures punishment that shatters lesser materials, including severe energy discharge. Its great peculiarity is obscurity: phrik is widely unknown, its properties and working closely held, so that even most weaponsmiths have never heard of it. Used in the finest electrostaffs, shockstaffs, and a handful of legendary hilts. A character who merely knows phrik exists is unusual.

Songsteel. SW A rare, light, very strong metal that resists lightsabers (AD 1) without the short-out. Prized for weapon hilts and polearm shafts that must survive blade contact. Lighter than beskar and easier to work, but still uncommon enough to mark a fine weapon. The duelist’s saber-resistant material of choice.

Sith Alchemical Metal. SW Material transmuted through Sith alchemy — saber-resistant (AD 1) and infused with the dark side, letting a dark-side wielder Deflect bolts with it as if it were a lightsaber. The process is lost to all but the deepest Sith lore; such items are relics of the old Sith Lords, not manufactured goods.

Structural & Industrial Metals

Durasteel. SW The galaxy’s workhorse structural alloy — strong, light, cheap, everywhere. Hulls, blades, armour plate, buildings. Hardened durasteel-cast armour gives DR 1 across the board. A lightsaber cuts it like any normal metal. The baseline against which exotic materials are measured.

Quadanium Steel. SW A tougher structural alloy used in heavy vehicle and starship plating (and the heaviest battle armour). Stronger than durasteel, heavier, costlier. The material of war machines and blast doors; bridges into Doc VI vehicle armour.

Agrinium. SW A rare metal notable for absorbing radiation and some energy — used in specialist shielding and the occasional energy-resistant plate. Niche but valued where radiation or energy weapons are the threat.

Dallorian Alloy. SW An ancient, near-legendary heat-resistant alloy — the stuff of old war droids and relics that endure where modern metals would melt. Effectively unobtainable as new material this era; found only in ancient constructs.

Neutronium. SW Fantastically dense and durable — used in trace amounts in the toughest constructs and as ballast/armour in capital-scale applications (Doc VI). Far too heavy and rare for ordinary personal gear; a curiosity at the personal scale.

Ultrachrome. SW A rare, expensive armour-plating alloy with a mirror-bright finish — used in some elite personal and vehicle armour for its blend of toughness and energy-deflection. A status material as much as a practical one.

Carbonite. SW Not armour but a freezing medium — used in carbonite rifles, cryoban devices, and (in deliberate, equipment-heavy process) full carbon-freezing for storage or transport. The ”frozen in carbonite” fate is a controlled industrial process, not a combat action.

Accessories

Weapon Accessories. SW Scopes and extended barrels add accuracy and range; suppressors quiet slugthrowers; custom grips add skill; improved cells and overchargers boost shots or damage (the overcharger at the cost of burning cores and overheating); hair triggers and beam splitters tune the firing; bayonets add a melee option. Off-the-shelf, one per weapon typically — except the cortosis edge, which is a rare found treatment, not a purchase.

Armour Accessories. SW Reinforcement adds DR vs a chosen type; mesh underlay resists stun; environmental seals make a suit vacuum-rated; shield integration, jetpack mounts, integrated weapons, servo upgrades, and climate control turn ordinary armour into a Mandalorian-style system. The path from ”armour” to ”armour system” — each upgrade adding cost, weight, and (for the electronic ones) a little more ion vulnerability.

Field Modifications & Custom-Crafting

What It Is. Beyond the catalogue accessories above, a character with the right shop, tools, and skill can hand-tune any weapon or piece of armour — trading one quality for another to suit how they fight. This is deliberately free-form: the GM and player agree a sensible give-and-take, write it on the item, and apply it at the table. The companion character sheet gives every weapon and armour piece a ⚙ Mods line for exactly this — a note that travels with that specific item.

The Trade Principle. A good modification gives something and costs something. Tighten a blaster’s recoil compensator and the same dampening bleeds energy from the bolt — −1 Recoil, −4 damage. Shorten a rifle to a carbine for handling and you lose range and accuracy. Up-armour a suit’s torso and it gains weight and encumbrance. The GM sets the exchange rate; the guiding rule is that no single mod should be pure upside, and the swing should stay within roughly a die or a couple of points so it tunes a weapon rather than replacing it.

Crafting It. Treat a modification as an Armoury (the relevant specialty) or appropriate Engineer roll, with time and a workshop set by scope: minutes-to-an-hour at a field kit for a simple swap, a proper bench and hours for anything structural. A failure wastes materials; a critical failure damages the item. Once installed, the change is permanent until re-worked — record it on the item’s Mods line so the table always sees the adjusted profile.

Manufacture Templates

What Templates Are. SW Templates capture the unique craft of the galaxy’s cultures and corporations — each with a clear benefit and a clear drawback, fully tabulated in Part XI. Mandalorian gear is endlessly modifiable but loses its mods if disabled; Echani gear is light and deadly but fragile; Verpine gear resists ion but demands its makers’ physiology; Rakatan relics are simply superior but alien; Sith alchemical weapons are saber-proof and dark. A templated item is always Rare — a prized find or mission reward, never everyday kit, and limited to one template (with the prototype, quick-draw, and cortosis/phrik exceptions). See Part XI for each template’s exact benefit and drawback.

Fin

End of the
Compendium

Every item, described · every rule, defined · ready for play

That is the complete personal-scale armoury of the Old Republic — every weapon, every armour, every device and material, with its numbers in the catalogue (Parts I-XI), its meaning in the Jargon Index at the front, its full description here in the Codex, and the quick-reference playbook in Appendix A. Vehicles and starships live in Doc VI; droids and the Force have their own books to come. Calibrate the numbers to your table, and may the fights be worthy of the gear.

Appendix A

The Compendium
Playbook

The interaction matrix · counter-loadouts · price reference · faction kits

The lookup layer. When a character goes shopping, loots a body, or sizes up a foe, this is the page to open.

① The Counter-Loadout Table — “If your foe has…”

Foe’s defenseWhat it stopsBring this instead
Personal energy shieldAll energy (blasters, sonic, ion)Slugthrower, needler, ripper, vibroblade — kinetic ignores shields.
Energy-weave (fiber/mesh/reflec)Blasters & energyVibroblade, slug, flechette — kinetic guts low kinetic-DR weave.
A lightsaber (deflects bolts)Blaster bolts (deflected)Sonic weapon (bends around), or melee with cortosis blade.
Heavy / battle plateLight fireHeavy blaster (AD 3), disruptor, anti-materiel slug.
Droids / war droids / cyborgsConventional fire (somewhat)Ion weapons & grenades — AD 5 vs mechanical.
Refined beskarNearly everythingDisruptor, saber thrust (AD 10), heavy weapons, or don’t fight head-on.
Cortosis blade (parries saber)Your lightsaber (shorts out)Blasters, slugs — don’t rely on the saber.
A fleeing target—Carbonite rifle, cryoban/adhesive grenade — immobilise.
Someone who can’t be left as a body—Sonic disruptor (atomises) — if you can stomach it.

Disruptor3-6d×2(10) cor ×4Everything (incl. beskar)(rare/illegal — its own problem)

④ Kolto / Medical Quick Reference SW

ItemHealingCostSaturation Cost
Bacta tankRecovery ×5facilityBypasses cap (slow)
Emergency kolto patch1d+2/turn × 3 turns230⅓ daily (3 = full day)
Kolto tankRecovery ×6facility (Manaan)Bypasses cap (slow)
Military kolto injector3d+6/turn × 3 turns1,800Full daily (locks out all)
Standard kolto medpack2d+4/turn × 3 turns750½ daily (2 = full day)
House Rule: one tier-worth of kolto/bacta per day. Regenerative implant = +1 tier. Over-saturation = HT or kolto poisoning.

⑤ Faction Loadouts SW

FactionTypical Kit
Republic TrooperBlaster rifle/carbine, Republic trooper armour, kolto medpacks, comlink, frag/ion grenades.
JediLightsaber, combat robes or armourweave, minimal gear. The Force (Doc IV).
Jedi SentinelLightsaber + cortosis vibroblade backup, weave armour, security spike — built to fight without the Force.
Sith WarriorLightsaber (synth-crystal), Sith battle armour or alchemical war blade, dark-side gear.
Sith InquisitorLightsaber or saberstaff, light robes, Force focus; sometimes lanvarok.
Sith TrooperBlaster rifle, Sith trooper armour, ion weapons for droids, grenades.
MandalorianNeo-Crusader armour (advanced/sealed; beskar if clan-blessed), assault rifle, vibroblade, thermal detonators, jetpack, wrist weapons, magnetic grapple.
Exchange operativeHold-out blaster, vibroknife, needler with poison, stealth field, Exchange light armour.
GenoHaradan assassinGenoHaradan-template gear, poison darts, sonic disruptor, stealth/cloak, neural band.
Czerka securityCorporate blasters, Czerka security armour, standardised; whatever Czerka makes that quarter.
Bounty hunterWhatever works — often Mandalorian-pattern: armour, varied weapons for varied bounties, capture gear (carbonite, stun, binders).
Chiss AscendancyCharric rifle and pistol, Chiss-pattern body armour and a sealed cold-suit, advanced sensor visor, encrypted comlink. Disciplined, sensor-led tactics over brute force.
Hutt Cartel enforcerHeavy blaster pistol or carbine, light battle armour or an armoured coat, vibroblade, carbonite or spice-gas grenade, kajidic comlink. Muscle equipped to whatever the boss funds that week.

Weapon & Armour Components — Slots, Attachments & Modifications

Every weapon and suit of armour is a platform with component slots. The catalogue below carries 120 weapon components and 16 armour components across five tiers. Roughly half are pure upgrades; half are trade-offs — a buff in exchange for a real drawback. On the character sheet, each item's 🔧 Modifications panel shows a dropdown per slot: pick a component and its stat deltas, weight, cost and effect text apply automatically. Custom attachments can still be written freehand alongside them.

TierDescriptionExamples
CivilianCommodity, legal almost anywhere. Mostly small bonuses or pure-trade modules.Reflex sight, foregrip, bipod, carry strap, ergonomic grip, serrated edge, low-light visor.
PoliceLicensed civilian or law-enforcement grade. Moderate bonuses, occasional drawbacks.Telescopic scope, suppressor, heavy match barrel, folding stock, Rubat crystal, shoto hilt.
MilitaryRestricted; lethal trade-offs in favour of capability.Sniper scope, thermal optic, smart-link HUD, high-output pack, dual-phase emitter, extended hilt, beskar core.
Exotic / Black-marketRare, expensive, often Force- or exotic-tech-based. The biggest swings — and the biggest costs.Force-attuned sight, inertial compensator, cortosis weave, Solari crystal, krayt dragon pearl, stealth field skin.
UniqueSingular artefacts. No price, no replica; they are found — or they find you.Heart of the Guardian, Mantle of the Force, Ghost-fire crystal.

The slot rule. Each component lists a slot (crystal, grip, core, power, sight, barrel, under, stock, frame, edge, pommel, haft, plating, underlay, systems, visor…). A weapon or armour piece carries one component per slot — fitting a new one replaces the old. This is enforced on the sheet: the slot dropdowns swap, never stack. Compatibility gates what is offered (a foregrip won't show on a lightsaber; a kyber crystal won't show on a blaster).

Factory standards. Components make up the weapon — every integral slot ships with a factory-standard part at zero modifier (Iron sights, a standard barrel, a standard hilt, an Ilum kyber, factory plating…). The catalogue stat-lines are the standard build; components below are departures from it. The sheet shows each slot's standard by name when nothing else is socketed, and selecting the standard again restores stock. Optional accessory slots (under-barrel, strap, aesthetic) default to none fitted. Where a slot isn't listed for a weapon or armour family at all, it isn't modifiable on that platform.

Prerequisites. Components state their prerequisites and the sheet flags them (⚠ requirement, ⚠ unmet). A dual-grip saberstaff hilt requires saberstaff form training (Doc VI); an extended hilt requires ST 11+; the Solari crystal answers only a strongly light-aligned hand. Prerequisites are GM-adjudicated — the sheet warns, the table rules.

Install classes. Class 1 — field swap: a Ready manoeuvre to minutes, no roll (training emitter, carry strap, power pack). Class 2 — workshop: hours and an Armoury roll (crystals, hilt configurations, barrels, armour plating). Class 3 — specialist: days, Armoury−2 or a professional (frame internals, servo-assist, stealth field skin). Unless noted, components here are Class 2.

Stacking with templates & treatments. Slot components stack freely with one manufacture template and with material treatments per their own rules (see Material Treatments and Manufacture Templates, this Part). Slot components stack with each other only across different slots.

Stat fields. Components may modify any combination of: Dmg±, damage dice±, dmg-per-die±, Skill±, Acc±, Parry±, Reach±, ST±, Rcl±, RoF±, Shots±, Wt±, Bulk±, DR±, Cost — and some carry a toggleable mode (the Ghost-fire crystal's Unseen blade; a dual-phase emitter's reach shift). Every numeric field stays hand-editable on the sheet for full custom work.

Drawbacks are intentional. "Overcharged cell" pumps +2 damage per shot — but burns out on a 17–18. "Suppressor" gives +3 Stealth (Hearing) — but costs 1 damage. "High-output pack" adds +1 RoF — and 1.2 kg and a point of Bulk. Roughly half the catalogue carries a real cost; the GM should not waive it when the player buys the upside.

Bulk & Weight — quick reference ⓘ

StatWhat it does
BulkHow unwieldy a ranged weapon is. Apply Bulk as a penalty to Move-and-Attack, to firing from awkward positions, and to Holdout / concealment. Components can worsen or improve it; melee weapons use ST and Reach instead.
WeightEvery kilogram counts toward encumbrance (Doc II): exceed your Basic Lift bands and Move and Dodge drop. Component weight deltas are added automatically on the sheet.
Weapon classBulk
Hold-out pistol (<1 kg)−1
Pistol (1–3 kg)−2
Heavy pistol (>3 kg)−3
Rifle, carbine, shotgun, crossbow−4 (−5 if ≥6 kg)
Launcher, projector−5 (−6 if ≥9 kg)
Emplaced / gunner weapons−6 (−8 if ≥15 kg)

Catalogue weapons print their exact Bulk where listed (the slugthrower & kinetic tables); where a table omits the column, derive it from the band above — the sheet ships every ranged weapon pre-filled by this rule.

Lightsaber components — the focusing crystal catalogue lives with the saber rules (Lightsaber Crystals, Part II), and hilt configurations (shoto, curved, duelling foil, saberstaff, extended/great) with Lightsabers & Variants. Saber emitters, lenses and cells appear under Melee components below. Lightsabers carry crystal, grip, emitter & lens, power-cell and pommel slots only — a blade of coherent plasma has no edge, haft or frame to treat.

Optics & Sights — slot: sight

Sniper scope (4-12x)Military+3 Acc · +1 Rcl+15,400—+3 Acc; +1 Rcl (heavy on muzzle); useless in melee range.
Thermal opticMilitary+1 Acc+0.76,600—+1 Acc; ignores darkness up to total. Doesn’t help vs reflective armour.
Auto-targeterExotic+1 Acc+0.513,000—+1 Acc; “Aim” manoeuvre is free; jams 1d turns on a fumble.
Force-attuned sightExotic+2 Acc+0.414,000—+2 Acc — but costs 1 FP per shot. Force users only.

Short / carbine barrelCivilian-1 Acc-0.4840—+1 Holdout; range ½D ×0.8. Cheap concealment trade.
Sound compensatorCivilian-1 Rcl+0.3780—-1 Rcl; louder report, -2 to Stealth (Hearing).
Vented barrel shroudCivilian—+0.4570—Run hot longer; no penalty for sustained automatic fire. No combat bonus.
Flash hiderPolice—+0.2450—No muzzle flash; foes can’t use flash to locate you in dim light.
Heavy match barrelPolice+1 Acc · -1 Rcl+12,300—-1 Rcl; +1 Wt. Quality barrel.
Suppressor / silencerPolice-1 dmg+0.51,800—+3 to Stealth Hearing rolls. -1 damage. Heats fast — 1 burst before glow.
Discharge focuserMilitary+1 dmg+0.53,300—+1 die to damage on first shot of an encounter; refocus = 5 sec.

Power Cells, Packs & Magazines — slot: power

ComponentTierEffectsWt (kg)CostPrereqNotes
Compressed gas magCivilian+10 shots · scales by frame †+0.2290—Compressed feed sized to the frame; safer than overcharging.
Extended power cellCivilian+10 shots · scales by frame †+0.5420—Bigger cell, sized to the frame (max +10 shots).
Slugthrower magazine extenderCivilian+10 shots · scales by frame †+0.5210—Extended box magazine sized to the frame. Sticks out.
Stable / safe cellCivilian-1 RoF+0.4540—-1 RoF; no critical-fail burnout. Civilian / training pack.
Standard power cellCivilian——found—Baseline cell. No effect.
Stun setting / moduleCivilian—+0.2780—Toggle to stun setting: damage becomes fatigue / HT-1 to stay conscious. -1 dmg to vehicles.
Tracer roundsCivilian—+0.1210—+1 to follow-up shots after first hit; you glow too (-2 Stealth).
Anti-personnel splatter roundsPolice+1 dmg+0.2420—+1 dmg vs unarmored; halve dmg vs DR 4+.
High-capacity drumPolice+30 shots · +1 Rcl · scales by frame †+1.5960—Huge mag sized to the frame; +1 Rcl from weight imbalance.
Stabilised power cellPolice—+0.13,200—Never falters: ignore environmental flame-out (rain, vacuum-adjacent, ion wash).
Armour-piercing roundsMilitary—+0.2780—Divide target DR by 2; -1 dmg.
Conductive cell (overheat)Military+2 RoF+0.44,200—+2 RoF; on crit-fail, weapon overheats and is hot to touch for 1d turns.
Diatium overcharge cellMilitary+1 dmg+0.16,800—+1 dmg; on any critical miss the cell shorts — blade gutters out, 1 Ready to reignite.
Disruptor cell (ionising)Military+1 dmg+0.611,000—Bypasses energy DR; 1d FP damage to wielder if used 3+ turns in a row.
High-output packMilitary+1 RoF · Bulk -1 · scales by frame †+1.24,400—+1 RoF; weight scales with the frame and Bulk worsens by 1. Runs hot — a malfunction roll of 17–18 vents the pack.
Overcharged cellMilitary+2 dmg · -15 shots · scales by frame †+0.32,700—Hotter shots, fewer of them; burns out on a to-hit roll of 17-18.
Backpack power feedExotic+2 RoF · +200 shots · Bulk -2 · scales by frame †+612,500ST 11+Cable-fed backpack cell; output scales with the frame. +6 kg, Bulk −2, and the cable can be cut (DR 4, 2 HP).

ComponentTierEffectsWt (kg)CostPrereqNotes

BipodCivilian-1 Rcl+0.6330—-1 Rcl when deployed prone or against cover. 2 sec to set up.
ForegripCivilian-1 Rcl+0.3290—-1 Rcl in standing or close. Slight weight penalty.
Tactical flashlightCivilian—+0.2120—Illuminate 30m. -2 to user’s Stealth (Vision). Foes see you.

Underbarrel shotgunPolice—+1.52,700—2-shot 12g; backup short-range close-quarters attack.

ComponentTierEffectsWt (kg)CostPrereqNotes

HK-series targeting coreUnique+1 skill · +1 Acc+0.4foundUnique — salvaged assassin-droid cognition (GM)+1 Acc, +1 skill against organic targets ("meatbags"). The core occasionally offers unsolicited advice on maximising lethality.

† Archetype fit — the frame decides the magnitude

A heavy pistol's extended cell is not a carbine's. Components marked scales by frame † resize their effect to the weapon class they are fitted to; the sheet applies the fitted values automatically and tags the install. Extended cells are capped at +10 shots on any frame. Frames follow the Bulk bands: hold-out (<1 kg pistol) · pistol (1–3 kg) · heavy pistol (>3 kg) · long-arm (rifle/carbine/shotgun/crossbow) · launcher · gunner.

ComponentHold-outPistolHeavy pistolLong-armLauncherGunner

ComponentTierEffectsWt (kg)CostPrereqNotes

Phrik laminateExotic+2 Parry+0.419,000—+2 Parry; unbreakable; +5000cr cost effective.
Taung war-edgeUnique+2 dmg—foundUnique — ancestral Mandalorian relic (GM)+2 dmg; an edge ground by the Taung before Mandalore the First. Counts as unrefined beskar for parrying sabers, once per scene.

ComponentTierEffectsWt (kg)CostPrereqNotes

Extended hilt (great saber)Military+1 damage dice · +1 reach · min ST +2+15,200ST 11+Two-handed greatsaber (8d→9d): +1 damage die, +1 hex reach; +2 min ST, ungainly in confined spaces.
Grip-mag retentionMilitary—+0.4780—+3 to retain; on disarm fail, weapon stuck to user — bound action to release.
Power-assist gripMilitary+1 dmg · min ST -1+0.64,200—Counts as ST+2 for swing damage; -1 ST required to wield. Cell-powered.
Force-attuned hiltExotic+1 Parry—8,700—+1 Parry to Force-Sensitive wielder. No effect for non-Force users.
Hilt of Exar KunUnique—+0.5foundUnique — one exists (GM)The first dual-blade, forged by the Dark Lord himself. Functions as a Dual-grip saberstaff hilt — and the hilt remembers the form: its wielder needs no saberstaff training. It whispers, faintly.

Pommels — slot: pommel

ComponentTierEffectsWt (kg)CostPrereqNotes
Counterweighted pommelCivilian+1 Parry+0.3240—+1 Parry in 2-handed grip; off-balance for 1H use (-1 Acc to throw).
Spiked pommelCivilian—+0.2150—Adds thr+1 cr backstrike attack at -2.
Stunner pommelPolice—+0.31,100—1/scene: stun pulse on butt-strike (HT vs stun for 1d sec).
Cortosis pommel capExotic+1 Parry+0.35,700—Reduces lightsaber damage by 1d on a successful Parry.

Hafts — slot: haft

ComponentTierEffectsWt (kg)CostPrereqNotes
Reach extender (haft)Civilian-1 Parry · +1 reach+0.4290—+1 reach; -1 Parry inside reach C.

Saber Internals — Emitters & Lenses — slot: core

ComponentTierEffectsWt (kg)CostPrereqNotes
Training emitterCivilian——900—Blade delivers stun-only crushing contact — bruises and lessons, never wounds. Swap freely (Class 1 install).
Dual-phase emitter (saber)Military—+0.210,000—Toggle reach C/1/2 as a Ready manoeuvre. Costs 1 FP on switch.
Precision focusing lensMilitary-1 dmg · +1 skill—7,200—Tighter beam: +1 skill, −1 dmg. A surgeon’s blade, not a butcher’s.

Straps & Carry — slot: strap

ComponentTierEffectsWt (kg)CostPrereqNotes
Carry strap (slung)Civilian—+0.260—+1 to retain; no combat bonus.
Concealing wrap (sheath)Police—+0.2290—+1 Holdout; -2 to Fast-Draw.

Aesthetic & Presentation — slot: aesthetic

ComponentTierEffectsWt (kg)CostPrereqNotes
Etched filigreeCivilian——2,400—+1 Status display; multiplies sticker cost. No combat bonus.
House crestCivilian——1,200—+1 reaction with allied House; -1 with rivals.
Mando-style paintCivilian——450—+1 reaction with Mandalorians (factional). -1 with Republic patrols.
Trophy notchesCivilian——found—+1 to Intimidation (visible kills).
Worn / battered lookCivilian——found—-1 to Merchant resale; +1 to Streetwise reactions.

Mandalorian doctrine. To a Mandalorian, armour is a weapons platform: mainline rigs accept wrist mounts (rocket racks, flamers, blades, grapples) and back mounts (jetpacks, rocket packs) on top of the standard four slots. Heavy and advanced suits spend that mass budget internally instead — no external hardpoints, but a second internal systems bay for stims, battle-nets, servos and life support. Every component has real weight, and it all counts toward encumbrance.

Armour slot capacity. What a suit can socket depends on what it is — a strong standard buys few sockets. Beskar IS the plating; robes have nothing to bolt plate onto; a powered rig takes the full kit. The sheet only offers the slots a family accepts.

Armour familySlots acceptedWhy
Battle, Powered, Faction suitsplating · underlay · systems · visor (4)Modular military rigs — built to be rebuilt.
Mandalorian mainline (Neo-Crusader & kin)plating · underlay · systems · visor · wrist · back (6)Armour as a weapons platform — wrist and back hardpoints are the culture.
Mandalorian heavy / advanced (Heavy Neo-Crusader, refined beskar full suit)plating/underlay · systems · internal aux · visor (4–5)The mass budget is spent: no external mounts, but a second internal systems bay.
Beskar plate (single piece)underlay · visor · wrist · back (4)Light plate — the mounts bolt straight to the beskar.
Civilian (vests, jumpsuits, flight suits)underlay · systems · visor (3)Light shells take liners and gadgets, not plate.
Reflec & Weave (mesh, fiber, reflec)underlay · systems · visor (3)The coating/weave is the protection — re-plating ruins it.
Robes & clothing (Jedi robes, zeyd-cloth)underlay · systems (2)Fabric. Nothing to bolt onto; everything to hide within.
Mandalorian iron plate (unrefined)underlay · visor (2)Very strong standard, few sockets: the plate is the armour and tolerates no drilling.
Matrix armour (exotic lattice)underlay · systems (2)A sealed crystalline weave — strong, and stubborn about it.

Armour components. Armour carries four slots — plating, underlay, systems, visor/helm — one component each, same install-class and stacking rules as weapons. DR effects split by type where noted (E = energy, K = kinetic; Split DR).

Armour — Plating — slot: plating

ComponentTierEffectsWt (kg)CostPrereqNotes
Duraplast laminationCivilian+1 DR(E) · +1 DR(K)+1.51,600—+1/+1 DR. Cheap, universal, slightly stiff.
Ablative overlayPolice+2 DR(E)+22,800—+2 energy DR; each hit that burns through chips it — after 3 such hits the overlay is spent.
Energy-dispersing meshMilitary—+17,600—Halves blaster AD against this armour (½-energy rule, Doc IX) if it doesn’t already.
Reactive kinetic plateMilitary+3 DR(K)+35,400—+3 kinetic DR; bulky — −1 Dodge while fitted.
Unrefined beskar trimMilitary+2 DR(E) · +2 DR(K)+49,000—+2/+2 DR. Mandalorian iron, unrefined grade — heavy and proud.
Cortosis-weave overlayExotic—+238,000—Anti-saber lattice: once per scene a lightsaber striking this armour shorts out for 1d turns (Doc IX cortosis rules). Not a purchase — found or commissioned.
Crystalline carbonite plateExotic+4 DR(E) · +1 DR(K)+429,000—+4 energy / +1 kinetic DR; shimmer-sheen — −1 to Stealth (Vision).
Phrik lattice plateExotic+2 DR(E) · +2 DR(K)+346,000—+2/+2 DR and the plated locations resist lightsabers (treat saber AD as 1 vs this plating).
Ordo ancestral plateUnique+3 DR(E) · +3 DR(K)+3foundUnique — Clan Ordo heirloom (GM)+3/+3 DR. Beskar plates carried through four generations of Ordo crusaders. Clan Ordo will notice you wearing it.

Armour — Underlay — slot: underlay

ComponentTierEffectsWt (kg)CostPrereqNotes
Padded concussion linerCivilian—+1900—+2 to HT checks vs knockdown and stun from blunt trauma.
Flex-weave jointsPolice—-13,000—−1 kg and removes up to −1 of armour DX penalty (min 0).
Thermal regulation suitPolice—+12,400—Climate comfort −30°C to +50°C; +2 vs heat/cold environment checks.
Ablative stealth coatMilitary—+0.59,600—+2 Stealth; sensor returns read one size class smaller. Burns off after 3 energy hits.
Bio-stabiliser weaveMilitary—+18,400—+2 to all HT rolls vs wounds, poison and stun while worn; auto-tourniquets a crippled limb.
Reactive gel layerMilitary+2 DR(K)+27,200—+2 kinetic DR; halves knockback distance.
Vacuum-seal underlayMilitary—+26,200Sealed helmet/visor fitted10 minutes hard-vacuum survival; requires a sealed helmet system.
Force-insulating linerExotic—+132,000—Ysalamir-derived weave: +2 to resist hostile Force powers targeting the wearer; the wearer's own Force rolls suffer −1 (the silence cuts both ways).

Armour — Systems — slot: systems

ComponentTierEffectsWt (kg)CostPrereqNotes
Antitox autosystemPolice—+15,800—Auto-administers antidotes: +4 HT vs poison/disease; 3 charges per fight.
Medisystem injectorPolice—+15,200—Auto-injects: 2 stim doses; as a free action once per fight, heal 2 HP or shrug one stun.
Jetpack mount & unitMilitary—+918,000Battle or powered rigFlight Move 12 in 20-second bursts (10 bursts per fuel cell); Piloting (Jetpack) to manoeuvre under fire. Mandalorian classic.
Personal shield socketMilitary—+0.54,800—Hardpoint for a personal energy shield (shield bought separately, Doc IX).
Servo-assist frameMilitary—+514,000Powered or heavy rig+2 ST for lifting/carry (not damage); armour counts 6 kg lighter for encumbrance.
Slicer interface suiteMilitary—+1.511,000—+2 to Computer Hacking / Electronics (Security) made through the suit's probes; stores 3 spikes.
Wrist weapon mountMilitary—+16,800—Integrates a hold-out weapon, dart launcher or flame jet (weapon bought separately); Fast-Draw automatic.
Cloaking mantleExotic—+254,000Power source (battle/powered rig)Active camouflage: +5 Stealth while moving slowly, invisible to droids at >10 m. Drops for the scene if you attack; ion shorts it.
Repulsor stabilisersExotic—+321,000—Micro-repulsors: ignore knockdown from damage, fall safely up to 20 m, +1 to Acrobatics.
Stealth field skinExotic—+1.521,000—+3 Stealth while moving slowly; shorts out for the scene if you attack or are hit by ion.
Shadow-weave of the CovenantUnique—+0.5foundUnique — Jedi Covenant relic (GM)+4 Stealth while stationary or moving slowly; the wearer reads as a faint blur to Force senses (−2 to Sense rolls against them).
Mandalorian hardpoints — wrist mounts (mainline rigs & light beskar plate)
Wrist rocket rackMilitary—+29,500—Mounts the Wrist rocket (Mandalorian) weapon (bought separately); fire as a Ready-free attack once per turn.
Wrist flamerMilitary—+2.57,200—1-metre cone, 2d burn, 6 charges; ignores Deflect (too close, too wide).
Retractable wrist bladePolice—+13,400—Concealed vibroblade (thr+1(2) imp, Knife skill); Fast-Draw automatic; +4 Holdout.
Grapple & line spoolPolice—+22,800—20 m micro-line, 200 kg rated; swing/ascend at Move 4; doubles as a snare (DX roll to entangle).
Wrist shield projectorExotic—+1.516,000—Snap-shield: once per turn block one ranged attack with a Block roll at skill 12; 10 charges per fight.
Mandalorian hardpoints — back mounts
Jetpack mount & unitMilitary—+918,000Battle or powered rigNow a back-mount component. Flight as listed; the unit's 9 kg counts toward encumbrance.
Rocket pack (assault)Military—+1826,000ST 11+Flight Move 12 in 30-second burns, 6 burns; a burn-assisted charge adds +2 to slam damage.
Grav-chutePolice—+64,200—Fall any distance safely; controlled descent at Move 6; repulsor cushion recharges daily.
Supply rack & harnessCivilian—+4900—Rigid stowage: 8 kg of carried gear does not count toward encumbrance.
Internal systems (aux) — heavy & advanced rigs only
Combat stim injectorMilitary—+18,400—Twice per fight as a free action: ignore shock and Stunning for 3 turns; HT roll after each use or −1 FP.
Encrypted battle-net coreMilitary—+16,200—Squad uplink: allies share Tactics bonuses; +2 coordinated attacks; unjammable short of ion.
Life-support extenderMilitary—+35,800—Sealed 24 h: vacuum, gas, underwater; auto-antitox on a failed HT vs poison (once/day).
Servo-assist musculatureExotic—+632,000—+2 ST for lifting, climbing, grappling (not damage); halve armour weight for encumbrance.
Auto-repair nanoforgeExotic—+428,000—Restores 1 lost DR per minute out of combat; rebuilds destroyed sections overnight.

Armour — Visor & Helm — slot: visor

ComponentTierEffectsWt (kg)CostPrereqNotes
Low-light visorCivilian—+0.31,400—Negates darkness penalties up to −3 (not total dark).
Sealed breather helmPolice—+0.82,600—Filters toxins/gas (+3 HT vs inhaled); 30 min internal air.

Beyond the catalogue. Unique and Legendary artefacts of the era — named weapons, masks, armour and relics, each existing exactly once and each carrying a true cost — are not listed here. They live in the GM's sealed Vault on the character sheet's Equipment tab, and they are found, never bought.

Tier-Balanced Armour DD & Unique Properties

An expansion of Doc XII Part I−II. The Phase 4 work had DD restricted mostly to cutting-only and to a handful of master-crafted suits. The sheet's Equipment values now broaden DD to apply by tier rather than by single damage type, and introduce a structured special field for unique-armour mechanics. The compendium tables for armour are unchanged in their DR/cost values — only the DD column is widened and a new "Special" column is implied. HOUSE

The Tier Ladder for Damage Divisor (Houserule)

Tier (price band)Default DD coverageRationale
Civilian ($0–$5k)NoneCivilian clothing relies on raw DR; no special matériel.
Militia / Security / Frontier ($5k–$45k)None — premium entries get ÷2 niche (e.g. anti-energy or anti-cut)Frontier kit may be specialised but not yet master-tuned.
Line Infantry & Standard Issue ($50k–$95k)÷2 vs cut OR vs energy; top of tier reaches ÷2 vs allMass-production military plate; balanced.
Special Operations & Specialist Suits ($150k–$250k)÷2 vs all (default); cortosis ÷5 saber, phrik ÷5 saber, disruptor-shielded ÷5 disruptorBespoke spec-ops kit. Niche materials add their own ladder rung.
Master-Crafted & Modular War-Plate ($180k–$1.2M)÷2 to ÷3 vs all (default); ÷5 vs all for top-tier; ÷10 vs all for beskar suitsThe very best. Beskar is in a class of its own; Crusader / Champion plate sits just below.

Worked example — Neo-Crusader vs Heavy Blaster. Heavy blaster pistol (4d burn, AD 3, avg 14 damage) vs Neo-Crusader base (energy DR 30, DD ÷2 vs all). Effective DR 30/3 = 10. 14 − 10 = 4 penetrating. ÷2 = 2 injury. The base catalogue rule that Neo-Crusader resists blasters “just via DR” was conservative; the broadened DD better reflects that this is purpose-built war-plate. Standard blasters at AD 1 still bounce off the full DR 30 (0 penetrating), so the suit's blaster-proof feel is preserved — only heavy blasters and beyond punch through, and even then they're halved.

Structured special Field (Sheet)

Each armour entry on the sheet can now carry a structured special object with the following properties. Each is summed/applied automatically when the suit is equipped, and a banner in the Equipment tab shows the aggregate.

FieldEffect when equippedExample bearers
stealthBonus: N+N to Stealth (visual) rollsSith Shadow +6, Reconnaissance stealth +6, Bothan covert +6, GenoHaradan master +4, Sniper's ghillie +3
sensorDefeat: N+N vs sensor / motion / life-form detectionSith Shadow +6, Reconnaissance +4, Bothan +3, Calo Nord +2
forceMuffle: N+N to hide your presence in the Force / resist Force detectionSith Shadow +2, Jedi Shadow armourweave +3, Jedi Battlemaster +2, Dread Master +2
parryBonus: N+N to active Parry (defensive armourweave / duelweave)Echani Master +1, Echani Sun-Guard +1, Jedi Battlemaster +1
stBonus: N+N to effective ST while suited (powered armour)Heavy powered +5, Powered battle +3, Star Forge relic +2
sealedLife: N hoursN hours of sealed life support (vac / NBC / underwater)Heavy powered 12h, EVA hardsuit 24h, Mandalorian Protector 8h, Beskar'gam 4h
selfRepair: N HP/turnAuto-regenerates N HP of suit integrity per turnDavik's Dallorian 1, Star Forge relic 2
cortosisShort: trueOn a successful Parry of a lightsaber, roll 3d6 vs 11 — success shorts the opposing blade for 1d turns (Saber Shorted condition)Sith Shadow, Cortosis-weave assault, Cortosis battle plate, Powered cortosis battlesuit
saberResistant: trueBeskar-class — lightsaber blades glance off the plate, on top of DD÷10 vs allRefined beskar plate, Mandalorian Protector, Beskar'gam
adaptiveShift: trueOnce per turn, shift +10 DR to one phase (E or K) on a Concentrate manoeuvreChampion's adaptive war-plate
tags: [...]Lookup tags for narrative / mechanical queries (e.g. has-tag ‘stealth-field’)Every entry that carries a notable property

Stealth Field Generator (Equipment) — Default +6

The portable Stealth Field Generator gear now grants +6 to Stealth (visual) while active and moving at half Move or less (was +3). Per Doc XII Part VIII: the visual phase-shift is genuinely powerful, but the suit shimmers on a critical observer roll, has minutes of battery, and announces you to any active sensor sweep. Built-in stealth fields on suits like Sith Shadow / Reconnaissance / Bothan now match this baseline.

Unique Weapon Properties (sheet `special` field)

Weapons can also carry mechanical specials:

  • woundMult — explicit wound multiplier (e.g. Disruptors ×4)
  • bypassDeflect — defeats Lightsaber Deflection (sonic weapons; sound bends around the blade)
  • vsEnergyShield — treats personal energy shield SR as 0 (sonic, vibro)
  • bypassEnergyShield — same as above (vibroknife, electroknife)
  • vsDroid / vsOrganic: false — ion weapons devastate droids/cyborgs/powered armour, near-harmless to organics
  • stunOption — weapon has a toggleable stun setting (Force pike, Electrostaff, Shockstaff, Stun baton)
  • saberResistant / saberResistantHaft — weapon's blade or haft resists lightsabers (Beskad, Electrostaff phrik shaft)
  • cortosisShort — on a successful parry of a lightsaber, can short the opposing blade (Cortosis vibrosword)
  • grappleCrush + bypassArmour: 'crush-in-grip' — armour penalty ignored when crushing held opponent (Crushgaunt)
  • heavyImpact — weapon delivers Heavy Impact (knockback + concussion) regardless of DR (Wrist rocket)
  • cone — area-effect cone weapon (Wrist flamethrower)
  • autofire — weapon can autofire mode (Ion carbine)
