  • deafenOnHit — on a strong hit, target rolls HT or is deafened (Aurial blaster)
  • tags: [...] — categorical lookup tags

Sheet integration. All of the above is wired live in KOTOR_Sheet_v31.html: aggregate auto-bonus banner in the Equipment tab armour header, per-row special summary in the DD/½eAD column, broadened DD reflected in the resolver's defend mode (which already auto-scans the wearer's armour for the highest applicable DD). The condition tracker (Doc XII Part IV) carries the Saber Shorted + Cortosis Edge Ready conditions for when these mechanics fire in play.

Armour Brands & Manufacturer Roster

Every armour entry on the sheet now carries a brand field naming the manufacturer or faction that produces it. Unmarked civilian / generic kit is attributed to an in-lore source of the era where possible. The brand is shown as a colour-coded pill in the Equipment tab, and the picker can be filtered by brand. HOUSE

Major Faction & Manufacturer Roster

Brand / FactionColourDoctrine & range
Republic Army / Navy / SpecForceRepublic blueMass-issue military line. Mk I → Vanguard → Heavy → SpecForce. New: Pathfinder Corps light armour, Marine boarding hardsuit, Senate Guard ceremonial, Medical Corps suit, Engineer Corps hardsuit, Navy officer's dress. Doctrine: standardisation, parts interchangeability, no exotic materials.
Sith Empire / Krath / Dread MastersSith redSith trooper Mk I → Mk III "Dread", Sith assault, Sith Lord battle armour, Marauder war-harness, Sith Shadow (Inquisitorius), Dread Master regalia. Krath cult militia / battle / sorcerer war-plate. Doctrine: hierarchy of power, force-attuned plating, intimidation as armour grade.
Mandalorian clansMando goldIron plate (unrefined) → Neo-Crusader light/base/heavy/advanced → Cassus Fett pattern → Crusader → Protector → Beskar'gam. Doctrine: clan-forged, every smith trained in beskar work, the suit IS the warrior.
Jedi OrderJedi greenTravel-cloth → combat robes → Zeyd-cloth armourweave → Shadow armourweave → Battlemaster armourweave. Doctrine: armour that does not announce itself; humility as design principle.
Czerka CorporationCzerka orangeCzerka security-line, frontier marshal's plate, phrik combat suit (license). Doctrine: arms dealer to anyone with credits; rugged, mass-produced, no ideology.
AratechAratech tealAR-77 / AR-220 / AR-440 powered series, Stalker recon, reinforced suit, sealed environment, hazard armour, prototype hardsuit, phrik line (joint). Doctrine: high-tech materials, prototype-tier R&D, premium powered platforms.
Verpine Hive (Roche)Verpine greenMatrix armour, fibre/zal-alloy, masterwork hardsuit, dispersion hardsuit, matrix-weave command, disruptor-shielded, exoskeletal frame. Doctrine: insectoid masterwork — molecular-level tolerances no human can match.
Echani Sciences / Sun GuardEchani silver-violetFibre suit, battle armour, ceremonial, Master's duelweave, Sun-Guard war-plate. Doctrine: armour for duelists — light, fast, parry-augmenting.
Cinnagar WeaveworksCinnagar yellowThe fibre/mesh/weave/war-weave family. Doctrine: anti-blaster weaves at industrial scale; the Old Republic's answer to massed blaster fire.
Underworld brandsUnderworld violetBothan Spynet covert suits, GenoHaradan blade/master suits, Calo Nord signature kit, Davik Kang's Dallorian-alloy suit, Exchange enforcer kit. Doctrine: stealth, signature aesthetics, mostly assassin / fixer / smuggler trade.
Corporate arms (BlasTech, Merr-Sonn, SoroSuub, Kuat, Tendrando, Adascorp, Sienar, Salus)Corporate blue-greyBlasTech sentinel/tactical/riot lines, Merr-Sonn Battlemaster/Defender/Shock-Trooper/Bounty/LP-9 Blast Vest, Adascorp Reflec Mk I/III, Sienar Agrinium, SoroSuub Spacer, Kuat Systems flight kit, Tendrando concealable, Salus Corp exec. Doctrine: capital-class arms makers; no ideology, just product line.
Rakata Star ForgeRakata violetStar Forge-pattern relics — self-regenerating, ancient, irreplaceable.
Civilian / Off-brand / Independent / Tapani / Tarisian / Eriadu / Thyrsus / FrontierGreyOff-the-rack civilian wear, frontier hunter craft, independent modular smiths, Tapani dueling outfitters, Tarisian Arena Champions guild, Eriadu Authority tactical-line, Thyrsus Sun-Guard. Doctrine: variable — local tradition or local need.

New Republic Issue Category (sheet v32)

The "Republic Issue" category collects six new Republic-faction entries to round out the line:

  • Republic Navy officer's dress (DR 4/4, 1,800 cr) — armourweave-lined dress uniform; +1 Status in Republic circles.
  • Republic engineer's hardsuit (DR 16/14, 12,000 cr) — Corps of Engineers field kit; +2 Mechanic and Explosives (Demolition); sealed 30 min.
  • Republic medical corps suit (DR 10/10, 6,500 cr) — quick-access kolto bays; +2 First Aid and Physician.
  • Republic Pathfinder light armour (DR 18/16, 42,000 cr) — scout class; +2 Stealth, +1 vs sensors, DD÷2 vs energy.
  • Republic ceremonial Senate Guard armour (DR 22/20, 55,000 cr) — old-pattern Guard armour; +1 Parry, sealed 1 hr, DD÷2 vs all.
  • Republic Marine assault hardsuit (DR 32/30, 110,000 cr) — Navy Marine boarding kit; sealed 6 hr, DD÷2 vs all, vacuum-rated.

Sheet integration. The Equipment tab Armour section now shows a colour-coded brand pill below each armour name (the colour maps to the faction's house colour), and a Brand filter dropdown above the Armour list lets you narrow the picker to a single manufacturer's line. All 121 armour entries are now branded — zero unbranded fall-throughs. Unmarked entries default to "Off-brand / generic" or an in-lore heuristic ("Jedi Order (uncategorised)" if the notes mention Jedi, etc.).

Cross-Document References

Combat mechanics: Docs I-II. Damage, DR, wounds, healing, ion/sonic/disruptor rules, the kolto recovery math: Doc III. Vehicles, starships, hyperdrives, named ship stat blocks, Basilisk war droids: Doc VI. Force powers, lightsaber construction & crystal attunement, the cybernetics-vs-Force tax in full, Sith alchemy: Doc IV. Droids — HK-series, war droids, astromechs, surgical droids — get their own dedicated book. This compendium covers personal-scale gear: what a character wields, wears, carries, and has installed.

Document VII · B

Trade, Goods & the Galactic Economy

The credit · cost of living · the goods index · cargo & speculation

Overview

What this covers. The credit, the cost of living, the goods index, the price-and-availability system, and cargo speculation for free-trader crews.

How to use it. Reach for it when money, downtime spending, or a cargo run matters; the price modifiers feed directly into the Equipment and Vehicles documents.

The Economy

Trade & the
Galactic Economy

Exports & imports · who controls what · scarcity & abundance · the price & availability system · the black market

A blaster has a price on a table; the galaxy has a price on everything. This chapter is the economy those numbers live inside — what flows where, who owns the flow, what is genuinely scarce, and the simple system that turns a single listed cost into the right cost for this world, in these times. It governs every material and every buyable thing in the compendium: the figure printed elsewhere is the baseline, and what follows is how the galaxy bends it.

The Galactic Economy SW

The Credit. The Republic credit is the galaxy’s coin, backed by the Core’s vast economy and accepted almost everywhere — though on some Hutt and frontier worlds it trades at a discount, or hard goods and favours talk louder than currency. The Republic mints it; that power is itself a weapon.

The Lanes Are the Arteries. SW Commerce rides the great hyperlanes (Doc VI): the Hydian Way, the Perlemian, the Corellian Run, the Rimma. Control a lane and you tax everything on it; cut one and you starve every world downstream. The navies that patrol them and the pirates that prey on them are both, in the end, about cargo.

War Is an Economy. SW The Mandalorian Wars and the Sith conflict that follow move staggering wealth — war materiel, salvage, displaced refugees, ruined and booming worlds. Fortunes are made supplying armies and looting the dead; whole sectors are beggared by a single blockade. A campaign set here is set inside that churn.

Exports & Imports by Region SW

The Shape of the Flow. The galaxy’s wealth runs inward: raw ore, fuel, food and labour pour from the Rim toward the Core, and finished goods — ships, droids, weapons, luxury — flow back out, marked up at every jump. The Core hoards manufacturing and finance; the Rim digs and grows; everyone in between takes a cut moving it along the lanes.

RegionExports / ProducesImports / NeedsControlled By
Core WorldsFinance, manufactured goods, starships, droids, luxuryRaw ore, food, labour, fuelGalactic Republic & the megacorps
Colonies & Inner RimHeavy industry, refined alloys, components, shipsOre, fuel, foodstuffsRepublic, trade guilds, corporations
Mid RimAgriculture, ore, timber, labourTech, weapons, medicine, droidsContested — Republic / local / Hutt
Outer RimRaw ore, tibanna, spice, salvageEverything manufactured, food, medicineHutts, Exchange, Czerka, frontier worlds
Hutt SpaceSpice, vice, slaves (illicit), smuggling servicesManufactured goods, luxury, droidsThe Hutt Cartels
ManaanKolto — the galactic monopolyEffectively everything; imports all needsNeutral Selkath
Mandalore sectorWar materiel, mercenary contractsFood, fuel, raw metalMandalorian clans (beskar stays home)
Unknown RegionsRelics, lost tech, untouched worlds— (uncharted)Sith expeditions; mostly nobody

Who Controls What SW

Money Is Power, and Vice-Versa. No single authority runs the galactic economy — it is carved up between a government, an empire, two crime networks, the great corporations, and a handful of species who happen to sit on something everyone needs. Knowing who holds a market tells you who to bribe, who to fear, and where a price will be gouged.

PowerEconomic GripLeverage
Galactic RepublicThe credit itself, Core industry, the lane-protecting navyMints the money; can protect a route — or blockade it
Sith EmpireConquered worlds, war production, plunderSeizes resources; floods captured markets and starves defiant ones
The Hutt CartelsOuter Rim trade, spice, slaving, gambling, smugglingOwn the Rim’s black economy; bribe whoever needs bribing
The ExchangeGalaxy-wide criminal syndicate, fences, black marketsMove anything illegal; effectively set contraband prices
Czerka CorporationMining, arms, droids, colonisation rightsLegal monopolies and private armies; strip-mine the frontier
Trade guilds & shippersFreight capacity, tariffs, route accessCan choke a world’s supply with tariffs or a strike
Mandalorian clansBeskar (hoarded), war materielBeskar never reaches the open market — that is the point
Selkath (Manaan)The kolto monopolyStay neutral; cut off any side that breaks the peace
Verpine & ArkaniansHigh-tech, prototypes, fine engineeringSell to the highest bidder; nearly impossible to source elsewhere

What’s Scarce, What Isn’t SW

Scarcity Sets the Price. The book’s listed prices assume a thing is available. Much of it isn’t, everywhere. Durasteel is dirt; beskar is a legend you cannot buy. This table is the GM’s quick read on how findable a commodity is and how its price behaves — the upstream cause of the modifiers in the next section.

CommodityAbundanceSource / Controlled ByPrice Behaviour
— Abundant — cheap & everywhere —
Durasteel & quadaniumAbundantMined & refined galaxy-wideCheap, stable — the baseline metal
Energy cells & reactor fuelAbundantEvery port and power-postCheap, stable; the everyday burn
Foodstuffs & waterRegionalAgri-worlds, moisture farmsStable — but spikes hard under blockade
— Limited — controlled or fluctuating —
Tibanna gasCommon but controlledGas-giant minesSwings with the mining worlds’ politics
BactaLimitedSpecialist producersModerate and rising as it spreads
KoltoScarceManaan — Selkath monopolyHigh, fixed by Manaan; war drives it higher
— Rare & hoarded —
CortosisAlmost mythicalA few near-exhausted minesEffectively priceless — scarcer even than beskar; spikes further when a Force war looms
PhrikRareVery few known sourcesVery expensive; militaries hoard it
BeskarVery rareMandalore — clan-controlledEffectively priceless; not sold to outsiders
Lightsaber crystalsRare / Force-significantIlum, Dantooine caves, AdeganNot commercial — earned, found, or fought for
Relics & Force artefactsUniqueTombs, ruins, the Unknown RegionsPriceless; collectors and Sith pay anything
— Contraband — illegal where the Republic writ runs —
SpiceControlled (illicit)Kessel & Hutt operationsBlack-market, volatile, addictive demand
SlavesContrabandOuter Rim, Hutts, the SithIllegal in the Republic; openly traded in Hutt space
Military & restricted armsControlledState arsenals, arms-dealersLegal to a soldier, contraband to a civilian

Cost of Living, Property & Provisions SW

Not every credit is spent on a blaster. A campaign lives or dies on the small economy around the heroes — what a meal costs, where they sleep, what a safe-house is worth, and how much a homestead on the Rim runs. The figures below sit on the same scale as the rest of this book (a glow rod is 10 cr; a blaster rifle 2,000 cr; refined beskar a fortune), so a GM can price almost anything by analogy. Treat them as typical core-to-mid-Rim prices; backwater worlds run cheaper and Core worlds dearer, often by half again either way.

Food, Drink & Lodging SW

Item / ServiceCost (cr)LCNotes
Ration bar (1 day)6—Bland nutrient block; keeps you upright.
Field rations (1 week, sealed)75—Standard traveller’s supply.
Cantina meal (hot)15—The galaxy runs on these.
Restaurant meal (fine)75—Core-world dining.
Drink — caf or ale1–3—Per glass.
Bottle, fine spirits150LicCorellian whiskey, Alderaanian wine; luxury import.
Spice / death sticks (dose)20–100IllegalAddictive contraband; Hutt & Exchange trade.
Spaceport bunk (night)30—Shared flophouse.
Inn room (night)75—Clean, private.
Quality hotel (night)230—Comfortable, serviced.
Luxury suite (night)750—Core-world opulence.
Apartment rent — slum (month)600—Cheap, rough district.
Apartment rent — middling (month)1,800—Respectable neighbourhood.
Apartment rent — upscale (month)6,000—Security, view, address.

Camping, Utilities & Field Provisions SW

ItemCost (cr)LCNotes
Bedroll & shelter tarp90—Basic field rest.
Camp kit (stove, mess, tools)150—One-pack camp set-up.
Environmental tent600—Sealed against weather.
Personal water purifier / still450—Days of clean water from anything.
Portable fusion generator3,600LicPowers a camp, workshop, or charge bank.
Spare power cells (pack)75—The galaxy’s universal energy currency.
Multitool / utility kit90—Cutters, drivers, sealant.
Backpack / load-bearing rig90—Carries the rest of this list.
Blank datacards (10)15—Storage & courier media.
Traveller’s clothing (outfit)60—Hard-wearing, unremarkable.
Fine clothing (outfit)1,500—Status, and the access it buys.
Cold-weather / desert kit360—Climate survival layer.
Spotting scope (civilian optics)600—Non-military magnification.

Property — Land, Housing & Buildings SW

PropertyCost (cr)LCNotes
— Land —
Frontier land (per hectare)1,500—Untamed, unzoned Outer Rim.
Agricultural parcel120,000LicZoned farmland; deed & permit.
Urban lot (small)75,000LicZoned; building permit required.
— Housing —
Spaceport hab-unit (buy)90,000—One-room dwelling.
Modest house180,000—Family home, modest world.
Comfortable house450,000—Space, security, a speeder pad.
Moisture farm (homestead + land)150,000LicOuter Rim staple; vaporators included.
Estate / villa3,000,000+LicThe seriously wealthy.
— Commercial & Buildings —
Workshop / repair bay540,000LicTools, lift, droid bay.
Warehouse600,000LicBulk storage; dock access.
Cantina / shopfront750,000LicBusiness permit required.
Private docking bay (owned)1,200,000LicBerth your own ship, no fees.
Small orbital station module6,000,000+ResHabitat or dock ring; heavily regulated.
Business premises rent (month)2,000–10,000—If you lease rather than buy.

Prices are typical, not fixed — apply a ×0.5 to ×2 swing for backwater vs Core, scarcity, and Reaction-based haggling (Doc I). Rents are monthly. Property usually carries a deed and, in settled space, a Lic permit; contraband (spice, death sticks) is Illegal everywhere the Republic writ runs.

Price & Availability — The Variation System SW HOUSE

Every Listed Price Is a Baseline. Throughout this book, a price is the standard-port, peacetime figure — what you’d pay at a well-supplied Mid-Rim spaceport in quiet times. The actual cost, and whether the thing is even there, shifts with where you are and what is going on. Apply the modifiers below to any base price in the compendium.

Availability Tiers. Every good sits in a tier, read mostly off its LC code (Legality, Era & Tag Codes). Distance and disorder push an item down the tiers: each step toward the frontier or into a blockade worsens availability by one tier, and a Rare or Unique item simply cannot be bought without the right connection, at any price.

Haggling. A Merchant contest (modified by Reaction, Doc I) shifts the final price by ±10–25%. Buying contraband or restricted goods needs a Streetwise roll or a standing contact first — no roll, no sale.

Availability Tiers

TierWhere You Find ItTypical Goods (by LC)
CommonIn stock at any settlementMost — and Lic goods: rations, tools, civilian gear
LimitedMajor worlds & proper spaceports onlyMany Res items, better armour & sensors
ScarceSpecialist suppliers or special-orderMil gear sold to civilians; fine & masterwork goods
RareFound, earned, or commissioned — never stockedThe Rare tag: beskar gear, prototypes, relics
ContrabandBlack market only, with a contactIllegal goods, and Mil hardware to civilians

Price & Availability Modifiers

SituationPriceAvailability
— Where you are —
At the source (buy where it’s mined/made/grown)×0.5Abundant locally
Core world×0.8 manufactured (×1.3 raw ore)Best stock in the galaxy
Standard port / Mid Rim×1.0The listed baseline price
Outer Rim / frontier×1.25Availability one tier worse
Backwater / no spaceport×1.5Two tiers worse; special-order only
— What’s happening —
Blockaded / besieged world×2–3Scarce; food & medicine worst hit
Active war zoneweapons ×0.7 or ×2; food & med ×2Chaotic — glut or desperation
Buying a rival faction’s goods×1.5 + riskRestricted; questions asked
Black market (contraband)×2–4Needs a contact — a Streetwise roll

Modifiers stack multiplicatively — a heavy blaster bought on a blockaded frontier world runs 2,000 × 1.25 (frontier) × 2 (blockade) ≈ 5,000 cr, if you can find one at all. The listed price is always the calm-day, good-port floor; the galaxy charges extra for distance, danger, and desperation.

The Galactic Goods Index SW

Beyond the weapons, armour and gear priced elsewhere, the galaxy trades in everything — the ore that becomes a hull, the gas that fires a blaster, the kolto that saves a life, the spice that ruins one. This index gathers the commodities a crew actually buys, hauls, and sells, grounded in the worlds and substances of the era. Every figure is a baseline (standard port, peacetime); read it through the Variation System above — at the source it is half this, on a blockaded frontier several times more, and the Rare lines cannot simply be bought at all.

A Note on Prices. The galaxy has never kept a tidy price index — a credit buys wildly different things on Coruscant and on a Hutt moon, and the games and records of the age rarely agree to the decimal. These figures are tuned to this book’s scale (a glow rod 10 cr; a blaster rifle 2,000 cr; refined beskar a fortune) and to each good’s known rarity and source, so everything prices consistently against everything else. Treat them as a GM’s working ledger, not a customs tariff.

Ores, Metals & Alloys

The Bones of Everything. Sold by the tonne for bulk metals, by the kilo for the exotic stuff. Prices climb steeply from common durasteel to the saber-resistant metals the whole combat system is built around (Part X).

GoodSource / OriginSold ByBase Price (cr)Avail.Notes / Control
— Common structural —
PlasteelGalaxy-widetonne120CommonCheap structural plastic-steel; hulls, crates, cheap armour backing.
DurasteelGalaxy-widetonne360CommonThe baseline structural metal of everything.
Quadanium steelGalaxy-widetonne600CommonHeat-resistant; hull plating, blast doors.
PlastoidRefineriestonne270CommonMoulded armour shells & trooper plate.
TransparisteelFrom lommite oresheet900CommonClear armoured viewports; lommite is the raw feed.
— Industrial & structural-grade —
DooniumMining worldstonne1,400LimitedWarship hull alloy; military demand keeps it moving.
AgriniumSpecialist minestonne2,700LimitedRadiation shielding; reactors & med bays.
NeutroniumDeep miningtonne7,500ScarceUltra-dense; capital armour & shield casings.
DoloviteMining worldstonne1,800LimitedDurable hull & frame alloy.
— Saber-grade & exotic (see Materials, Part X) —
SongsteelRare depositskg6,000ScarceLight, strong, saber-resistant; master weaponsmiths prize it.
PhrikVery few sourceskg27,000RareSaber-resistant (DD÷5); militaries hoard it.
Mandalorian iron (beskar)Mandalore onlykg—
priceless		RareClan-controlled; not sold to outsiders at any ordinary price.
Dallorian alloyLost techniquekgvaries, vastRareSelf-repairing; near-impossible to source.
Cortosis oreA few near-exhausted mineskg—
beyond price		MythicThe rarest material in the galaxy — scarcer even than beskar; most warriors never see a confirmed piece. Shorts a lightsaber; spikes further when a Force war looms.

Gases, Fuels & Energy

What Powers the Galaxy. The everyday fuels are cheap and everywhere; tibanna and hyperfuel are common but controlled; rhydonium is a fortune nobody sane wants in their hold (see Fuel, Range & Running Costs).

GoodSource / OriginSold ByBase Price (cr)Avail.Notes / Control
Reactor fuel cellsEvery portRF unit30CommonThe everyday sublight & power burn.
Energy cellsEvery portcell25 (2 recharge)CommonRepulsorlifts, droids, gear.
Combustion fuelRefineries, Rimlitre3CommonCheap ground-vehicle & industrial fuel.
Tibanna gasGas-giant mines (Bespin-type)flask750LimitedBlaster charge & hyperdrive coolant; politically sensitive.
Hyperfuel (hypermatter)Refineries, depotsHF unit150LimitedJumps only; volatile, licensed.
RhydoniumVolatile-gas worldsdrum1,200ScarceWildly explosive raw fuel; hauling it is a death-wish job.
Glowgas / glitter-gasSpecialist extractorsflask360LimitedLighting, signage, and refined into other products.

Medical & Biological

The Trade in Staying Alive. SW Kolto is the era’s defining medical good and Manaan’s neutral knife at everyone’s throat; bacta is the rising challenger. War spikes all of it.

GoodSource / OriginSold ByBase Price (cr)Avail.Notes / Control
KoltoManaan onlylitre300ScarceSelkath monopoly — the era’s premier healing fluid; war drives it up.
Kolto, raw (alazhi + arkanian extract)Manaan seafloorbatch180ScarceThe unrefined feedstock; leaving Manaan is restricted.
BactaSpecialist producerslitre360LimitedEmerging rival to kolto; spreading, still pricey.
Biochemical compoundsBio-labsvial120LimitedStims, antidotes, med-kit feedstock.
Biological samplesField / labssample45CommonResearch & pharma raw input.
Medpac suppliesPharma worldscase600CommonBulk field-medicine; always in demand near a war.

Crystals, Gems & Force-Significant

From Industry to the Sacred. Power and colour crystals are commercial; Corusca gems are luxury; Adegan and Ilum crystals and dragon pearls cross from commodity into the Force, and stop being things you can reliably buy.

GoodSource / OriginSold ByBase Price (cr)Avail.Notes / Control
Power crystals (common)Mineral worldscrystal450LimitedFocus & amplify energy; tools, weapons, electronics.
Colour crystalsVarious cavescrystal900LimitedTune a saber’s hue; minor mechanical tweaks.
Corusca gemsGas-giant depthsgem15,000ScarceHardest known gem; luxury & industrial cutting.
Adegan crystalsAdega / Ossus, Dantooinecrystal7,500RareClassic lightsaber crystals; not truly commercial.
Ilum crystalsIlumcrystal— (earned)RareJedi pilgrimage crystals; taken, never bought.
Krayt dragon pearlTatooine krayt dragonspearl75,000+RareSaber-grade & priceless trophy; you hunt for it.
Artifact fragmentsRuins & tombsfragmentvariesRareArchaeological & Force-relic value; collectors pay anything.

Spice & Contraband

The Shadow Economy. SW Spice is the Rim’s real currency. All but andris is illegal under the Republic and openly sold in Hutt space; prices here are black-market baselines — apply the contraband modifiers and a Streetwise roll to find a seller.

GoodSource / OriginSold ByBase Price (cr)Avail.Notes / Control
Glitterstim spiceKesseldose300ContrabandThe premium spice; mild telepathy, fiercely addictive.
Ryll spiceRylothdose180ContrabandCommon spice; also has a legitimate medical refinement.
Carsunum / sweetblossomOuter Rimdose240ContrabandSedative spice; popular vice.
Andris spiceDesert worldsdose120LimitedThe legal one — a preservative & seasoning.
Death sticksUrban undercitiesstick60ContrabandCheap, ruinous narcotic; shortens the user’s life.
Unrefined raw spiceKessel minestonne12,000ContrabandBulk feedstock; the smuggler’s real cargo.

Foodstuffs, Drink & Agricultural

Bulk, Perishable, and Priceless When Starving. The least glamorous and most essential trade. Food and water are cheap at the source and ruinous under siege — the medicine-run logic applies double to a hungry world.

GoodSource / OriginSold ByBase Price (cr)Avail.Notes / Control
Grain & staple cropsAgri-worldstonne240CommonThe galaxy’s bulk calorie trade; spikes under blockade.
Processed rationsFactory worldscase90CommonShelf-stable; feeds armies & spacers.
Fresh produce / livestockAgri-worldstonne450RegionalPerishable; high margin to a hungry city.
Water (potable)Wet worldstonne60RegionalWorthless on Manaan, gold on Tatooine.
Tarisian aleTaris (pre-bombardment)bottle90ScarceRarer and dearer now that Taris is glass.
Corellian whiskey / wineCorellia, Alderaanbottle150LimitedCore-world luxury export.
Juma juiceCantinas galaxy-wideglass9CommonThe spacer’s standard pour.
Exotic delicaciesCore & Hutt courtscase1,500ScarceStatus food; the rich and the Hutts pay absurdly.

Textiles, Luxury & Cultural

Portable Wealth and Status. Light, valuable, and easy to move — the cargo of choice for a trader who can get the right buyer. Doubles as bribes, laundering, and the social currency of gifts.

GoodSource / OriginSold ByBase Price (cr)Avail.Notes / Control
Common textiles / fabricIndustry worldsbolt75CommonClothing, bedding, bulk cloth.
Luxury fabricsCore artisansbolt1,200LimitedFine garments; courts & corporate elites.
Art & sculptureCore, collectorspiece3,000+LimitedStatus, laundering, and genuine value.
Cultural artifactsOld worlds, ruinspiecevariesScarceMuseums, collectors, and the Sith who covet the past.
Gemstones & jewelleryMining worldspiece1,500+LimitedPortable wealth; bribes in solid form.
Precious metals (aurodium, etc.)Mints & minesingot6,000ScarceBacks high-value notes; the Hutts love it.
Companion / courting giftsEverywhereitem10–2,000CommonTrophies, memorabilia, delicacies — the social economy.

Manufactured Trade Goods

The Goods That Flow Outward. Finished products from Core and factory worlds, marked up with every jump toward the frontier that can’t make them. Salvage runs the other way in this war-torn age.

GoodSource / OriginSold ByBase Price (cr)Avail.Notes / Control
Raw electronics / circuitryFactory worldscrate360CommonRepairs, slicing, droid & ship parts.
Ship components / sparesShipyardscrate2,400LimitedAlways wanted at a frontier dock far from a yard.
Droid parts & chassisIndustry worldsunit1,200LimitedThe galaxy runs on droids; parts trade everywhere.
Scavenged metals & salvageBattlefields, wreckstonne180CommonWar-era boom trade; cheap to buy, fiddly to move.
Power converters / generatorsManufactoriesunit900CommonMundane, ubiquitous, quietly essential.
Manufactured weapons (lot)Arms worldscratevariesLimitedLegal to states; Mil/Illegal to civilians (see LC).

Units are the typical trade lot — tonne or crate for bulk, kilo for exotics, dose or flask for refined goods. To trade for profit, buy near the source, cross the distance (paying RF + HF and the monthly nut), and sell into demand; the Trade, Cargo & Speculation table shows the routes that pay. Every price here obeys the Variation System: this is the calm-day, good-port floor.

The Black Market & Smuggling SW

How Forbidden Gear Moves. Restricted, Military, and Illegal goods don’t sit on a shelf — they pass through fences, Hutt brokers, corrupt quartermasters, and the Exchange. Finding a seller takes a Streetwise roll or an established contact; the price runs ×2–4 over any notional “list,” and climbs with heat and distance.

The Risk Premium. The markup buys silence as much as the goods. A nervous market — recent crackdown, a war, a bounty in the air — pushes prices to the top of the range and shrinks the pool of sellers. Get caught carrying contraband and the LC code is the charge sheet: Mil and Illegal goods mean confiscation, fines, or a cell.

Who to Know. The Exchange moves anything for a cut. The Hutts run the Rim’s vice and slaving. Czerka will quietly sell arms to both sides of a war. A crew’s contacts are worth more than its credits — a fence on the right world turns Rare into merely expensive.

Trade, Cargo & Speculation SW

Buy Low, Haul Far, Sell High. A free-trader’s living is the gap between where a thing is made and where it’s wanted — minus the fuel and fees to cross the distance (Fuel, Range & Running Costs). The bigger the gap, the bigger the margin, and the bigger the reason someone is stopping you: tariffs, pirates, blockades, the law.

CargoBuy WhereSell WhereTypical Margin
Raw ore / metalOuter Rim (at the mine)Core / Colonies+50–100%
Manufactured goods, droidsCore WorldsRim & frontier+50–100%
FoodstuffsAgri-worldsCities, mining worlds+30–60%
Medicine / koltoManaan, CoreA blockaded or plague world+200–400% (and danger)
Luxury goods, artCoreFrontier elites, Hutt courts+100%
Spice (illegal)Kessel, Hutt spaceAnywhere with addicts & no lawHuge — and huge risk

Margins are gross — subtract fuel (RF + HF), docking, and the monthly nut before counting profit, and remember a fat margin usually means a fat risk. A medicine run to a besieged world pays for the ship; getting shot down pays for nothing. This is the loop the whole campaign economy turns on.

Document VIII

Droids & Artificial Beings

The five degrees · combat & the ion rules · building & owning droids · the legends

Overview

What this covers. What a droid is, droids in combat, the five degrees, building and owning droids, upgrades, and the full droid stat-block codex.

How to use it. Treat droids as characters with parts: read the degree that fits your droid, then build or buy from the stat blocks.

Contents

Navigating the
Droid Book

Front matter · the degrees · combat · construction · the codex · appendices

The Shape of the Book. Like Doc VII, this volume opens with a Reference (the droid-specific jargon), then the rules — what a droid is (Part I) and how droids fight and break (Part II). The heart is the bestiary, organised by the canonical five degrees (Parts III-VII): every model with full stats and a description. Then come the builder’s chapters — constructing and owning droids (Part VIII), upgrading them (Part IX), and the named legends as ready set-pieces (Part X). The Codex describes every model and system in prose; Appendix A holds the quick-reference and encounter tables.

SectionContentsFind it for…
— Reference (front matter) —
Jargon IndexDroid-specific abbreviations, the degree codes, system & condition terms (plus Doc VII’s shared notation)Decoding a droid stat block
— The Rules —
Part IWhat a Droid Is — the five degrees, droid traits, intelligence & the heuristic processorUnderstanding droids
Part IIDroids in Combat — ion & EMP, disruptors, slicing, droid DR, shutdown vs destruction, repairFighting & fixing droids
— The Bestiary (by degree) —
Part IIIFirst-Degree — medical, surgical, analytical & scientific droidsDoctors & researchers
Part IVSecond-Degree — astromech, utility, technical & engineering droidsMechanics & pilots’ aides
Part VThird-Degree — protocol, translation, hospitality & service droidsDiplomats & servants
Part VIFourth-Degree — combat, security & assassin droids (HK, war droids, sentinels, probes)Enemies & guards
Part VIIFifth-Degree — labour, loader, mining & menial droidsWorkers & background
— The Builder’s Chapters —
Part VIIIBuilding & Owning Droids — chassis, processors, locomotion, appendages, sensors, costs, droid PCs & companionsMaking a droid
Part IXDroid Upgrades & Modification — armour, weapons, shields, motivators, restraining bolts, ion-hardening, memory wipesImproving a droid
Part XThe GURPS Droid — the Machine meta-trait, reading a stat block, building a droid characterThe rules under the hood
Part XIThe Stat Blocks — ~34 full GURPS 4e droid archetypes by degree, each as an NPC block and a playable-PC buildReady-to-play droids
— The Codex & Appendices —
The CodexNarrative description of every droid model and system — the lore companion to the Part XI statsWhat a droid is
Appendix AQuick-reference: droids by degree, threat & cost; the GM encounter tableFast reference
Reference

Jargon Index

Droid degrees, systems & condition terms — plus the shared notation, read first

Droid stat blocks borrow the whole notation of the Equipment Compendium — damage in GURPS dice, armour divisors (N), split DR shown eenergy/kkinetic, the legality codes — and add a small vocabulary of their own. The droid-specific terms are here; for the shared weapon and armour notation, Doc VII’s Jargon Index is the master reference.

The Five Degrees

DegreeRoleTypical Examples
First-degreeMedical, surgical, analytical, scientificSurgical, communications & research droids
Second-degreeAstromech, engineering, technical, utilityUtility/astromech, planning & repair droids
Third-degreeProtocol, social, translation, serviceProtocol droids, hospitality, tutors
Fourth-degreeCombat, security, assassinationAssassin droids, war droids, sentinels, probes
Fifth-degreeLabour, menial, mining, loadingBulk loaders, maintenance, utility drones

The degree describes a droid’s intended function and intelligence, not its threat — a heavily-modified fifth-degree loader can be deadlier than a stock sentinel. Lower degree numbers generally indicate more sophisticated cognition.

Droid Stat-Block Terms SW

TermMeaning
DegreeThe droid’s class (1st-5th, above) — sets baseline role, IQ, and legal scrutiny.
ProcessorThe droid’s brain. Basic = follows orders, no learning. Heuristic = learns, adapts, develops personality (can be a droid hero).
LocomotionHow it moves: walking (legs), wheeled, tracked, hovering, flying, repulsor. Sets Move & terrain.
AppendagesManipulators: hand (fine work), tool (built-in instruments), claw, probe. Number limits simultaneous tasks.
Sensor packageThe droid’s senses. Basic, improved (+Per), or specialised (life-form, motion, multispectral).
VocabulatorLets a droid speak aloud (Basic and other languages). Without it, a droid communicates only in Binary beeps.
Translator unitLanguage capability; the higher the number of languages, the better the diplomat.
SR (droid)Shield Rating — some droids mount integral energy shields, reducing energy damage like a personal shield (Doc VII).
Shutdown thresholdThe system-shock level (from ion/EMP) at which a droid powers down — analogous to a living being’s unconsciousness.
DisabledAt 0 HP a droid is inert but repairable — not destroyed. See Part II.
DestroyedPast its negative-HP threshold a droid is wrecked beyond field repair (salvage only).

Droid Condition & Control Terms SW

TermMeaning
Droid traitsThe bundle every droid shares: no need to breathe/eat/sleep, immune to poison & disease & mind-affecting effects, takes ion damage, repaired not healed, no Fatigue Points.
Ion damageAnti-machine energy. AD 5 vs droids; tracks as system shock toward the shutdown threshold rather than (only) HP. Near-harmless to flesh.
SlicingHacking a droid’s processor (Computer skill) to issue commands, extract data, or seize control.
Restraining boltA control device forcing obedience to a holder’s commands; standard on owned and slave droids.
Memory wipeErasing a droid’s accumulated experience — resets a heuristic droid to factory behaviour, removing learned skills & personality.
Heuristic driftThe tendency of un-wiped heuristic droids to develop quirks, opinions, and (eventually) personality over years of operation.
Behavioural inhibitorProgramming that prevents a droid harming its masters or sapient beings — routinely removed from assassin and war droids.

Shared Notation (from Doc VI)

SymbolMeaning
(N)Armour divisor — divides target DR before subtracting. Ion vs droids is (5).
DR en/knDamage Resistance, energy (blue) / kinetic (red). Droid plating uses the same split.
burn / pi / cut / cr / ion / corDamage types (Doc VII Jargon). Ion & cor (disruptor) matter most against droids.
LCLegality Class: — / Lic / Res / Mil / Illegal / Rare.
Acc · RoF · ST · Bulk · RclWeapon stats (Doc VII) — droids use the same weapons as everyone else.
Part I

What a
Droid Is

The five degrees · droid traits · intelligence & the heuristic processor

A droid is a self-aware machine — somewhere between a tool and a person, and the galaxy has never quite decided which. In the Old Republic they are everywhere: piloting freighters, translating treaties, performing surgery, and killing on command. Most are property. Some are companions. A rare few, left running long enough without a memory wipe, become something close to individuals. This chapter defines what every droid shares and how the five degrees divide them.

Droid Traits — What Every Droid Shares

The Mechanical Body. SW A droid does not breathe, eat, sleep, or tire. It is immune to poison, disease, suffocation, vacuum, and the ordinary need for rest. It has no Fatigue Points — it cannot be exhausted, only damaged or drained of power. It runs until its power cell dies (typically years) or until something breaks it.

The Mechanical Mind. SW A droid is immune to mind-affecting effects — fear, mind tricks, illusions, telepathy, most Force persuasion (Doc IV). A Jedi cannot wave a hand and convince a droid of anything; this is precisely why crime lords and Sith favour droid guards. The flip side: droids can be sliced (hacked) and bolted (controlled) by purely technical means a living guard would be immune to.

Repaired, Not Healed. SW A droid recovers no HP on its own. Kolto and bacta do nothing for it. It is restored with the Mechanic skill, spare parts, and time (Part II) — never with medicine. A droid with a good mechanic in the party is as durable as one with a good medic; without one, damage is permanent until a workshop is found.

Vulnerable to Ion. SW The great weakness: ion weapons, EMP, and ionised effects that flesh shrugs off will seize a droid (Part II). Every combatant who expects droids carries ion. This is the balancing weakness against an otherwise tireless, fearless, poison-proof opponent.

The Heuristic Processor — Can It Learn?

Basic Processors. SW Most droids run basic processors: they execute their programming competently and do not deviate. A basic-processor labour droid loads cargo perfectly and forever; it will not improvise, grow, or surprise you. Mechanically it has fixed skills and no capacity to improve. The galaxy runs on millions of these.

Heuristic Processors. SW A heuristic processor learns from experience — it adapts, improvises, accumulates skill, and over time develops quirks and personality (heuristic drift). Heuristic droids can be droid heroes (player characters or named NPCs), earning and spending character points like any other — heuristic units left un-wiped long enough to become individuals, with skills, opinions, and names of their own. The cost is unpredictability: an old heuristic droid has opinions.

The Memory Wipe. SW Owners who want obedience over capability wipe their heuristic droids periodically — erasing accumulated experience, learned skills, and any budding personality, resetting the droid to factory behaviour. A droid that ”remembers too much” is a droid that might develop its own agenda. Many droids fear the wipe; some hide their growth to avoid it (Part IX).

Intelligence by Degree

Lower Number, Sharper Mind. SW Counter-intuitively, the lower the degree number, the more sophisticated the cognition. First-degree medical and scientific droids are brilliant specialists; fifth-degree labour drones are barely more than smart tools. A protocol droid (third) out-thinks a loader (fifth) but is out-thought by a research analyst (first). Combat droids (fourth) are tactically sharp but socially and intellectually narrow — built to fight, not to converse.

Role-Bound Programming. SW A droid’s degree shapes what it does well and what it cannot do at all without modification. A surgical droid cannot fly a starfighter; an astromech cannot perform diplomacy. Stepping outside the programmed role requires modification (Part IX) or a heuristic processor flexible enough to learn. This role-binding is why a party assembles droids the way it assembles specialists.

The Five Degrees at a Glance

DegreeFunctionCognitionCombat RoleExamples (this book)
FirstMedical, surgical, analytical, scientificHigh, specialistSupport; rarely fightsSurgical, communications & research droids
SecondAstromech, engineering, technical, utilityPractical, adaptiveSupport; can shock/stunUtility/astromech, planning & repair droids
ThirdProtocol, translation, social, serviceHigh social, low practicalNon-combatantProtocol droid, hospitality droid
FourthCombat, security, assassinationTactically sharp, narrowFront-line threatAssassin, war, sentinel & probe droids
FifthLabour, mining, loading, menialMinimalImprovised; tough but unskilledBulk loader, maintenance, utility drone

Degree is a guide, not destiny. A modified droid (Part IX) can step well outside its degree’s combat role — a weaponised loader is a brutal surprise.

Part II

Droids in
Combat

Ion & EMP · disruptors · slicing · droid DR · shutdown vs destruction · repair

Fighting a droid is not like fighting a person. It feels no pain and no fear, it bleeds no blood, and it will keep shooting with a limb blown off until its processor fails. But it has weaknesses a living foe lacks: a single ion bolt can drop a war droid that a dozen blaster hits left standing, and a slicer with thirty seconds can turn an enemy’s guards against it. This chapter is the rules for breaking, seizing, and fixing droids — and it ties directly into the damage-and-armour system of Doc VII.

Running a droid fight — the short version. A droid acts on its own kit skills, not its owner’s: HK-47 shoots at Beam Wpns (Rifle) 16 whether or not anyone is watching. Roll 3d6 ≤ skill + range + Aim exactly as a character would — droid weapons are personal-scale, so armour divisors (AD) divide DR as normal. Incoming fire is component-native: DR soaks (÷AD), then compare penetration to the droid’s DT (6 light / 8 military / 10 heavy) — ≥DT cripples the struck part ◐, ≥2×DT destroys it ✕. The character sheet’s droid cards carry an Armament table seeded from each droid’s kit (own skill level, damage, AD, power-cell shots) plus the ⚔ Incoming-hit resolver, so a droid plays like an extension of your character with no extra bookkeeping.

Droid Hit Points & Damage

HP, No FP. SW A droid has Hit Points like any being, scaled to its size and build (a tiny astromech ~8-12, a war droid ~25-45, a heavy combat unit more). It has no Fatigue Points — it cannot be tired, winded, or stunned by exhaustion. Effects that would cost FP simply don’t apply.

No Bleeding, No Shock. SW Droids don’t bleed (Doc III) and don’t suffer the shock penalty from wounds — a droid at 1 HP fights exactly as well as a droid at full. There is no ”wounded and faltering” droid; it is either operational or it is not. Some war droids (the relentless pattern) are explicitly built to fight at full effectiveness until destroyed.

Crippling & Severing. SW Limb and component hits work as in Doc III: blow off a droid’s weapon arm and it can’t use that weapon; destroy its locomotion and it can’t move. Because droids feel no shock, called shots to disable specific systems are a favoured tactic — take out the gun arm rather than chip away at HP.

Ion Damage — the Droid-Killer

Armour Divisor (5). SW Ion weapons (Doc VII Part V) hit droids at armour divisor 5 — they tear through droid plating that would shrug off a blaster. An ion bolt that does little to the HP of a heavily-armoured droid still floods its systems.

System Shock & Shutdown. HOUSE Track ion damage as system shock separately from HP. When accumulated system shock meets the droid’s shutdown threshold (typically equal to its HP, or listed in its block), the droid powers down — inert but undamaged — for 1d minutes, after which it reboots unless secured. This is how you capture a droid intact: drop it with ion, then bolt or slice it before it reboots. Ion does little or no HP damage; it disables rather than destroys.

EMP & Ion Grenades. SW An EMP grenade (Doc VII Part VI) forces every droid in its radius to make a HT-equivalent roll (vs the grenade’s rating) or shut down outright. Ion grenades deal area ion damage at AD 5. Against a droid swarm these are decisive — one good throw shuts down a room of guards.

Ion-Hardening. SW Better droids (and upgraded ones, Part IX) mount ion-shielding that reduces incoming ion or raises the shutdown threshold. Verpine-built and military droids are notably ion-resistant. Never assume ion is an auto-win against a high-end unit.

Disruptors vs Droids

Molecular Breakdown. SW Disruptors (Doc VII Part V) are as horrific to droids as to flesh — AD 10, ×4 wound, eating through plating and circuitry alike. A disruptor doesn’t shut a droid down; it destroys it, often past salvage. Rare and illegal, but the surest way to put down a heavy combat droid permanently.

Slicing — Hacking the Enemy

The Computer Contest. SW A slicer with access (physical port, or wireless within range) makes a Computer skill contest against the droid’s processor rating. Success can: issue it a single command, extract its data/memory, shut it down, or — with a sustained effort — seize ongoing control. Heuristic and military droids resist far better than basic units; a restraining bolt or a hardened processor raises the difficulty.

Turning the Guards. SW The dramatic payoff: slice an enemy’s security droids and they fight for you. This is why facilities air-gap and bolt their droids, and why a party slicer is worth a squad. Slicing takes time and proximity, so it’s a stealth-and-infiltration tool, not a combat reflex.

Droid DR & Armour

Plating Is Split DR. SW A droid’s armour plating uses the same split DR as Doc VII: shown eenergy/kkinetic. A durasteel-plated assassin droid resists blasters and blades much like a soldier in battle armour — but remember everything takes ion at AD 5 regardless of plating, and a disruptor ignores it.

Built-In Shields. SW Some droids (planning units, certain war droids) mount integral energy shields with a Shield Rating (SR) reducing energy damage, exactly like a personal shield (Doc VII Part X) — and, like those, doing nothing against kinetic or ion. Kinetic weapons and ion remain the answer to a shielded droid.

Shutdown, Disabled & Destroyed — the Droid Condition Ladder

StateTriggerConditionRecovery
OperationalAbove 0 HP, shock below thresholdFully functional — no shock penalties ever—
Shut down (ion)System shock ≥ shutdown thresholdInert but undamaged; powered offReboots in 1d minutes unless secured/bolted/sliced
DisabledHP reaches 0Inert, damaged, non-functional — but intactMechanic skill + parts + time; fully recoverable
DestroyedHP past negative threshold, or disruptorWrecked beyond field repairSalvage for parts only; a workshop rebuild is a project

The key distinction: ion shuts down (temporary, intact — for capture), HP loss disables (repairable), and only destruction or a disruptor is permanent. Capturing a droid intact almost always means dropping it with ion, not blasters.

Repairing a Droid

Repair TaskSkill & RequirementTimeNotes
Field patchMechanic (Robotics), basic tools~10 min per attemptRestores some HP between fights; limited without parts.
Full repairMechanic + spare parts~1 hr per major componentRestores a disabled droid to operational.
Reboot a shut-down droidNone (auto) or Computer to force early1d minutes / instantSecure or bolt it first if it’s hostile.
Rebuild a destroyed droidMechanic at penalty, workshop, donor partsDays to weeksA project, not a field action; cheaper than buying new if you have the chassis.
Repair droid (assistant)A repair droid does this for youAs above, +bonusSecond-degree repair units (Part IV) are the party’s ”droid medic.”

Droids never benefit from kolto, bacta, first aid, or natural healing. A party that fields droids needs a Mechanic the way a party of organics needs a medic — or a repair droid to do the job.

Part III

First-Degree
Droids

Medical · surgical · analytical · scientific — the brilliant specialists

First-degree droids are the galaxy’s mechanical intellectuals — surgeons, researchers, diagnosticians, and analysts. They rarely fight, and when forced to they are poor at it, but a first-degree medical droid can keep a whole party alive, and a research unit can crack a problem no organic specialist could. The era’s medicine, run on kolto and bacta (Doc VII), leans heavily on these droids; a surgical droid can install the cybernetics of Doc VII Part IX better than most living surgeons.

DroidDegree / SizeHPDRKey Skills / FunctionCostLCMechanics & Notes
Surgical droid1st / Medium18e2/k2Surgery, Physician, Diagnosis (expert)66,000LicSW +2 to Surgery/Physician; can install cybernetics (Doc VII IX) as a full surgical suite. Non-combatant; flees or freezes under fire.
Medical assistant droid1st / Small10e1/k1First Aid, Diagnosis27,000LicSW +2 First Aid; carries kolto supplies. Common in clinics & medbays.
Communications droid1st / Small8e1/k1Computer, Comms, Logistics, many languages33,000LicSW +2 Computer; coordinates worker-droid teams; can briefly man comms/sensor stations. Fragile.
Research analyst droid1st / Medium14e2/k2Sciences, Research, Analysis (expert)84,000LicSW +3 to one science specialty; the lab’s mechanical mind. Helpless in a fight.
Bio-analysis droid1st / Small10e1/k1Diagnosis, Toxicology, Forensics48,000LicSW +2 to identify toxins, diseases, & causes of death; favoured by investigators.

First-degree droids are support units. Their value is skill, not combat — protect them. All take ion at AD 5 and are repaired, not healed (Part II).

Part IV

Second-Degree
Droids

Astromech · engineering · technical · utility — the practical problem-solvers

Second-degree droids keep the galaxy running: they repair starships, plot hyperspace jumps, slice computers, and fix the party’s other droids. The astromech is the era’s great labourer — the earliest utility units are squat, disc-headed droids that pilots prize. These droids carry light defensive tools (shock probes, stun rays) and can hold their own against a single thug, but they are built to mend, not to murder.

DroidDegree / SizeHPDRKey Skills / FunctionCostLCMechanics & Notes
Utility / astromech droid2nd / Small12e3/k3Mechanic, Computer, Astrogation45,000LicSW +2 Mechanic/Computer; electroshock probe (1d ion, melee); built-in stun ray (as stun pistol). Heuristic-capable — can grow into a hero. The party’s repair droid & slicer.
Astromech droid (general)2nd / Small12e3/k3Mechanic, Piloting aid, Astrogation39,000LicSW Repairs & co-pilots starships (Doc VI); stores hyperspace routes. Electroshock probe for defence.
Repair droid2nd / Small14e3/k3Mechanic (Robotics) expert51,000LicSW +3 to repair droids & machines; the party’s ”droid medic.” Carries spare parts & tools.
Infrastructure-planning droid2nd / Tiny (hovering)10e2/k2 + SR 10Strategy, Administration, Logistics750,000MilSW Brilliant planner; integral SR 10 energy shield. Heuristic — prone to seizing control of what it manages (a famous failure mode). Hovers; no manipulators of its own.
Engineering droid2nd / Medium20e4/k4Engineer, Mechanic, Demolition72,000LicSW +2 Engineer; builds, breaches, & maintains. Heavier frame than an astromech.
Slicer droid2nd / Small10e2/k2Computer (Intrusion) expert63,000ResSW +3 to slice systems & other droids (Part II); the infiltrator’s companion. Restricted for obvious reasons.

Second-degree droids are the party’s toolkit. The utility/astromech line in particular is heuristic-capable and famously produces heroes in its own right. Defensive weapons only — they shock and stun, they don’t slay.

Part V

Third-Degree
Droids

Protocol · translation · hospitality · service — the social specialists

Third-degree droids are built for people — translating, negotiating, hosting, advising on etiquette. A good protocol droid knows six million forms of communication and exactly which fork to hand a Hutt. They are non-combatants almost without exception, and their value is entirely social: getting the party through a door, a customs checkpoint, or a delicate negotiation that would otherwise end in blasterfire. (The assassin droids, Part VI, are built to look like harmless protocol droids — a disguise that has cost many targets their lives.)

DroidDegree / SizeHPDRKey Skills / FunctionCostLCMechanics & Notes
Protocol droid3rd / Medium14e2/k2Diplomacy, Protocol, dozens of languages60,000LicSW +2 Diplomacy; fluent in thousands of languages; advises on etiquette & custom. Fussy, fragile, indispensable at court. Non-combatant.
Translator droid3rd / Small10e1/k1Linguistics, Translation36,000LicSW Real-time translation of most languages; the explorer’s & trader’s aide.
Hospitality droid3rd / Medium12e2/k2Savoir-Faire, Carousing, Administration48,000LicSW Runs cantinas, hotels, & estates; +2 to host, manage staff, & smooth social friction. Knows everyone’s business.
Tutor droid3rd / Small10e1/k1Teaching, broad Knowledge42,000LicSW Educates the young of wealthy families; broad but shallow knowledge. Patient to a fault.
Service/valet droid3rd / Medium12e2/k2Housekeeping, Personal service30,000—SW Domestic servant; manages a household. Ubiquitous in wealthy homes.

Third-degree droids do not fight. Their power is social — and their resemblance to the HK assassin droids (Part VI) is exactly what makes those killers so effective. Trust a protocol droid at your peril when the Czerka logo is filed off.

Part VI

Fourth-Degree
Droids

Combat · security · assassination — the killers

Fourth-degree droids are built to fight, and the era’s wars made them sophisticated. Czerka’s assassin droids disguise themselves as servants; Duwani’s support droids resupply and reinforce the lines; Ubrikkian’s relentless war droids fight at full effectiveness until the moment they’re destroyed. They are tactically sharp, fearless, tireless, and immune to the mind tricks a Jedi would use on a living guard. Their weaknesses are the droid weaknesses: ion drops them, slicing turns them, disruptors erase them. Bring the right tools or bring a great many blasters.

DroidDegree / SizeHPDR (plating)ArmamentCostLCThreat & Notes
Assassin droid (early)4th / Medium30e18/k18 (battle armour)Blaster rifle (5d burn)170,000Illegal, RareSW Czerka-era assassin disguised as a servant droid. Skilled marksman (Combat Reflexes, aimed fire). Behavioural inhibitor removed. Drawback: illegal to own; draws lethal attention.
Assassin droid (advanced)4th / Medium38e20/k18Blaster rifle + vibroblade + integral weapons270,000Illegal, RareSW Cold, articulate, deadly in numbers; disguises as a protocol droid. Self-destructs on defeat to deny salvage. Drawback: illegal; the self-destruct endangers whoever fells it.
War droid (mobile support)4th / Medium (jetpack)26e16/k14Pulse-wave rifle, heavy sonic pistol, shatter beam260,000MilSW Duwani resupply-and-combat-support pattern; flies/walks/swims via jetpack. Carries materiel to troops & fights alongside them. Shatter beam cracks armour.
War droid (relentless)4th / Medium34e20/k18Blaster rifle (rapid fire)170,000MilSW Ubrikkian sentinel-upgrade pattern — “an assassin droid by another name.” Fights at full effectiveness until destroyed (over 85% combat capability even when wrecked). Too sophisticated for the civilian security market.
Sentinel droid4th / Medium22e14/k14Blaster rifle or twin blasters78,000MilSW Kellenech personal-security guard; imposing build. Weak alone, dangerous in numbers — fights as a coordinated squad. The galaxy’s common armed guard.
Probe droid4th / Small (hovering)16e10/k8Light blaster + sensor suite96,000MilSW Reconnaissance & surveillance; superb sensors, self-destructs to avoid capture. Hovers; reports to a master before engaging. The scout you didn’t see.
Patrol droid4th / Medium (wheeled)20e12/k12Blaster + stun63,000ResSW Automated law-enforcement; patrols, challenges, & subdues. Stun-first programming; calls reinforcements.
War droid (heavy assault)4th / Large45e25/k25Repeating blaster (6d+2(3)) + missiles390,000MilSW Battlefield heavy unit; bridges toward vehicle scale (Doc VI). Slow but devastating; ion or heavy weapons required to stop it.
Security droid (armed)4th / Medium18e10/k10Blaster carbine + stun baton57,000ResSW Corporate & facility guard; the standard armed droid behind a locked door. Sliceable if you can reach its port.

DR shown eenergy/kkinetic (Doc VII). Every droid here takes ion at AD 5 (Part II) and can be sliced — the two great answers to a combat droid. Disruptors destroy them outright. Weapon damage figures use Doc VII’s blaster scale.

Fighting the Combat Droids

The Assassin-Droid Problem. SW Assassin droids open with surprise — they look like servants until the blaster comes up. Against a party, an HK fights as a disciplined marksman: aimed shots, use of cover, target priority (it shoots the medic first). They feel no fear and never break. The counter is to deny the surprise (scan for hidden weapons, distrust the too-helpful protocol droid) and bring ion. Advanced assassin units self-destruct on defeat — don’t stand over the body.

War Droids in the Open. SW Support and relentless war droids are built for the battlefield, not the corridor. In the open they bring heavy firepower and (the support pattern) mobility via jetpack; the relentless pattern’s refusal to degrade means you can’t wear it down — you must destroy it. Ion grenades and ion rifles (Doc VII) are the efficient answer; otherwise expect to spend a lot of blaster fire.

Sentinels & Numbers. SW A lone sentinel is a speed bump; a squad of six is a wall. They coordinate fire and flank. Break them with area effects — a frag or ion grenade in the cluster — before they bring their numbers to bear. The same is true of patrol and security droids.

Probes & the Unseen. SW A probe droid rarely fights; it watches and reports. The danger is what it tells its master. Kill it fast and quietly (it self-destructs, so expect a small blast) or slice it to feed false data. If you’ve seen one probe, assume the enemy has seen you.

Part VII

Fifth-Degree
Droids

Labour · mining · loading · menial — the workers

Fifth-degree droids are the galaxy’s mechanical muscle — bulk loaders, miners, maintenance drones, and menial workers with minimal cognition and maximal durability. They are background, not threat, until someone bolts a weapon onto one. A mining droid’s industrial cutter or a bulk loader’s crushing arms make a brutal improvised weapon, and their heavy frames soak punishment. The cheapest path to a dangerous droid is to weaponise a labour unit (Part IX) — crime lords and desperate colonists do it constantly.

DroidDegree / SizeHPDRFunction / Improvised ThreatCostLCMechanics & Notes
Bulk loader droid5th / Large40e8/k10Heavy lifting; crushing arms (thr+2d cr)48,000LicSW Enormous strength; slow. Unarmed but its loader arms crush like a vibro-axe. A favourite chassis to weaponise. Tough — soaks fire.
Mining droid5th / Medium28e6/k8Excavation; industrial cutter (3d(2) cut)42,000LicSW Built for rock; the cutter is a real weapon. Rugged frame; common on frontier worlds & in Czerka operations.
Security/maintenance droid5th / Medium20e5/k6Upkeep & basic guarding; stun baton30,000LicSW Dual-purpose janitor-guard; minimal combat skill but persistent. Patrols & reports.
Bulk-handling utility droid5th / Medium18e4/k5Cargo & general labour23,000—SW The galaxy’s forklift. No weapons; useful as cover & muscle. Ubiquitous in docks & warehouses.
Agricultural droid5th / Medium16e3/k4Farming; harvest tools (1d+2 cut)18,000—SW Tends crops; tools cut flesh in a pinch. Background droid on agriworlds.
Power/utility drone5th / Small10e3/k3Maintenance of power systems15,000—SW Tends reactors & conduits; can sabotage same if sliced. Tiny, ignorable — until it isn’t.

Fifth-degree droids are background and muscle, not designed threats — but their durability and improvised weapons (a loader’s arms, a miner’s cutter) make a cheaply-weaponised labour droid (Part IX) genuinely dangerous. Never assume the loader is harmless.

Part VIII

Building &
Owning Droids

Chassis · processors · locomotion · appendages · sensors · droid PCs & companions

A droid can be bought whole, built from parts, or grown into a companion over a campaign. This chapter is the construction kit: pick a chassis (which sets size, HP, and base cost), a processor (basic or heuristic), locomotion, appendages, and a sensor package, then add systems. The same components let a GM stat any droid not in the bestiary and let a player build a droid character or a party companion.

Droids Are Capital, Not Kit

SW Droids are expensive — deliberately so. Where a blaster rifle runs around a thousand credits and a fine suit of armour a few thousand, even a humble utility droid costs fifteen thousand, a combat droid runs to the tens of thousands, and a heavy war droid or a brilliant planning unit reaches six figures. A droid is a vehicle-scale purchase (Doc VI): a major investment, a thing a party saves for or salvages, not a thing they pick up in a shop. The heuristic processor that makes a droid a true companion alone costs more than a small armoury. Treat acquiring a good droid as an achievement — and losing one as a real blow.

Building a Droid — the Steps

1. Chassis (size & frame). SW The chassis sets the droid’s size, base HP, base DR, carrying capacity, and a chunk of the cost. Tiny (astromech-adjacent, HP ~8), Small (utility, HP ~10-12), Medium (humanoid, HP ~14-30), Large (loader/war, HP ~40+). Bigger frames cost and weigh more but carry more armour and weaponry.

2. Processor (the brain). SW Basic processors are cheap, obedient, and fixed — fine for labour and security. Heuristic processors cost far more, learn and adapt, can be droid heroes, and risk developing personality (and being wiped for it). Choose basic for a tool, heuristic for a companion.

3. Locomotion. SW Walking (versatile, stairs), wheeled (fast on flat, stymied by terrain), tracked (slow, all-terrain), hovering/repulsor (smooth, ignores ground), flying (jetpack/repulsorlift — mobile but fuel/power-limited). Sets Move and what terrain stops the droid.

4. Appendages. SW Hand appendages do fine work (tools, weapons, manipulation); tool appendages are built-in instruments (welders, scanners, probes); claws grip and crush; the count limits how many things a droid does at once. A surgeon needs many fine hands; a loader needs two strong arms.

5. Sensor Package. SW Basic (sees & hears like a person), improved (+Per, better range), or specialised (life-form scanner, motion sensor, multispectral/IR — the probe-droid suite). Better sensors make better scouts, guards, and marksmen.

6. Systems & Upgrades. SW Vocabulator (speech), translator unit (languages), armour plating, weapon mounts, shields, ion-hardening, and the rest — see Part IX. Stack within the chassis’s capacity; a Medium frame holds more than a Tiny one.

Droid Player Characters & Companions

Heuristic Required. SW Only a heuristic droid can be a player character or a true companion — basic processors can’t grow or earn character points. A droid PC builds like any character but applies the droid traits (Part II): no FP, ion-vulnerable, repaired not healed, immune to mind effects, sliceable, bolted by anyone who controls it. These are real advantages and real liabilities, and they roughly balance.

The Companion Droid. SW A party’s owned droid (the astromech, the protocol unit, the salvaged HK) is a companion run by the GM or a player. It grows with the party if heuristic — learning skills, developing the quirks that make T3 and HK memorable. The relationship is the point: a long-running un-wiped droid becomes family, which is exactly why the option to wipe it carries weight.

Ownership, Bolts & Freedom. SW Most droids are property. A restraining bolt (Part IX) compels obedience; removing it is the first step to a free droid, and a heuristic droid without a bolt may develop its own agenda. The galaxy’s law treats droids as objects; whether your table treats a heuristic companion as a person is a story question worth asking.

Droid Construction Quick-Costs

ComponentOptions & EffectCostNotes
Chassis — TinyHP ~8, DR e1/k1, minimal carry12,000Drones, tiny utility units.
Chassis — SmallHP ~10-12, DR e2/k221,000Astromech, utility, slicer frames.
Chassis — MediumHP ~14-30, DR e3/k342,000Humanoid; protocol, combat, surgical.
Chassis — LargeHP ~40+, DR e6/k6, high carry84,000Loaders, heavy war droids.
Basic processorFixed skills, obedient, no growth+2,500Tools & labour.
Heuristic processorLearns, adapts, can be a hero+30,000Companions & specialists; wipe-able.
Walking locomotionVersatile; handles terrain & stairs+3,500Move 4-6.
Wheeled/trackedFast (wheel) / all-terrain (track)+1,800Wheel stymied by rough ground.
Hovering/repulsorIgnores ground terrain+9,000Probes & planners; power-hungry.
Hand appendage (each)Fine manipulation; holds weapon/tool+1,400More = more simultaneous tasks.
Tool appendage (each)Built-in instrument (welder, scanner…)+1,200Specialist function.
Basic sensorsHuman-equivalent senses+1,000Standard.
Improved sensors+2 Per, extended range+5,500Scouts, guards, marksmen.
Specialised sensorsLife-form / motion / multispectral+12,000Probe-droid suite.
VocabulatorSpeech in Basic & other tongues+700Without it, Binary only.
Translator unit+languages (more = better diplomat)+2,500Protocol function.

Build a droid by summing chassis + processor + locomotion + appendages + sensors + systems (Part IX). Costs are campaign benchmarks; the bestiary models are pre-built examples of these parts assembled.

Part IX

Droid Upgrades &
Modification

Armour · weapons · shields · motivators · restraining bolts · ion-hardening · memory

A stock droid is a starting point. The galaxy’s mechanics, crime lords, and tinkerers modify constantly — bolting armour onto a loader, a blaster onto an astromech, a behavioural inhibitor off an assassin. This chapter is the upgrade catalogue: what you can add, what it does, and the drawbacks. Modification uses the Mechanic (Robotics) skill and the droid’s spare capacity; a Tiny frame holds little, a Large frame holds a great deal.

UpgradeEffectCostDrawback / Notes
— Protection —
Armour plating+DR (e.g. e+4/k+4 per grade)9,000+/gradeSW Adds weight, slows Move at higher grades. Doesn’t help vs ion (AD 5) or disruptors.
Energy shield (integral)SR 5-20 vs energyper Doc VII shieldSW Nothing vs kinetic or ion; once/encounter; charges. As planning units & some war droids.
Ion-hardening+ion DR or +shutdown threshold48,000SW The key defence vs the droid-killer. Verpine-grade is best; expensive.
Reactive platingOnce/fight, halve one hit’s damage66,000SW Single-use per fight; bulky.
— Weaponry —
Electroshock probeMelee ion touch (1d ion)8,400SW Standard astromech defence; ion vs other droids.
Integral flamerWrist/chassis flame projector (Doc VII)24,000SW Short-range cone; area denial. Fuel-limited.
Self-destruct chargeDetonates on defeat (deny salvage)21,000SW Probe & assassin-droid standard. Damages the droid’s killer & destroys its data. Illegal on civilian droids.
Stun rayBuilt-in stun pistol12,000SW Non-lethal capture tool; utility-droid standard.
Weapon mount (melee)Mounts a vibro-weapon or industrial toolweapon +2,200SW Turns a loader’s arm into a crusher; cheap & brutal.
Weapon mount (ranged)Mounts a blaster/ion/sonic weapon (Doc VII)weapon +4,500SW Hands-free; the weapon’s own stats apply. Mount limit by chassis size.
— Cognition & Control —
Behavioural inhibitor (remove)Lets droid harm sapientsserviceSW Standard on combat droids; illegal to remove on civilian units. The line between servant & weapon.
Heuristic upgradeBasic → heuristic processor (learns)90,000SW Enables growth & hero status; risks personality & wipe.
Memory wipeResets droid to factory; erases growthserviceSW Removes learned skills & personality. Owners use it for control; droids dread it.
Restraining boltCompels obedience to holder750SW Cheap control; a heuristic droid resents it. Removable with Mechanic + access.
Skill softwareAdds a skill at a fixed level14,000+SW Plug-in expertise; basic droids stuck at the chip’s level.
Slicing countermeasures+to resist being sliced (Part II)27,000SW Hardens the processor; military standard.
— Mobility & Utility —
Improved sensor suite+Per, specialised detection5,500-12,000SW Makes a scout or marksman; probe-droid grade at the top.
Jetpack / repulsorliftAdds flight (short bursts)54,000SW War-droid-style mobility; power/fuel-limited. Ion-vulnerable systems.
Magnetic feet / climbingCling to hulls & walls9,600SW Boarding & maintenance; T3 feature.
Tool/utility appendageAdds a built-in instrument3,600+SW Welder, scanner, cutter, slicer-spike, etc.

Modification uses Mechanic (Robotics) and the chassis’s spare capacity. The most common field mod is the cheapest: bolt a weapon onto a labour droid. The most consequential is invisible: pulling a behavioural inhibitor turns a servant into a killer.

Part X

The GURPS
Droid

Meta-traits · reading a stat block · building a droid character

Everything to this point has described droids in play. This chapter is the machinery underneath: the GURPS 4e traits that make a droid a droid, how to read the stat blocks that follow in Part XI, and — for anyone who wants to play a droid as a character — exactly how to build one with character points. A droid is built like any GURPS character, plus a package of meta-traits that grant its mechanical nature and impose its mechanical weaknesses. The two roughly balance, which is why a droid PC can sit at the same table as an organic on the same point budget.

The Machine Meta-Trait SW

What ”Machine” Bundles. Every droid takes the Machine meta-trait — a single package standing in for a long list of advantages and disadvantages that all droids share. Buying it once is cleaner than listing twenty traits on every block. It is, on net, slightly favourable, and its components are spelled out below so you can adjust for an unusual droid.

Advantages in the Package. Doesn’t Breathe [20], Doesn’t Eat or Drink [10], Doesn’t Sleep [20], Immunity to Metabolic Hazards [30] (poison, disease, suffocation), Injury Tolerance: No Blood, Unliving [25] (and often No Brain, No Vitals for full units), Sealed [15], Vacuum Support [5], Mind immunity — Immunity to mind-affecting effects, the big one against the Force (Doc IV).

Disadvantages in the Package. No Fatigue Points (cannot exert, run on power instead), Electrical [−20] (vulnerable to ion & EMP, Part II), Maintenance (periodic Mechanic care), Restricted Diet: power cells, Reprogrammable [−10] (obeys a recognised owner / restraining bolt — the control disadvantage), and Social Stigma (Valuable Property / Subjugated) [−10] in most of the galaxy.

Healing Is Repair. A droid cannot benefit from natural healing, First Aid, Physician, kolto, or bacta. It is restored only with Mechanic (Robotics) and parts (Part II). Mechanically this is the Unhealing (Repair Only) limitation on its HP — a defining droid trait, and the reason a droid party needs a mechanic the way an organic party needs a medic.

Attributes for Droids

ST — Built, Not Grown. A droid’s ST is whatever it was manufactured with — a loader may have ST 20+, an astromech ST 6. Because droids don’t tire, high ST has no fatigue cost to them. ST also sets HP unless the chassis overrides it.

DX & IQ — the Processor’s Reach. DX reflects servo precision and balance; IQ reflects the processor. A basic processor caps usefully around IQ 8-10 (competent at its task, no judgement); a heuristic processor can reach IQ 12+ and, crucially, can improve over play. Combat droids buy up DX; analytical droids buy up IQ.

HT — Build Quality. HT is the droid’s reliability and resistance to system shock. It governs the HT rolls against ion shutdown (Part II) and how well the frame holds together. Military droids buy HT high; cheap labour units run HT 10 or less.

Secondary Characteristics. HP comes from ST and chassis (a droid’s ”health”); Will and Per from IQ (Per drives the sensor package); droids have no FP (they track power, not fatigue). Basic Speed and Move follow DX/HT and the locomotion type (Part VIII).

Building a Droid Character

The Point Budget. A droid PC is built on the campaign’s normal starting points (this campaign: 250), spending exactly like an organic — but the Machine meta-trait, the chassis, and any built-in systems are paid for in points (for a PC) or credits (for a purchased companion, Part VIII). A player-character droid pays points; an owned droid the party buys pays credits. Don’t pay twice.

Processor = Character Growth. Only a heuristic droid PC can earn and spend character points (learn skills, raise attributes, develop quirks). A basic-processor droid is, by definition, not a hero — it cannot improve, so it cannot be a player character. The heuristic processor is therefore mandatory for a droid PC, and is the in-fiction reason your droid can grow.

Skills Are Software. A droid buys skills with points like anyone, but its skills represent loaded software and accumulated heuristic experience. A freshly-built droid has its manufacturer’s skill suite; a long-running heuristic droid has whatever it has learned. A memory wipe (Part IX) is, mechanically, the involuntary loss of earned character points — which is why droids fear it.

Playing the Disadvantages. The droid disadvantages are real roleplay, not just point rebates: Reprogrammable means someone with the right access can command you; Social Stigma (Property) means much of the galaxy treats you as a thing; Electrical means the one enemy with an ion blaster is your nightmare. A well-played droid PC leans into these, and a good GM gives them weight.

The Droid Meta-Trait, Costed

ComponentTypePointsEffect
Doesn’t BreatheAdv20No air needed; immune to drowning & choke.
Doesn’t Eat or DrinkAdv10Runs on power cells, not food.
Doesn’t SleepAdv20Operates continuously; no rest needed.
Immunity to Metabolic HazardsAdv30Immune to poison, disease, suffocation.
Injury Tolerance (No Blood, Unliving)Adv25No bleeding; reduced wounding from impaling/piercing.
Immunity to Mind Control / AffectingAdv30Immune to Force mind tricks, fear, illusions (Doc IV).
Sealed + Vacuum SupportAdv20Survives vacuum & hostile atmospheres.
No Fatigue Pointsspecial—Tracks power, not fatigue; never tires. (Tracked via power cell, not FP.)
ElectricalDis−20Ion & EMP affect it (Part II); the defining droid weakness.
MaintenanceDis−5 to −15Needs periodic Mechanic care or degrades.
Unhealing (Repair Only)Dis−20No natural/medical healing; Mechanic (Robotics) & parts only.
ReprogrammableDis−10Obeys recognised owner / restraining bolt.
Social Stigma (Valuable Property)Dis−10Treated as property, not a person, in most of the galaxy.

Bundle these as the Machine meta-trait rather than listing each on every block. The net cost varies by build (~+95 in advantages, ~−65 in disadvantages → roughly +30 points for a typical full droid). Tiny utility droids drop some advantages (no vacuum rating, less injury tolerance); heavy combat droids add more. The stat blocks in Part XI show the net per droid.

Part XI

The Stat
Blocks

Full GURPS 4e droid archetypes — every degree, ready to read & play

Here is the heart of the builder’s book: complete GURPS 4e stat blocks for droid archetypes across all five degrees, drawn from across the Old Republic era — the worlds of the Jedi Civil War, the ancient Sith Wars, and the long Galactic War still to come. Each is an archetype, not a named individual, and each is built to be genuinely exceptional at its role — a dedicated, finished machine, not a stripped-down minimum. Read every block two ways: it is a drop-in NPC the GM can field tonight, and it is a worked example of how a droid of that kind is built — the attributes, traits, and skills that define it — for any player who wants to make a droid character of their own (Part X) or construct one in play (Part VIII). The two go hand in hand: building a droid PC and building a droid in-world are the same act, costed in points or in credits.

How to Read These Blocks

Attributes show [point cost] in brackets. The Machine line is the meta-trait package from Part X (its net value folded into the total). Skills follow the campaign sheet: Skill (Attribute/Difficulty) level [points]. DR is shown eenergy/kkinetic. The pts figure (top-right of each block) is the character-point cost to build that droid — a solid, role-dedicated droid runs around 150–220 points, support specialists and elites climb higher, and the deadliest combat units rival a seasoned organic hero. The Credits line is the buy price (Part VIII). To play one as a PC, build it on your campaign points exactly as written and add a heuristic processor so it can grow.

The bestiary runs by degree — first through fifth — so a reader can leaf to the kind of droid they need. Combat and companion-likely droids get the fullest treatment; near-identical menial drones are grouped. Every block is built from the same construction kit (Part VIII) and the same Machine meta-trait (Part X), so you can see exactly how the parts add up — and tweak any of them into a custom unit of your own.

Machine Meta-Trait
Doesn’t Breathe/Eat/Sleep · Immunity to Metabolic Hazards · Immunity to Mind-Affecting · Injury Tolerance (No Blood, Unliving) · Sealed · No FP · Unhealing (repair only) · Electrical · Maintenance · Reprogrammable · Social Stigma (property)
Advantages
High Manual Dexterity 4 [20] · Multiple Arms (4 fine manipulators, Extra-Flexible) [24] · Photographic Memory [10] (encyclopedic medical banks) · Versatile [5] · Single-Minded [5] · Reduced Time (Surgery) 2 [10] · Daredevil (steady under pressure)? no — Unfazeable [15]
Disadvantages
Pacifism (Cannot Harm Innocents) [−10] · Truthfulness [−5] · Sense of Duty (patients) [−5] · Cowardice [−10] (flees combat)
Skills
Surgery/TL (IQ/VH) 19 [24] · Physician/TL (IQ/H) 18 [16] · Diagnosis/TL (IQ/H) 17 [12] · Pharmacy/TL (IQ/H) 16 [8] · Physiology (3 species) (IQ/H) 16 [varies] · Electronics Op (Medical)/TL (IQ/A) 16 [4] · First Aid/TL (IQ/E) 16 [2]
Loadout & Notes
Integral surgical suite (acts as full operating theatre; installs cybernetics, Doc VII Pt IX) · kolto/bacta dispensers · diagnostic sensors. Credits: ≈ 22,000 (Part VIII). Non-combatant: under fire it shields its patient or flees. Heuristic upgrade lets it learn new specialties (alien biology, cybernetics, neurosurgery).
