Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5, Mathematics (Applied)−4 · Aided by a nav-computer or an astromech

The art of plotting a hyperspace jump and navigating realspace — the single most important skill aboard any starship that wants to arrive where it intended, intact. A successful roll lays in a safe course; failure scatters the jump, wastes fuel, or worse. This is an operation performed in minutes under pressure, not a workbench craft, so it carries no work-hours — but taking extra time to plot carefully grants the usual bonus, and a nav-computer or a Droid Programming (Navigation) astromech lends its rating. The Maker’s connection: an astromech is only as good as the navigation core written into it.

Standard use Plot a safe hyperspace jump between points. Time Minutes with a nav-computer; longer to chart a new route. Rolls One roll per jump plotted; a fail risks a misjump. Modifiers + with current nav-data; −2 for old data; −4 to −6 for uncharted or shifting routes.
Lockpicking
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5 · Electronic locks pair with Electronics Operation (Security)

Defeating mechanical and simple electronic locks by hand — the burglar’s and the stranded engineer’s craft. An operation resolved in seconds to a few minutes, not a project; tougher locks impose a penalty, and a serious electronic security system calls instead for Computer Hacking or Electronics Operation (Security). No work-hours; use the scene’s clock.

Standard use Open a mechanical or electronic lock without the key or code. Time 1 minutes per attempt — Seconds to minutes per lock. Rolls One roll per lock; a fail may jam it. Modifiers + with proper picks/slicer; −2 improvising; − by lock quality.

Part V · The Maker’s Force-Crafts

The crafts that produce the galaxy’s Force-touched artefacts: lightsabers, holocrons, alchemised metals, imbued relics. The campaign’s firm principle — the building is IQ; the Force is for operating. A gifted non-sensitive engineer can construct any of these objects flawlessly. What they cannot do is make the object live: a saber they assemble will not safely ignite for them, a holocron they build stays an empty lattice, an alchemised blade they forge holds no dark resonance. Each craft below is a learnable IQ skill; each notes plainly where a Force-user must take over to give the work its soul.

Lightsaber Engineering † SW
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Very Hard · Defaults Engineer (Force-Tech)−4, Armoury (Lightsabers)−3 · Prereq Armoury (Lightsabers) or Engineer (Force-Tech)

The design and master-construction of a lightsaber — the precise alignment of emitter matrix, focusing crystal, power cell, and field energiser into a stable plasma blade. Where Armoury (Lightsabers) covers competent assembly from a known plan, Lightsaber Engineering covers original design, exotic configurations, and the fine tolerances that separate a reliable blade from one that fails mid-duel. A successful roll produces a working saber from prepared parts and an attuned crystal; the margin sets the blade’s stability and quality. Building the saber is wholly technical and requires no Force sensitivity. Igniting and wielding it safely — and bonding to its crystal — requires the Force; an unattuned blade is unstable in an untrained hand.

Standard use Design, build, or repair a lightsaber and tune its crystal. Time Days to build; hours to tune or repair. Rolls One extended roll per saber. Modifiers + with a Force-tech bench & a focusing crystal; −3 for exotic configurations.
  • Standard / Single: the classic single-blade hilt.
  • Dual-Phase & Specialist: variable-length blades, crossguard vents, shoto and training configurations.
  • Double-Bladed / Saberstaff: the staff hilt with an emitter at each end — twin power cells, balanced energisers, the build the Saberstaff Form is fought with. SW
  • Paired / Jar’Kai Sets: matched blades built to be wielded together — balanced mass, harmonised crystals to prevent blade-feedback when crossed.

Configurations default to each other at −4.

Crystal Attunement † Force to operate
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults none · Prereq Force sensitivity (to attune); the cutting/shaping alone defaults to Jeweler−5

The craft of cutting, shaping, and seating a focusing crystal — usually kyber — into a lightsaber or holocron lattice. The physical work (faceting the crystal, fitting it to the emitter) is fine jewellery-craft and can be done by anyone with the skill. The attunement — the resonance that lets a crystal hold and colour a blade, or anchor a holocron’s matrix — is where the Force enters. A Force-user must bond the crystal: a Jedi’s patient communion yields a clear, stable resonance; a Sith’s domination “bleeds” the crystal to a b-loody red and a harsher edge. A non-sensitive can seat a pre-attuned crystal perfectly, but cannot attune a raw one.

  • Kyber (Lightsaber): the living crystal of the Jedi and Sith blade; the harmonic that sets blade colour and stability.
  • Lattice (Holocron): the carved crystal seed-pieces that give a holocron its capacity to store distinct Force workings.
  • Focusing Gems (Apparatus): the crystals of Force-detector arrays and other Force-Tech instruments.
Holocron Crafting † SW
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard (the Inscribed apex is Very Hard) · Specialties Archive / Jedi / Sith · Defaults none; specialties default to each other at −3 · Prereq Lightsaber Engineering or Engineer (Force-Tech), and Crystal Attunement (Lattice); Sith additionally requires Sith Alchemy (Talismans & Wards). A functioning holocron requires the Force to make in every tradition — the empty lattice is IQ, the living holocron is not.

The construction of a holocron — the crystalline lattice that stores knowledge under the guard of a gatekeeper, a recorded intelligence that decides who may open the holocron and teaches what is sealed inside. Building the empty lattice is supreme fine engineering, and a skilled non-sensitive can cut and assemble one flawlessly — but a bare lattice is an inert box. Making it a true holocron — attuning its crystal, sealing knowledge into it, and waking its gatekeeper — requires the Force in every tradition; this is the campaign’s rule that the building is IQ, but the living is the Force. The four traditions differ not in the box but in what they store, who their gatekeeper opens for, and how it teaches. The first three are this skill’s specialties; the fourth — the Inscribed Voice — is the apex (see below). A full account of gatekeepers and the step-by-step making of each kind follows the specialties.

Build the lattice An extended IQ task; the empty frame needs no Force, but waking its gatekeeper and sealing its contents always do. Time Archive ~80 work-hours at −2; Jedi/Sith cube or pyramid ~120 at −4. Rolls One Holocron Crafting (specialty) roll per 40-hour block, worst result governing; a Sith lattice also takes one Sith Alchemy (Talismans & Wards) roll for its trap-ward. Materials 1 lattice frame + kyber seed-pieces (cut and seated by Crystal Attunement) — neutrally attuned for Archive, light-communed for Jedi, dark-bled for Sith.

Archive (Standard) — Hard. The plainest holocron: a knowledge-vault holding ordinary learning — languages, star-charts, schematics, histories, science — never a Force power. Create: build the lattice (IQ) and seat neutrally-attuned seed-crystals; a Force-user of any alignment then wakes a simple gatekeeper (a brief imbuing). Store: record each subject as an IQ task of ~16 work-hours, rolling the Knowledge or Science skill being recorded to render it correctly; a Force-user then seals each subject (~half a day apiece) so the gatekeeper can present it. Teach: once made, anyone may open and consult it — reading needs no Force and no roll. On a recorded subject it tutors at the master rate of 150 hours/point (Force Training 9.5) and grants +1 to a single relevant skill roll while consulted.

Jedi (Light) — Hard. The classic cubic, light-aligned holocron, carrying an imprinted gatekeeper — a partial copy of the maker’s mind that teaches in dialogue. Create: cube frame + light-attuned kyber seed-pieces; a light or neutral Force-user communes the seeds to a clear resonance (Crystal Attunement). Store: the maker imprints a teaching through meditation — a Force working of roughly 1 day per tier of the power or lore being sealed, rolling Holocron Crafting (Jedi), and the maker must know the recorded power at skill 14+; the gatekeeper persona is set in the same rite. A teaching is not truly “known” by the holocron until the maker has cast it in earnest once. Teach: opens only for a light or neutral reader the gatekeeper judges worthy (a reaction or Force-skill check in a contested case). For a recorded power it satisfies the “a teacher or holocron may be required” gate on the rarest powers and counts as a master — 150 h/point, better for a great gatekeeper. It shuts itself to a dark-side reader.

Sith (Dark) — Hard; requires Sith Alchemy. The pyramidal, dark-aligned holocron: a more autonomous, manipulative gatekeeper in a lattice warded to kill the unworthy. Create: pyramid frame + bled (red) kyber seeds; the lattice is alchemically bound and trap-warded — a Sith Alchemy (Talismans & Wards) roll sets a lethal ward (resolve as that talisman’s effect; see Sith Alchemy). Store: the maker pours in knowledge and a shard of will — a dark working of ~1 day per tier, rolling Holocron Crafting (Sith) plus a Will roll at completion; each binding risks corruption (a Dark-Side check) and spends a fragment of the maker’s self. Teach: opens for a dark-side user, or for anyone who wins a Quick Contest of Will to dominate the gatekeeper; an unworthy reader who forces it triggers the ward. As a teacher it counts as a master for dark powers and alchemy (150 h/point), but every lesson is itself a Quick Contest of Will — lose, and the gatekeeper plants a compulsion or feeds the student’s corruption as it teaches.

Apex — the Inscribed Voice (Very Hard). The successor craft to the Rakatan and Kwa holocron-makers builds no archives: it forges holocrons from rare holocron-fragments into active ritual instruments — faces, soul-binds, shells, and Mist-Fold anchors — and wields them in combat. It is not a specialty of this skill; it is learned only within the Inscribed Voice tradition, at the hardest scale, and is detailed there (see The Inscribed · Holocrons, the Currency of the Craft).

Gatekeepers & the Making of a Holocron SW

What a gatekeeper is. A gatekeeper is the recorded intelligence bound into a holocron at its making — not a true living mind, but a Force-imprinted persona that wakes when the holocron is opened. It does three things no mere data-crystal can: it judges who may access the holocron (by passcode, by alignment, or by trial), it presents and explains the contents in dialogue rather than as a passive page — this is why a holocron teaches where a book only informs — and it guards what it holds. Every functioning holocron has one; waking it is the Force step that turns an inert lattice into a holocron, and an unimprinted lattice is just a beautiful box. How deep and how willful the gatekeeper is varies entirely by tradition.

Archive gatekeeper. A simple recorded persona — a librarian or index, set by the maker. It answers questions within its recorded subjects, has no independent will and no agenda, and judges nothing beyond a basic identity or passcode the maker assigns. It will open and recite for anyone the maker permitted; this is the only gatekeeper a non-sensitive can usefully consult unaided. Its teaching is patient but literal — it knows what was put in it and nothing more.

Jedi gatekeeper. A partial imprint of the maker’s own consciousness — a conversational echo carrying the maker’s knowledge, manner, and judgement. It teaches Socratically, tests a student’s intent and need, and judges worthiness by alignment — refusing a dark-side reader outright and often setting a trial or a question before it yields its deepest teachings. Benevolent, but bounded by the maker’s outlook and era; an ancient Jedi gatekeeper may be wise, gentle, and centuries out of step with the present.

Sith gatekeeper. A shard of the maker’s living will — far more autonomous, retaining the maker’s ambition, cunning, and malice. It teaches, but it also bargains, flatters, tempts, and lies; it tests a student by adversity and may attempt to dominate or possess a weak-willed reader (a Quick Contest of Will). It opens for the dark, or for one who masters it, and turns its lethal ward on the unworthy. The most dangerous are effectively the preserved personality of a long-dead Sith Lord, patiently working its own ends through whoever opens it.

Inscribed gatekeeper (apex). The Inscribed Voice takes the gatekeeper to its furthest, darkest extreme: the bound voices and soul-tethers of captured consciousnesses, which do not merely teach but power the holocron’s active workings. This is its own craft and its own moral weight — see The Inscribed · Holocrons, the Currency of the Craft.

Procedure at a glance. Each tradition’s making, in order — the materials, the roll, the time, and who must be present. One roll covers a 40-hour block; longer steps roll once per block, worst result governing (Working-Hours Reference).

  • Archive holocron. 1) Build the lattice — Holocron Crafting (Archive), −2, ~80h, 1 frame. 2) Cut & seat the seeds — Crystal Attunement (Lattice), ~24h; a Force-user of any alignment attunes them neutrally. 3) Wake the gatekeeper — a brief imbuing (~half a day); the maker sets the access permission. 4) Record & seal knowledge — per subject, an IQ task ~16h rolling the recorded Knowledge/Science skill, then a Force-user seals it (~half a day). → Anyone may then open and study; tutors at 150 h/point, +1 to a relevant roll.
  • Jedi holocron. 1) Build the cube — Holocron Crafting (Jedi), −4, ~120h. 2) Attune the seeds — Crystal Attunement (Lattice); a light/neutral user communes them to a clear resonance. 3) Imprint the gatekeeper — the maker meditates a partial copy of their own mind into the lattice and sets the alignment/worthiness gate. 4) Seal each teaching — ~1 day per tier, rolling Holocron Crafting (Jedi); the maker must know the power at skill 14+, and it is not truly “known” until cast in earnest once. → Opens for the worthy light/neutral reader; teaches its powers and lore at 150 h/point (better for a great gatekeeper).
  • Sith holocron. 1) Build the pyramid — Holocron Crafting (Sith), −4, ~120h. 2) Bleed & seat the seeds — Crystal Attunement (Lattice); a dark user bleeds the kyber red by domination. 3) Ward it — one Sith Alchemy (Talismans & Wards) roll sets the lethal trap on the unworthy. 4) Bind the gatekeeper — the maker pours in knowledge and a shard of will over ~1 day per tier, rolling Holocron Crafting (Sith) plus a Will roll at completion; a Dark-Side check applies and a fragment of self is spent. → Opens for the dark or for one who wins a Will contest to dominate it; teaches dark powers and alchemy at 150 h/point, but every lesson is itself a Will contest, and the ward injures any unworthy reader who forces it.
  • Inscribed holocron (apex). Not built by this skill. Forged from rare holocron-fragments + frames over a 7-day (small) or 30-day (large) ritual, with faces, soul-binds, and shells rather than a single sealed archive — see The Inscribed.
Sith Alchemy † SW
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Very Hard · Defaults none · Prereq Chemistry or Metallurgy (any); the empowering ritual requires the Unchained (dark side)

The forbidden science of transmuting matter and flesh by joined craft and dark ritual — alchemised metals, warped beasts, and bound talismans. The technical half (the furnaces, reagents, metallurgical procedure, surgical grafting) is a learnable IQ craft; a non-sensitive alchemist’s assistant can perform every physical step. The empowering half — pouring dark-side energy into the work to make Sith metal hungry for a blade’s edge, or a beast obedient and unnatural — requires a user of the Unchained Path, and stains the maker as surely as any other dark working. Without that ritual, the products are merely well-made; with it, they become genuinely Sith.

Standard use Work Sith alchemy — corrupt matter, forge alchemical items or beasts. Time Days to weeks; dark-side ritual. Rolls One extended roll per working. Modifiers + in a Sith forge; − by ambition; each use risks corruption.
  • Alchemised Metal: Sith swords and armour — metal made preternaturally hard and lightsaber-resistant, forged to drink the dark side. (Pairs with Smith and Metallurgy.)
  • Bioalchemy: the warping of living creatures — war-beasts, leviathans, twisted guardians. Grim, slow, and cruel.
  • Talismans & Wards: amulets and focii that store or amplify dark-side workings; the lethal traps that ward a Sith holocron or tomb.
Jedi Artisan (Force Imbuement) † SW
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Very Hard · Defaults none · Prereq the relevant base craft (Smith, Armoury, Weaver…); the imbuing meditation requires Force sensitivity (light or neutral)

The light-and-neutral counterpart to Sith Alchemy: the craft of imbuing well-made objects with the Force through patient meditation rather than domination. The base object is made by an ordinary craft skill; the artisan then works the Force into it over time. The imbuing meditation requires a Force-user. Produces the galaxy’s Force-touched gear — robes and armour that resist Force damage (the DR-vs-Force rule), focusing relics that steady a wielder’s own powers, and meditation aids. Quieter than Sith work, and it exacts no corruption — only time, and a calm mind.

Standard use Imbue an object with the Force for a lasting effect. Time Days; meditative ritual. Rolls One extended roll per item. Modifiers + in a calm, attuned place; − for ambitious effects.
  • Force-Touched Armour & Robes: garments and plate that carry DR specifically against Force-power damage (Lightning, Push impact, and so on).
  • Focusing Relics: objects that steady or sharpen the wielder’s own Force use — meditation crystals, attuned foci.
  • Wards & Seals: the light-side counterpart to dark talismans — protective imbuements on a place or object.

Who can attempt these. Every Force-craft above is an IQ skill anyone may learn and roll for the building. The purple-tagged steps — attuning a crystal, sealing a holocron, empowering an alchemical work, imbuing a relic — are the only points that require the Force, and they hook into the relevant Force book (Crystal Attunement to the wielder’s tradition, Holocron sealing to the Inscribed Voice, Sith Alchemy to the Unchained Path, Jedi Artisan to the Living Path or Common Force). A mixed workshop — a brilliant engineer and a single Force-user partner — can produce anything in this Part between them.

Part VI · Worked Maker’s Chains

What does it actually take to make the galaxy’s great objects? Each chain below lists the skills, specialties, prerequisites, and order of work. One character with every skill can do the whole chain alone (slowly); a workshop splits the links among specialists. Times and exact target numbers are the GM’s call — these chains define the shape of the work.

I. Refined Beskar Plate — a Mandalorian vambrace

1. Ore. Acquire raw Mandalorian iron. Common enough; the value is in the working, not the rock.

2. Refine. Metallurgy (Mandalorian Iron / Beskar) — the clan secret, no default of any kind. Success refines the ore to full-density beskar stock. Failure wastes it; critical failure yields brittle stock that will fail under a real blow. This is the gate: without this specialty you can only ever make Tier-1 “sturdy metal,” never saber-resistant beskar.

3. Forge. Smith (Armour Plate) shapes the refined stock into a vambrace. The Metallurgy roll is complementary — well-refined stock makes the forging easier. Working beskar without the matching Metallurgy specialty is at −4 and cannot reach the proper grade.

4. Fit. Armoury (Body Armour) mounts the finished plate to a harness so it can be worn and integrated with a suit.

→ Result: a beskar vambrace, DR 55–60, with a Damage Divisor of ÷10 against all damage — the blade skitters off. Protects only the location it covers. A full suit is a campaign arc, not an afternoon.

II. A Protocol Droid — from scratch

1. Design. Droid Engineering (Third-Degree) — lay out a service chassis built around a linguistic processor: humanoid form for etiquette, vocabulator integration, presentation plating.

2. Parts. Machinist fabricates the frame, joints, and servos; Electronics Repair (Computers) builds the processor and vocabulator hardware; Electronics Repair (Comms) for the linguistic-interface gear.

3. Assemble. Droid Mechanic (Third-Degree) builds the chassis into a working body — limbs, plating, power, sensors.

4. Core. Droid Programming (Linguistic / Translation) — the defining link. This writes the translation matrices and etiquette databases that make it a protocol droid rather than a humanoid shell. Without this specialty you have a body that walks but cannot do the one job it exists for. Optionally add Droid Programming (Heuristic / Personality) for genuine character.

→ Specialisation note: the builder needs the Third-Degree specialty of Droid Engineering and Droid Mechanic, and the Linguistic specialty of Droid Programming. A Fourth-Degree (combat) droidsmith could attempt all of it at the cross-degree default penalties — and would likely produce a stiff, socially clumsy unit.

III. A Lightsaber

1. Design. Lightsaber Engineering (by configuration — Single, Saberstaff, Jar’Kai pair…), or work from a known plan with Armoury (Lightsabers).

2. Crystal. Crystal Attunement (Kyber) cuts and seats the crystal. The cutting is craft; the attunement — bonding the kyber, setting its colour and stability — requires a Force-user. A Sith bleeds it red; a Jedi communes for a clear resonance.

3. Assemble. Armoury (Lightsabers) fits emitter, lens, cell, and casing around the attuned crystal into a working hilt.

→ Operate: the finished saber is a technical object — but igniting and wielding it safely needs the Force; an unattuned or unbonded blade is unstable in untrained hands. A non-sensitive can build a perfect saber for a Force-user to wield.

IV. A Holocron

1. Choose the tradition. Archive (mundane knowledge, no Force), Jedi (light gatekeeper), Sith (dark gatekeeper + lethal ward), or the Inscribed Voice’s forged combat-holocron (the apex — built not here but in that tradition, from rare fragments).

2. Lattice. Holocron Crafting (Archive / Jedi / Sith) builds the crystalline frame — supreme fine engineering, IQ, no Force.

3. Seeds & attunement. Crystal Attunement (Lattice) cuts and seats the kyber seed-pieces in every tradition; a Force-user then attunes them — neutral for an Archive, a Jedi communes them clear, a Sith bleeds them red. A Sith lattice is also trap-warded with Sith Alchemy (Talismans & Wards).

4. Wake the gatekeeper & seal the contents. Every tradition needs the Force here. Archive: a Force-user wakes a simple gatekeeper and seals each recorded subject (the data itself is an IQ task, ~16h/subject, rolling the recorded skill). Jedi/Sith: the maker imprints the gatekeeper and seals each teaching — ~1 day per tier, and a Sith binding also takes a Will roll and risks corruption.

→ Result: a holocron that teaches its recorded subjects at the master rate (150 h/point, Force Training 9.5). An Archive vault, once made, may be opened and read by anyone; a Jedi cube opens for the light; a Sith pyramid opens for the dark (or one who dominates its gatekeeper) and kills the unworthy. An empty lattice — built but never woken — is a beautiful, inert box.

V. An Alchemised Sith Blade

1. Refine & forge. Metallurgy and Smith (Edged Weapons) produce a superbly-made blade — the physical sword.

2. Alchemise. Sith Alchemy (Alchemised Metal) performs the furnace-and-reagent procedure — the technical, learnable half.

3. Empower. A user of the Unchained Path pours the dark side into the work, making the metal preternaturally hard, lightsaber-resistant, and hungry — and staining the maker as any dark working does.

→ Result: a true Sith sword. Skip step 3 and you have merely an excellent blade; the dark resonance is what the Force adds.

Part VII · The Working-Hours Reference

A single bench reference for the time and difficulty of every craft, repair, and processing task in the galaxy. Each table lists what a skill can make or do, a roll modifier applied to that skill, and the work hours — the total hours of hands-on labour the job takes, not the calendar time it spans. The universal rules below govern how every figure flexes.

Universal Rules

Work Hours, not calendar time. Every “Work Hours” figure is the total hours actually spent working the task. To get calendar duration, spread those hours across a real schedule — a focused maker manages roughly 8 productive hours a day, so a 40-hour job is about a working week, a 120-hour job about three. A workshop running shifts, or several hands on parallel links, finishes in proportionally less calendar time. Hours marked with a range scale with the size and quality of the piece.

Long jobs & rolls. A single roll covers any job of 40 work hours or less. Past that, roll once per 40-hour block of effort; the worst result governs the batch (a bad block is a setback, not always a ruin). Critical failure on any block ruins materials and the hours spent.

Taking Extra Time. Doubling the work hours gives +1 to the roll; ×4 gives +2; ×8 gives +3; ×16 gives +4 (the cap). Patient work is safe work.

Rushing. Halving the hours costs −2; quartering them costs −4. Below that the task cannot be hurried — some processes simply take their time (curing, forging heat-cycles, bacta growth).

Tools & Workshop. Field / improvised conditions: −2 to −5. Proper portable kit or a standard workshop: 0. A master facility (clan forge, shipyard, Jedi sanctum): +1 to +2, and may waive the “no default” barrier on a borrowed exotic specialty.

Teamwork. A helper with a relevant skill rolls first; success lends +1 (+2 on a critical) to the lead. Independent links of a Maker’s Chain run in parallel across specialists — total project time collapses toward the single longest link, not the sum.

Margin. A wide success yields a superior item (GM may grant a small stat bonus, reduced weight, or better quality). Ordinary failure wastes part of the hours; critical failure ruins the work (and on weapons, sabers, reactors, or explosives, may injure the maker).

Drafting & Blueprints — Before You Build

Complex items are built from a plan. Drafting it is a Design roll (Engineer or the item’s design skill) taking the design hours below; the roll’s margin sets the blueprint grade, and building without a plan applies the no-plan penalty. Full rules: Part I·C.

Item complexityConcept / MentalWorking Sketch (¼)Full Blueprint
Simple device (comlink, single tool, basic gear)minutes2–68–24
Complex device (vehicle subsystem, standard droid, saber)~0.5 hr10–2040–80
Major system (starship, advanced droid, holocron, great work)1–2 hr50–150160–600
A Master Blueprint needs full hours plus banked extra time (or a crit / margin-10+ Design roll): build at +2, build time −25%. The per-skill Design rows below are the Full-Blueprint times for that item.
Prep you broughtBuild penaltyBuild timeQuality ceiling
Master / Sound / Working-Draft Blueprint+2 / +1 / +0normal (−25% for Master)up to masterwork
Working Sketch only−2+50%Working Draft
Concept / nothing (improvise)−4+50% (+100% if novel)Working Draft
Expert+ in the skill (RSL +4 +)no penalty — works from memorynormalper the Mastery Ladder

The Maker’s Force-Crafts

The line, once more. Every Force-craft is an IQ task anyone may attempt for the building. The purple steps — attuning, sealing, empowering, imbuing — are not craft rolls and carry no work-hours; they are Force workings that hook into the relevant book.

Lightsaber Engineering — Building Sabers

IQ / Very Hard · configurations: Single, Dual-phase, Saberstaff, Jar’Kai pair
OutputModWork HoursNotes
Assemble from a known plan−28Or use Armoury (Lightsabers).
Design & build a single-blade saber−224Original work.
Dual-phase / specialist hilt−340Variable blade, vents, shoto.
Saberstaff (double) / Jar’Kai matched pair−460–80Twin cells / harmonised crystals.
All builds are IQ. Igniting, bonding, and wielding require the Force.

Crystal Attunement — Cutting & Seating

IQ / Hard · cutting is craft; attuning is a Force working
TaskModWork HoursNotes
Seat a pre-attuned crystal+02Fitting work only.
Cut & facet a kyber crystal−28Physical jewellery-craft.
Carve holocron lattice seed-pieces−224Sets the holocron’s capacity.
Attune / bond a raw crystal——Force working. Jedi commune; Sith bleed it red.

Holocron Crafting — Building & Recording

IQ / Hard · specialties: Archive, Jedi, Sith (the Inscribed apex is Very Hard — see The Inscribed)
TaskModWork HoursNotes
Archive lattice (standard knowledge-vault)−280Neutrally-attuned seeds; a Force-user wakes its gatekeeper.
Jedi cube / Sith pyramid lattice−4120Attuned kyber seeds; a Sith lattice adds a trap-ward (Sith Alchemy).
Record a mundane subject (Archive)+016IQ task rolling the recorded skill; a Force-user then wakes the gatekeeper / seals it (~½ day).
Seal a teaching / imprint a gatekeeper (Jedi/Sith)—~1 day / tierForce work. Not “known” until cast in earnest once; Sith adds a Will roll.
Forged fragment-holocron (Inscribed apex)—ritual7-day small / 30-day large forging ritual; see The Inscribed.

Sith Alchemy — The Technical Half

IQ / Very Hard · the physical procedure; empowering is an Unchained ritual
Output (procedure)ModWork HoursNotes
Alchemise metal (Sith sword / armour)−460Furnace & reagent work.
Talisman / ward fabrication−440Focus for dark workings.
Bioalchemy (warping a creature)−6160Grim, slow surgical-ritual craft.
Empower the work——Unchained ritual — stains the maker.

Jedi Artisan — Force Imbuement

IQ / Very Hard · build the base object, then imbue (a Force working)
OutputModWork HoursNotes
Prepare Force-touched armour / robe (base craft)−224Then imbue.
Focusing relic (meditation crystal, attuned focus)−340Steadies the wielder’s own powers.
Ward / seal on a place or object−340Light-side counterpart to talismans.
The imbuing meditation——Force work, light or neutral. Exacts time, not corruption.
Field Works & Salvage

Construction & Salvage

Engineer/Architecture IQ/A · Scrounging IQ/E · Salvage IQ/A
TaskModWork HoursNotes
Erect a prefab shelter / outpost module+08Kit construction.
Fortify a position / field works−124Cover, emplacements, defences.
Scrounge for a needed part+01–4Searching, not making.
Strip salvage from a wreck+08Recover usable parts & materials.
Build a permanent structure / base—industrialWorkforce and time, not a personal job.

Allied Processing & Care

TaskModWork HoursNotes
Hazardous Materials — safe handling / containment+00.5–2Per batch or system; failure risks leak / contamination.
Hazardous Materials — disposal / decontamination−14–8Reactor waste, spills, unstable stock.
Veterinary — treat a beast (injury / illness)+00.5–3As Physician, for creatures; bioalchemy beasts at −2.
Astrogation — plot a hyperspace jumpvariesreal-time*Minutes under pressure; extra time gives the usual bonus. See below.

Real-Time Tasks (not on this scale)

Some skills resolve in seconds to minutes under pressure, not project hours, and so carry no work-hours figure. These are operations, not crafts: Astrogation plotting a hyperspace jump, Slicing a live system (a Quick Contest vs the security rating, per the Computers rules), Lockpicking or defeating an electronic lock (a roll taking from ten seconds to a few minutes), Explosives (EOD) disarming a live device, and the operation of vehicles, sensors, and weapons in play. Use the scene’s clock, not the workbench’s.

Part VIII · Output Catalogues

Exactly what each making-skill can produce, in detail. The entries in Parts II–V define what a skill is; these catalogues spell out the concrete goods it turns out, sorted by specialty. A maker can build anything in their skill’s catalogue; rarer or restricted items (noted) may need plans, a licence, or a Master’s hand. Pure repair, operation, and knowledge skills (Mechanic, Physician, Slicing, the sciences) are not listed — they fix, use, and inform rather than manufacture.

Engineering & Fabrication
Engineer — produces designs & plans

Engineer makes no objects directly; it produces the working plans others build from. A design, once proven, can be built repeatedly by the assembly trades.

  • Droids: full droid architectures by degree — medical, astromech, protocol, combat, labour
  • Vehicles & Starships: speeder, swoop, walker, and starfighter classes; subsystem and drive layouts
  • Weapons & Power: blaster and slugthrower internals, heavy-weapon systems, reactors, power cells, capacitor banks
  • Electronics: sensor suites, comm arrays, computer and droid-brain architectures
  • Cybernetics & Force-Tech: implant schematics; saber emitters, holocron lattices, Force-detector arrays (operation needs the Force)
Machinist — mechanical parts & tools

The fabrication of parts from prepared stock — the components every assembler needs.

  • Drivetrain & motion: gears, servomotors, actuators, linkages, bearings, springs
  • Structure: frame members, brackets, mounts, fasteners, housings
  • Tools & jigs: custom tools and fixtures for use with Armoury, Droid trades, and Lockpicking
  • Precision: tight-tolerance components, micromachine parts, replacement parts for any mechanical device
Vehicle & Starship Assembly — finished craft

A named Maker’s Chain (Engineer + Mechanic): turning parts and plans into working vehicles.

  • Ground & air: landspeeders, airspeeders, swoops, speeder bikes, repulsor sleds, walkers
  • Space: starfighters and shuttles (team work), light-freighter components and refits
  • Scale note: capital-ship hulls are drydock-and-workforce industry, beyond a personal build
Materials, Metalwork & Chemical Goods
Metallurgy — refined stock

Raw ore into worked stock of known grade — the metal every smith and assembler depends on.

  • Common: durasteel, plasteel, structural alloys, ferrocrete reinforcement stock
  • Heavy & dense: quadanium and capital-hull plate, doonium, dense shielding alloys
  • Exotic (specialty): cortosis-weave (saber-shorting), phrik (saber-resistant), Mandalorian iron → refined beskar (clan secret)
  • Process: tempering, heat-treatment, and re-alloying of existing stock
Smith — forged goods

Refined stock shaped by the forge into plate, blade, and fitting.

  • Armour plate: vambraces, breastplates, greaves, pauldrons, helms — including beskar plate (with the matching specialty)
  • Edged weapons: vibroblade cores, beskads, swords, knives, axe and polearm heads, the blade-stock of a Sith sword
  • Structure & fittings: hafts, frames, brackets, tools, ceremonial and clan metalwork
Jeweler — gem & fine work

The fine craft of gems and precious metal — and the physical cutting behind crystal work.

  • Cut stones: faceted gems and crystals; the physical faceting of a kyber crystal
  • Lattice work: carved holocron lattice seed-pieces (the crystal frame, before sealing)
  • Adornment: settings, fine ornamental metalwork, signet and clan pieces
Chemistry — compounds & reagents

The chemical feedstock of half a dozen other trades.

  • Reactive: acids, corrosives, oxidisers, propellants, explosive fillers (for Explosives)
  • Utility: solvents, adhesives, fuels, lubricants, industrial chemicals
  • Dispersal: smoke, gas, and aerosol compounds; reagents for Pharmacy and Metallurgy
Pharmacy & Poisons — drugs & toxins

The bench of healing and harm — the same knowledge turned to opposite ends.

  • Pharmaceuticals: stimpacks, combat stims, anaesthetics, painkillers, antidotes, anti-tox, bacta additives
  • Poisons: contact, ingested, inhaled, and injected toxins; paralytics and neurotoxins
  • Spice & narcotics: glitterstim, ryll, andris and recreational spice — the Hutts’ trade (restricted)
Explosives — charges & devices

Everything that goes bang, by intent and on schedule.

  • Charges: breaching charges, shaped demolition charges, det-tape, improvised explosives
  • Munitions: thermal detonator assembly, proximity and pressure mines, grenade fills
  • Triggers: timers, remote and proximity detonators, slicing- or sensor-linked initiators
Electronics, Droids & Computers
Electronics Repair — circuitry & devices

Builds and repairs the galaxy’s electronics, by specialty.

  • Sensors: scanners, life-form detectors, scopes, targeting arrays, motion and proximity sensors
  • Comms: comlinks, transceivers, holoprojectors, encryption hardware, relay beacons
  • Security: locks, alarms, surveillance gear, restraining bolts, slicing-countermeasure hardware
  • Computers: droid logic boards, ship computers, datapads, memory cores
  • Medical: bacta-tank controllers, diagnostic scanners, surgical-droid instruments, prosthetic interfaces
Droid Mechanic — droid bodies

The physical droid — chassis and systems, ready for a core.

  • Chassis: full droid bodies by degree; frames, plating, sealed and armoured shells
  • Articulation: manipulator arms, tool-arms, leg/tread/repulsor locomotion, sensor heads
  • Internals: servomotor banks, power cells, scomp-links, restraining-bolt sockets
Droid Programming — behavioural cores

The droid’s mind — the core that makes a chassis a being-of-sorts.

  • Linguistic: translation matrices and etiquette databases — the protocol droid’s soul
  • Navigation: astrogation and ship-systems cores — the astromech’s craft
  • Combat logic: threat assessment and weapon discipline — war droids and assassins (handle with care)
  • Medical / analytical: diagnostic and research logic
  • Heuristic: adaptive learning and genuine personality — the root of every droid that became a character
Computers — software & code

Written, not forged: the programs that run and break systems.

  • Programs: utilities, control software, ship- and droid-system code, behavioural sub-routines
  • Security: encryption (Cryptography), intrusion-countermeasure (ICE), and the slicing tools that beat them
  • Note: operating these — live slicing — is a real-time contest, not a build
Forgery & Counterfeiting — the false-true

Documents and credentials made to deceive.

  • Documents: travel papers, cargo manifests, permits, official seals
  • Identity: identity chips, security passes, cloned chipcodes (with Electronics Security)
  • Currency & goods: counterfeit credits and certificates of authenticity (heavily restricted)
Medical, Cybernetic & Biological
Cybernetics & Bioelectronics — implants

The machine-flesh bridge — built on the bench, fitted in surgery.

  • Limbs: prosthetic arms, legs, and hands — restoring crippled or severed limbs
  • Senses & organs: cybernetic eyes and ears, internal organ and life-support implants
  • Neural: brain implants, skill-chips, interface jacks and neural ports (e.g. a Basilisk skull-mount), reflex boosters
Bioengineering — engineered life

The technical shaping of living matter (the half of bioalchemy that needs no ritual).

  • Cloning: grown and decanted organisms from genetic material — the Kaminoan art
  • Genetics: tailored modifications, resistances, and designed traits
  • Tissue: vat-grown organs, grafts, and biological components for medical and cybernetic use
The Maker’s Force-Crafts

Reminder. Every item below is built by an IQ craft. The Force-only finishing step — attuning, sealing, empowering, imbuing — gives it life, and that alone needs a Force-user.

Lightsaber Engineering / Armoury (Lightsabers) — sabers

The hilt and blade-apparatus, ready for an attuned crystal.

  • Single: standard single-blade hilts, shoto (short), training sabers
  • Variant: dual-phase (variable length), crossguard/vented hilts, specialist emitters
  • Multi: double-bladed saberstaffs, matched Jar’Kai pairs (harmonised to cross safely)
Crystal Attunement — cut crystal

The physical cutting and seating of focusing crystals (attunement is the Force step).

  • Lightsaber: faceted kyber seated for a stable blade
  • Holocron: carved lattice seed-pieces that set storage capacity
  • Apparatus: focusing gems for Force-detector arrays and instruments
Holocron Crafting — the lattice

The crystalline frame that — once sealed — stores Force knowledge.

  • Jedi: cubic, light-aligned holocrons
  • Sith: pyramidal, dark-aligned holocrons (often warded with lethal traps)
  • Great works: multi-face great lattices and soul-bind frames
Sith Alchemy — the procedure

The technical alchemical work (empowering is the Unchained ritual).

  • Metal: alchemised Sith swords and armour — hard, saber-resistant, hungry for the dark
  • Talismans: amulets, focii, and the wards that guard a holocron or tomb
  • Bioalchemy: the substrate for warped beasts and guardians (with Bioengineering)
Jedi Artisan — imbued goods

The base objects for light-and-neutral imbuement (imbuing is the Force step).

  • Protective: Force-touched armour and robes carrying DR vs Force-power damage
  • Focusing: meditation crystals and attuned relics that steady the wielder’s own powers
  • Wards: protective seals on a place or object — the light-side answer to dark talismans
Field Works
Construction & Salvage — structures & recovery

Shelter raised and materials reclaimed in the field.

  • Shelter: prefab shelters, outpost modules, field bridges
  • Defence: fortifications, emplacements, bunkers, breastworks
  • Recovery: salvaged components and materials stripped from wrecks (the start of many a chain)

Part IX · Combat & Weapon Skills

The skills of the fight. Unlike the crafts — which are knowledge, and so keyed to IQ — combat skills are reflex, timing, and trained coordination, and so are keyed to DX (a few command and targeting skills use IQ). They mirror the GURPS weapon skills one-for-one wherever the port is clean, dressed in the galaxy’s own terms: Beam Weapons becomes Blasters, Guns becomes Slugthrowers, and so on. A weapon you have never handled imposes a familiarity penalty (−2) until you have spent time with it, even within a skill you know. The master weapon-to-skill table at the end of this Part covers every arm in common use.

Ranged — Energy & Slug Weapons
Blasters †
Attribute DX · Difficulty Easy · Defaults DX−4, other Blasters specialty−4, Slugthrowers (any)−4

The galaxy’s signature arms — ranged weapons that fire bolts of superheated, magnetically-bottled plasma. This is GURPS Beam Weapons in Star Wars dress, and it governs nearly every energy sidearm and long-arm a character will carry. A successful roll hits; the bolt’s damage, range, and recoil come from the weapon. Ion weapons (anti-droid), disruptors (banned almost everywhere), and stun settings all fire on the same skill — only their effect differs. Specialise:

Standard use Fire a blaster — pistol, carbine, or rifle. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack per turn. Rolls One roll to hit per shot or burst. Modifiers + from Aim, a brace, and a scope; − from range, target movement, cover, darkness, or a called shot.
  • Pistol: blaster pistols, hold-out blasters, heavy blaster pistols, ion pistols, disruptor pistols, sidearms of every make
  • Rifle: blaster rifles, blaster carbines, heavy blaster rifles, sniper blasters, ion rifles, disruptor rifles — any shouldered energy long-arm
  • Cannon: man-portable heavy beam weapons fired from the shoulder or a brace — the lighter end of crew-served beams (heavier mounts use Heavy Weapons or Gunner)

Specialties default to each other at −4. The bowcaster, though a beam weapon, is traditionally fired on Crossbow — see below.

Slugthrowers †
Attribute DX · Difficulty Easy · Defaults DX−4, other Slugthrowers specialty−2, Blasters (any)−4

Kinetic projectile weapons — the old, cheap, and stubborn arms that throw solid slugs, flechettes, or needles by chemical or gas propellant. This is GURPS Guns. Slugthrowers are crude beside a blaster, but they are cheap, hard to trace by energy signature, function where blasters are jammed or forbidden, and a slug ignores the tricks that stop a bolt. Specialise:

Standard use Fire a slug-throwing firearm at a target. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack (a turn). Rolls One attack roll per shot. Modifiers + to Aim; − for range, movement, cover (Doc II).
  • Pistol: slug pistols, machine pistols, hold-out slug guns
  • Rifle: slug rifles, carbines, hunting rifles, marksman’s rifles
  • Scattergun: shotguns and scatterguns — spread-shot close-range weapons
  • Repeater: automatic slug weapons — submachine guns and squad automatic slugthrowers
  • Needler: needlers and flechette guns, often paired with poisons or tranquilisers

Specialties default to each other at −2.

Crossbow
Attribute DX · Difficulty Easy · Defaults DX−4

The skill of the bowcaster and of traditional crossbows. The Wookiee bowcaster is a beam weapon by its bolt, but a crossbow by its mechanism — a hand-spanned energy quarrel launched along a polearm-stiff frame, demanding the strength to cock it (a high ST requirement) and the same skill as any crossbow. A successful roll looses a bolt accurately; reloading takes time and muscle.

Standard use Shoot a crossbow at a target. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack; reload between shots. Rolls One attack roll per bolt. Modifiers + to Aim; − for range & movement.
Bow
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5

Drawing and loosing arrows from a hand-drawn bow — the weapon of primitive worlds, hunters, and traditionalists across the galaxy. Less common than the blaster, but silent, cheap, and unjammable.

Standard use Shoot a bow at a target. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack per turn. Rolls One attack roll per arrow. Modifiers + to Aim; − for range, wind, movement.
Sling
Attribute DX · Difficulty Hard · Defaults DX−6

Hurling stones or bullets from a sling — a primitive skill, surprisingly deadly in a trained hand, found among low-tech peoples and improvising survivors.

Standard use Hurl a stone or bullet with a sling. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack; wind-up. Rolls One attack roll per shot. Modifiers + to Aim; − for range & movement.
Blowpipe
Attribute DX · Difficulty Hard · Defaults DX−6

Firing darts or needles from a blowpipe — near-silent and almost always paired with a toxin or tranquiliser (see Pharmacy & Poisons). The weapon of hunters, assassins, and certain primitive cultures.

Standard use Shoot a dart, often poisoned, from a blowpipe. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack. Rolls One attack roll per dart. Modifiers + at short range; − beyond; poison applies separately.
Heavy, Crew-Served & Vehicle Weapons
Heavy Weapons †
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5, Blasters (Cannon)−4 (beam types), Gunner (same)−4

Man-portable and crew-served support weapons — the arms too big for a single specialty above. This consolidates the heavy end of GURPS Guns and the portable end of Gunner. A successful roll lands the burst, salvo, or shell on target; many of these weapons need a brace, a bipod, or a loader. Specialise:

Standard use Operate a crew-served or heavy ranged weapon. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack; setup may take a turn. Rolls One attack roll per shot or burst. Modifiers + braced/aimed; − for movement & range.
  • Direct Fire: repeating blasters and blaster cannons (the tripod-mounted E-Web and its kin), portable ion cannons, autocannon
  • Launchers: missile and rocket launchers, anti-armour and anti-vehicle tubes, portable concussion launchers
  • Grenade Launcher: launched grenades and mini-ordnance — arcing or direct

Specialties default to each other at −4.

Gunner †
Attribute DX · Difficulty Easy · Defaults DX−4

Firing vehicle- and emplacement-mounted weapons — the gunner’s station aboard a starfighter, a turret, a walker, or a wall battery. GURPS Gunner exactly. The mount handles the weight and recoil; the gunner provides the aim, leading the target and timing the shot. Specialise:

Standard use Fire a vehicle- or ship-mounted weapon. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack per turn. Rolls One attack roll per shot; vehicle scale. Modifiers + with a fire-control lock; − for evasive targets & range.
  • Beams: laser and blaster cannons, turbolasers, ion cannons — the energy batteries of ships and emplacements
  • Cannon: mass-driver cannon, projectile autocannon, slug-firing vehicle weapons
  • Missiles: concussion missiles, proton torpedoes, guided anti-ship and anti-vehicle ordnance
  • Rockets: unguided rocket pods and barrage launchers
  • Tractor / Support: tractor-beam projectors and other non-damaging mounted systems (operation; pairs with Electronics Operation)

Specialties default to each other at −4. Capital-scale and indirect fire is Artillery.

Artillery †
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Indirect and capital-scale fire — lobbing ordnance at targets you cannot directly see, and the deliberate, calculated gunnery of siege engines and orbital bombardment. Keyed to IQ, because it is a problem of plotting and correction more than reflex. GURPS Artillery. Specialise in Beams, Cannon, Bombs (orbital / aerial), Guided Missile, or Torpedoes; specialties default to each other at −4.

Standard use Direct indirect or area fire onto a target. Time 1 minutes per attempt — A turn to lay; rounds to adjust. Rolls One roll to lay; adjust each round. Modifiers + with a spotter (Targeting & Spotting); − for unseen or moving targets.
Melee — The Lightsaber, Vibroweapons & Blades
Lightsaber †
Attribute DX · Difficulty Very Hard · Defaults none — it cannot be defaulted

The most demanding personal weapon in the galaxy, and the reason this skill stands alone at Very Hard — the hardest difficulty there is. A lightsaber’s blade is weightless, yet the contained energy loop fights every change of direction with a fierce gyroscopic resistance; the hilt has no guard; and the blade shears through nearly anything it touches, including its wielder. A fumble is not a stumble — it is a severed limb (the blade does impaling + burning, and a critical miss strikes the wielder or an ally). For this reason the skill has no default: you do not pick up a lightsaber and improvise. You train, for years — or you bleed. This is the skill the Compendium’s Lightsaber parry math refers to (parry = skill ÷ 2 + 3), and upon which the Nine Forms are built. Specialties: none — configuration and style are expressed through the Forms, not separate skills; a shoto, a saberstaff, and a paired Jar’Kai set are all wielded on this one skill, with the relevant Form governing how.

Standard use Attack or parry with a lightsaber. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack or defence per turn. Rolls One attack roll; a parry as an active defence. Modifiers + for Forms & high skill; − for darkness without Force-sense, deceptive attacks.

The Force is the wielder’s true edge. A Force-sensitive uses precognition and battle-sense to place a weightless blade exactly — which is why the Jedi and Sith make it look effortless. A non-sensitive may learn Lightsaber, but works at a further −4 and suffers every fumble in full; without the Force to guide it, the elegant weapon is mostly a danger to its owner. The Nine Forms (Doc IV — the Living Force) are this skill’s techniques: they buy off Rapid-Strike and Deceptive penalties, grant the deflection of blaster bolts, and define each duelist’s style. A high Relative Skill Level here (the Mastery Ladder, Part I·B) is what separates a padawan from a blade-master. Lightwhips — flexible energy-blade weapons — also use Lightsaber, at a further familiarity penalty.

Broadsword
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5, other one-handed melee−4

One-handed swords and heavy single-edged blades wielded with one hand. In the galaxy this covers the Mandalorian beskad, the heavier vibroswords, cortosis war-blades, and ceremonial and dueling swords on a hundred worlds. The workhorse blade skill.

Standard use Attack or parry with a one-handed sword or vibrosword. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack/defence per turn. Rolls One attack roll; parry as a defence. Modifiers + for Weapon Master; − for posture & deceptive attacks.
Shortsword
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−4, other one-handed melee−4

Short, balanced one-handed blades and batons: short vibroblades, gladius-type swords, stun batons swung as clubs, and tonfa-style arms. Faster than a broadsword, deadly in close quarters.

Standard use Fight with a short blade or baton. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack/defence. Rolls One attack roll per attack. Modifiers + Weapon Master; − reach in close.
Two-Handed Sword
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5, Broadsword−4

Great blades gripped in both hands — large vibroswords, Mandalorian heavy blades, and the two-handed war-swords of various warrior cultures. Reach and power at the cost of guard.

Standard use Fight with a great two-handed blade. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack/defence. Rolls One attack roll. Modifiers + for reach & power; − in confined space.
Knife
Attribute DX · Difficulty Easy · Defaults DX−4, Shortsword−3, Lightsaber/Main-Gauche−3

Daggers and combat knives in the hand — the ubiquitous vibroknife and vibroshiv, survival knives, and the assassin’s blade. Everyone carries one; few master it.

Standard use Fight with a knife or vibroknife. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack/defence. Rolls One attack roll. Modifiers − reach vs longer weapons; + for surprise.
Axe / Mace
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5, Two-Handed Axe/Mace−4

One-handed axes, maces, and hammers, including vibro-axes and powered war-maces — weapons that crush and cleave where a blade would skitter off armour.

Standard use Fight with a one-handed axe or mace. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack/defence. Rolls One attack roll. Modifiers + vs armour (crushing); − cannot retreat-parry well.
Two-Handed Axe / Mace
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5, Axe/Mace−4

Great axes, war-mauls, and heavy two-handed crushing weapons — slow, but they break armour, bone, and battle-droid alike.

Standard use Fight with a great axe or maul. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack/defence. Rolls One attack roll. Modifiers + power vs armour; − slow recovery.
Polearm
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5, Spear−4, Staff−4

Long hafted weapons with a head — the force pike, the vibro-halberd, the IG-100 MagnaGuard’s electrostaff (wielded as a polearm), war-glaives, and vibro-lances on foot. Reach is life.

Standard use Fight with a polearm at reach. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack/defence. Rolls One attack roll. Modifiers + reach; − in close quarters.
Spear
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5, Polearm−4, Staff−2

Spears, lances, and thrusting polearms, including the Tusken gaderffii (gaffi stick) used point-first and the vibrospear. May be thrown (use Thrown Weapon).

Standard use Fight or throw with a spear. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack/defence. Rolls One attack roll. Modifiers + reach & set vs charge; − in close.
Staff
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5, Polearm−3, Spear−3

The quarterstaff and every two-ended hafted weapon — combat staves, the electrostaff and shockstaff swung double-ended, the gaderffii used as a staff, and the energy-bo. Famously good for defense.

Standard use Fight with a quarterstaff or combat staff. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack/defence. Rolls One attack roll; strong parry. Modifiers + to parry; − vs heavy crushing.
Whip
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5

Whips, shock-whips, and energy lashes — reach weapons that entangle, disarm, and stun. (The Force-powered lightwhip uses Lightsaber, not this skill.)

Standard use Strike, disarm, or entangle with a whip. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack. Rolls One attack roll. Modifiers + to disarm/grab; − for damage; ineffective vs armour.
Flail
Attribute DX · Difficulty Hard · Defaults DX−6, Kusari−4

Flails, meteor hammers, and chain weapons — hard to master because they can foul the wielder, but they wrap around shields and parries. Includes the kusari and weighted chain.

Standard use Fight with a flail or chain weapon. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack/defence. Rolls One attack roll. Modifiers + ignores shield/parry partly; − can foul the wielder.
Parrying Blades (Main-Gauche, Sai, Jitte, Tonfa)
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5; each defaults to its kin−3

The family of off-hand and parry-specialist melee weapons — main-gauche and parrying daggers, sai and jitte (built to catch and break blades), and tonfa (riot batons and stun-tonfa). Each is its own skill, defaulting to the others at −3; grouped here for brevity. Favoured by duelists, guards, and Echani martial artists.

Standard use Parry and trap with a defensive close weapon. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack/defence. Rolls One attack roll; strong parry. Modifiers + to parry & disarm; − reach.
Lance
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5

The couched lance of a mounted warrior — for beast-riders charging on a dewback, bantha, or tauntaun, and for repulsor-cavalry on speeder bikes. Delivers crushing or impaling force at the gallop.

Standard use Strike from a mount or vehicle with a lance. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack on the charge. Rolls One attack roll. Modifiers + on a charge (momentum); − useless when stationary.
Thrown & Projected Weapons
Thrown Weapon †
Attribute DX · Difficulty Easy · Defaults DX−4

Hurling a balanced weapon by hand. Specialise by form — Knife (thrown vibroknives, balanced blades), Axe/Mace, Spear (and gaderffii), Dart, Stick, and Disc (Mandalorian throwing blades, chakram, bolas). Each specialty is bought separately and defaults to its kin at −4.

Standard use Throw a balanced weapon at a target. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack. Rolls One attack roll per throw. Modifiers + to Aim; − for range & movement. Specialise by weapon.
Throwing
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−3

The general skill of throwing anything not built to be thrown — rocks, improvised objects, and, crucially, grenades and thermal detonators. Lobbing a live detonator into a squad and judging the bounce is a Throwing roll; a fumble drops it at your feet.

Standard use Throw an unbalanced object accurately. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack. Rolls One attack roll per throw. Modifiers − for awkward objects, range, movement.
Liquid Projector †
Attribute DX · Difficulty Easy · Defaults DX−4

Aiming a stream weapon — the Mandalorian gauntlet flamethrower, military flame projectors, and chemical or acid sprayers. Specialise in Flamethrower or Sprayer; a hit bathes the target in fire or caustic spray (see the relevant damage rules).

Standard use Use a flamer, sprayer, or liquid-stream weapon. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack; sustained streams possible. Rolls One attack roll per burst. Modifiers + at short range; − for wind; ignores some cover.
Net & Lasso
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5 each

Capture weapons — thrown and weighted nets (including stun and shock nets), the bounty hunter’s whipcord and grappling line, and the lasso. A hit entangles rather than wounds, pinning prey for capture alive. Net and Lasso are separate skills, grouped here.

Standard use Entangle a target with a net or thrown loop. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack. Rolls One attack roll; a Contest to hold. Modifiers + at short range; − for a fast or strong target.
Unarmed Combat
Brawling
Attribute DX · Difficulty Easy · Defaults DX−2

Rough, untrained fighting — punches, kicks, headbutts, and the natural weapons of clawed and fanged species (Wookiee fists, Trandoshan claws, Bothan teeth). Cheap, fast to learn, and surprisingly effective in a cantina.

Standard use Fight unarmed — punches, kicks, headbutts. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack/defence. Rolls One attack roll. Modifiers + at high skill; − vs armed or armoured foes.
Boxing
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults none

Trained pugilism — disciplined striking with the fists, with better reach and parry than brawling. The sport and the science of the punch.

Standard use Trained striking with the fists. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack/defence. Rolls One attack roll; can parry. Modifiers + to punch damage & parry; − no kicks.
Wrestling
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults none

Trained grappling — clinches, takedowns, and holds. Underpins the galaxy’s grappling arts and pairs with the Compendium’s grappling rules.

Standard use Grapple and control a foe with the whole body. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack/defence. Rolls A Contest to grapple, pin, or break free. Modifiers + for ST & size; − vs slippery or armed foes.
Sumo Wrestling
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults none

The art of shoving, off-balancing, and overpowering with mass — favoured by large and heavy-worlder species (Herglics, Houk, Besalisks) who fight to push, pin, and topple.

Standard use Shove, throw, and overbear with mass. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack. Rolls A Contest to push or down a foe. Modifiers + for ST & mass; − vs nimble foes.
Karate
Attribute DX · Difficulty Hard · Defaults none

Hard-style striking martial arts — the foundation of the galaxy’s great unarmed disciplines, most famously Teräs Käsi (the “steel hand,” said to let a master face even a lightsaber barehanded) and the striking forms of the Echani. A trained strike is a real weapon. Build named styles atop this skill as techniques.

Standard use Trained striking martial art. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack/defence. Rolls One attack roll; can parry. Modifiers + to damage at high skill; works with kicks & blocks.
Judo
Attribute DX · Difficulty Hard · Defaults none

Soft-style grappling martial arts — throws, locks, and the redirection of force. The Echani and the K’tara tradition draw on this. Excellent for disarming and for taking a foe alive.

Standard use Throwing-and-locking grappling art. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack/defence. Rolls A Contest to throw, lock, or counter. Modifiers + to throw & counter; great for parrying unarmed.

Cinematic & Meditative Martial Arts Skills

The disciplines of the trained master — most require Trained by a Master or a cinematic campaign, and several support Force-users as readily as unarmed artists. Each is its own skill, bought separately.

Immovable Stance
Attribute DX · Difficulty Hard · Defaults none

A Trained-by-a-Master rooting discipline. The practitioner sets their stance so completely that no ordinary force can shift them — useful against grapples, slams, shoves, and Force telekinesis alike.

Standard use Resist being moved, knocked down, or thrown. Time Reaction (no turn cost). Rolls Quick Contest of Immovable Stance vs the attacker's ST, slam, shove, or relevant Force roll. Modifiers Only while standing and aware; negated if surprised or off-balance.
Flying Leap
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults none

Channel inner focus into a single explosive bound. A cinematic art that turns a normal jump into a soaring leap far beyond mundane reach.

Standard use Multiply jumping distance for one leap. Time Concentrate 1 turn, then jump. Rolls One IQ-based Flying Leap roll; margin of success adds to jump distance. Modifiers Penalty for heavy encumbrance; cannot be hurried.
Light Walk
Attribute DX · Difficulty Hard · Defaults none

Move so lightly that the ground barely registers the passage — crossing fragile, slick, or noise-making surfaces as though weightless.

Standard use Tread silently and lightly across treacherous or fragile footing. Time Used as you move. Rolls DX-based Light Walk in place of Stealth/footing rolls. Modifiers Bonus to avoid triggering pressure plates, thin ice, dry leaves, etc.
Zen Archery
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Very Hard · Defaults none

A meditative ranged discipline. After centring, the archer or shooter looses without relying on sight — striking by intuition and the Force rather than the eye.

Standard use Make a ranged attack at an unseen or unsightable target. Time Concentrate 1 turn, then attack. Rolls Roll Zen Archery instead of the normal weapon skill for the shot. Modifiers Replaces blindness/darkness penalties with the standard −5 only.
Invisibility Art
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Very Hard · Defaults none

Cloud the minds of observers so they simply fail to notice the practitioner, even in plain sight. A cinematic mental art, not true invisibility.

Standard use Become unnoticed by living, sighted observers. Time Concentrate 1 turn to activate. Rolls Quick Contest of Invisibility Art vs each observer's Per. Modifiers Fails vs cameras/droids; aggressive action ends it.
Mental Strength
Attribute Will · Difficulty Easy · Defaults Will-4

Disciplined resistance of the mind against intrusion, domination, and mental attack — the trained counterpart to raw Will.

Standard use Resist mind control, fear, interrogation, and mental Force powers. Time Reaction (no turn cost). Rolls Roll Mental Strength in place of Will to resist a mental effect. Modifiers As per the attacking effect's modifiers.
Hypnotic Hands
Attribute DX · Difficulty Hard · Defaults none

Slow, rhythmic hand-weaving that lulls a single watching subject into a brief trance — a classic cinematic close-range mesmerism.

Standard use Entrance one watching, non-hostile subject. Time Concentrate 1 turn while gesturing. Rolls Quick Contest of Hypnotic Hands vs subject's Will. Modifiers Needs eye contact and free hands; one subject at a time.
Power Blow
Attribute Will · Difficulty Hard · Defaults none

Focus the body and spirit into a single devastating strike that shatters what an ordinary blow could not.

Standard use Multiply the damage of one melee or unarmed attack. Time Concentrate 1 turn before the attack. Rolls Power Blow roll; success at least doubles basic damage (more at higher margins). Modifiers Cannot be combined with most other concentrate-based feats.
Pressure Points
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults none

Strike nerve clusters and vital points to cause fatigue, numbness, or crippling far out of proportion to the force used.

Standard use Attack a pressure point for special effect rather than raw damage. Time One attack. Rolls Hit a targeted location, then roll Pressure Points for effect. Modifiers Useless against foes without a humanoid nervous system.
Pressure Secrets
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Very Hard · Defaults none

The advanced art beyond Pressure Points — delayed strikes, sustained crippling, and effects the untrained believe impossible.

Standard use Inflict delayed or advanced pressure-point effects. Time One attack. Rolls Hit the location, then roll Pressure Secrets for the chosen advanced effect. Modifiers Requires Pressure Points first; humanoid physiology only.
Breaking Blow
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults none

Concentrate force to break inanimate objects — boards, bricks, bars, weapons — ignoring much of their resistance.

Standard use Shatter or break an inanimate object. Time Concentrate 1 turn before striking. Rolls Breaking Blow roll; success ignores part of the object's DR. Modifiers Against living foes use Power Blow instead.
Kiai
Attribute HT · Difficulty Hard · Defaults none

A focusing shout that channels the whole body's energy — startling, staggering, or briefly stunning a foe through sheer presence.

Standard use Stun or unbalance a foe with a focused shout. Time One turn (the shout). Rolls Quick Contest of Kiai vs the target's Will. Modifiers Range a few metres; the deaf and the resolute are immune.
Blind Fighting
Attribute Per · Difficulty Very Hard · Defaults none

Fight effectively without sight, sensing foes by sound, air, and the Force rather than the eye.

Standard use Negate darkness and blindness penalties in melee. Time Used as you fight. Rolls Roll Blind Fighting each turn to ignore sight-based combat penalties. Modifiers Does not help with ranged attacks (see Zen Archery).
Body Control
Attribute HT · Difficulty Very Hard · Defaults none

Master involuntary functions — heartbeat, bleeding, temperature, and resistance to poison or shock — through deep trained discipline.

Standard use Control normally involuntary bodily processes. Time Concentrate as needed. Rolls HT-based Body Control roll for the chosen effect (slow heart, resist toxin, etc.). Modifiers Sustained effects may require repeated rolls.
Body Sense
Attribute DX · Difficulty Hard · Defaults DX-6, Acrobatics-3

Instantly reorient after teleportation, tumbling, or violent disorientation so the body knows up from down and acts at once.

Standard use Recover orientation after sudden displacement. Time Reaction. Rolls Body Sense roll on arrival; failure means stunned/penalised that turn. Modifiers Penalty for especially disorienting transitions.
Breath Control
Attribute HT · Difficulty Hard · Defaults none

Regulate the breath to hold air longer, steady the hands, and resist airborne poisons and the effects of thin atmosphere.

Standard use Hold breath or steady the body through breathing discipline. Time Concentrate 1 turn. Rolls Breath Control roll for the desired benefit. Modifiers Bonus to resist suffocation and inhaled agents.
Push
Attribute DX · Difficulty Hard · Defaults none

Deliver telekinetic-seeming knockback by touch or near-touch — moving a foe without injuring them, a hallmark cinematic art.

Standard use Knock a foe back without dealing injury. Time One attack. Rolls Push roll; treat margin as the ST of the shove for knockback. Modifiers No damage; purely a displacement effect.
Sensitivity
Attribute Per · Difficulty Hard · Defaults none

Perceive hostile intent, killing aura, and the subtle disturbances that precede violence — reading a room before a blade is drawn.

Standard use Sense imminent hostility or killing intent. Time Reaction or short concentration. Rolls Quick Contest of Sensitivity vs a hostile actor's Will/Stealth. Modifiers GM may give bonuses for strong, focused intent.
Autohypnosis
Attribute Will · Difficulty Hard · Defaults none

Place oneself into a trained trance to resist pain and fear, recall information perfectly, or steel the mind before an ordeal.

Standard use Self-induce a useful mental state. Time Concentrate 1 minute (less with skill). Rolls Autohypnosis roll for the chosen effect (ignore pain, perfect recall, etc.). Modifiers Cannot be used reactively against surprise.
Meditation
Attribute Will · Difficulty Hard · Defaults none

Enter a calm, centred state that restores focus, supports many Force and mental disciplines, and steadies the will.

Standard use Enter a meditative state to recover focus or prepare a working. Time Several minutes of stillness. Rolls Meditation roll to enter and sustain the trance. Modifiers Interruption may require a fresh roll.
Mind Block
Attribute Will · Difficulty Average · Defaults none

Shield the surface of the mind so that probes, empaths, and mind-readers find only silence — and hostile mental contacts meet resistance.

Standard use Conceal thoughts and resist mental detection. Time Reaction (no turn cost). Rolls Quick Contest of Mind Block vs the intruding power's skill. Modifiers Active concealment; a focused mind blocks better.
Parry Missile Weapons
Attribute DX · Difficulty Hard · Defaults none

Bat aside or catch incoming thrown weapons and slow projectiles barehanded — a signature cinematic feat of the trained master.

Standard use Parry a thrown weapon or slow missile unarmed. Time Reaction (a defence). Rolls Parry Missile Weapons roll as an active defence against the incoming attack. Modifiers Cannot stop blaster bolts or fast firearms — see lightsaber Deflect.

Master Weapon-to-Skill Reference

Every arm in common use, and the skill that wields it. When in doubt, match the weapon’s form, not its maker.

WeaponSkillNotes
Blaster pistol, hold-out, heavy blaster pistol, disruptor pistolBlasters (Pistol)Stun setting same skill.
Ion pistolIon Pistol (DX/A)Ion arc handling is its own discipline — no Blasters default.
Blaster rifle, carbine, heavy rifle, sniper, disruptor rifleBlasters (Rifle)
Ion carbine, ion rifleIon Rifle (DX/A)As Ion Pistol — separate skill, slightly harder.
Ion cannon (portable), mounted ion gunsIon Gunner (DX/A)Crew-served / braced ion weapons.
Wookiee bowcasterCrossbowHigh ST to cock; energy quarrel.
Slug pistol / rifle / scattergun / repeater / needlerSlugthrowers (by type)Kinetic; harder to trace.
Repeating blaster, E-Web, blaster cannon (portable), rocket/missile tube, grenade launcherHeavy WeaponsCrew-served / braced.
Ship & turret laser/turbolaser, vehicle cannon, concussion missiles, proton torpedoesGunner (by type)Mounted; capital/indirect = Artillery. Mounted ion = Ion Gunner.
Thermal detonator, grenades (thrown)ThrowingJudge the bounce.
Flamethrower (Mando gauntlet), chemical/acid sprayerLiquid Projector
Lightsaber, shoto, saberstaff, dual Jar’KaiLightsaber (VH)Style = the Forms (Doc IV).
LightwhipLightsaberExotic; familiarity penalty.
Vibrosword, beskad, war-blade (1-handed)Broadsword
Vibroblade (short), gladius, stun baton (swung)Shortsword
Heavy vibrosword, two-handed war-bladeTwo-Handed Sword
Vibroknife, vibroshiv, combat knifeKnifeThrowable: Thrown (Knife).
Vibro-axe, war-mace, powered hammerAxe/Mace (or Two-Handed)
Force pike, vibro-halberd, MagnaGuard electrostaffPolearm
Spear, vibrospear, gaderffii (point)Spear
Combat staff, electrostaff/shockstaff (double-ended), gaderffii (staff)Staff
Whip, shock-whipWhipLightwhip = Lightsaber.
Net, whipcord launcher, lasso, bolasNet & Lasso / Thrown (Disc)Capture, not kill.
Fists, claws, headbutt (untrained / species natural)BrawlingTrained: Boxing / Karate.
Teräs Käsi, Echani strikingKarate (+ techniques)Named styles as techniques.
Grapples, throws, holds; K’taraWrestling / JudoSee grappling rules.

Skill Defaults — rolling a weapon you haven't trained

Standard GURPS defaulting applies: if you lack a weapon's governing skill, roll the best alternative below. Guns specialties default to one another at −2; Beam Weapons specialties and cross-family swaps (blaster ↔ slugthrower of the same form) default at −4; any firearm falls back to DX−4 and any melee weapon to DX−5. Worked example: a standard blaster pistol rolls Blasters (Pistol), or Blasters (Rifle) −4, or Slugthrowers (Pistol) −4, or DX−4. The character sheet computes these live — hover the ⓘ beside any weapon.

Governing skillDefaults (best applies)
Blasters (Pistol)Blasters (Rifle) −4 · Slugthrowers (Pistol) −4 · DX−4
Blasters (Rifle)Blasters (Pistol) −4 · Slugthrowers (Rifle) −4 · DX−4
Slugthrowers (Pistol)Slugthrowers (Rifle) −2 · Blasters (Pistol) −4 · DX−4
Slugthrowers (Rifle)Slugthrowers (Shotgun) −2 · Slugthrowers (Pistol) −2 · Blasters (Rifle) −4 · DX−4
Slugthrowers (Shotgun)Slugthrowers (Rifle) −2 · DX−4
Gunner (Beams)DX−4 (crewed weapons don't borrow small-arms training)
Heavy Weapons (Rockets / Missiles)Heavy Weapons (Grenade Launcher) −4 · DX−4
Heavy Weapons (Grenade Launcher)Heavy Weapons (Rockets / Missiles) −4 · DX−4
Ion Pistol / Ion Rifle / Ion GunnerDX−4 only — ion arc handling is its own discipline; no Blasters default
Crossbow · Thrown WeaponDX−4
KnifeShortsword −3 · DX−4
ShortswordBroadsword −2 · Knife −4 · DX−5
Broadsword (incl. vibroblades)Shortsword −2 · Two-Handed Sword −4 · DX−5
Two-Handed SwordBroadsword −4 · DX−5
Saber (incl. fira)Shortsword −4 · Broadsword −4 · DX−5
Two-Handed Axe / MacePolearm −4 · DX−5
SpearStaff −2 · Polearm −4 · DX−5
StaffSpear −2 · Polearm −4 · DX−5
PolearmSpear −4 · Staff −4 · DX−5
BrawlingDX (everyone can throw a punch)
LightsaberNo default. The blade requires Trained by a Master (Doc I); untrained ignition is how museums acquire severed hands.

Part X · Piloting, Driving & Spacer Skills

The skills of moving through the galaxy — flying its ships, driving its speeders, and living in its hard vacuum. Vehicle-control skills are DX-based reflex; the command and lore skills of the spacer’s life lean on IQ. These port one-for-one from GURPS (Piloting, Driving, Spacer, Free Fall, Vacc Suit, Battlesuit, Shiphandling), in Star Wars dress. Astrogation — plotting the jump itself — lives back in Part IV·C.

Flying & Driving
Piloting †
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5, other Piloting specialty−2 to −4

Direct hands-on control of a flying craft — throttle, stick, and maneuver, in atmosphere and in space. The dogfighter’s and the bush-pilot’s skill. A successful roll holds a maneuver, threads a canyon, or wins a moment of a furball; failure stalls, scrapes, or overshoots. Specialise by hull class — the controls of a nimble snubfighter and a wallowing freighter have little in common:

Standard use Fly an aircraft, repulsorcraft, or starfighter. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Ongoing; one roll per maneuver or hazard. Rolls One roll per stunt, dogfight pass, or emergency. Modifiers + for a good craft; − for damage, weather, or evasion. Specialise by craft.
  • Starfighter: snubfighters, interceptors, bombers — the agile war-craft of space and atmosphere
  • Space Transport: freighters, light transports, shuttles — the everyday hulls a smuggler or trader flies
  • Airspeeder: atmospheric flying speeders and air-cars that ride high on repulsorlift
  • Capital Ship: direct helm control of a large warship or liner — ponderous; commanding her in battle is Shiphandling

Specialties default to each other at −2 (similar craft) to −4 (very different). A nav-computer or astromech aids the jump, not the flying.

Driving †
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5, other Driving specialty−4

Hands-on control of a ground or low-skimming vehicle — everything that rides the surface rather than the sky. A successful roll holds the line through a chase, a skid, or rough terrain. Specialise:

Standard use Drive a ground or repulsor vehicle. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Ongoing; one roll per hazard. Rolls One roll per stunt or hazard. Modifiers + for a known vehicle/road; − for terrain, speed, pursuit.
  • Repulsorlift: landspeeders and repulsor groundcars — the galaxy’s everyday transport, skimming low and fast
  • Speeder Bike: speeder bikes and racing swoops — brutally fast, lethally unforgiving (a botched roll at speed is a fireball)
  • Walker: AT-series and other legged war-machines — the “mecha” of the galaxy, balanced as much as steered
  • Wheeled / Tracked: groundcars, haulers, tanks, and treaded vehicles where wheels and tracks still turn

Specialties default to each other at −4.

Shiphandling †
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

The command of a large vessel in motion — not the helm directly, but the captain’s art of conning a capital ship or great transport through battle, traffic, and tight quarters: judging her turn, her momentum, and her crew. Keyed to IQ. Specialise in Starship (space) or Spaceship-of-the-line; this is the skill that fights a cruiser, where Piloting (Capital Ship) merely steers her.
