Standard use Command and maneuver a capital ship. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Ongoing; one roll per maneuver. Rolls One roll per maneuver or crisis; ship scale. Modifiers + with a trained crew; − for damage or unfamiliar vessel.
The Spacer’s Life — Zero-G, Vacuum & Powered Suits
Spacer
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Easy · Defaults IQ−4

The broad, hard-won familiarity of someone who has lived and worked in space — the feel of a ship’s routines and watches, reading her mood by the hum of her drives, knowing spaceport and customs procedure, finding your way around an unfamiliar hull, and the hundred small competencies of shipboard and station life. It will not fly the ship (that is Piloting) or fix her (that is Mechanic), but the spacer always knows what to grab, who to ask, and which warning light actually matters. The skill that marks the difference between a groundsider and a void-born.

Standard use The broad competence of life and work in space. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Varies. Rolls One roll per shipboard task. Modifiers + aboard a familiar ship; − in an emergency.
Free Fall
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5

Moving with control in zero or micro-gravity and in free fall — pushing cleanly off a bulkhead, crossing a weightless hold, fighting in null-g, and managing a controlled drop. Crucially, this also governs jetpack and rocketpack flight: the Mandalorian’s soaring arcs are Free Fall rolls, and a fumble aloft is a very public crash. Pairs with Vacc Suit for spacewalks.

Standard use Control your body in weightlessness or a fall. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Seconds per maneuver. Rolls One roll per maneuver. Modifiers + with handholds/thrusters; − tumbling or in a spin.
Vacc Suit
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5

Operating a spacesuit or sealed armour in vacuum — managing life support, moving without tearing or fouling the suit, and working with gloved hands in hard vacuum or hostile atmosphere. The EVA spacer’s survival skill, and what lets a sealed-armour warrior fight in the void. A failed roll fumbles a seal, a tether, or a step at a very bad moment.

Standard use Work effectively in a vacuum suit. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Ongoing. Rolls One roll per delicate task in-suit. Modifiers + for a good suit; −2 for fine work; − if the suit is damaged.
Battlesuit
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5, Vacc Suit−2

Operating powered armour in motion and in combat — moving with servo-assisted limbs as if they were your own, using the suit’s strength without crushing what you mean to hold, and bringing its integrated systems to bear. From Mandalorian powered shells to heavy assault armour, the suit amplifies a trained wearer and hobbles an untrained one.

Standard use Operate powered battle armour. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Ongoing. Rolls One roll per demanding maneuver. Modifiers + for a tuned suit; − if damaged or unfamiliar.

Where the rest live. Plotting a hyperspace jump is Astrogation (Part IV·C). Firing a ship’s guns is Gunner (Part IX). Keeping the drives running is Mechanic (Part II); slicing her computer is Computer Hacking (Part IV). A complete spacer is a small constellation of skills — which is exactly why a good crew divides them up.

Part XI · The Point Ledger — Buying Anything

The master rule of acquisition, in one place. Everything in this Compendium that grants capability has a character-point price — every skill, power, Form, technique, advantage, implant, even a personal starship or a tame war-droid. Training (time and deeds) is the default way to gain them in play; spending points is the always-available alternative. There is no ceiling: if a thing can be had at all, it can be bought, however extreme the number — a Tier V power or a full-body prosthesis costs a fortune in points, but the option is never closed. The campaign’s constant holds throughout: 200 hours of study = 1 character point, and a thing costs the same whether earned at the desk or bought outright.

The two ledgers, one price. Train it or buy it — the cost is identical. Training pays in time and story (work-hours, deeds, the slow climb); points pay in build budget. Most things in play are earned; but a player may always open the points ledger instead, at character creation or with earned points later.

The one thing points can never buy. A Crossing — the soul-turning deed at the heart of each Force tradition’s tier (The Path, Doc IV) — is never for sale. Points buy capability; they never buy the turning of the self. Everything else has a price.

The Master Cost Reference
What you are buyingPoint costNotes / where detailed
Skill (Easy → Very Hard)1 / 2 / 4 / 8 / 12… to attribute−2 / −1 / +0 / +1 / +2Standard GURPS progression by difficulty. The whole skill list, Parts II–X.
Attribute (ST/DX/IQ/HT, Force)per the attribute’s own costLifts every keyed skill — the broad, dear path the Mastery Ladder warns against.
Force power — Tier I / II / III / IV / V5 / 10 / 20 / 35 / 60 ptsA fixed permission; capped by your tradition tier. Doc IV; The Path §3.2.
Saber Form — per tier4 pts / tier (~16–20 a full Form)Atop the Lightsaber skill. The Nine Forms, Doc IV.
Form-Neutral Technique (Trakata, Sokan…)2–5 ptsBy prerequisite and power. Doc IV.
Tutaminis & other Force-skill disciplinesas a Very Hard skillThe roll is the skill; the mastery gate is Relative Force Level (Doc IV).
Tradition tier (Mastery capstones)buy-past surcharge: 10 / 15 / 20 pts eachStanding & the Crossing are never bought. The Path §3.3.
Bonded Blade signature5 ptsNeeds a bonded saber + a crystal that accepts you (a Sith forces it to listen). Doc IV.
Inscribed working — per tier3 pts / tier (+ proven in play)The proving is never waived. Doc IV.
Advantage / Perkits listed point costStandard GURPS traits (Combat Reflexes, High Pain Threshold, Wealth…).
Disadvantage / Quirkreturns points (negative cost)The dark traditions’ mandatory Disadvantages hand points back — the cheap climb’s hidden price.
Cybernetic implantsee the implant point table (below) — plus its credit costAn implant is bought twice: in credits (to obtain) and in points (the trait it grants). Doc VII.
Companion / owned being (droid PC, war-beast)as an Ally, by its own point total & frequencyA heroic droid is built on points like any character (Doc VIII).
Personal vehicle / starshipas a Signature Gear / Vehicle advantage, scaled to valueOwning the Ebon Hawk is an advantage, not just credits. Doc VI.
Cybernetics — The Point Price of the Machine

Every implant grants a mechanical benefit, so every implant has a point cost as well as its credit price (Doc VII). You pay credits to obtain and install it, and points for the advantage it becomes part of you — an implant that gives +2 ST costs the points of +2 ST, and so on. The drawbacks (Force tax, ion vulnerability, dependency) are taken as the matching Disadvantages, which return points — so a heavily-augmented cyborg both spends and recoups, and the net cost reflects how much of them is still flesh.

ImplantPoint cost (the trait it grants)Paired drawback (returns pts)
Biofeedback Regulator / neural socket1–5 pts (enabler / Payload-like)—
Skill / coordination chipas the skill it holds (fixed level)Sliceable (if networked): −pts
Sensory eye / ear (low-light → multi-spectrum)as the sense advantage (Night Vision, Hyperspectral…)Electronic (ion-vulnerable): −pts
Reflex Booster (+1 Basic Speed & Dodge)~20 pts (Enhanced Dodge + Basic Speed)Ion vulnerability
Regenerative / Cardio (extra HP, 2× healing)as Regeneration + extra HPDependency
Dermal weave (+2 DR) / subdermal plate (+4 DR)as Damage Resistance (Tough Skin / Sealed)Ion-vulnerable; heavy
Myomer muscle-weave (+1 → +6 ST)as the Lifting/Striking ST gainedIncreased Consumption; deepest grades add Force tax
Skeletal reinforcement (+1 → +3 internal DR)as DR (limited to torso/limbs) + Unbreakable Bones perkHeavy; mass toward Force-tax thresholds
Prosthetic limb (clinic → military → masterwork)restores lost function (0 pts) → +ST/Move/tools as advantagesForce tax; ion-vulnerable (masterwork halves tax)
Full-body prosthesisextreme — the sum of every advantage it grants (often 100+ pts)Heavy Force tax + massive ion vulnerability (large −pts)
The Force Tax (−1/−2/−3 to Force at 25%/50%/near-total cybernetic mass) is itself a Disadvantage for Force-users — it returns points, and is the mechanical face of “the less of you remains.”
The Extreme End

Nothing is off the table for points, and some things are meant to be ruinously expensive — the price is the point. A few markers of the far end, so a player knows the deep water is real and reachable:

  • A Tier V Force power: 60 pts and standing at Legend in a tradition that grants it — rarely sold, always at GM approval.
  • A full-body prosthesis: the summed cost of immense ST, DR, and integrated systems — often 100+ pts before the drawbacks return their share.
  • A capital starship as Signature Gear: scaled to its millions in credits — a campaign-defining advantage, not a casual buy.
  • Mastering a whole tradition (Tier V): the Crossing cannot be bought at all — the one hard wall. Everything around it can.

The principle, last of all. Train it or buy it — the door is always open, the price simply changes form. The only thing points cannot purchase is the turning of a soul; for everything else, name the cost and pay it.

Part XII · The General Skill List

The rest of the galaxy’s ordinary competences — the movement, social, knowledge, craft, and combat skills every being draws on, alongside the specialist trades the earlier parts detail. These are not a separate category or an appendix; they are simply the remaining skills of the setting, listed here in full so that everything a character might roll lives in one book. Each entry gives its governing attribute, difficulty, defaults, a standard use with its time and rolls, and the circumstances that help or hinder it — the same treatment as every other skill in this Codex.

Movement, Survival & the Body
Beast Riding
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5, Animal Handling−3

Controlling a living mount — the dewback, bantha, tauntaun, varactyl, or any saddle-broken beast across the galaxy’s wild worlds. A successful roll guides, urges, or stays a mount through difficulty; a failure may unseat the rider or panic the animal. The reflavour of the old Riding and Mount skills, for a setting where beasts are still ridden where machines cannot go. Specialise by the kind of beast where they differ greatly (a tauntaun is not a varactyl).

Standard use Control a living mount through difficulty. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Ongoing; one roll per challenge. Rolls One roll to guide, urge, or stay a mount. Modifiers + for a trained beast; − for a wild or panicked one. Specialise by beast.
Drop Operations
Attribute DX · Difficulty Easy · Defaults DX−4, Free Fall−3

The controlled descent — jump-troop drops, repulsor-pack landings, glide-chute and grav-chute work, and stepping off a gunship into a hot zone and arriving in one piece. The galaxy’s version of the old parachuting skill, for descents from dropships, towers, and orbit-skimming craft. Pairs with Free Fall for the weightless half of the fall.

Standard use Make a controlled descent by chute, pack, or glide. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Seconds to a minute per drop. Rolls One roll per descent. Modifiers + with good gear; − for wind, a hot zone, or low altitude.
Sealed Suits & Hostile Environments
Pressure Suit Ops
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5, Vacc Suit−2

Working in a sealed pressure or diving suit under crushing load — deep-water salvage, high-pressure gas worlds, and the heavy sealed rigs used where a vacc suit is not enough. Manage the seals, the buoyancy, and the slow grace of a pressurised rig. The deep-pressure cousin of Vacc Suit.

Standard use Work in a deep-pressure or diving suit. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Ongoing. Rolls One roll per task in-suit. Modifiers + for a good rig; − for depth or fine work.
Aquatic Breather Ops
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−4

Diving and working underwater with a breather rig — rebreathers, gill-packs, and the sealed dive gear of water worlds like Manaan and Mon Cala. Movement, gas management, and useful work beneath the surface. The galaxy’s reflavour of scuba work.

Standard use Dive and work underwater with a breather rig. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Ongoing. Rolls One roll per task; gas-management. Modifiers + in calm water; − for depth, current, fine work.
Hazard-Seal Suit
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5, Vacc Suit−2, Pressure Suit Ops−2

Operating a sealed hazard suit against chemical, biological, radiological, and toxic-atmosphere threats — the rig a slicer-saboteur, a plague-world medic, or a spice-refinery worker wears to survive what the air would do to bare skin. Pairs with Hazardous Materials for what is being handled.

Standard use Work in a sealed hazard suit against toxins/rads. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Ongoing. Rolls One roll per task; a fail risks exposure. Modifiers + for a good seal; − by hazard severity.
The Watercraft Trades
Watercraft Piloting
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5

Steering boats, skiffs, and surface watercraft on the galaxy’s seas and rivers — from a fishing skiff on Naboo to a hydro-skimmer on an ocean world. The reflavoured boating skill, for the many worlds where water still carries traffic.

Standard use Steer a boat or surface watercraft. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Ongoing. Rolls One roll per maneuver or hazard. Modifiers + in calm water; − for storms, rapids, pursuit.
Watercraft Crew
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

The deckhand’s and helmsman’s craft aboard a surface vessel — lines, watches, navigation by sea and star, and the working knowledge of a ship that floats rather than flies. The seafaring cousin of Spacer.

Standard use Crew a surface vessel — lines, helm, watch. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Ongoing. Rolls One roll per shipboard task. Modifiers + with a trained crew; − in heavy weather.
Submersible Piloting
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5, Watercraft Piloting−4

Piloting a submersible beneath the waves — the deep-diving craft of Manaan’s kolto harvesters, ocean-world explorers, and underwater salvagers. Depth, ballast, and the dark.

Standard use Pilot a submersible beneath the waves. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Ongoing. Rolls One roll per maneuver. Modifiers + with good instruments; − for depth or damage.
Aquatic Operations
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−4

The broad competence of someone who has lived and worked beneath the waves — submersible routines, deep-water hazard sense, and the procedures of underwater stations and harvest rigs. The aquatic mirror of Spacer.

Standard use The broad competence of underwater life and work. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Varies. Rolls One roll per underwater task. Modifiers + familiar waters; − in an emergency.
Recording, Spotting & the Helm
Holography
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−4

Capturing and composing holo-images and holo-recordings — the galaxy’s ubiquitous medium, from a smuggler’s evidence-cam to a Jedi archivist’s record to the propaganda holos of a Sith court. Framing, lighting in three dimensions, and the craft of a convincing (or faked) holo. The reflavour of photography for an age of light-sculpted images.

Standard use Capture or compose a holo-image or recording. Time 4 hours per attempt — Seconds to minutes per shot. Rolls One roll per shot or composition. Modifiers + with good gear & light; − for haste, motion, low light.
Targeting & Spotting
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Calling fire for someone else — spotting targets, relaying coordinates, and correcting indirect and orbital fire onto a point you can see but the gunner cannot. The forward observer’s skill, essential to Artillery and capital bombardment.

Standard use Spot targets and relay or correct fire for a gunner. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Per round of fire. Rolls One roll to call or correct fire. Modifiers + with clear line of sight & comms; − for smoke, range, movement.
Starship Crew
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5, Spacer−2

The working competence of a rated crew-member aboard a starship — standing a watch, running a station, handling lines and locks and loading, and the hundred routines that keep a vessel under way. Where Spacer is the broad feel of the void-born life and Piloting flies the ship, this is the trained hand that crews her.

Standard use Stand a watch or run a station aboard a starship. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Ongoing. Rolls One roll per station task. Modifiers + with training & a good ship; − in a crisis.
Combat & Physical Skills
Acrobatics
Attribute DX · Difficulty Hard · Defaults DX−6

Tumbling, flips, vaulting, and dramatic recovery — the body-control of an acrobat, free-runner, or blade-dancer. Grants a bonus to acrobatic dodges and to keeping your feet through chaos. The Echani and many duelists train it.

Standard use Tumble, flip, vault, or recover dramatically. Time 3 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Seconds per stunt. Rolls One roll per stunt; +2 to an acrobatic dodge. Modifiers + with room to move; − for cramped space or bulk.
Aerobatics
Attribute DX · Difficulty Hard · Defaults DX−6, Acrobatics−4

Acrobatic control of the body in flight — in low gravity, on a repulsor-board, or under a jetpack. Mid-air spins, recoveries, and stunts for the daredevil and the freefall combatant.

Standard use Acrobatic control of the body in flight. Time 3 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Seconds per maneuver. Rolls One roll per mid-air stunt. Modifiers + with thrust/wings; − for wind or a tumble.
Aquabatics
Attribute DX · Difficulty Hard · Defaults DX−6, Acrobatics−4

Acrobatic grace underwater — the swimming, twisting, fighting body-control of an aquatic species or a trained diver on a water world. The wet cousin of acrobatics.

Standard use Acrobatic grace underwater. Time 3 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Seconds per maneuver. Rolls One roll per stunt. Modifiers + for a swimmer’s build; − for current or gear.
Axe Mace
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5, Two-Handed Axe/Mace−4

One-handed axes, maces, and war-hammers, including vibro-axes and powered mauls. A crushing weapon for those who would rather break a foe than cut them.

Standard use Fight with a one-handed axe or mace. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack/defence. Rolls One attack roll. Modifiers + vs armour; − recovery.
Bolas
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5, Thrown Weapon−4

Throwing weighted cords that wrap a target’s legs and bring them down — a primitive but effective capture weapon, favoured by hunters and some bounty hunters.

Standard use Throw weighted cords to bring down a target. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack. Rolls One attack roll; entangles legs. Modifiers + short range; − vs fast or large targets.
Climbing
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5

Scaling walls, cliffs, rigging, and sheer surfaces with or without gear. One roll per significant pitch; the right tools and a rope ease it considerably.

Standard use Scale a wall, cliff, or sheer surface. Time 3 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Minutes per pitch. Rolls One roll per significant pitch. Modifiers + with rope & gear; − for sheer, wet, or overhanging surfaces.
Dropping
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−4

Dropping objects accurately onto a target from above — from a gantry, a balcony, or a moving craft. A niche but genuinely useful skill for ambushers and saboteurs.

Standard use Drop an object accurately onto a target below. Time 3 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack. Rolls One attack roll. Modifiers + from a stable perch; − for height & a moving target.
Environment Suit
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5, Vacc Suit−2

Operating a general protective environment suit — the sealed gear worn against heat, cold, dust, and thin atmosphere short of full vacuum. The everyday cousin of Vacc Suit for hostile but survivable worlds.

Standard use Work in a general protective environment suit. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Ongoing. Rolls One roll per delicate task. Modifiers + for a good suit; −2 for fine work.
Erotic Art
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5

The art of physical intimacy and seductive performance. A social and entertainment skill of certain professions and courts across the galaxy.

Standard use Seductive or intimate performance. Time 30 minutes per attempt — A scene. Rolls One roll per performance. Modifiers + for rapport; − for a hostile or wary subject.
Escape
Attribute DX · Difficulty Hard · Defaults DX−6

Slipping bonds, restraints, binders, and tight spaces — the contortionist’s and prisoner’s art of getting free of ropes, cuffs, and confinement.

Standard use Slip bonds, restraints, or a tight space. Time 3 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Minutes per attempt. Rolls One roll; a fail may tighten the bonds. Modifiers + for slack or oil; − for tight, locked, or expert binding.
Fast-Draw
Attribute DX · Difficulty Easy · Defaults none

Drawing a weapon, tool, or item so fast it costs no action on a success — the gunslinger’s and duelist’s edge in the first instant of a fight. Specialise by item type (blaster pistol, lightsaber, knife, ammo).

Standard use Ready a weapon or item instantly. Time 1 seconds (one turn) per attempt — A free action on success. Rolls One roll when you draw. Modifiers + with a quick-draw holster; − for an awkward carry.
Filch
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5, Pickpocket−4, Sleight of hand−5

Stealing small objects unnoticed — lifting an item from a shelf, a table, or a crate without being seen. The shoplifter’s and scavenger’s deft hand.

Standard use Lift a small object from a shelf or table unseen. Time 3 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Seconds. Rolls A Quick Contest vs onlookers’ Perception. Modifiers + for distraction/crowd; − for watchful eyes.
Force Whip
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5, Whip−3

A powered energy whip — a flexible lash of contained energy or shock-cord, less exotic than the Force-user’s lightwhip but a real weapon for entangling, disarming, and stunning at reach.

Standard use Strike or entangle with an energy whip. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack. Rolls One attack roll. Modifiers + to disarm/grab; ineffective vs heavy armour.
Forced Entry
Attribute DX · Difficulty Easy · Defaults none

Breaking through doors, hatches, windows, and barriers by main force — kicking, ramming, prying, and shouldering your way in fast. The breacher’s and raider’s skill.

Standard use Break through a door or barrier by force. Time 3 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Seconds to a minute. Rolls One roll; ST-based. Modifiers + with a ram/tool; − for a reinforced barrier.
Garrote
Attribute DX · Difficulty Easy · Defaults none

Strangling or choking a foe from behind with a cord, wire, or chain — the silent assassin’s killing tool. A successful surprise attack can incapacitate without a sound.

Standard use Strangle a foe from behind with a cord. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack from surprise. Rolls One attack roll; then a Contest to maintain. Modifiers + from total surprise; useless face-to-face.
Innate Attack
Attribute DX · Difficulty Easy · Defaults none

Aiming a natural or built-in ranged attack — a species’ spat venom or electrical discharge, a built-in weapon, or a Force-user’s aimed power. Specialise by the attack’s form (beam, projectile, breath, gaze).

Standard use Aim a natural or built-in ranged attack. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack. Rolls One attack roll. Modifiers + to Aim; − for range & movement. Specialise by attack.
Jitte Sai
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5

The family of blade-catching parrying weapons — jitte, sai, and trident-daggers built to trap, bind, and break an opponent’s weapon rather than to cut. The arms of guards, peace-keepers, and disarming specialists.

Standard use Parry and trap with a jitte or sai. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack/defence. Rolls One attack roll; strong parry. Modifiers + to disarm; − reach.
Jumping
Attribute DX · Difficulty Easy · Defaults none

Leaping farther and higher than a standing effort — clearing a gap, a barricade, or a chasm. The athletic difference between making the jump and falling short.

Standard use Leap a gap, barrier, or height. Time 3 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One action. Rolls One roll if the distance is challenging. Modifiers + with a run-up; − encumbered or from a stand.
Knot-Tying
Attribute DX · Difficulty Easy · Defaults none

Tying secure, useful, and quick-release knots — for climbing, binding a prisoner, lashing cargo, or rigging a trap. A small skill that saves lives and catches the careless.

Standard use Tie a secure or quick-release knot. Time 3 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Seconds to a minute. Rolls One roll if it matters. Modifiers + with good rope; − improvising.
Kusari
Attribute DX · Difficulty Hard · Defaults DX−6, Flail−4

The weighted chain, meteor hammer, and rope-dart — flexible reach-weapons that entangle, disarm, and strike around a guard. Hard to master and dangerous to the careless, but deadly in trained hands.

Standard use Fight with a weighted chain weapon. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack/defence. Rolls One attack roll. Modifiers + reach & entangle; − can foul wielder.
Lasso
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5

Catching a target with a thrown loop of cord or whipcord — the beast-handler’s and bounty-hunter’s capture tool, used to rope a fleeing mount, beast, or being.

Standard use Catch a target with a thrown loop. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack. Rolls One attack roll; Contest to hold. Modifiers + short range; − vs fast targets.
Leatherworking
Attribute DX · Difficulty Easy · Defaults none

Working hide, leather, and synth-leather — harness, straps, armour-backing, and goods. A traditional craft on frontier and primitive worlds.

Standard use Make or mend leather and hide goods. Time 4 hours per attempt — Hours per item. Rolls One roll per item. Modifiers + with tools; − improvising.
Main Gauche
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−4, Knife−3, Smallsword−3

The off-hand parrying dagger — held in the second hand to catch and turn an enemy’s blade while the main weapon works. Favoured by duelists, Echani blade-dancers, and anyone who fights two-handed without a second full sword.

Standard use Parry and stab with an off-hand dagger. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack/defence. Rolls One attack roll; good parry. Modifiers + as off-hand parry; − reach.
Monowire Whip
Attribute DX · Difficulty Hard · Defaults DX−6

A near-invisible monomolecular whip — a filament fine enough to shear through flesh and light armour, as dangerous to a clumsy wielder as to the target. A rare, illegal, and feared assassin’s weapon.

Standard use Strike with a monomolecular whip. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack. Rolls One attack roll. Modifiers cuts most armour; − extreme danger to a careless wielder on a fail.
Net
Attribute DX · Difficulty Hard · Defaults DX−6

Casting and managing a weighted or stun-net to entangle prey — the tool of beast-catchers, slavers, and arena fighters. A hit ensnares rather than wounds, dropping a foe for capture.

Standard use Entangle a target with a net. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack. Rolls One attack roll; Contest to escape. Modifiers + short range; − vs strong/fast foes.
Pickpocket
Attribute DX · Difficulty Hard · Defaults DX−6, Filch−4, Sleight of hand−5

Lifting items from a person without their knowledge — a credit-chit, a keycard, a holdout blaster. The classic thief’s art, opposed by the mark’s Perception.

Standard use Lift an item from a person unseen. Time 3 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Seconds. Rolls A Quick Contest vs the mark’s Perception. Modifiers + in a crowd/distraction; − for a wary or close-watched mark.
Rapier
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5, Saber−3, Smallsword−3

The light thrusting blade and the duelist’s art — vibrorapiers and fencing swords on the galaxy’s more decadent and ceremonial worlds. Speed and reach over power; a weapon of finesse and first blood.

Standard use Fence with a light thrusting blade. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack/defence. Rolls One attack roll; good parry. Modifiers + reach & speed; − vs heavy weapons.
Saber
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5, Rapier−3, Broadsword−4

The cavalry saber and curved cutting blade — a beast-rider’s and duelist’s sword, balanced for the cut from horseback or speeder. (Not to be confused with the lightsaber, which is its own Very Hard discipline.)

Standard use Fight with a cavalry/cutting sword. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack/defence. Rolls One attack roll. Modifiers + from a mount; − in close.
Sewing
Attribute DX · Difficulty Easy · Defaults none

Making and mending cloth and soft goods — robes, packs, field repairs, and improvised gear. A humble craft that keeps a wanderer clothed and equipped.

Standard use Make or mend cloth goods. Time 4 hours per attempt — Minutes to hours. Rolls One roll per item. Modifiers + with kit; − field repair.
Shield
Attribute DX · Difficulty Easy · Defaults DX−4

Using a shield — physical or energy — to block incoming attacks and cover yourself and allies. Covers the personal energy shields and riot shields of the era as well as old-fashioned plate.

Standard use Block attacks with a shield. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One defence per turn. Rolls A Block roll. Modifiers + vs ranged; − vs flanking attacks.
Sleight of hand
Attribute DX · Difficulty Hard · Defaults DX−6

Palming, concealing, and manipulating small objects unseen — the conjuror’s and cardsharp’s craft, useful for hiding a blade, switching a chip, or a misdirecting flourish.

Standard use Palm or manipulate a small object unseen. Time 3 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Seconds. Rolls A Quick Contest vs Perception. Modifiers + with misdirection; − for close scrutiny.
Smallsword
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5, Rapier−3, Main-Gauche−3

Light dueling and court swords — the small, fast blades worn where carrying a heavier weapon would be a provocation. The skill of the gentleman-spacer and the back-alley fencer.

Standard use Fence with a light court blade. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack/defence. Rolls One attack roll; good parry. Modifiers + speed; − vs heavy weapons.
Spear Thrower
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5

Using an atlatl or spear-thrower to launch a spear or dart far beyond arm’s strength — a primitive ranged skill found on low-tech worlds and among traditionalist hunters.

Standard use Launch a spear or dart with an atlatl. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack. Rolls One attack roll. Modifiers + range over hand-throw; − for movement.
Sports
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5

Playing an athletic game or sport — grav-ball, shockball, podracing-adjacent contests, and the countless games of the galaxy. Specialise by the sport.

Standard use Play an athletic game well. Time 30 minutes per attempt — A match. Rolls One roll per key play. Modifiers + for training; − vs better opposition. Specialise by sport.
Stage Combat
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5

Performing choreographed, theatrical fighting that looks lethal but is not — the entertainer’s and holo-actor’s craft. Also useful for faking a fight or a death.

Standard use Perform a fight that looks lethal but is not. Time 30 minutes per attempt — A scene. Rolls One roll per choreographed sequence. Modifiers + rehearsed; − improvised.
Stealth
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5, IQ−5

Moving silently and staying unseen — sneaking past sentries, shadowing a mark, and keeping to cover. One of the most-used skills in the galaxy, opposed by the watcher’s Perception.

Standard use Move unseen and unheard. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Ongoing; one roll per watcher. Rolls A Quick Contest vs each observer’s Perception. Modifiers + darkness, cover, slow pace; − hard surfaces, haste, bulk.
Throwing Art
Attribute DX · Difficulty Hard · Defaults DX−6, Thrown Weapon−4

The cinematic, precision art of throwing — pinpoint accuracy, trick throws, and pinning a foe’s sleeve to a wall. The dedicated specialist’s elevation of plain Thrown Weapon.

Standard use Cinematic precision throwing. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack. Rolls One attack roll. Modifiers + trick shots; pin sleeves, snuff flames.
Tonfa
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5

The tonfa and side-handle baton — the riot-stick and stun-baton of the galaxy’s peace-keepers and the Echani striking arts. Excellent for blocking, striking, and taking a foe alive.

Standard use Strike and block with a tonfa or baton. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack/defence. Rolls One attack roll; strong block. Modifiers + to subdue; − lethal damage.
2H Axe Mace
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5, Axe/Mace−4

Great axes, war-mauls, and two-handed crushing weapons swung with the whole body. The weapon that breaks armour, bone, and battle-droid where an edge would skitter away. Slow to recover, devastating on a hit.

Standard use Fight with a great axe or maul. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack/defence. Rolls One attack roll. Modifiers + vs armour; − recovery.
2H Flail
Attribute DX · Difficulty Hard · Defaults DX−6, Flail−4

The two-handed flail and great chain-weapon — hard to master because the head can foul its own wielder, but it wraps around shields and parries that would stop a rigid weapon. The province of a few dedicated warriors.

Standard use Fight with a two-handed flail. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack/defence. Rolls One attack roll. Modifiers + bypasses guard; − can foul wielder.
2H Sword
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average · Defaults DX−5, Two-Handed Axe/Mace−4

Wielding a great blade in both hands — the large vibroswords, Mandalorian war-blades, and two-handed cortosis swords of the galaxy’s heavy duelists. Reach and crushing power, paid for in guard. One roll to strike; the blade’s mass does the rest.

Standard use Fight with a two-handed sword. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — One attack/defence. Rolls One attack roll. Modifiers + reach & power; − confined space.
Typing
Attribute DX · Difficulty Easy · Defaults none

Entering text and commands quickly and accurately on a keypad or console. A small modern competence, occasionally the difference in a timed slicing job.

Standard use Enter text or commands quickly. Time 1 minutes per attempt — Per task. Rolls One roll if speed matters. Modifiers + familiar layout; − under stress.
Dancing
Attribute DX · Difficulty Average

Performing dances of many kinds — the courtly, the ceremonial, the seductive, and the cantina floor. The entertainer’s art, a fine cover, and on some worlds a martial discipline in disguise (the Echani are said to dance their duels).

Standard use Perform a dance. Time 30 minutes per attempt — A performance. Rolls One roll per performance. Modifiers + for training; − unfamiliar dance.
Knowledge, Social & Professional Skills
Accounting
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Keeping, reading, and auditing financial records — and spotting the fraud, the skimming, and the laundered credits hidden within them. The skill of the corporate auditor, the suspicious quartermaster, and the slicer following the money.

Standard use Read or audit financial records; spot fraud. Time 1 hour per attempt — Hours per set of books. Rolls One roll per audit. Modifiers + with full records; − for deliberately hidden fraud.
Acting
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Convincingly playing a role or feigning an emotion — passing as someone you are not, faking grief or loyalty, holding a cover under pressure. The infiltrator’s, spy’s, and con-artist’s core craft.

Standard use Play a role or feign an emotion convincingly. Time 30 minutes per attempt — A scene. Rolls A Quick Contest vs an observer’s IQ/Detect Lies. Modifiers + rehearsed/in costume; − for a suspicious audience.
Administration
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Running an organisation and navigating its bureaucracy — knowing which form, which official, and which procedure moves a thing along. The skill that gets a ship cleared, a prisoner released, or a requisition approved.

Standard use Navigate a bureaucracy to get something done. Time 30 minutes per attempt — Hours to days. Rolls One roll per request. Modifiers + with the right contacts; − for obstruction or red tape.
Anthropology
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Understanding the structure, customs, and beliefs of a culture — how a people are organised, what they value, and how to move among them without giving offence. Vital in a galaxy of thousands of species and societies.

Standard use Understand a culture’s structure and customs. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Minutes to recall; longer to study. Rolls One roll per question. Modifiers + with field time; − for an alien or hidden culture.
Archaeology
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Studying ancient ruins, artifacts, and dead cultures — reading a Rakatan vault, dating a Sith tomb, or recognising what a relic was for. The treasure-hunter’s and scholar’s lore of the deep past.

Standard use Identify or date a ruin, relic, or dead culture. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Minutes to identify; longer to excavate. Rolls One roll per find. Modifiers + with references; − for unknown civilisations.
Architecture
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Designing and understanding buildings and structures — how they stand, where they are weak, where the hidden spaces and service-ways run. Useful to a builder, a burglar, and a saboteur alike.

Standard use Understand or design a building and its weak points. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Minutes to assess; longer to design. Rolls One roll per structure. Modifiers + with plans; − from the outside only.
Area Knowledge
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Easy · Defaults IQ−4

Knowing the geography, people, factions, and rumours of a place — a city, a world, a sector. The local’s and the well-briefed agent’s sense of where things and people are. Specialise by area.

Standard use Recall the geography, people, and rumours of a place. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Instant recall. Rolls One roll per question. Modifiers + for your home area; − for somewhere you barely know. Specialise by area.
Artist
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Creating visual art — painting, sculpture, holo-design, illustration. The skill of beauty and forgery both. Specialise by medium.

Standard use Create a work of visual art (or judge/forge one). Time 4 hours per attempt — Hours to days. Rolls One roll per work. Modifiers + with good materials; − for unfamiliar media. Specialise by medium.
Astronomy
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Mapping and understanding stars, worlds, and astronomical phenomena — the science beneath astrogation, useful for predicting hazards and reading the deep sky. Distinct from the jump-plotting of Astrogation.

Standard use Map or interpret stars, worlds, and astronomical phenomena. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Minutes to observe; longer to analyse. Rolls One roll per observation or question. Modifiers + with a telescope/sensor; −2 without instruments.
Brain Hacking
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

The intrusive science of reading and rewriting a living mind by technological means — mind-probes, memory-wipes, and droid-assisted interrogation. A dark Czerka-and-Sith art, feared across the galaxy.

Standard use Read or rewrite a mind by technological means. Time 1 hour per attempt — Minutes to hours. Rolls A Contest vs the subject’s Will. Modifiers + with a mind-probe; − vs trained resistance; risks harm.
Camouflage
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Easy · Defaults IQ−4

Concealing yourself, others, or objects from view — blending a position into terrain, hiding a cache, breaking up an outline. The scout’s and ambusher’s craft, working with Stealth.

Standard use Hide a position, object, or person from view. Time 4 hours per attempt — Minutes to set. Rolls A Quick Contest vs searchers’ Perception. Modifiers + with natural cover/material; − on bare ground.
Carpentry
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Easy · Defaults IQ−4

Working wood and similar soft structural materials — building, framing, and repair on worlds where timber is still used. A humble frontier craft.

Standard use Build or repair in wood and soft materials. Time 4 hours per attempt — Hours. Rolls One roll per job. Modifiers + with tools; − improvising.
Cartography
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Making accurate maps and charts — of terrain, cities, tunnels, or star-lanes. The surveyor’s and explorer’s record of where things are.

Standard use Make an accurate map or chart of terrain, routes, or stars. Time 4 hours per attempt — Hours per area surveyed. Rolls One roll per map or survey. Modifiers + with survey instruments; −2 from memory or partial data.
Connoisseur
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Judging the quality and authenticity of fine things — art, wine, weapons, antiques. Telling the masterwork from the fake and the priceless from the merely old. Specialise by field.

Standard use Judge the quality or authenticity of fine goods. Time 5 minutes per attempt — Minutes. Rolls One roll per appraisal. Modifiers + for a known field; − for clever fakes. Specialise by field.
Cooking
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Preparing good food from what is at hand — morale aboard ship, a convincing cover as a galley-hand, or simply not poisoning the crew. A small skill that earns surprising goodwill.

Standard use Prepare good food from available ingredients. Time ~1 hour per meal. Rolls One roll per meal. Modifiers + with a galley & stock; − improvising in the field.
Counterfeiting
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Creating convincing fake currency, credit-chits, and valuables — and spotting the same. A serious crime in every system, and a lucrative one.

Standard use Produce fake currency or valuables, or detect them. Time 4 hours per attempt — Hours per batch. Rolls One roll per batch; opposed when passed. Modifiers + with proper equipment; − for hard-to-fake credits.
Criminology
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Understanding crime, criminals, and how the law pursues them — methods, patterns, and the workings of the underworld and those who police it. The investigator’s and the canny crook’s knowledge.

Standard use Understand crime, criminals, and investigation. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Minutes to recall. Rolls One roll per question. Modifiers + with case data; − for an unfamiliar underworld.
Current Affairs
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Easy · Defaults IQ−4

Knowing recent news and events in a field — politics, the underworld, a faction, a region. The well-connected being’s grasp of what just happened and who is talking about it. Specialise by field.

Standard use Recall recent news and events in a field. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Instant. Rolls One roll per question. Modifiers + if plugged into the feeds; − if isolated. Specialise by field.
Diagnosis
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Determining what is wrong with a patient — injury, illness, poison, or implant failure — before treatment begins. The physician’s first and most important roll, distinct from the treatment itself.

Standard use Determine what is wrong with a patient. Time 1 hour per attempt — Minutes. Rolls One roll before treatment. Modifiers + with a medscanner; − for rare or alien conditions.
Diplomacy
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Negotiating and influencing through reasoned, respectful persuasion — the honourable counterpart to Fast-Talk. The skill that brokers a truce, sways a council, or talks down a standoff.

Standard use Win a concession through courteous negotiation. Time 5 minutes per attempt — Minutes to a scene. Rolls One roll per negotiation; retry only with a new argument. Modifiers + strong position/shared interest; − hostility, weak hand, recent insult.
Disguise
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Altering appearance to pass as someone else or as a type of person — a different rank, species-passing where possible, or simply forgettable. Works with Makeup and Acting.

Standard use Alter appearance to pass as another or a type. Time ~10 minutes. Rolls A Quick Contest vs scrutiny. Modifiers + with Makeup & kit; − for species mismatch or close inspection.
Economics
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Understanding markets, trade, currency, and the forces that move wealth across the galaxy — the knowledge of a financier, a guild-master, or a smuggler reading where the profit is.

Standard use Understand and predict markets and trade. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Minutes to hours. Rolls One roll per analysis. Modifiers + with data; − for chaotic or rigged markets.
Electrician
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Installing, wiring, and repairing electrical and power systems — the practical trade beneath Electronics Repair, for power runs, lighting, and the mains of a building or ship.

Standard use Install or repair electrical/power systems. Time ~1 hour. Rolls One roll per job. Modifiers + with tools; − live or improvised work.
Expert Skill
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Deep specialist knowledge of a narrow field not covered by another skill — from Force lore to xenobiology to droid history. Specialise tightly; the GM defines what each covers.

Standard use Deep specialist knowledge of a narrow field. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Instant recall. Rolls One roll per question. Modifiers + with references; − at the edge of the field. Specialise tightly.
Farming
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Raising crops and managing land — the work of agriworlds and moisture farms across the galaxy. A common origin and a useful cover.

Standard use Raise crops and manage land. Time 4 hours per attempt — Seasonal; daily work. Rolls One roll per season or crisis. Modifiers + good land/gear; − for blight or poor soil.
Fast-Talk
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Deceiving or persuading quickly with a glib, confident tongue — talking past a guard, spinning a cover story, closing a shady deal before the mark thinks twice. The con’s edge, opposed by the listener’s IQ or Detect Lies.

Standard use Deceive or persuade quickly with a glib tongue. Time 5 minutes per attempt — Seconds to minutes. Rolls A Quick Contest vs IQ/Detect Lies. Modifiers + plausible story; − for an alert or hostile mark.
Finance
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

High-level management of money and investment — moving large sums, structuring deals, and the strategy of wealth. The skill of magnates, guild treasurers, and crime-lords laundering an empire.

Standard use Manage or move large sums; structure deals. Time 4 hours per attempt — Hours to days. Rolls One roll per deal. Modifiers + with institutions; − for scrutiny or laundering.
Forensics
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Analysing physical evidence from a scene — residue, wounds, tool-marks, and trace — to reconstruct what happened. The investigator’s science.

Standard use Reconstruct events from physical evidence. Time ~1 hour per scene. Rolls One roll per scene. Modifiers + with a lab; − for a contaminated scene.
Forgery
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Creating and detecting fake documents, credentials, seals, and identichips — the papers that get a being past a checkpoint or into a restricted dock. A daily tool of the spy and the smuggler.

Standard use Create a convincing fake document or credential, or spot one. Time 4 hours per attempt — Minutes to hours to forge. Rolls One roll to forge; a Quick Contest vs an inspector’s skill. Modifiers + with samples & gear; −2 to −5 for high-security documents.
Freight Handling
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

The trade of moving cargo — loading, manifesting, securing, and routing freight through docks and customs. The honest face of smuggling, and a useful cover for it.

Standard use Load, manifest, and route cargo. Time 4 hours per attempt — Per shipment. Rolls One roll per shipment. Modifiers + with proper gear; − for contraband or customs.
Gambling
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Playing games of chance and skill for stakes — sabacc, pazaak, dice — and reading the table, the odds, and the cheat across from you. The cantina staple.

Standard use Play games of chance/skill; spot cheating. Time 5 minutes per attempt — A session. Rolls One roll per session or big hand. Modifiers + for the odds; − vs a sharper. Detect cheating opposed.
Games
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Easy · Defaults IQ−4

Knowing and playing a non-athletic game well — sabacc, dejarik, pazaak, strategy boards. Specialise by game. A social lubricant and an occasional source of trouble.

Standard use Play a non-athletic game well. Time 30 minutes per attempt — A match. Rolls One roll per game. Modifiers + training; − vs a master. Specialise by game.
Gardening
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Easy · Defaults IQ−4

Tending plants, gardens, and grounds — ornamental, agricultural, or simply a convincing cover as the help. A quiet craft.

Standard use Tend plants and grounds. Time 4 hours per attempt — Daily. Rolls One roll per season. Modifiers + with the right climate; − for exotic flora.
Geography
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Knowing the lay of worlds and regions — terrain, climate, settlements, and routes. The traveller’s and strategist’s sense of the ground. Specialise by scope.

Standard use Recall terrain, settlements, and routes. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Instant. Rolls One roll per question. Modifiers + home region; − for far places. Specialise by scope.
Geology
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Reading rock, terrain, and mineral deposits — finding ore, water, and crystal, and judging whether ground is stable or about to give way. The prospector’s and miner’s science.

Standard use Read rock, terrain, and mineral deposits. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Minutes to survey. Rolls One roll per site. Modifiers + with a scanner; − for hidden strata.
Gesture
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Easy · Defaults IQ−4

Communicating by hand-signs and body language — silent battlefield signals, a shared thieves’ cant, or making yourself understood across a language barrier.

Standard use Communicate by signs and body language. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Seconds. Rolls One roll per message. Modifiers + shared code; − complex ideas.
Group Performance
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Directing or coordinating a group of performers — a troupe, a chorus, a propaganda spectacle. The skill of the showrunner and the rally-organiser.

Standard use Direct or coordinate a group of performers. Time 30 minutes per attempt — A production. Rolls One roll per performance. Modifiers + rehearsed troupe; − for a ragged group.
Hidden Lore
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Secret or restricted knowledge that the galaxy at large does not share — Sith history, the workings of a criminal syndicate, the truth behind a cover-up. Specialise tightly by subject.

Standard use Recall secret or restricted knowledge. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Instant. Rolls One roll per question. Modifiers + with a source; − for the deepest secrets. Specialise by subject.
History
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Knowing past events and their significance — the wars, dynasties, and turning-points that shaped the galaxy. The scholar’s grasp of how the present came to be. Specialise by era or region.

Standard use Recall past events and their significance. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Instant. Rolls One roll per question. Modifiers + for a known era; − for obscure or suppressed history.
Holdout
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Concealing a weapon or small object on your person — and spotting one concealed on another. The smuggler’s and bodyguard’s eye, opposed at a checkpoint by Search.

Standard use Conceal a weapon/object, or find one hidden. Time 5 minutes per attempt — Seconds. Rolls A Quick Contest vs Search at a checkpoint. Modifiers + small item/loose clothing; − for a thorough frisk.
Housekeeping
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Easy · Defaults IQ−4

Running a household or domestic space well — and the convincing cover of the unremarkable servant who is always present and never suspected.

Standard use Run a household; pass as unremarkable help. Time 4 hours per attempt — Daily. Rolls One roll per task. Modifiers + familiar household; − in a strange one.
Intelligence Analysis
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Turning raw information into usable intelligence — sifting reports, spotting the pattern, predicting the enemy’s move. The spymaster’s and war-planner’s craft.

Standard use Turn raw information into usable intelligence. Time 1 hour per attempt — Hours. Rolls One roll per assessment. Modifiers + with corroborating data; − for deception or noise.
Interrogation
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Extracting information from an unwilling subject — through questioning, pressure, and reading the answers. The grim skill of the inquisitor and the desperate; its methods vary from patient to brutal.

Standard use Extract information from an unwilling subject. Time 5 minutes per attempt — Minutes to hours. Rolls A Contest vs the subject’s Will. Modifiers + leverage/rapport or pressure; − vs trained resistance.
Law
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Knowing legal codes, rights, and procedure — Republic law, local statute, or the byzantine regulations of a Hutt court. The advocate’s knowledge and the careful criminal’s shield. Specialise by jurisdiction.

Standard use Recall legal codes, rights, and procedure. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Instant to minutes. Rolls One roll per point of law. Modifiers + with statutes to hand; − for an alien jurisdiction. Specialise.
Leadership
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Inspiring, directing, and holding together followers under stress — rallying a squad, steadying a frightened crew, getting people to follow you into danger. The officer’s and captain’s gift.

Standard use Inspire and direct followers under stress. Time 30 minutes per attempt — Per crisis. Rolls One roll to rally or command. Modifiers + for loyalty & reputation; − for fear or mistrust.
Linguistics
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Understanding the deep structure of languages — how they are built, how they relate, and how to crack an unknown tongue. The scholar’s science beneath mere fluency.

Standard use Analyse a language’s structure; crack an unknown tongue. Time about 1 work-day (~8h) per attempt — Hours to days. Rolls One roll per language. Modifiers + with samples; − for an isolate or alien grammar.
Literature
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Knowing written works, their authors, and their meaning — the educated being’s grasp of the galaxy’s letters. Specialise by tradition.

Standard use Recall written works and their context. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Instant. Rolls One roll per question. Modifiers + for a known tradition; − for the obscure.
Makeup
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Easy · Defaults IQ−4

Altering appearance cosmetically — ageing, disguise, species-passing where features allow, or simply looking the part. Works hand-in-hand with Disguise.

Standard use Alter appearance cosmetically; support disguise. Time ~10 minutes. Rolls One roll; aids Disguise. Modifiers + with a kit; − improvising.
Market Analysis
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Reading markets to predict where prices and demand will move — the trader’s and speculator’s edge, and the smuggler’s sense of what to carry and where to sell it.

Standard use Assess where a price is heading; buy/sell/hold. Time 1 hour snap; a day considered. Rolls One roll per market examined. Modifiers +2 local data/informant; −2 unfamiliar sector; −4 manipulated market.
Masonry
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Easy · Defaults IQ−4

Working stone and similar hard structural materials — building, cutting, and repair. A traditional craft on many worlds and in old fortifications.

Standard use Build or repair in stone. Time 4 hours per attempt — Hours. Rolls One roll per job. Modifiers + with tools; − improvising.
Merchant
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Buying, selling, appraising, and haggling — knowing what a thing is worth and getting the better end of the deal. The trader’s, fence’s, and bazaar-runner’s daily skill.

Standard use Buy, sell, appraise, and haggle. Time 5 minutes per attempt — Minutes per deal. Rolls A Quick Contest when haggling. Modifiers + knowing the goods; − for an unfamiliar market or shrewd trader.
Meteorology
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Predicting weather and atmospheric conditions — storms, seasons, and the violent skies of hostile worlds. Vital to pilots, farmers, and anyone caught in the open.

Standard use Predict coming weather. Time 4 hours per attempt — Minutes. Rolls One roll per forecast. Modifiers + with instruments/data; − for violent or alien climates.
Mimicry
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Imitating sounds, voices, and animal calls convincingly — faking a comm-voice, luring or calming a beast, or covering a noise. Specialise in speech or animal sounds.

Standard use Imitate a sound, voice, or animal call. Time 5 minutes per attempt — Seconds. Rolls A Quick Contest vs listeners. Modifiers + a heard sample; − for an unheard target. Specialise speech/animal.
Musical Composition
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Writing music — the creative craft behind performance, and the structure beneath a memorable theme, anthem, or cantina tune.

Standard use Write music. Time 2 hours per attempt — Hours to days. Rolls One roll per piece. Modifiers + with an instrument; − for an unfamiliar style.
Musical Instrument
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Playing a musical instrument well — the entertainer’s art and a reliable cover and income across the galaxy. Specialise by instrument.

Standard use Play an instrument well. Time 30 minutes per attempt — A performance. Rolls One roll per performance. Modifiers + a fine instrument; − sight-reading new work. Specialise.
Naturalist
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Understanding plants, animals, and ecosystems — what lives on a world, what is dangerous, what is edible, and how it all fits together. The field-biologist’s and survivalist’s lore.

Standard use Understand plants, animals, and ecosystems. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Minutes. Rolls One roll per question. Modifiers + familiar biome; − for alien life.
Navigation
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Finding your way and plotting a course across terrain, sea, or sky — the planetary counterpart to hyperspace Astrogation. Specialise by environment (land, sea, air).

Standard use Find your way and plot a course on a world. Time 30 minutes per attempt — Minutes to hours. Rolls One roll per leg. Modifiers + with instruments/charts; − in featureless or alien terrain. Specialise.
Packing
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Loading and balancing cargo on mounts, vehicles, and craft — getting the most aboard, keeping it secure, and knowing what a beast or hold can bear.

Standard use Load and balance loads on mounts/vehicles. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Minutes. Rolls One roll per load. Modifiers + proper gear; − overloading.
Paleontology
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

The study of ancient life — fossils, extinct species, and the deep biological past of a world. A scholarly science with occasional bearing on Sith and Rakatan bio-relics.

Standard use Study ancient life and fossils. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Minutes to recall. Rolls One roll per find. Modifiers + references; − for unknown taxa.
Panhandling
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Easy · Defaults IQ−4

Begging for money, aid, or sympathy effectively — the street-survivor’s craft, and a convincing cover for an agent who wants to be overlooked.

Standard use Beg for money, aid, or sympathy. Time 30 minutes per attempt — Minutes. Rolls One roll per attempt. Modifiers + a sympathetic crowd; − a hostile or jaded one.
Performance
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Entertaining an audience — theatre, holo-drama, oratory, spectacle. The professional entertainer’s skill, and a fine cover and source of access.

Standard use Entertain an audience. Time 30 minutes per attempt — A show. Rolls One roll per performance. Modifiers + a warm crowd; − a tough room.
Pharmacy
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Knowing drugs, medicines, stims, and their effects — preparing, dispensing, and recognising them. The galaxy’s legitimate apothecary and its spice-chemist share this knowledge. (Distinct from the SW Pharmacy & Poisons crafting skill, which makes them.)

Standard use Recall or dispense drugs and their effects. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Minutes. Rolls One roll per question. Modifiers + references; − for rare compounds.
Philosophy
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Reasoning about meaning, ethics, and existence — the contemplative tradition that underlies many a Force-school’s code and many a politician’s justification. Specialise by tradition.

Standard use Reason about meaning, ethics, existence. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Minutes. Rolls One roll per question. Modifiers + a known tradition; − for alien thought. Specialise.
Physiology
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Knowing how living bodies work — the science beneath medicine, surgery, and poison, and the key to where a body is vulnerable. Specialise by broad species type.

Standard use Know how living bodies work; find weak points. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Minutes. Rolls One roll per question. Modifiers + for a known species; − for alien anatomy.
Poetry
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Composing and reciting verse — the courtly art, the rabble-rousing chant, and the coded message hidden in a song.

Standard use Compose and recite verse. Time 30 minutes per attempt — Minutes to hours. Rolls One roll per piece. Modifiers + inspiration/form; − on demand.
Poisons
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Knowing toxins, venoms, and their delivery and effects — recognising, applying, and treating them. The assassin’s and the wary survivor’s knowledge. (The SW Pharmacy & Poisons skill covers manufacturing them.)

Standard use Recognise, apply, or treat toxins. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Minutes. Rolls One roll per use. Modifiers + a kit; − for exotic venoms.
Politics
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Working the levers of power and faction — alliances, leverage, and the unwritten rules of who owes whom. The senator’s, courtier’s, and crime-boss’s craft.

Standard use Work alliances, leverage, and faction. Time 30 minutes per attempt — Per maneuver. Rolls One roll per move. Modifiers + standing/contacts; − as an outsider.
Propaganda
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Shaping public opinion through media and message — the holo-broadcast, the rumour, the staged spectacle. A weapon of governments, rebellions, and Sith courts alike.

Standard use Shape public opinion through media. Time 30 minutes per attempt — Per campaign. Rolls One roll per message. Modifiers + control of the channels; − a sceptical audience.
Prospecting
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Finding valuable mineral, crystal, and resource deposits — reading the land and the scanner for where wealth lies. The frontier fortune-seeker’s skill.

Standard use Find valuable mineral or crystal deposits. Time 4 hours per attempt — Hours to days. Rolls One roll per site. Modifiers + a scanner; − for depleted or hidden ground.
Psychology
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Understanding minds, motives, and mental states — what drives a person, how they will react, and how to help or manipulate them. The counsellor’s and the manipulator’s insight. Specialise by broad species type.

Standard use Understand minds and motives; predict reactions. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Minutes. Rolls One roll per subject. Modifiers + observation time; − for an alien mind. Specialise.
Public Speaking
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Addressing and moving a crowd — the rally, the sermon, the courtroom, the senate floor. Distinct from one-to-one persuasion; this is the art of the many.

Standard use Address and move a crowd. Time 30 minutes per attempt — A speech. Rolls One roll per address. Modifiers + a friendly crowd & good acoustics; − a hostile one.
Research
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Finding information in archives, databases, and records — knowing where to look, how to cross-reference, and how to draw the answer out of a mountain of data. The scholar’s and slicer’s patient skill.

Standard use Find information in archives and databases. Time 2 hours per attempt — Hours. Rolls One roll per query. Modifiers + good archive access; − for buried or restricted data.
Savoir-Faire
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Easy · Defaults IQ−4

Knowing the etiquette and unwritten rules of a social class or group — high society, the military, the underworld, a court. Moving smoothly where an outsider would give themselves away. Specialise by milieu.

Standard use Move smoothly within a social class or group. Time 5 minutes per attempt — Per situation. Rolls One roll to avoid a faux pas. Modifiers + for your own milieu; − in an alien one. Specialise.
Shadowing
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Following a person or vehicle without being noticed — tailing a mark through a crowd or traffic, blending and breaking off. The surveillance agent’s craft, opposed by the target’s Perception.

Standard use Follow a mark without being noticed. Time 30 minutes per attempt — Ongoing. Rolls A Quick Contest vs the mark’s Perception each leg. Modifiers + a crowd; − empty streets or a wary mark.
Smuggling
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Moving contraband past inspection — knowing where to hide it, how to route it, and how to read the inspector. The defining skill of the galaxy’s favourite rogues.

Standard use Move contraband past inspection. Time 30 minutes per attempt — Per shipment. Rolls A Quick Contest vs inspectors. Modifiers + a good hide/bribe; − tight security or a tip-off.
Sociology
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Understanding how whole societies function — their classes, institutions, and pressures, and how they change. The big-picture counterpart to anthropology and psychology.

Standard use Understand how a whole society functions. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Minutes. Rolls One roll per question. Modifiers + study time; − for an alien society.
Soldier
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

The broad competence of military life — drill, field-craft, the care of gear, the routines of a unit, and how an army works. The trooper’s baseline.

Standard use The baseline competence of military life. Time 4 hours per attempt — Varies. Rolls One roll per routine. Modifiers + unit training; − green or alone.
Speed-Reading
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Reading and absorbing text far faster than normal — getting through a datapad of reports, a manual, or an archive in a fraction of the time.

Standard use Read and absorb text far faster than normal. Time 10 minutes per attempt — Minutes. Rolls One roll per document. Modifiers + familiar subject; − dense or technical text.
Strategy
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Planning campaigns and large-scale operations — the general’s and admiral’s art of moving forces, resources, and time toward victory. Distinct from the moment-to-moment of Tactics. Specialise by theatre (land, space).

Standard use Plan a campaign or large operation. Time about 1 hour per attempt — planning a campaign or operation is extended work, hours to days. Rolls One roll per plan. Modifiers + good intelligence; − for fog of war. Specialise by theatre.
Streetwise
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Navigating the criminal underworld — finding the fence, the fixer, the safe-house, and the right palm to grease, without ending up dead or arrested. The survivor’s knowledge of the galaxy’s underside.

Standard use Find the fixer, fence, or safe-house. Time 5 minutes per attempt — Minutes to hours. Rolls One roll per contact sought. Modifiers + local rep; − a strange city or hostile turf.
Tactics
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Maneuvering forces in a fight for advantage — positioning, timing, and the small-unit cunning that wins a skirmish. The combat-leader’s edge, distinct from grand Strategy.

Standard use Maneuver forces in a fight for advantage. Time about 10 minutes per attempt — setting up an engagement; longer to plan a complex battle. Rolls One roll for the setup or a key moment. Modifiers + knowing the ground/enemy; − an ambush.
Teaching
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Instructing others in a skill or subject effectively — the master’s ability to pass on what they know, which underlies all apprenticeship and training across the galaxy.

Standard use Instruct another in a skill or subject. Time about 1 work-day (~8h) per attempt — Sessions over time. Rolls One roll per course of study. Modifiers + good materials & a willing student; − a hostile or slow one.
Teamster
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Driving and managing teams of draft animals or beasts of burden — the wrangler’s craft on worlds where living muscle still hauls the load. Specialise by animal.

Standard use Drive and manage a team of draft beasts. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Ongoing. Rolls One roll per haul or crisis. Modifiers + a trained team; − spooked animals. Specialise by animal.
Traps
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Building, finding, and disarming traps — from a snare in the wild to a tripwire-charge in a corridor to a security-rigged door. The dungeon-delver’s and saboteur’s wary skill.

Standard use Build, find, or disarm a trap. Time 4 hours per attempt — Minutes per trap. Rolls One roll per trap. Modifiers + proper tools/time; − rushed or improvised.
Ventriloquism
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Hard · Defaults IQ−6

Throwing your voice convincingly so it seems to come from elsewhere — a distraction, a deception, or a way to seem to be where you are not.

Standard use Throw your voice convincingly. Time 5 minutes per attempt — Seconds. Rolls A Quick Contest vs listeners. Modifiers + a distraction; − close scrutiny.
Weather Sense
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Reading the sky and the air to predict coming weather without instruments — the spacer’s, sailor’s, and frontier-dweller’s instinct for the storm ahead.

Standard use Read the sky to predict weather without instruments. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Minutes. Rolls One roll per forecast. Modifiers + a familiar climate; − an alien one.
Writing
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Composing clear, effective prose — reports, letters, propaganda, fiction, or a convincing forged dispatch. The educated being’s most basic tool of influence.

Standard use Compose clear, effective prose. Time 2 hours per attempt — Minutes to hours. Rolls One roll per piece. Modifiers + a known form; − an unfamiliar register.
Animal Handling
Attribute IQ · Difficulty Average · Defaults IQ−5

Training, directing, and calming animals — beasts of burden, war-beasts, mounts, and the dangerous fauna a being might tame or face. Specialise by animal type.

Standard use Train, direct, or calm an animal. Time 30 minutes per attempt — Minutes per attempt; longer to train. Rolls A Contest vs the animal’s will. Modifiers + a tame beast; − a wild or hostile one. Specialise by type.
Endurance, Perception & Will Skills
Carousing
Attribute HT · Difficulty Easy · Defaults HT−4

Socialising, drinking, and revelling well — holding your liquor, working a cantina, and earning goodwill (and information) in the galaxy’s endless bars and feasts. A reaction bonus among the carousing crowd, and the occasional rough morning.

Standard use Socialise and revel to earn goodwill or information. Time 30 minutes per attempt — An evening. Rolls One roll per session. Modifiers + buying rounds; − a hostile crowd; risks a rough morning.
Flight
Attribute HT · Difficulty Average · Defaults HT−4

Sustained flight under your own power or wings — for winged species and those with flight-granting gear or Force support. The endurance and control of staying aloft, distinct from a vehicle’s Piloting.

Standard use Sustained flight under your own power or wings. Time 1 hour per attempt — Ongoing. Rolls One roll per demanding maneuver. Modifiers + open air; − wind, weight, fatigue.
Hiking
Attribute HT · Difficulty Average · Defaults HT−5

Covering long distances on foot without breaking down — the marcher’s and explorer’s endurance over rough ground and long days. A successful roll lets you keep a hard pace longer than the unfit.

Standard use Cover long distances on foot. Time 1 hour per attempt — Per day’s march. Rolls One roll per day. Modifiers + good ground/gear; − rough terrain or heavy load.
Lifting
Attribute HT · Difficulty Average · Defaults HT−4

Applying your full strength over time without injury — the deadlift, the long carry, the heave that strains the body. The skill of getting the most out of raw ST in a sustained effort.

Standard use Apply full strength over time without injury. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Seconds to minutes. Rolls One roll per major effort. Modifiers + braced/leveraged; − awkward load.
Running
Attribute HT · Difficulty Average · Defaults HT−5

Sustaining speed over distance — the sprinter’s and pursuer’s wind. Improves your effective Move in a chase and lets you keep running when others must stop.

Standard use Sustain speed over distance. Time 1 hour per attempt — Per chase. Rolls One roll per extended run. Modifiers + for training; − for terrain or fatigue.
Sex Appeal
Attribute HT · Difficulty Average · Defaults HT−3

Influencing others through physical attraction and charm — the first-impression power of presence and allure. A social tool, opposed by the target’s Will.

Standard use Influence another through attraction. Time 5 minutes per attempt — Minutes. Rolls A Quick Contest vs the target’s Will. Modifiers + rapport/grooming; − a wary or uninterested target.
Singing
Attribute HT · Difficulty Easy · Defaults HT−4

Singing well — the entertainer’s art, a soothing or rousing voice, and a reliable cover and source of coin across the galaxy.

Standard use Sing well. Time 30 minutes per attempt — A performance. Rolls One roll per performance. Modifiers + a fine voice/song; − a tough crowd.
Swimming
Attribute HT · Difficulty Easy · Defaults HT−4

Moving and staying afloat in water — crossing a river, surviving a wreck, or fighting in the shallows. Vital on the galaxy’s water worlds and a quiet killer on the rest.

Standard use Move and stay afloat in water. Time 1 hour per attempt — Ongoing. Rolls One roll per crossing or crisis. Modifiers + calm water; − current, gear, exhaustion.
Body Language
Attribute Per · Difficulty Average · Defaults Per−4, Detect Lies−4, Psychology−4

Reading a being’s posture, tells, and unconscious movement — sensing tension, deception, or intent before a word is spoken. The interrogator’s, gambler’s, and bodyguard’s edge.

Standard use Read posture and tells for intent or deception. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Seconds. Rolls A Quick Contest vs the subject. Modifiers + observation time; − a trained or alien subject.
Detect Lies
Attribute Per · Difficulty Hard · Defaults Per−6, Body Language−4, Psychology−4

Telling when someone is lying — reading the micro-tells, the inconsistencies, and the false notes. Opposed by the liar’s Acting or Fast-Talk; the truth-seeker’s essential sense.

Standard use Tell when someone is lying. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — During a conversation. Rolls A Quick Contest vs Acting/Fast-Talk. Modifiers + knowing the person; − a skilled liar.
Fishing
Attribute Per · Difficulty Easy · Defaults Per−4

Catching fish and aquatic life by line, net, or trap — a survival staple and a quiet living on water worlds.

Standard use Catch fish or aquatic life. Time 2 hours per attempt — Hours. Rolls One roll per attempt. Modifiers + good gear/spot; − barren or unfamiliar waters.
Lip Reading
Attribute Per · Difficulty Average · Defaults Per−10

Reading speech from the movement of lips at a distance — eavesdropping across a room or through a viewport where no sound carries. The surveillance agent’s trick.

Standard use Read speech from lip movement at a distance. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — During observation. Rolls One roll per exchange. Modifiers + a clear view; − distance, angle, an alien mouth.
Observation
Attribute Per · Difficulty Average · Defaults Per−5, Shadowing−5

Watching a scene or area effectively — noticing what matters, spotting the lookout, the tail, or the thing that is subtly wrong. The scout’s and detective’s active attention, distinct from simple Search.

Standard use Watch a scene and notice what matters. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Seconds to minutes. Rolls One roll per scene. Modifiers + time & vantage; − distraction or concealment.
Scrounging
Attribute Per · Difficulty Easy · Defaults Per−4

Finding or improvising needed items from junk, ruins, and unlikely places — the salvager’s and survivor’s knack for turning up exactly the part, tool, or scrap the moment demands.

Standard use Find or improvise a needed item from junk. Time 10 seconds (one turn) per attempt — Minutes to hours. Rolls One roll per item sought. Modifiers + a rich pile; − barren surroundings.
Search
Attribute Per · Difficulty Average · Defaults Per−5

Methodically finding hidden objects, compartments, and people — frisking a suspect, tossing a room, or sweeping a ship for contraband. Opposed by Holdout and Camouflage.
